VOID WAR ENCOUNTER CHEATSHEET

Use the search bar on the left to search for encounters by typing in text from the dialogue box.

Some generic encounters with no choices have been omitted for brevity.

Contact recalcitr.ant on Discord or create an issue report on Github if you find an encounter with choices that is missing from the cheatsheet.

Up to date as of Release v1.2.6a - Long March.

AbandonedResearch

oTxtAbandonedResearch_intro

There is an abandoned research facility here. Entry to the interior sections is barred by heavy blast doors, but power may be temporarily restored to the facility by reactivating its backup generators. Even so, there would be no guarantee the security measures could be bypassed.

  • −5 missiles
  1. Activate the backup generators to access the interior (Spend 5 Munitions)
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtAbandonedResearch_activate1B

      You restore power to the facility systems and open the blast doors. You recover the advanced technologies within.

      • Random ship module
      1. Continue
    • oTxtAbandonedResearch_activate1A

      Unfortunately you were not able to defeat the security protocols before power ran out. You may attempt to disable the security systems again, but you will need additional time.

      • −10 missiles
      1. Activate the backup generators a second time (Spend 10 Munitions)
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtAbandonedResearch_activate2A

          You manage to disable the security systems. You discover some advanced technologies within the interior sections of the facility.

          • Random ship module
          1. Continue
        • oTxtAbandonedResearch_activate2B

          The security systems have been disabled. You recover some advanced technology found deeper inside the facility. Unfortunately, a malefic being has also followed you back to your ship.

          • Random ship module
          • 1 Demonic Crew boarder
          1. Continue
      2. Leave
        oTxtAbandonedResearch_activate1Leave

        It would not be worthwhile to make another attempt. You move on.

        1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtAbandonedResearch_leave

    Whatever is inside is not worth the cost of finding out. You move on.

    1. Continue
  3. (Machine Priest) Circumvent the blast doors to access the interior
    oTxtAbandonedResearch_activateBlue

    Your machine priest crewman divines the critical point of weakness in the blast doors and dismantles them. You recover the advanced technologies within.

    • Random ship module
    1. Continue

AbandonedShipDemons

oTxtAbandonedShipDemons_intro

You come across an abandoned starship. It looks like it may have useful material aboard.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtAbandonedShipDemons_investigateB

      You board the ship and manage to secure a few supply containers.

      • Random moderate reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtAbandonedShipDemons_investigateC

      You board the ship to see if there is anything worth salvaging. Unfortunately everything on board appears to be contaminated with a strange corruption and cannot be recovered.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtAbandonedShipDemons_investigateE

      There is something wrong with the starship. Malign energy radiates from its contorted structure as you detect rising energy levels from its engines. Its weapons systems stir to life and the possessed vessel turns to attack.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Possessed ship
    • oTxtAbandonedShipDemons_investigateD

      You board the starship and encounter a delirious crewman. Something seems amiss. The crewman is barely coherent. "They killed everyone..."

      1. Bring him back to your ship
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtAbandonedShipDemons_bringBack1

          You bring the crewman back to your ship. After a few hours, he seems to calm down. He is grateful for your rescue and decides to join your crew.

          • Imperial Recruit
          1. Continue
        • oTxtAbandonedShipDemons_bringBack2

          You escort the crewman back to your ship. Suddenly his eyes begin to bleed. He screams one last time before his chest erupts in a shower of gore. "The monsters... they are here!"

          • 2–4 Demonic Crew boarders
          1. Continue
      2. Leave him
        oTxtAbandonedShipDemons_leaveHim

        You decide there is nothing you can do for him. You leave him to his fate.

        1. Continue
  2. Move on
    oTxtAbandonedShipDemons_moveOn

    You decide to move on.

    1. Continue

AllVaultsSecuredTest

oTxtAllVaultsSecuredTest Unreachable — not referenced by any event

ALl VAULTS SECURED

  1. Continue

AncientHuskRack

oTxtAncientHuskRack_intro

You discover a husk production facility within an abandoned mining outpost. It can be reactivated, but the machinery requires chemical fuels.

  • −5 missiles
  1. Reactivate husk production (Spend 5 Munitions)
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtAncientHuskRack_activate1B

      The biomass used by the production line has long denatured. You unseal the delivery capsule to discover not a husk, but an ulcerated torso. Nevertheless, it is animated and capable of obeying commands.

      • Groveler
      1. Continue
    • oTxtAncientHuskRack_activate1A

      You restart the machinery using missile propellants. The production line rumbles and produces a newly-formed husk.

      • Husk
      • −3 missiles
      1. Activate husk production a second time (spend 3 Munitions)
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtAncientHuskRack_activate2C

          You initiate husk production again. The machinery malfunctions and delivers a berserk husk.

          • 1 Renegade Husk boarder
          1. Continue
        • oTxtAncientHuskRack_activate2B

          You initiate husk production again. The machinery rumbles and produces another husk.

          • Husk
          1. Continue
            oTxtAncientHuskRack_activate2B_1

            A loud grinding echoes throughout the outpost as the husk production machinery fails. This facility will not be able to produce any more husks.

            1. Continue
        • oTxtAncientHuskRack_activate2A

          You initiate husk production again. The machinery rumbles and produces another husk.

          • Husk
          • −2 missiles
          1. Activate husk production a third time (spend 2 Munitions)
            Random outcome (1/4 each):
            • oTxtAncientHuskRack_activate3C

              You continue husk production. The machinery malfunctions and delivers a berserk husk.

              • 1 Renegade Husk boarder
              1. Continue
            • oTxtAncientHuskRack_activate3D

              You continue husk production. The machinery malfunctions and produces a number of hostile animated biomass.

              • 3–5 Greater Corpulent or Ghoul Magus boarders
              1. Continue
            • oTxtAncientHuskRack_activate3B

              You continue husk production. The machinery rumbles and produces another husk.

              • Husk
              1. Continue
                oTxtAncientHuskRack_activate3B_1

                The facility has run out of biomass. It will not be able to produce any more husks.

                1. Continue
            • oTxtAncientHuskRack_activate3A

              You continue husk production. The machinery rumbles and produces another husk.

              • Husk
              1. Continue
                oTxtAncientHuskRack_activate3A_1

                A loud grinding echoes throughout the outpost as the husk production machinery fails.This facility will not be able to produce any more husks.

                1. Continue
          2. Leave
            oTxtAncientHuskRack_activate1Leave

            The machinery within the outpost falls silent as the generators consume the remaining fuel. You proceed onwards.

            1. Continue
      2. Leave
        → oTxtAncientHuskRack_activate1Leave
  2. (Flux-Priest) Restart production by alternate means
    oTxtAncientHuskRack_bt_fluxPriest

    You dispatch a flux-priest from your crew to manage the reactivation of the husk production line. Soon, the production facility is producing husks again.

    1. Continue
      oTxtAncientHuskRack_bt_fluxPriest2

      You are able to collect a few before your flux-priest shuts down the production line again due to the decayed state of the biomass stocks. You depart from the facility.

      • Husk
      • Husk
      • Husk
      1. Continue
  3. Leave
    oTxtAncientHuskRack_leave

    The discovery is of immaterial value to you. You move on.

    1. Continue

AncientHuskRack_activate2B

→ oTxtAncientHuskRack_activate2B_1

AncientHuskRack_activate3A

→ oTxtAncientHuskRack_activate3A_1

AncientHuskRack_activate3B

→ oTxtAncientHuskRack_activate3B_1

AncientHuskRack_bt

→ oTxtAncientHuskRack_bt_fluxPriest

AnthrochimeraSighting

oTxtAnthrochimeraSighting_intro

You find a badly damaged raider vessel near an auburn planet in the system you enter. The surviving crew are all shellshocked and badly traumatised. Delirious with fever and horror, the ship's captain explains that they were attacked by a horrific monstrosity. It explains the damage of the ship -- parts of it seem to be crushed and contused, the hull twisted and torn. Unusual battle-scars.

  1. Continue
    oTxtAnthrochimeraSighting_intro2

    The captain, wild-eyed, grabs you and begs you to go see if any of his comrades survived the encounter. He hands you a small data-container holding coordinates.

    1. Continue
      oTxtAnthrochimeraSighting_intro3

      Suddenly, the captain begins hyperventilating. Blood begins to gush from his eyes, ears, nose and mouth. He panics and screams and the bleeding becomes supernaturally profuse. In a few moments, the man is almost completely exsanguinated.

      1. Continue
        oTxtAnthrochimeraSighting_intro4

        You turn around and see the captain's malady affecting his crew as well. In a few torturous moments only corpses remain. You inspect the coordinates from the captain's data-container.

        • Quest marker added to map: oTxtAnthrochimeraSightingQuest_intro
        1. Continue

AnthrochimeraSightingQuest

oTxtAnthrochimeraSightingQuest_intro

Entering the marked system, you discover a quiet battlefield. Raider and Ariokine ships litter the area. You see no monsters here, but you find multiple ships that were mangled and twisted apart much like the earlier raider vessel.

  1. Continue
    oTxtAnthrochimeraSightingQuest_ambush

    As you examine these broken down vessels, two damaged Ariokine ships emerge from the battlefield, rushing in to attack.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Blood ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtAnthrochimeraSightingQuest_ambush_win

    You put the first enemy ship out of its misery quickly.

    • Crew recalled to ship
    1. Continue
      oTxtAnthrochimeraSightingQuest_ambush_win2

      The second ship is in your range, but begins preparing an escape.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Blood ship
      After the fight:
      oTxtAnthrochimeraSightingQuest_ambush_win3

      The battle is over. You spend some time scavenging the ship wrecks in the area. You find arcane texts filled with images, graphs, symbols; evidence of a ritual of some kind. On the last page of the manual there is a gruesome illustration of a mound of melted-together corpses, and the word "Anthrochimera."

      • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
      • Fleshy Reagents ×3
      1. Continue
        oTxtAnthrochimeraSightingQuest_ambush_win4

        You also discover evidence of the blood cult's plans for this battle against the raiders. It was meant to be the first part of an offensive against the clans, but they clearly underestimated their enemy. A third system is marked. Perhaps you can find answers there?

        • Quest marker added to map: oTxtAnthrochimeraSightingQuestFinale_intro
        1. Continue

AnthrochimeraSightingQuestFinale

oTxtAnthrochimeraSightingQuestFinale_intro

Entering this system, you find another battlefield, though this one seems to be much more recent. You notice a large asteroid nearby, on an otherwise empty stretch of space. As you explore this second battlefield, you find another Ariokine ship. It tries to set up an ambush like in the previous system you entered, but you know better now.

  1. Continue
    oTxtAnthrochimeraSightingQuestFinale_battle

    You target the enemy ship, weapons primed, and engage in battle.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Blood ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtAnthrochimeraSightingQuestFinale_battle_win

    The enemy ship falls. For a moment, everything is quiet. Suddenly, you are contacted by a raider captain. His face is twisted in a visage of absolute dread, and you see a fearful, manic glint in his eyes. "Hide! Hide this instant! The monster, it is right here! Hide, and pray to whatever god you worship!"

    1. Continue
      oTxtAnthrochimeraSightingQuestFinale_anthrochimera

      Before you can question the delirious captain, you notice movement. The large asteroid you saw earlier begins to move. And you notice, to your horror, that this is no regular piece of void-stone.

      1. Continue
        oTxtAnthrochimeraSightingQuestFinale_anthrochimera2

        You see thousands and thousands of bodies, melted together into a sickening, congealed mass of agony. You begin noticing their faces. Human faces. But alive in the cold dark of outer space. Some foul, unholy sorcery is keeping them alive. Eternal, constant pain, screaming mute into space. Undying. Why can they not die?

        1. Continue
          oTxtAnthrochimeraSightingQuestFinale_anthrochimera3

          You notice that you are bleeding from your eyes, nose, ears, and mouth. You can not stop yourself from seeing, from hearing this... thing. The Anthrochimera. The raider captain contacts you again and screams, his voice snaps you out of this profane trance. You can do nothing but hide. How is a ship supposed to fight something the size of a small moon?

          • All crew damaged (50%)
          1. Continue
            oTxtAnthrochimeraSightingQuestFinale_anthrochimera_end

            You avert your gaze. You wait, and then, silence. Hours pass. Somehow, the creature leaves the system through a subspace rift. The raider captain is long gone. You scavenge what you can from the battlefield.

            • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
            • Storm Cannon II
            • Blood Spear
            • Stygian Reagents ×2
            1. Continue

AnthrochimeraSightingQuestFinale_anthrochimera

→ oTxtAnthrochimeraSightingQuestFinale_anthrochimera_end

AnthrochimeraSightingQuestFinale_battle

→ oTxtAnthrochimeraSightingQuestFinale_battle_win

AnthrochimeraSightingQuest_ambush

→ oTxtAnthrochimeraSightingQuest_ambush_win

ArmamentProduction

oTxtArmamentProduction_intro

There is a defunct starship armament production facility here. It can be reactivated, but the machinery requires chemical fuels.

  • −10 missiles
  1. Reactivate armament production (Spend 10 Munitions)
    oTxtArmamentProduction_activate

    You restart the machinery using missile propellants. The production line rumbles and fabricates a starship grade armament before grinding to a halt.

    • Random ship weapon
    1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtArmamentProduction_leave

    The facility is of no use to you. You move on.

    1. Continue

AshenWarriorRumors

oTxtAshenWarriorRumors_intro

A heavily-armed Imperial ship appears on your viewport. They look like they were recently engaged in combat. "Oh, thank the throne. You are not one of them."

  1. Ask them what they mean
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtAshenWarriorRumors_askB

      "The ashen warriors. They look human, but they - " The transmission cuts off as cannon fire impacts the Imperial ship, ripping it to shreds.

      1. Continue
        oTxtAshenWarriorRumors_askB2

        A new ship reveals itself. "Some secrets must remain secrets."

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs random faction ship
    • oTxtAshenWarriorRumors_askA

      "They are... superhuman. Not augmented, not like our Templars or the cyber-hulks of the machine priests. Nor is it sorcery either. They look human, but they are not like us. Their strength and intellect are a sight to behold. And by the throne, their ships..."

      1. Continue
        oTxtAshenWarriorRumors_askA2

        More Imperial ships suddenly drop out of subspace and open fire on each other. Your contact breaks off to defend themselves and you are forced to do the same. You overhear confusion and outrage over the comms. "...the Immortal King has no power here!... "

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Empire ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        oTxtAshenWarriorRumors_askA3

        You survey the carnage and detect no more hostile activity. Then you hear someone coughing over your comms, a dying man's last words. "They did this. The ashen warriors..."

        1. Continue
  2. Attack them
    oTxtAshenWarriorRumors_attack

    "You fool. Do you not realize we have bigger problems here? We are being manipulated."

    1. Continue
    After the rewards from the fight:
    oTxtAshenWarriorRumors_attack2

    You hear the last words of a dying man on your comms. "They did this. The ashen warriors... they are responsible... for all of it."

    1. Continue
  3. Ignore them
    oTxtAshenWarriorRumors_ignore

    You ignore them. The Imperial ship makes no further attempt to contact you.

    1. Continue

BldNothing

oTxtBldNothing_intro

[...]

  1. Continue

BloodAltar

oTxtBloodAltar_intro

Your ship arrives at the next jump node. In the distance, you notice a vast crimson altar.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtBloodAltar_investigateB

      You investigate the altar and find a gruesome sacrificial chamber. An ancient, rusted weapon lies beside a bowl of blood.

      • Thirsting Glaive
      1. Continue
    • oTxtBloodAltar_investigateA

      You approach the altar and spot a hidden entrance beneath it. Inside is a stash of rare ingredients. They could be useful.

      • Random Blood Cult consumable
      1. Continue
    • oTxtBloodAltar_investigateC

      You dock with the altar. Suddenly, you hear an eerie chanting. Cultists emerge from the shadows, their white robes stained with blood.

      • 2 Blood Priest boarders
      1. Continue
      After the boarders are defeated:
      oTxtBloodAltar_investigateC_1

      You pick up an unusual item from one of the bodies.

      • Random Blood Cult consumable
      1. Continue
    • oTxtBloodAltar_investigateD

      Your multispex picks up a faint life signature coming from within. As you approach, you see a wounded cultist lying on the ground, clutching a bleeding wound. He begs for help.

      1. Help him
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtBloodAltar_help

          You take him to back to your ship, but unfortunately him wounds are too great and he does not survive.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtBloodAltar_help3

          You take him back to your medical facilities and he manages to make a full recovery. He decides to join your crew.

          • Blood Priest
          1. Continue
        • oTxtBloodAltar_help2

          You bring him back to your ship and treat him using what basic medical supplies you have. He manages to make a full recovery.

          1. Continue
            oTxtBloodAltar_help2a

            You agree to drop the cultist off at the nearest outpost. He sends you the coordinates of a secret supply cache as a gesture of thanks.

            • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
            1. Continue
      2. Refuse
        oTxtBloodAltar_refuse

        You refuse and leave him to die, but not before he curses you with the blood of his dying breath. The curse conjures an infernal storm upon your vessel. You prepare to repel boarders.

        • 2–3 warp breaches spawned
        • 1–2 Fleshling, Blood Golem, or Tendril boarders
        1. Continue
      3. (Auto-Surgeon) Repair his injuries
        oTxtBloodAltar_reconstruct

        You bring him back to your ship and load him into your auto-surgeon. The arcane contraption reconstructs his damaged tissue with blinding speed. He thanks you, then offers to join your crew.

        • Recruit enemy commander as crew
        1. Continue
  2. Move on
    oTxtBloodAltar_moveOn

    You decide to move on.

    1. Continue.

BloodAltar_investigateC

→ oTxtBloodAltar_investigateC_1

BloodBodyRitual

oTxtBloodBodyRitual_intro

You encounter a vessel bearing the markings of the Ariokine blood cults. It rotates slowly in place while rings of ethereal energy twist across its hull. You hear a strange chanting over the comms.

  1. Hail them
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtBloodBodyRitual_hailB

      You hail the cultists. They respond with anger. "You have interrupted our sacred rites. This will not stand."

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Blood ship
    • oTxtBloodBodyRitual_hailA

      A cultist responds to your hail. "We are performing an important ritual and require a living body. Our sensors show that you have a suitable candidate aboard your ship. One of your crew. Do not worry. The ritual will not be fatal." You decide whether or not to trust them.

      1. Volunteer yourself
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtBloodBodyRitual_volunteer1

          The cultists bring you aboard their ship and make several incisions across your body while chanting in a circle. A halo of blood swirls out from your chest before arranging itself into the shape of an 8-pointed star. After a few moments, the star dissipates. The cultists thank you for your assistance. "Take this, for your service to Ariok."

          • Random Blood Cult equipment
          • Commander damaged (50%)
          1. Continue
        • oTxtBloodBodyRitual_volunteer3

          You board the cultist ship and lie down at the center of their ritual chamber. Shadowy figures dance around you. Dark energy swirls out from your gaping mouth and coalesces into a group of humanoid creatures. The cultists complete the ritual. They offer you one of the creatures in thanks. "Ariok's divine hand has brought forth a fruitful harvest."

          • Blood Golem
          1. Continue
        • oTxtBloodBodyRitual_volunteer2

          The cultists take you aboard their ship and amputate several of your limbs. As you bleed out onto the deck, a ring of robed figures chants an ominous verse. Despite the incredible pain, you feel strangely resolute.

          1. Continue
            oTxtBloodBodyRitual_volunteer2a

            After the ritual, the priests restore your limbs with vat-grown replacements. They offer you a reward for your help. "Behold, the blessings of Ariok."

            • Random Blood Cult equipment
            1. Continue
      2. Volunteer a random crewman
        Random outcome (1/4 each):
        • oTxtBloodBodyRitual_crew1

          After taking your crewman aboard their ship and conducting their ritual, the cultists bring them back to you. However, you notice a significant change in your crewman's demeanor.

          • Blood Golem
          • Lose a random non-demonic crew member
          1. Continue
        • oTxtBloodBodyRitual_crew3

          The cultists take your crewman and use them in their ritual. Unfortunately the cultists were either lying about the risks, or simply incompetent. Your crewman does not survive the ritual.

          • Lose a random crew member
          1. Continue
        • oTxtBloodBodyRitual_crew4

          The cultists take your crewman and use them in their ritual. Afterwards, they return your crewman along with a small reward. "We are blessed with a bountiful harvest, thanks to Ariok's grace."

          • Random small reward
          1. Continue
        • oTxtBloodBodyRitual_crew2

          The cultists retrieve your crewman and perform their ritual. After a period, they return your crewman, along with an additional creature. "Ariok's providence has granted us a bountiful harvest."

          • Blood Golem
          1. Continue
      3. Decline
        oTxtBloodBodyRitual_decline

        "Very well."

        1. Continue
  2. Ignore them
    oTxtBloodBodyRitual_ignore

    You ignore them and move on.

    1. Continue
  3. (Blood Cultist) Tap into the energies
    oTxtBloodBodyRitual_bt_bloodCultist

    Your cultist crew member is empowered by the energies streaming from the Ariokine vessel. They are struck by a revelatory vision, which they later transcribe as a psychomancy.

    • Summon Fiend
    1. Continue

BloodBodyRitual_bt

→ oTxtBloodBodyRitual_bt_bloodCultist

BloodChoirShip

oTxtBloodChoirShip_intro

You encounter an Ariokine choir ship. These vessels carry within them a chorus of countless initiates singing litanies in praise of the Lord of Blood. The dark energies conjured by this ritual are one of the ways the cult spreads its influence.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtBloodChoirShip_investigateC

      Hideous creatures begin coalescing across your ship as you get closer. The cult vessel jumps out, leaving you to deal with the abominations.

      • 2–3 Fleshling, Tendril, Stygian Worm, Fiend, or Blood Golem boarders
      1. Continue
      After the boarders are defeated:
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtBloodChoirShip_investigateC1

        One of the creatures leaves behind some occult equipment.

        • Random Blood Cult equipment
        1. Continue
      • oTxtBloodChoirShip_investigateC2

        One of the creatures leaves behind an occult weapon.

        • Random Blood Cult weapon
        1. Continue
    • oTxtBloodChoirShip_investigateB

      As you approach, you begin to hear the chanting of all the initiates aboard the cultist vessel. Suddenly, the voices vanish. The cultist vessel has been possessed by an infernal will. It turns towards you and attacks.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Possessed ship
    • oTxtBloodChoirShip_investigateA

      The cultists inform you the choir ship is seeking suitable initiates. They wish to choose a worthy candidate from among your crew.

      1. Give up a random crewman
        oTxtBloodChoirShip_sacrifice

        The cultists select one of your crew. They then transfer over a cryo-vault containing one of their eldritch constructs. "The Lord of Blood blesses those who seek his wisdom."

        • Blood Golem
        • Lose a random non-demonic crew member
        1. Continue
      2. Decline
        Random outcome (1/4 each):
        • oTxtBloodChoirShip_declineA

          The cultists do not seem disappointed, but cease further communications. You move on.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtBloodChoirShip_declineD

          The cultists are incensed. You hear maddening chants within your mind, as the cultist vessel is overtaken by an infernal will and attacks.

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Possessed ship
        • oTxtBloodChoirShip_declineB

          The cultists are disappointed. Before leaving, one of them seems to take special interest in you. They give you a strange weapon before leaving. "This hallowed weapon will surely find its way to the blood-blessed."

          • Random Blood Cult weapon
          1. Continue
        • oTxtBloodChoirShip_declineC

          The cultists seem displeased but take no overt action. You move on.

          1. Continue
            oTxtBloodChoirShip_declineC1

            Suddenly, your vessel is rocked by an explosion. The cultists had planted a demolition charge at some point.

            • −1 hull HP
            1. Continue
  2. Attack
    oTxtBloodChoirShip_Attack

    You attack the blood cultists before their rituals can progress any further.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Blood ship
  3. Leave
    oTxtBloodChoirShip_leave

    You ignore the cultists and move on.

    1. Continue
  4. (Blood Cultist) Recruit an initiate
    oTxtBloodChoirShip_bt_bloodCultist

    Aided by your cultist crewmember, you induct one of the initiates to serve aboard your vessel.

    • Blood Priest or Elder Blood Mage
    1. Continue

BloodChoirShip_bt

→ oTxtBloodChoirShip_bt_bloodCultist

BloodCultAttackEmpire

oTxtBloodCultAttackEmpire_intro

You detect an Imperial ship under attack by a blood cult warship.

  1. Attack the blood cult warship
    oTxtBloodCultAttackEmpire_attack

    You move to engage the cultists.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Blood ship
    After the rewards from the fight:
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtBloodCultAttackEmpire_winA

      The Imperial commander gruffly acknowledges your help, and ejects a small supply crate before jumping out.

      • Random Imperial armor
      1. Continue
    • oTxtBloodCultAttackEmpire_winB

      You attempt to make contact with the Imperial ship but it appears to have escaped.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtBloodCultAttackEmpire_winC

      Suddenly an Imperial reinforcement fleet arrives and begins engaging you. They must have mistaken you for an enemy. You escape, but not without suffering minor damage.

      • Crew recalled to ship
      • 1–2 breaches spawned
      1. Continue
  2. Leave the Imperials to their fate
    oTxtBloodCultAttackEmpire_leave

    You decide to not interfere. The cultist warship subsequently overwhelms the Imperial vessel, then jumps out. You move in and recover the few valuables left.

    • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
    1. Continue
  3. (Hull Reaper) Attack the Imperial ship
    oTxtBloodCultAttackEmpire_bt_hullReaper1

    You prime your hull reaper system and engage the beleaguered Imperial ship. Your vessel's hull is regenerated as the Imperial ship crumbles beneath the assault.

    • +3–5 hull HP
    1. Continue
      oTxtBloodCultAttackEmpire_bt_hullReaper2

      Startled by the suddenness of your vessel's appearance, the blood cult warship withdraws. You salvage the remnants of the destroyed Imperial ship and move on.

      • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue

BloodCultAttackEmpire_bt

→ oTxtBloodCultAttackEmpire_bt_hullReaper1

BloodCultPsykRaid

oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_intro

A group of cultists is planning an attack on a groundside psyk-chem production facility controlled by a rival sect. They have contacted you looking to recruit mercenaries to aid their raiding party.

  1. Continue
    oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_intro2

    Psyk-chems refer to a class of powerful combat stimulants notorious for being both suicidally toxic and wildly effective. The locations of psyk-chem laboratories are tightly guarded by the blood cults. A raid on such a facility could prove lucrative.

    1. Agree to help
      oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_agree

      You agree to help. The cultists ask you which role you wish to play.

      1. Attack the facility's orbital defenses
        oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_attack

        You volunteer to attack the orbital defenses, distracting the rival sect's warships long enough for the raiding force on the ground to secure the psyk-chems.

        1. Continue
          oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_attack2

          On the day of the raid, you arrive at the target location with a host of allied vessels. A fleet of enemy warships is waiting to greet you.

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Blood ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_attack3

          There are too many enemy vessels. Your allied ground forces need more time to secure the psyk-chems.

          • Crew recalled to ship
          1. Attack another vessel
            oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_attackAnother

            You target lock another ship.

            1. Continue
            Fight! vs Blood ship
            After the rewards from the fight:
            oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_raidEnd

            The raiding forces have successfully secured the psyk-chems. You receive the signal to retreat. You disengage from the enemy fleet and make way for the rendezvous point.

            1. Continue
              oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_raidEnd2

              The cultists congratulate you on a job well done. They offer you a sizable reward for your assistance.

              • Random moderate reward or Random large reward
              1. Continue
          2. Retreat
            Random outcome (1/2 each):
            • oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_retreatB

              You retreat, abandoning your allies, and forfeiting any chance of reward.

              1. Continue
            • oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_retreatA

              You decide to retreat, abandoning your allies. Later you receive an anonymous package containing an explosive device. It detonates, injuring you.

              • Commander damaged (50%)
              1. Continue
      2. Evacuate the raiding party on the ground
        oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_evacuate

        You volunteer to evacuate the raiding party.

        1. Continue
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_evacuateA

            When you arrive on the surface, the raiding force is nowhere to be found. Gunshots ring out from the nearby psyk-chem facility, then quickly fall silent. It becomes clear that the raid has failed. You move on.

            1. Continue
          • oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_evacuateB

            You land outside the perimeter of the psyk-chem facility and make contact with the raiding force. They clamber aboard your ship with several tanks of military-grade psyk-chem in tow. You make your escape.

            1. Continue
              Random outcome (1/2 each):
              • oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_evacuateB_b

                The members of the cult express their appreciation for your help and offer you generous compensation.

                • Random moderate reward or Random large reward
                1. Continue
              • oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_evacuateB_a

                You discover that several of the tanks were damaged during transport, causing them to leak raw psyk-chem into your ship. This sends your crew into a violent, hallucinogenic frenzy. You manage to calm them down but not without sustaining heavy injuries.

                • All crew damaged (50%)
                1. Continue
                  Random outcome (1/2 each):
                  • oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_evacuateB_a1

                    Nevertheless, you complete your mission. The cultists commend you on your performance and offer you a substantial payment for your aid.

                    1. Continue
                  • oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_evacuateB2_2

                    Unfortunately, too many of the tanks were damaged for them to be of any value to your employers. They express their disappointment, but not press further.

                    1. Continue
      3. Decline the offer
        oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_declineOffer

        After hearing of their plan, you decide to decline the offer.

        1. Continue
    2. Decline
      oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_declineOffer2

      You decline.

      1. Continue
    3. (Chaos Knight) Augment the raiding party with your Chaos Knight
      oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_bt_warKnight1

      You decide to augment the cultist raiding party with your Chaos Knight.

      1. Continue
        oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_bt_warKnight2

        You monitor the communications channels of the raiding force. Your chaos knight leads the raiding party, carving a bloody path through the facility. Even the chem-fueled zealotry of the defenders proves no match against the overpowering martial prowess of your chaos knight.

        1. Continue
          oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_bt_warKnight3

          The raiding party eventually secures the sought-after psyk-chems.

          1. Continue
            oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_bt_warKnight4

            Suddenly, you hear startled cries of alarm. Perhaps affected by the chem-laced blood suffusing the air, your chaos knight has apparently gone berserk and turned upon the allied cultists.

            1. Continue
              oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_bt_warKnight5

              The raiding party is exterminated in short order. You extract your chaos knight, who has brought back a quantity of psyk-chem equipment along with some equipment stripped from the slain.

              • Random Blood Cult equipment
              • Psyk-Chem Injectors
              1. Continue
                oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_bt_warKnight6

                Suddenly, you find yourself under attack by a vessel belonging to the betrayed cultists. They are intent on avenging their lost comrades.

                1. Continue
                Fight! vs Blood ship

BloodCultPsykRaid_bt

→ oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_bt_warKnight1

BloodCultPsykRaid_evacuateB

→ oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_evacuateB_a

BloodCultPsykRaid_evacuateB2

→ oTxtBloodCultPsykRaid_evacuateB2_2

BloodFeast

oTxtBloodFeast_intro

You enter an Imperial system, and head towards the lone colony located on the only habitable planet. A blue pearl against the void of space lies ahead. The colony is dominated by a gargantuan crimson tower that pierces the sky. A Haematite pleasure spire; a center of depravity created by blood cultists and corrupted Imperial nobles. Pain and pleasure, feast and slaughter all blend together to birth inhuman monstrosities. As you approach, you are hailed. The voice tells you there is a grand feast, and you are invited.

  1. Attend the feast
    oTxtBloodFeast_investigateA

    You enter the pleasure spire and your senses are instantly overloaded. You see every excess and depravity imaginable blended together. Its stench is intoxicating and you feel faint.

    1. Continue
      oTxtBloodFeast_investigateA1

      You blink, and suddenly you are seated at a table filled with a cornucopia of pulsing meat and bowls of sanguineous intoxicants. A bloated once-human figure of a noble raises a golden chalice, a rictus grin extending from ear to ear.

      1. Partake
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtBloodFeast_investigateA_rescue3

          You partake in the feast and revel in its depravity. Unfortunately the cursed rite is soon interrupted by an Imperial attack fleet. You make it back to your ship and try to escape, but a warship blocks your path. Your ship is boarded, and their leader manages to sabotage your ship.

          • Destroy random system
          • 3–4 Imperial Recruit, Imperial Enforcer, or Imperial Marshal boarders
          1. Continue
          After the boarders are defeated:
          oTxtBloodFeast_investigateA_rescue3_win

          The last boarder is dead. You loot their corpses and leave.

          • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
          • Random Imperial equipment
          1. Continue
        • oTxtBloodFeast_investigateA_rescue

          Hours pass in a crimson haze, images blur together. You eat, drink, carve, and butcher. With every blink, another act of depravity. Your body is pushed to its limits, barely able to keep up with the overwhelming sensation. Ariok smiles upon you. An Ariokine cultist joins you, and gives you the location of another grand feast.

          • Bloodwitch
          • Debuff applied to all crew
          • Quest marker added to map: oTxtBloodFeastQuest_intro
          1. Continue
        • oTxtBloodFeast_investigateA_rescue2

          After hours of feasting and bloodshed you find yourself back on board your ship.

          1. Continue
            oTxtBloodFeast_investigateA_rescue2_1

            You realise that the system has been attacked by the Imperium and they are letting none escape. An Imperial vessel engages you in a battle, and sends soldiers to board your ship.

            1. Continue
            Fight! vs Empire ship
            After the rewards from the fight:
            oTxtBloodFeast_investigateA_rescue2_win

            The last Imperial is dead. You find some equipment among the wreckage.

            • Random moderate reward
            1. Continue
      2. Run
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtBloodFeast_run2

          You see the depraved feast begin and decide to run. You manage to escape back to your ship. As you depart, your ship's intruder alarms suddenly go off. You have been boarded.

          • 3–4 Blood Golem boarders
          1. Continue
          After the boarders are defeated:
          oTxtBloodFeast_run2_win

          The last cultist is dead. You loot their corpses and escape.

          • 1 random equipment item
          1. Continue
        • oTxtBloodFeast_run

          You cannot bring yourself to join whatever rituals these cultists have in store. They attack you immediately, wounding you. You barely escape with your life.

          • Commander damaged (50%)
          1. Continue
            oTxtBloodFeast_run1

            You board your ship and the spire bristles like it is alive. Blood cult warships detach from the towering structure and attack.

            1. Continue
            Fight! vs Blood ship
            After the fight:
            oTxtBloodFeast_run_victory

            The first cultist vessel has been destroyed, but more are approaching. You quickly salvage what you can and flee.

            • Crew recalled to ship
            1. Continue
        • oTxtBloodFeast_run_survivors

          You see the depraved feast begin and decide to run. As you run towards your ship, you find a small room filled with imprisoned humans and blood thrall guards. Some of the prisoners are still alive, though they do not respond to your orders. You can save them, but you know that it will make escaping the system far more dangerous.

          1. Save whoever you can
            oTxtBloodFeast_run_survivors_rescue

            You rush into the prison and manage to rescue a few crewmembers. Upon your return to the ship you notice that the Ariokine cultists have mustered a powerful attack force against you.

            • 3–4 Elder Blood Mage, Mouth of Ariok, or Blood Knight boarders
            1. Continue
            Fight! vs Blood ship
            After the rewards from the fight:
            oTxtBloodFeast_run_survivors_rescue1

            The first ship is destroyed.

            • Crew recalled to ship
            1. Continue
              oTxtBloodFeast_run_survivors_rescue2

              The first ship is destroyed. A second cult warship attacks you.

              1. Continue
              Fight! vs Blood ship
              After the rewards from the fight:
              oTxtBloodFeast_run_survivors_rescue3

              The last ship breaks apart. The survivors thank you. They give you coordinates to a location targeted by the Ariokine cult. One of them decides to join your crew.

              • Imperial Acolyte or Corsair
              • Quest marker added to map: oTxtBloodFeastQuest_intro
              1. Continue
          2. Leave them to their fate
            oTxtBloodFeast_run_survivors_leave

            You manage to escape. As you board your ship, your intruder alarms suddenly go off. Your ship has been infiltrated.

            • 2–3 Blood Golem boarders
            1. Continue
            After the boarders are defeated: → oTxtBloodFeast_run2_win
  2. Decline the invitation and move on
    oTxtBloodFeast_moveOn

    You move on.

    1. Continue

BloodFeastQuest

oTxtBloodFeastQuest_intro

You reach the coordinates given to you by the survivors you rescued from the pleasure spire. You approach what first seems like a small asteroid belt, only to realize they are fragments of a large transport ship. No, a cruise ship. Even its hull screams luxury, lavishly decorated with gold and carnelian. The construction of the ship is extravagant. The ship is very damaged, and the damage seems to be relatively recent. Whoever attacked the base might still be near.

  1. Investigate the cruise ship
    oTxtBloodFeastQuest_investigate

    Your scouts investigate the ship thoroughly. It reeks of excess, not unlike the Haematite spire you visited earlier. However, no Ariokine corruption can be seen here, only swathes of burned compartments and thousands of corpses.

    1. Continue
      oTxtBloodFeastQuest_investigate1

      The Imperials must have got here first. Nestled deep within the ship your scouts find a sealed vault door. Its highly advanced alarm systems are still powered. This is clearly a trap. You are certain that breaking into the vault will alert its defenders. However, there could be something valuable in the vault.

      1. Break into the vault
        oTxtBloodFeastQuest_vaultA

        The vault door yields and the alarm is tripped. Two ships appear on your sensors, and your crew barely manages to ransack the vault before the attack comes.

        • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
        • Flayer Beam or Displacement Field Emitter or Rift Bomb
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Blood ship
        After the fight:
        oTxtBloodFeastQuest_vaultA_victory

        The first cultist ship has been neutralized. You immediately prepare to engage the second vessel.

        • Crew recalled to ship
        1. Continue
          oTxtBloodFeastQuest_vaultA_victory1

          The second ship charges towards you. Klaxons sound on your bridge, warning of hostile targeting locks.

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Blood ship
          After the fight:
          oTxtBloodFeastQuest_vaultA_victory2

          The second ship is destroyed.

          1. Continue
            oTxtBloodFeastQuest_vaultA_victory3

            You salvage what you can from the wreckage before moving on.

            • Random moderate reward
            1. Continue
      2. Annihilate the ship and scavenge the remains
        oTxtBloodFeastQuest_vaultC

        Your weapons make short work of the already wrecked cruise ship. Your crew manage to scavenge some scrap. As the ship breaks apart, an Imperial assault ship emerges from the void and attacks.

        • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
        • Random Blood Cult equipment
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Empire ship
      3. (Machine Priest) Access the ship systems and scramble the alarms
        oTxtBloodFeastQuest_bt_techB1

        Your machine priest crew member comprehends the alarm system's arcano-circuitry and manages to delay its activation, giving you more time to explore.

        1. Continue
          oTxtBloodFeastQuest_bt_techB2

          The vault door yields and the alarm is tripped. Two ships appear on your sensors, and your crew barely manages to ransack the vault before the attack comes. However, your ship is prepared for the fight because you tampered with the alarm systems.

          • Random large reward
          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Blood ship
          After the fight:
          oTxtBloodFeastQuest_bt_techB2_victory

          The first ship has been defeated. You prepare to attack the second vessel.

          • Crew recalled to ship
          1. Continue
            oTxtBloodFeastQuest_bt_techB2_victory1

            The second cultist ship moves in to attack.

            1. Continue
            Fight! vs Blood ship
            After the fight:
            oTxtBloodFeastQuest_bt_techB2_victory2

            The second ship has been destroyed.

            1. Continue
              oTxtBloodFeastQuest_bt_techB2_victory3

              You salvage what is left before leaving.

              • Random small reward
              1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtBloodFeastQuest_leave

    You leave the system.

    1. Continue

BloodFeastQuest_bt

→ oTxtBloodFeastQuest_bt_techB1

BloodFeastQuest_bt_techB2

→ oTxtBloodFeastQuest_bt_techB2_victory

BloodFeastQuest_vaultA

→ oTxtBloodFeastQuest_vaultA_victory

BloodFeast_investigateA

→ oTxtBloodFeast_investigateA_rescue

BloodFeast_investigateA_rescue2

→ oTxtBloodFeast_investigateA_rescue2_1

BloodFeast_investigateA_rescue3

→ oTxtBloodFeast_investigateA_rescue3_win

BloodFeast_run

→ oTxtBloodFeast_run_survivors
oTxtBloodFeast_run_victory1 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

A second ship joins the fight.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Blood ship
After the rewards from the fight:
oTxtBloodFeast_run_victory2

The second blood cult ship explodes. You take what you can and flee.

  • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
  • Random cult consumable
  1. Continue

BloodFeast_run2

→ oTxtBloodFeast_run2_win

BloodFeast_run_survivors

→ oTxtBloodFeast_run_survivors_leave

BloodOceanTemple

oTxtBloodOceanTemple_intro

Within a sunless system you spot a rogue planet. Oceans of deep crimson cover its griseous surface, producing a soft, strange glow. You detect a single structure along the largest of its oceans.

  1. Investigate
    oTxtBloodOceanTemple_investigate

    You touch down near the structure, an aged Ariokine temple. Abandoned for undoubtedly centuries, its granite facade is crumbling, and its ruby trim is without lustre. The crimson ocean before you laps at the coast, its waters viscous yet sweet smelling.

    1. Explore the temple
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtBloodOceanTemple_exploreB

        While much of the temple is inaccessible, its atrium leads to a small storeroom. You take what few items are of use to you.

        • Very small amount of ⬗ Scrap
        • Fleshy Reagents ×2
        1. Continue
      • oTxtBloodOceanTemple_exploreA

        The temple is as dilapidated inside as it is outside. Rubble from fallen pillars and ceilings bar access to much of its interior. You are unable to find anything of use.

        1. Continue
    2. (Blood Cultist) Examine the ocean
      oTxtBloodOceanTemple_examine1

      Your blood cultist approaches the ocean shores, bends down before it, and drinks. They identify the liquid as Red Ichor -- a rare and invigorating substance, and a gift from Ariok. Energized from their drink, they fashion together a horrid beast from the liquids below.

      • Blood Golem
      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtBloodOceanTemple_examine2_B

          Having finished their ritual, the ocean begins to bubble, and the ground begins to shake. The cultist and their new servant hurry back aboard, and you blast off.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtBloodOceanTemple_examine2_A

          Though a success, your cultist's ritual has given life to the ichor before them. Crimson spawn emerge from the shore forcing you to evacuate, though not before you are boarded.

          • 1 Blood Golem boarder
          • 3–4 Thrall boarders
          1. Continue
    3. (Scavenger) Scavenge the temple
      oTxtBloodOceanTemple_scavenge

      Though the temple is in total disrepair, your scavenger approaches this ruin as a challenge. Deftly navigating through hidden passageways and mounds of rubble, they penetrate the inner sanctum and retrieve its riches.

      • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
      • Chaos Armor
      • Fiendish Reagents ×2
      1. Continue
    4. Leave
      oTxtBloodOceanTemple_leave2

      There is something wrong with this world. You return to your ship and leave.

      1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtBloodOceanTemple_leave1

    There is nothing to be done here. You log the planet's location and leave.

    1. Continue

BloodOceanTemple_examine2

→ oTxtBloodOceanTemple_examine2_A

BloodOrbitalSalvage

oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_intro

You find a multitude of wrecks in low orbit around a gas giant, many so low as to be falling into the atmosphere of the planet. You recognize the distinctive hull configurations of both Imperial and Ariokine warships.

  1. Salvage wrecks
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_salvageA

      You approach the highest orbiting wreck. The salvage operation is uneventful. By the time it concludes the remaining wrecks have all irrecoverably fallen into the gas giant's atmosphere.

      • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
    • oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_salvageB

      You approach the nearest wreck. You detect anomalous energies aboard, but you cannot pinpoint their locations. There are other hulks to salvage, but they are slowly falling deeper into the gravity well.

      1. Proceed with salvage
        oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_salvageDemon

        Your salvage operation is successful and you recover some useful material from the wreck.

        • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
        1. Continue
          oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_salvageDemon2

          On your way back you notice the anomalous readings detected earlier have now migrated onto your ship.

          • 2–4 Fleshling, Blood Golem, or Tendril boarders
          1. Continue
      2. Salvage a different wreck
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_salvageSafe

          You move to another wreck, one free of any anomalous readings, and manage to recover some useful material.

          • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
          1. Continue
        • oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_salvageRisk

          You plunge deeper towards the gas giant. While the salvage operation concludes successfully, you soon discover you are having trouble escaping the gravity of the planet.

          • Random moderate reward
          1. Increase power to engines
            oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_escape

            You command increased output to thrusters. Your vessel slowly manages to escape the gravity well, but the long duration spent in the intense radiation of the gas giant has adversely affected the crew.

            • Debuff applied to all crew
            1. Continue
          2. Attempt to skip off the upper atmosphere
            oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_bounce

            You accelerate down towards the gas giant in hopes that a gravity assist would give you sufficient velocity to skip off the top of the atmosphere. While you are successful, the severe acceleration induced by the skip has strained the health of your crew.

            • All crew damaged (30%)
            1. Continue
  2. Search for survivors
    oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_rescue

    In spite of harsh radio-interference you discover signs of life from one of the wrecks. Unfortunately, it is in a rapidly decaying orbit. Mounting a rescue operation may incur significant risk of becoming trapped in the gravity well.

    1. Attempt a rescue
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_elder

        In the carcass of the ship you discover a barely conscious Ariokine cultist. You bring him aboard, and after coming to, he pledges his service to you for having saved him from an ignominious death.

        • Blood Priest
        1. Continue
      • oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_captain

        In the wreck of the ship you discover an Imperial captain. Despite being dazed and barely conscious, he protests vigorously against his removal from his ship. You have him sedated and moved aboard.

        1. Continue
          Random outcome (1/4 each):
          • oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_captainA

            Soon after you leave orbit, the Imperial captain comes to. Greatly angered from being cheated of his honorable death, he goes on a rampage.

            • 1 Imperial Recruit boarder
            1. Continue
          • oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_captainB

            The Imperial captain awakens soon after you leave orbit. He is grateful to be rescued. Since he has lost his ship, he offers to serve under your command.

            • Imperial Marshal
            1. Continue
          • oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_captainC3

            The Imperial captain has suffered severe mutagenic effects from the long exposure to the radiation. He eventually awakens, but with no memory of his past.

            • Aberrant
            1. Continue
          • oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_captainC2

            The long duration of radiation exposure suffered by the Imperial captain seems to have had deleterious effects on his physiology. He awakens as a gnarled, corpse-like entity. It seems to respond to commands.

            • Greater Corpulent or Ghoul Magus
            1. Continue
    2. Ignore it and salvage the wrecks instead
      oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_rescueIgnore

      A rescue attempt would be too risky. You set your sights on the wrecks orbiting at higher altitude. Perhaps something useful can be recovered from them.

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • → oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_salvageA
        • → oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_salvageB
    3. Leave the area and move on
      oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_rescueLeave

      The risk is too great. You move on.

      1. Continue
  3. Leave
    oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_introLeave

    Whatever value in the wrecks is likely not worth the danger of recovering them.

    1. Continue
  4. (Blood Psyker) Scry for survivors
    oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_bt_bloodPsyker1

    Your psyker conducts a gruesome ritual to locate any remaining survivors.

    1. Continue
      oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_bt_bloodPsyker2

      You are directed to a distant vessel. You rescue a stranded cultist, who brings with him some useful supplies.

      • Random Blood Cult equipment
      • Elder Blood Mage
      1. Continue
        oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_bt_bloodPsyker3

        The vessel spirals into the gas giant below, joining the rest of the wreckage. You move on.

        1. Continue
oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_captainC Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Unfortunately, the struggle has also prolonged your stay sufficiently to trap you in the gas giant's gravity well. You command for emergency power to the thrusters, and slowly escape from the planet.

  1. Continue
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • → oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_captainC3
    • → oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_captainC2

BloodOrbitalSalvage_bt

→ oTxtBloodOrbitalSalvage_bt_bloodPsyker1

BloodSlaveTransport

oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_intro

You come across a transport vessel bearing cult markings. It appears to be hauling slaves.

  1. Hail them
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_hailD

      The transport vessel refuses your hails and arms its weapons.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Blood ship
    • oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_hailC

      The transport vessel refuses your hail and immediately activates its subspace drive. In moments, the ship is gone.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_hailB

      You attempt to hail the transport vessel. They refuse all communication and begin spooling their subspace drive. Moments later, an explosion rocks your ship as a second ship emerges from hiding, weapons armed. The first ship jumps out. You have fallen into a trap.

      • Damage to random system
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Blood ship
    • oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_hailA

      The commander of the transport vessel responds. "We have an excess of ritual slaves this cycle. Would you like to purchase some?"

      1. Purchase a slave for 25 scrap
        oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_purchase

        "Wonderful."

        • Random crew member
        1. Continue
      2. Decline
        oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_refuse

        "What will we do with all of these slaves..."

        1. Continue
      3. Attack the slaver scum
        oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_attackSlaver

        You move in to attack. Unfortunately the slaver anticipated your decision and managed to infiltrate several men onto your ship while you were distracted.

        • 2 Blood Priest, Elder Blood Mage, Ritualist, Mouth of Ariok, or Blood Golem boarders
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Blood ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_attackSlaver1

          Unfortunately none of the slaves survived the combat.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_attackSlaver2

          Unfortunately all of the slaves were killed in the attack.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_attackSlaver3

          You manage to rescue a survivor.

          • 1 Any War Cult crew or Husk boarder
          1. Continue
  2. Intercept them
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_interceptA

      You move to intercept. The transport vessel takes this as a threat and arms its weapons.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Blood ship
    • oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_interceptC

      You move to intercept. Unfortunately, you have fallen into a trap. The transport vessel changes course, and a second ship drifts into view, weapons primed. A decrepit face materializes on your viewfeed. "We require additional flesh."

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Blood ship
    • oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_interceptB

      You move to intercept. The transport vessel detects your weapons signature and immediately begins ejecting its cargo. The flurry of new signatures prevents you from achieving a precise lock on their ship, giving them just enough time to escape.

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_interceptB2

          You turn back to retrieve the cargo, which consists mostly of stasis pods. A couple of them contain functioning husks.

          • Husk
          • Husk
          1. Continue
        • oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_interceptB1

          You turn back to retrieve the cargo, which consists mostly of stasis pods. Most of them contain corpses. One of them contains a living crewman.

          • Random crew member
          1. Continue
  3. Ignore them
    oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_ignore

    You continue your journey.

    1. Continue
  4. (Blood Knight) Contact the cultists through your blood knight
    oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_bt_bloodKnight1

    Your blood knight contacts the cultists. Having conducted a series of ritual signs and gestures peculiar to the Ariokine cults, the cultists invite your crewman aboard.

    1. Order your blood knight to betray the cultists
      oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_bt_bloodKnightAttack1

      Your blood knight unhesitatingly complies with your order. Bolstered by the unholy energies radiating from the rituals conducted aboard the cultist vessel, your crewman massacres a number of the cultists before they can react.

      1. Continue
        oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_bt_bloodKnightAttack2

        The cultist crew soon recovers from the surprise of the betrayal and rallies against the attack. Your blood knight escapes back to your vessel, bringing back a few psychomantic wafers stripped from the slain.

        • Blood Spear
        • Coagulate
        • Summon Thrall
        1. Continue
          oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_bt_bloodKnightAttack3

          The cultist vessel turns to attack your ship. However, you are well-prepared to meet the threat, having primed your weaponry ahead of time.

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Blood ship
    2. Continue without interference
      oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_bt_bloodKnight2

      The cultists recognize your blood knight as a venerable kin. Over the course of days they regale your crewman with unholy festivities and rites.

      1. Continue
        oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_bt_bloodKnight3

        At length, the cult vessel departs. Your crewman returns, accompanied by a couple of newfound allies.

        • Ritualist
        • Blood Priest
        1. Continue
          oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_bt_bloodKnight4

          The cultists propose to install an altar to their dark deity aboard your vessel.

          • Altar of Blood
          1. Continue

BloodSlaveTransport_bt

→ oTxtBloodSlaveTransport_bt_bloodKnight1

BloodSurpriseAttack

oTxtBloodSurpriseAttack_intro

You briefly detect an Ariokine vessel on scan without it detecting you in return. Your signature has been masked by the nearby ion storm. This may be a good opportunity to gain the upper hand.

  1. Pursue
    Random outcome (weighted):
    • 1/2
      oTxtBloodSurpriseAttack_AttackA

      You close in on the blood cult ship. Your weapons are already charged by the time you arrive.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Blood ship
    • 1/4
      oTxtBloodSurpriseAttack_AttackC

      You close in on the blood cult ship. Unfortunately, your approach is detected, and the cultists quickly turn to engage.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Blood ship
    • 1/4
      oTxtBloodSurpriseAttack_AttackB

      You attempt pursuit. Unfortunately, you fail to regain sensor contact with the cultist vessel, and they manage to escape.

      1. Continue
  2. Ignore
    oTxtBloodSurpriseAttack_leave

    You decide not to risk it and slip away.

    1. Continue
  3. (Improved Sensors) Manually tune your sensors to track the enemy
    oTxtBloodSurpriseAttack_AttackBlue

    You are able to make full use of your improved sensor suite to locate the Ariokine vessel undetected. The cultist ship is caught by complete surprise.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Blood ship

BloodTransportPair

oTxtBloodTransportPair_intro

You detect a small fleet moving away from the burned-out remains of an Imperial colony. They bear the markings of the blood cults. Several cult transports present themselves as opportune targets. One appears to be carrying a prisoner in a reinforced stasis vault. Another is transporting a group of high-ranking cult members.

  1. Attack the one carrying a prisoner
    oTxtBloodTransportPair_securePrisoner

    You power up your weapons.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Blood ship
    After the fight:
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtBloodTransportPair_securePrisoner1

      You recover the stasis vault and find an enraged war cultist. He attempts to commandeer your ship.

      • 1 War Cult crew boarder
      1. Continue
    • oTxtBloodTransportPair_securePrisoner2

      You recover the stasis vault and find an intact servo husk.

      • Servo-husk or Combat Husk
      1. Continue
    • oTxtBloodTransportPair_securePrisoner3

      You recover the stasis vault and find an Imperial psyker. He offers to join your crew.

      • Imperial Scribe, Imperial Acolyte, Imperial Sorcerer, Imperial Magister, or High Magister
      1. Continue
    • oTxtBloodTransportPair_securePrisoner4

      You recover the stasis vault and find a ragged cultist. They decide to join your crew.

      • Weak cultist crew
      1. Continue
  2. Attack the one transporting cult members
    oTxtBloodTransportPair_secureCult1

    You advance on the target, weapons primed.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Blood ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtBloodTransportPair_secureCult2

    You locate a surviving cultist. He is terrified, and pledges to serve your cause, in exchange for his life. You wonder if you can trust him.

    1. Accept
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtBloodTransportPair_accept

        The cultist joins your crew.

        • Blood Priest
        1. Continue
      • oTxtBloodTransportPair_accept2

        You accept his pledge. Unfortunately he was only waiting for you to drop your guard. He attacks you as soon as he steps foot aboard your vessel.

        • 1 Blood Priest boarder
        1. Continue
    2. Kill him
      oTxtBloodTransportPair_kill

      You finish him off. Not worth the risk.

      1. Continue
  3. Ignore them
    oTxtBloodTransportPair_ignore

    You ignore them and move on.

    1. Continue

BloodspireDefence

oTxtBloodspireDefence_intro

You enter the system and notice a small trading outpost built into an asteroid. You dock your ship and go down to resupply. In the snaking, claustrophobic hallways you find a small coven of Ariokine worshippers taking part in a strange, intoxicating ritual.

  1. Continue
    oTxtBloodspireDefence_intro2

    They notice you, and their leader approaches. "Our prayers have been answered. One of Ariok's dwellings is under attack by those who worship steel and circuitry. Drive them away, and you will be rewarded."

    1. Promise to drive the machine cultists away
      oTxtBloodspireDefence_accept

      You accept the task. You are given the coordinates to a nearby system.

      • Quest marker added to map: oTxtBloodspireDefenceQuest_intro
      1. Continue
    2. Decline
      oTxtBloodspireDefence_decline

      You refuse to take part in this scheme. You return to your ship.

      1. Continue

BloodspireDefenceQuest

oTxtBloodspireDefenceQuest_intro

You enter the system and immediately see a battle taking place. A machine cult battleship flanked by smaller warships is attacking a cloud of crimson, petal-shaped battle ships unfurling from a vast sanguine spire growing from the third planet from the sun.

  1. Continue
    oTxtBloodspireDefenceQuest_defend

    You rush in to defend the spire. Cruising among countless Ariokine defenders, you target an enemy warship. However, the attackers boast formidable defensive weaponry: an ionic burst hits your ship as you attack.

    • Ionize up to 3 random systems
    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Techno ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtBloodspireDefenceQuest_defend_win

    A robotic screech rings out through the communications channels as the ship breaks apart. Around you the battle rages on.

    • Crew recalled to ship
    1. Continue
      oTxtBloodspireDefenceQuest_defend2

      The cold intelligence leading the machine cult force diverts another ship to attack you. Multiple assault sleds impact your ship and the enemy readies its guns.

      • 3–4 Husk, Servo-husk, or Combat Husk boarders
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Techno ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtBloodspireDefenceQuest_defend_win2

      The enemy ship explodes violently and you see the Ariokine defenders rushing the battleship. Its fusion reactors overload, imploding with blue arcs of high-energy plasma. The defenders return to the spire and it unfurls into an impossibly large flesh-flower.

      1. Continue
        oTxtBloodspireDefenceQuest_defend_win3

        A new Sanguine World is born, and you see red tendrils snaking across the planet's surface. A small craft laden with gourmet food, fine wines, slaves and narcotics is sent to your ship. The shuttle also contains more immediate rewards. You leave the system.

        • Large amount of ⬗ Scrap
        • Random Blood Cult consumable
        • Random Blood Cult consumable
        • Blood Golem
        1. Continue

BloodspireDefenceQuest_defend

→ oTxtBloodspireDefenceQuest_defend_win

Boarders

oTxtBoarders_weatherAsteroid Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You arrive within a dense asteroid field. Several small rocks impact your hull. Moments later, your intruder alarms go off. You have been boarded.

  • Random boarders teleport in
  1. Continue
After the boarders are defeated:
oTxtBoarders_win

The intruders have been eliminated. You recover some equipment from the remains.

  • 1 random equipment item
  • 2 random equipment items
  • 3 random equipment items
  1. Continue
oTxtBoarders_weatherDemonStorm Unreachable — not referenced by any event
  • Random boarders teleport in
  1. Continue
After the boarders are defeated: → oTxtBoarders_win
oTxtBoarders_weatherIonStorm Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Dense gas suffuses the region creating electromagnetic interference strong enough to disrupt reactor output. Several individuals use this opportunity to board your ship.

  • Random boarders teleport in
  1. Continue
After the boarders are defeated: → oTxtBoarders_win
oTxtBoarders_weatherPlagueStorm Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Your ship drops back into realspace and is immediately enveloped by a cloud of infernal miasma. Warning sirens suddenly begin to blare. Enemy intruders have been detected.

  • 3–4 Greater Corpulent boarders
  1. Continue
After the boarders are defeated: → oTxtBoarders_win
oTxtBoarders_weatherSolarFlare Unreachable — not referenced by any event

As your ship emerges from subspace, you find yourself dangerously close to a massive dwarf star, its searing heat threatening to engulf you. Just as you try to regain control, you hear the piercing sound of your intruder alarms.

  • Random boarders teleport in
  1. Continue
After the boarders are defeated: → oTxtBoarders_win

BossEventAttack

oTxtBossEventAttack

The enemy ship moves in to attack.

  1. Continue

BossEventBoarderAutomata

oTxtBossEventBoarderAutomata Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Warning klaxons sound as a number of battle automata materialize aboard your vessel.

  • 2 Assault Cyborg boarders
  1. Continue

BossEventBoarderEidolon

oTxtBossEventBoarderEidolon Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You feel the presence of an unseen power as infernal beings materialize aboard your vessel.

  1. Continue

BossEventBoarderInfernalKnight

oTxtBossEventBoarderInfernalKnight Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Warning klaxons sound as a Void Centurion teleports aboard your vessel.

  1. Continue

BossEventBoarderRedeemer

oTxtBossEventBoarderRedeemer Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Lightning crackles as an ominous specter appears aboard your vessel. "It matters not that I should fall... I will be redeemed."

  1. Continue

BossEventBoarderTemplar

oTxtBossEventBoarderTemplar Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Warning klaxons sound as Imperial Templars teleport aboard your vessel, and you prepare for battle.

  • 2 Templar Champion boarders
  1. Continue
oTxtBossEventBoarderTemplar_wave Unreachable — not referenced by any event

More Imperial Centurions teleport aboard your vessel.

  • 2 Centurion boarders
  1. Continue
After the fight:
oTxtBossFight_win

The enemy has been vanquished.

  1. Continue
    oTxtBossFight_win2

    At last, you have arrived at the Vault of Souls. Within it you find a grand altar, ablaze with argent fire.

    1. Continue
      oTxtBossFight_win3

      You plunge your hand into the flames. The searing heat of eternity washes over you and your crew. Your souls sublimate into the infinite ether, heralding the Age of Pandemonium.

      1. Continue
        oTxtBossFight_win4

        You have fulfilled your destiny. Your mission is complete.

        1. Continue

BossEventBoarderTentacles

oTxtBossEventBoarderTentacles Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Horrific tentacles suddenly tear through the deck plates.

  • 5 Tendril boarders
  1. Continue

BossEventBoarderWarMooks

oTxtBossEventBoarderWarMooks Unreachable — not referenced by any event

An infernal force materializes enemy reinforcements aboard the accursed enemy vessel. You detect multiple intruders aboard your ship as well.

  • 6 Shock Husk boarders
  • 2 Gladiatrix boarders
  1. Continue

BossEventDemonHeartPhase2

oTxtBossEventDemonHeartPhase2_bloodT1

Powerful energies surround the enemy vessel's systems, closing fissues and rejoining broken circuitry. In a moment, the vessel's systems are renewed to readiness. Horrific tentacles tear through the deck plates across your ship.

  • 4 Tendril boarders
  1. Continue
oTxtBossEventDemonHeartPhase2_bloodT2

Suddenly, protective wards materialize around the enemy vessel's systems, shielding them from harm. Horrific tentacles tear through the deck plates across your ship.

  • 8 Tendril boarders
  1. Continue
oTxtBossEventDemonHeartPhase2_bloodT3

Suddenly, protective wards materialize around the enemy vessel's systems, shielding them from harm. Horrific tentacles tear through the deck plates across your ship.

  • 8 Tendril boarders
  1. Continue
oTxtBossEventDemonHeartPhase2_empireT1

You detect strange readings. Suddenly, protective wards materialize around the enemy vessel's systems, shielding them from harm.

  1. Continue
oTxtBossEventDemonHeartPhase2_empireT2

Suddenly, protective wards materialize around the enemy vessel's systems, shielding them from harm.

  1. Continue
oTxtBossEventDemonHeartPhase2_empireT3

Suddenly, protective wards materialize around the enemy vessel's systems, shielding them from harm.

  1. Continue
oTxtBossEventDemonHeartPhase2_plagueT1

Infernal lightning crackles across your decks, knocking out your systems one by one. Subspace rifts tear open across the accursed vessel, and demonic beings pour forth to renew the fight.

  • Ionize random system
  1. Continue
oTxtBossEventDemonHeartPhase2_plagueT2

Suddenly, a malign force ripples through your vessel, heavily damaging your systems.

  • Destroy Weapons
  • Destroy Boarding
  • Destroy Engines
  1. Continue
oTxtBossEventDemonHeartPhase2_plagueT3

Suddenly, a malign force ripples through your vessel, heavily damaging your systems.

  • Destroy Weapons
  • Destroy Boarding
  • Destroy Engines
  1. Continue
oTxtBossEventDemonHeartPhase2_warT1

Suddenly, protective wards materialize around the enemy vessel's systems, shielding them from harm. You hear the sound of intruder alarms. A number of hostile machine slaves have materialized aboard your ship.

  • 4 Shock Husk boarders
  1. Continue
oTxtBossEventDemonHeartPhase2_warT2

Suddenly, protective wards materialize around the enemy vessel's systems, shielding them from harm. You hear the sound of intruder alarms. A pair of war machines have materialized aboard your ship.

  • 2 Assault Cyborg boarders
  1. Continue
oTxtBossEventDemonHeartPhase2_warT3

Suddenly, protective wards materialize around the enemy vessel's systems, shielding them from harm. You hear the sound of intruder alarms. A pair of war machines have materialized aboard your ship.

BossEventPhase2

oTxtBossEventPhase2_bloodT1

Powerful energies surround the enemy vessel's systems, closing fissues and rejoining broken circuitry. In a moment, the vessel's systems are renewed to readiness. Horrific tentacles tear through the deck plates across your ship.

  • 4 Tendril boarders

BossEventSpawnDemonHeart1

oTxtBossEventSpawnDemonHeart1 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Without crew to man it, the enemy vessel loses power and soon falls silent. The respite is only momentary however, as seconds later, the monstrous starship releases a sudden burst of dark energy.

  1. Continue
    oTxtBossEventSpawnDemonHeart2

    A malefic, hungering force has assumed control of the ship.

    • Enemy rooms deoxygenated
    Trigger (CurrentShipHP 33percent): → oTxtBossEventOxygenate_TEST

→ oTxtBossEventSpawnDemonHeart2

BossEventSpawnDemonHeartBlood

oTxtBossEventSpawnDemonHeartBlood Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You have exterminated the enemy crew. The empty warship falls silent. Suddenly, the vessel begins maneuvering again. Its command throne glows with infernal light - a malefic, hungering force has assumed control of the ship.

  • Enemy rooms deoxygenated
  • Demon Heart: Husk Rack disabled
  • Demon Heart: Blood Pit disabled
