CivilianPossessed

oTxtCivilianPossessed_intro

A civilian transport hails you requesting aid. One of their crew members has suffered a violent seizure, and their on-board medical units are malfunctioning. They want to transfer their patient to your ship for immediate treatment.

  1. Agree to help
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtCivilianPossessed_agreeB

      You bring the patient aboard and treat them with your on-board medical units. They make a quick recovery. The civilians thank you and offer you some of their spare supplies.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtCivilianPossessed_agreeA

      The civilians are grateful for your help. As they prepare their shuttle for transfer, their unconscious patient suddenly starts convulsing. His eyes glow with malevolent energy. He speaks. "This ship is now under my control. Submit, or prepare yourself for an excruciating death."

      1. Continue
        oTxtCivilianPossessed_agreeA1

        To compound the dire situation, a corrupting influence ripples through the entire vessel, mutating it into an a bristling attack craft.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Possessed ship
    • oTxtCivilianPossessed_agreeC

      You secure the patient and affix her to one of your on-board medical units. After several hours she regains consciousness. She looks up at you weakly. "Thank you. You saved my life. I... I....hunger... for souls!"

      1. Continue
        oTxtCivilianPossessed_agreeC2

        The patient's eyes light up with hellish fire. His fingers elongate into long talons and you barely dodge out of the way as he swipes at your throat. Screams echo across the lower decks. You are under attack.

        • 2–4 Demonic Crew boarders
        1. Continue
        After the boarders are defeated:
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtCivilianPossessed_agreeC2_1

          You try to make contact with the civilians but it appears all of them died in the attack.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtCivilianPossessed_agreeC2_2

          You make contact with the civilian ship. Only one person survived the demonic attack. With nowhere else to turn, they decide to join your crew.

          • Imperial Recruit
          1. Continue
    • oTxtCivilianPossessed_agreeD

      You bring the patient aboard your ship and attempt to treat him. Unfortunately he never regains consciousness. After a few hours, his pulse flatlines. There is nothing else you can do but return the body to his crewmates.

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtCivilianPossessed_agreeD2

          The civilians do not take this news well. With nothing more to be done, they activate their drives and depart.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtCivilianPossessed_agreeD1

          The civilians are deeply saddened by the loss. Nevertheless they remain grateful for your help. They offer you what supplies they can spare.

          • Random small reward
          1. Continue
        • oTxtCivilianPossessed_agreeD3

          Minutes after they receive the body, you start hearing screaming over the comms. It is difficult to make out what's happening. You gather that their friend has somehow risen from the dead. Then you hear a disturbing string of impossible words, spoken by a sinister, inhuman voice. "Gth'lk kzlc'th, pksh'zn, pksh'zn, pksh'zklacc..."

          1. Continue
            oTxtCivilianPossessed_agreeD4

            Eldritch lightning slams into your hull, depositing throngs of unnatural beasts. Malign energy begins sweeping across the civilian ship, slaving it to the will of an invisible, infernal master. As monsters devour the civilian crew, the newly possessed starship turns its weapons on you.

            • 1–3 Demonic Crew boarders
            1. Continue
            Fight! vs Empire Raider ship
  2. Refuse
    oTxtCivilianPossessed_refuse

    You turn them down.

    1. Continue
  3. (Auto-Surgeon) Treat the patient with your advanced medical facilities
    oTxtEZcivilianPossessed_bt_medBay1

    The patient appears to be exhibiting novel symptoms unrecognized by your advanced surgical automatons. Nevertheless, the machinery conducts successive surgical procedures and preserves the patient's life.

    1. Continue
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtEZcivilianPossessed_bt_medBay2a

        Unfortunately, the patient is permanently disfigured. The crew of the civilian ship are horrified and quickly depart, leaving you to deal with the aftermath.

        • Aberrant
        1. Continue
      • oTxtEZcivilianPossessed_bt_medBay2b

        You discover the patient had given birth during their ordeal. However, the offspring do not appear normal. The crew of the civilian ship express gratitude for saving their crew member but also terror at the hideous appearance of their offspring and quickly depart, leaving you to deal with the aftermath.

        • Blightling
        1. Continue

CivilianPossessed_agreeC2

→ oTxtCivilianPossessed_agreeC2_1

ColonyDrop

oTxtColonyDrop_intro

An extremist group has infiltrated a local space colony and is threatening to blow the fusion pylons responsible for maintaining the colony's orbit. Without its fusion pylons, the colony would fall into the dwarf planet below, unleashing a tidal wave of nuclear fire that would sweep across two-thirds of its habitable land mass. Recent Imperial census data shows a local civilian population of approximately […] billion.

  1. Contact the extremists
    oTxtColonyDrop_contact1

    "Where was the Empire when the raiders came to slaughter our brothers and enslave our sons and daughters? When our hydro-fields dried up and our nutri-vats sat empty? All our prayers have fallen on deaf ears. We have had enough."

    1. Tell them violence won't solve anything
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtColonyDrop_noViolence1

        The conversation is tense, but you manage to talk down the extremists and convince them to surrender to the authorities. The local magistrate thanks you for your assistance and invites you to come to the execution.

        • Random small reward
        1. Continue
      • oTxtColonyDrop_noViolence2

        "You really think to appeal to us with such childish notions? Pathetic. It's too late now... Let it all burn... "

        1. Continue
          oTxtColonyDrop_noViolence2_1

          A flash of azure sears your vision as the fusion pylons ignite. You watch helplessly as the colony plunges into the planet below, parting its thin atmosphere and shattering its crust in a slow-motion, apocalyptic spectacle. Nothing more can be done here.

          1. Continue
    2. Remind them of their faith in the Immortal King and His plan
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtColonyDrop_faith1

        "Yes... the Immortal King has not abandoned us. We must not give up." The extremist liaison is overcome with emotion. He convinces the extremist group to turn themselves in. The colonists celebrate you as a hero and donate what they can to your cause. One of them joins your crew.

        • Random small reward
        • Imperial Recruit ×0
        1. Continue
      • oTxtColonyDrop_faith2

        The extremists laugh in your face. "Hyperion eh? The "God-King" eh? You still believe in that crap? We have seen the true gods of this reality. And their power is real."

        1. Continue
          oTxtColonyDrop_faith2_1

          An explosion rocks the feed as a squad of Imperial arbitrators breaks into the control room. Several extremists are gunned down in the initial barrage. Suddenly tendrils of energy swirl from the floor and something bursts out of the chest of one of the arbitrators in a crimson shower of blood. The thing lets out a bestial roar.

          1. Continue
            oTxtColonyDrop_faith2_2

            The feed cuts out. You see the same violet tendrils seeping in through the deck of your ship and hastily ready your weapons.

            • 2–4 Demonic Crew boarders
            1. Continue
            After the boarders are defeated:
            oTxtColonyDrop_faith2_3

            The colony falls into the planet. The psychic death screams of billions seems to add yet more fuel to the malevolent energy blanketing the region, and you do everything you can to get out of there before you are overrun.

            • 1–4 Demonic Crew boarders
            1. Continue
            After the boarders are defeated:
            oTxtColonyDrop_faith2_4

            One of the entities drops an unusual object.

            • Random cult equipment
            1. Continue
    3. Nod in agreement
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtColonyDrop_agree2

        Your response is interrupted when a kill team of Imperial arbitrators breaks into the pylon control room and guns down the extremists. They clear the room, check the communication logs, and pinpoint your location. Moments later, an Imperial strike craft peels off from its fleet to intercept you.

        1. Fight
          oTxtColonyDrop_agree2_fight

          The Imperial vessel has closed to attack range. An assault sled crashes into your hull, disgorging an Imperial boarding force.

          • 1–3 Imperial Recruit or Imperial Scribe boarders
          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Empire ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          oTxtColonyDrop_agree2_fight2

          Having underestimated your ship's combat abilities, the remaining arbitrators retreat. You leave before reinforcements arrive.

          1. Continue
        2. Escape
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • oTxtColonyDrop_agree2_escapeA

            You decide to get out of there.

            1. Continue
          • oTxtColonyDrop_agree2_escapeB

            You decide to flee. As you power up your drives, you receive a message from an unknown sender. They express solidarity with your cause and send you coordinates to a nearby supply cache.

            • 60%: Random cult equipment / 40%: Random equipment item
            1. Continue
      • oTxtColonyDrop_agree1

        "It's settled then. The sacrifice of Colony Yulia-755 B will serve as a rallying cry for decades to come." The extremists detonate the fusion pylons. Yulia-755 B collides into the dwarf planet with apocalyptic force. Billions of lives are lost. You recover some useful material from nearby debris.

        • Random small reward
        1. Continue
          oTxtColonyDrop_agree1_1

          Suddenly, you find yourself under a dark cloud. Twisting rivers of blood stretch endlessly toward the horizon. A deep-throated laughter reverberates across the plains.

          1. Continue
            oTxtColonyDrop_agree1_2

            You awaken from your vision. An unusual relic rests on the deck beside you. It is accompanied by a few scorch marks and what appears to be a couple of human teeth.

            • 60%: Random War Cult equipment / 40%: Random equipment item
            1. Continue
  2. Move on
    oTxtColonyDrop_ignore

    You do not have time for this.

    1. Continue
  3. (Engine 4) Prepare for the worst
    oTxtColonyDrop_bt_combined

    The extremists appear determined, and you ready your vessel for the worst. As the fusion pylons begin detonating, you secure your ship to the colony's superstructure and command maximum power to engines.

    1. Continue
      oTxtColonyDrop_bt_combined2

      Despite your vessel's drive thrusters already operating beyond maximum emergency thrust, it appears inadequate to counteract the sheer mass of the falling colony.

      1. Continue
        oTxtColonyDrop_bt_combined3

        Alarms blare as your engines begin to overload. At this moment, you notice a number of other starships gathering around yours. Nearby starships witnessing your action have come to your aid. Slowly, the combined effort is able to accelerate the colony away.

        • 1 system damage to Engines
        1. Continue
          oTxtColonyDrop_bt_combined4

          The colony is no longer in a viable orbit and will need to be abandoned, but the danger to the planet below has been averted. Your ship and crew are treated to festivities and showered with gifts before your eventual departure.

          • Random large reward
          1. Continue
oTxtColonyDrop_agree2B Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Your response is interrupted when a kill team of Imperial arbitrators breaks into the pylon control room and guns down the extremists.

  1. Fight off the arbitrators
    → oTxtColonyDrop_agree2_fight
  2. Escape before the arbitrators arrive
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • → oTxtColonyDrop_agree2_escapeA
    • → oTxtColonyDrop_agree2_escapeB

ColonyDrop_agree1

→ oTxtColonyDrop_agree1_1

ColonyDrop_agree2

→ oTxtColonyDrop_agree2_escapeA

ColonyDrop_bt

→ oTxtColonyDrop_bt_combined

ColonyDrop_faith2

→ oTxtColonyDrop_faith2_1

ColonyDrop_noViolence2

→ oTxtColonyDrop_noViolence2_1

ColonyEvacuation

oTxtColonyEvacuation_intro

You receive a jumble of panicked broadcasts. They are originating from a nearby colony.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/4 each):

    • You get closer to the colony and realize that massive riots have overtaken the populace. Imperial arbitrators have been deployed to pacify the masses.

    • A panicked civilian appears on your viewfeed. "Help! The Imperial arbitrators have gone crazy! They are taking our families and shipping them off to the asteroid mines."

    • You dock with the colony and are immediately assailed by throngs of panicked civilians. "The Empire, their arbitrators have accused us of insurrection, and now they are shooting us! Help!"

    • You learn that riots had been breaking out all over the colony. Imperial arbitrators were deployed to quell the unrest and within hours, the riots devolved into a full on war. Civilians are dying in droves.

    In all cases:
    oTxtColonyEvacuation_considerOptions

    You consider your options.

    1. Rescue as many civilians as you can
      Random outcome (1/3 each):
      • oTxtColonyEvacuation_rescueB

        You deploy your crew onto the colony and gather as many refugees as you can. Once your ship is at capacity, you transport the passengers to a neighboring colony. The survivors thank you for your help. One of them offers you a small reward.

        • Random small reward
        1. Continue
      • oTxtColonyEvacuation_rescueC

        You deploy your crew onto the colony to try and rescue the civilians. Unfortunately the crowds are out of control. One of your crew is nearly killed in the chaos. With the situation rapidly deteriorating, you have no choice but to move on.

        • Random crew damaged (90%)
        1. Continue
      • oTxtColonyEvacuation_rescueA

        You try to rescue as many civilians as you can, but without anyone to help, the panicked civilians quickly overwhelm you and stampede across your ship. You are forced to disengage the docking clamps. Several civilians stream out the airlock, their depressurized bodies staring back at you lifelessly. Your hull suffers minor damage.

        • −1 hull HP
        • Commander damaged (30%)
        1. Continue
    2. Fight off the arbitrators
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtColonyEvacuation_fightA

        You engage an arbitrator vessel, hopefully buying time for the civilians to escape.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Empire Large ship
        After the fight:
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtColonyEvacuation_fightA_1

          The arbitrator vessel has been dispatched. The remaining arbitrator fleet jumps away in a retreat.

          • Crew recalled to ship
          1. Continue
            Random outcome (weighted):
            • 3/4
              oTxtColonyEvacuation_afterFightB

              The following day, you receive a message along with a small supply package. "You saved my family. I know it is not much, but please, take it."

              • Random small reward
              1. Continue
            • 1/4
              oTxtColonyEvacuation_afterFightA

              Several days later, a noble family makes contact with you and sends you a sizable reward. "We would not be alive right now without your heroism."

              • Random large reward
              1. Continue
        • oTxtColonyEvacuation_fightA_2

          You watch as the last of the colonial transports leave the system before moving on.

          • Crew recalled to ship
          1. Continue
            Random outcome (weighted):
            • 3/4 → oTxtColonyEvacuation_afterFightB
            • 1/4 → oTxtColonyEvacuation_afterFightA
      • oTxtColonyEvacuation_fightB

        You and your crew mount a counter-offensive against the Imperial arbitrators. The battle is hard-won, but in the end, everyone manages to make it out intact. Hundreds of citizens managed to escape while you kept the arbitrators busy. You retreat to your ship at the last moment, after having sustained multiple injuries.

        • All crew damaged (70%)
        1. Continue
          Random outcome (weighted):
          • 3/4 → oTxtColonyEvacuation_afterFightB
          • 1/4 → oTxtColonyEvacuation_afterFightA
    3. Avoid getting involved
      oTxtColonyEvacuation_ignore

      You close the channel and move on.

      1. Continue
  2. Ignore them
    → oTxtColonyEvacuation_ignore
  3. (Terror Amplifier) Help the civilians defeat the Arbitrators
    oTxtColonyEvacuation_bt_stun

    You realize the quickest way to ensure victory over the Imperial Arbitrators would be to incapacitate their formations with your vessel's terror amplifier. Aided by the colonists, you send precise blasts of debilitating energy deep into the colony. The civilians quickly overwhelm and decimate the hapless Imperials. Amidst the subsequent celebrations, you are offered spoils taken from the defeated Imperials.

    • Random ship weapon
    1. Continue
oTxtColonyEvacuation_avoid Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You decide not to get involved.

  1. Continue
oTxtColonyEvacuation_rescueD Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You take your crew to the colony and attempt to rescue the civilians. Shockingly, by the time you arrive, all of the riots seem to have stopped. A mysterious winged apparition floats silently above the crowds. A moment later, every single person, arbitrators included, drops to their knees. "Fear not, within me resides the will of the Immortal King. He has heard your pleas, and has come to bring you salvation."

  1. Continue
    oTxtColonyEvacuation_rescueD2

    With nothing more to be done, you collect your crew and depart.

    1. Continue

ColonyEvacuation_bt

→ oTxtColonyEvacuation_bt_stun

ColonyEvacuation_fightA

→ oTxtColonyEvacuation_fightA_1

ColonyOverthrow

oTxtColonyOverthrow_intro

You arrive at an orbital colony in the midst of celebrating the overthrow of its Imperial governor.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/6 each):
    • oTxtColonyOverthrow_investigateC

      You obtain a weapon from one of the colonists, alleged to have been liberated from the personal collection of the deposed governor.

      • 60%: Random cult equipment / 40%: Random equipment item
      1. Continue
    • oTxtColonyOverthrow_investigateE

      You find the celebratory atmosphere belies an almost anarchic state of disorder. You return to your vessel to discover a salvage crew cutting off pieces of plating from your ship.

      • −1 hull HP
      1. Continue
    • oTxtColonyOverthrow_investigateA

      A few of the more militant rebels are consumed by the thrill of victory. They commandeer an Imperial vessel and launch assault sleds at your ship, having mistaken you for an enemy.

      • 1–2 Raider crew boarders
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the fight:
      oTxtColonyOverthrow_investigateA_1

      You retrieve what you can and return to your ship.

      • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
      • Crew recalled to ship
      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtColonyOverthrow_afterward3

          You depart the colony shortly before the arrival of a large Imperial fleet. From a distance you watch them unleash their weaponry against the unfortunate orbital colony, obliterating it.

          • Crew recalled to ship
          1. Continue
        • oTxtColonyOverthrow_afterward1

          The colony's intercoms begin blaring demands for the colonists to return to their habitation blocks. Rebel militiamen begin appearing in packs to beat the disobedient into submission. A few groups of rebels rush to secure docked vessels. You quickly leave before the situation gets out of hand.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtColonyOverthrow_afterward2

          An Imperial fleet arrives. They demand the unconditional surrender of all insurrectionists under the threat of their combined firepower. You undock and escape before the Imperials can fully encircle the colony. Nevertheless, a few warships manage to inflict some minor damage to your ship on your way out.

          • Crew recalled to ship
          • 1–3 breaches spawned
          1. Continue
    • oTxtColonyOverthrow_investigateB

      The atmosphere within the colony is suffused with optimism and gaiety as the rebel colonists indulge in their newfound liberty. You are able to freely procure a quantity of ordnance from the colony's armory.

      • 4–10 missiles
      1. Continue
    • oTxtColonyOverthrow_investigateF

      You obtain some valuables, freely given away in the euphoric spirit of celebration. You arrive back at your ship to discover a few of the rebel colonists waiting for you. "Sorry, we cannot let you leave with that."

      • Random moderate reward
      • 2–3 Raider crew boarders
      1. Continue
    • oTxtColonyOverthrow_investigateD

      You witness several starships being stripped of their life support systems. Evidently, the colony's own water recyclers had been destroyed during the coup. You negotiate to acquire the armament from one of the unspaceworthy ships.

      • Random ship weapon
      1. Continue
  2. Leave
  3. (Raider Outlaw) Join the celebration
    oTxtColonyOverthrow_bt_raider

    You have arrived at an opportune moment. The rebels are flush with euphoric optimism from their recent victory, and see in you and your crew the means to extend their struggle against the Imperial order. You are given equipment and supplies looted from elsewhere in the colony. You take what you can, and move on before the celebrations wind down.

    • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
    • Random cult equipment
    1. Continue
oTxtColonyOverthrow_leave Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You leave the colonists to their business and press onwards.

  1. Continue

ColonyOverthrow_bt

→ oTxtColonyOverthrow_bt_raider

ColonyOverthrow_investigateA

→ oTxtColonyOverthrow_investigateA_1

ColonyTraining

oTxtColonyTraining_intro

Upon re-entering realspace, you are bombarded with a flurry of distress signals originating from a nearby civilian colony.

  1. Respond to distress signals
    oTxtColonyTraining_respond

    A portly woman, likely the colony's governess, flicks onto your screen. She informs you of the colony's situation -- that once frequent Imperial patrols have ceased entirely, and that they have been subject to countless pirate raids. Her people appear thin, hopeless, and desperate. She begs you to aid them.

    1. Offer protection
      oTxtColonyTraining_protectA1

      Accepting their pleas, you station your ship in low orbit, monitoring the system for the pirates' return. Within days a raider landing ship and an escort are spotted in approach towards the colony. The escort moves to engage -- if not distract you as the landing ship descends on the colony.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider Small ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtColonyTraining_protectA2

        Though now reduced to still-hot debris, the escort fulfilled its goal in distracting you. The landing ship was able to get on and off the planet, its haul filled with what little the colony had left. The survivors can offer nothing but their deepest thanks.

        1. Continue
      • oTxtColonyTraining_protectB1

        Having swiftly dispatched its escort, you set your sights on the landing ship. While its raid was a success, you quickly close in on it, preventing the lightly armed ship from escaping. Rather than fire or try to escape, it opens a comms channel. "Commander, surely we can reach some resolution."

        1. Negotiate
          oTxtColonyTraining_negotiate

          While their rapacious reputation is not without warrant, the raider clans are ultimately pragmatic. After a short period of negotiation, you manage to secure the release of all captured colonists, though not the colony's possessions. The freed colonists are overjoyed upon reuniting with their family members. Indebted, one colonist pledges to serve on your ship.

          • Imperial Recruit
          1. Continue
        2. Destroy
          oTxtColonyTraining_destroy

          There can be no diplomacy with these raiders. You knock out the landing ship's engines, causing it to slowly tumble into the planet itself. Both the crew and captured colonists are at best critically injured from the crash, but the ship's loot and hull remain largely intact. The colonists solemnly recover the prior, you scavenge the latter.

          • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
          • +1–2 hull HP
          1. Continue
    2. (Raider Outlaw) Provide training to militias
      oTxtColonyTraining_train1

      Versed in the tactics of their foes, you send your raider crewmember to aid the colonists. Despite initial skepticism, the colonists take well to their new advisor, and develop counter-strategies against pirate invasion.

      1. Continue
        oTxtColonyTraining_train2

        During this period of training, you remain in orbit monitoring the system. With the colonists at last prepared, you retrieve your crewmember, and no sooner do you detect two ships enter the system -- a raider landing ship, the enduring tormentor of this colony, and its escort for protection. The landing ship accelerates towards the colony, the escort towards you.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Raider Small ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        oTxtColonyTraining_train3

        You rush to the colony after finishing off the escort. By the time you arrive, the planetside situation is stable. The colonists successfully outmaneuvered and repelled the raiders, leaving the colony largely intact. Teary-eyed, the governess personally thanks you, and awards you full scrapping rights to the landing ship.

        • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
        • Hull Integrity Patch
        1. Continue
    3. (War Cultist) Oversee total war measures
      oTxtColonyTraining_total1

      While the colony's defenses have been whittled away by the pirates, your Kromic cultist presents a number of stopgap solutions. Mass accelerators are fashioned into anti-ship batteries, medical supplies are converted into psyk-chems. The pirates will no longer have easy prey.

      1. Continue
        oTxtColonyTraining_total2

        You assist your cultist in augmenting the colony's defences, and monitor the system planetside. Days later, and with the colony fully prepared for war, you detect a pair of oncoming raider ships. You blast off into orbit, the colonists cheering you on raucously as you ascend. The larger landing ship rockets towards the colony as the smaller escort peels off to engage you.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Raider Small ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        oTxtColonyTraining_total3

        The moment the escort explodes, your comms are filled with exuberant chatter from the colony. The colonists not only knocked out the landing ship as it descended, but quickly overwhelmed its crew -- they were watching the battle above in support of you. In a frenzied reversal of fortune, the raiders have been taken as slaves by the colonists, who offer you one as thanks. You set them to work on board as the rest of your crew salvages the landing ship.

        • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
        • Corsair
        1. Continue
    4. Rob the colonists
      oTxtColonyTraining_rob

      Seldom is there any difference between the desperate and the vulnerable. Under threat of bombardment, the colonists scrape together a meager offering.

      • Very small amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
    5. Leave
      oTxtColonyTraining_leave

      This isn't your problem to solve. You close comms with the governess and leave.

      1. Continue

Convoy

oTxtConvoy Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You approach one of the transport vessels in the convoy.

CoreNothing

oTxtCoreNothing_intro

[...]

  1. Continue

CrewRecallBeforeFight

oTxtCrewRecallBeforeFight Unreachable — not referenced by any event

There is no time to waste. You quickly activate your recall beacon before the next engagement.

DeathCultAttackEmpire

oTxtDeathCultAttackEmpire_intro

You detect an Imperial ship under attack by a death cult monitor.

  1. Attack the death cult monitor
    oTxtDeathCultAttackEmpire_attack

    You move to engage the cultists.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Plague ship
    After the rewards from the fight:
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtDeathCultAttackEmpire_winA

      The Imperial commander curtly acknowledges your help, and ejects a small supply crate before jumping out.

      • Random Imperial weapon
      1. Continue
    • oTxtDeathCultAttackEmpire_winB

      After dispatching the enemy, you turn your attention back to the Imperial vessel. Unfortunately it is no where to be found. It must have escaped.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtDeathCultAttackEmpire_winC

      An Imperial reinforcement fleet arrives and mistakes you for the enemy. You are forced to flee, but not without sustaining some minor damage.

      • Crew recalled to ship
      • 1–2 breaches spawned
      1. Continue
  2. Leave the Imperials to their fate
    oTxtDeathCultAttackEmpire_leave

    You decide to not interfere. The cultist warship decimates the Imperial vessel. You move in and recover the few valuables left after the death cult vessel jumps out.

    • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
    1. Continue
  3. (Hull Reaper) Attack the Imperial ship
    oTxtDeathCultAttackEmpire_bt_hullReaper1

    You prime your hull reaper system and engage the beleaguered Imperial ship. Your vessel's hull is regenerated as the Imperial ship crumbles beneath the assault.

    • +3–5 hull HP
    1. Continue
      oTxtDeathCultAttackEmpire_bt_hullReaper2

      Startled by the suddenness of your vessel's appearance, the death cult monitor withdraws. You salvage the remnants of the destroyed Imperial ship and move on.

      • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue

DeathCultAttackEmpire_bt

→ oTxtDeathCultAttackEmpire_bt_hullReaper1

DeathCultCrippled

oTxtDeathCultCrippled_intro

You arrive at the jump node just in time to see a death cult vessel land a crippling strike on another starship. The second starship also bears the heraldry of the death cults.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeathCultCrippled_intro2

    The first vessel departs, leaving only the crippled starship drifting helplessly before you.

    1. Contact the ship
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtDeathCultCrippled_contact2

        You attempt to contact the ship, however its communications relay appears to be damaged. Furthermore, it is not as crippled as it initially appeared. It locks on to your ship and begins firing.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Plague ship
      • oTxtDeathCultCrippled_contact

        You contact the commander of the crippled ship. He explains his falling out with his rival. After the battle, his rival left him here to die. "Let me join you. All I ask in return is the chance to hunt down my enemies."

        • Recruit enemy commander as crew
        1. Continue
    2. Finish off the survivors and salvage what you can
      Random outcome (1/3 each):
      • oTxtDeathCultCrippled_finishOff2

        You move in to salvage material from the crippled ship. By the time you get there, all life signs aboard have disappeared.

        • reward_scrapLow or reward_scrapMedium
        1. Continue
      • oTxtDeathCultCrippled_finishOff1

        You move in to finish off the crippled ship. Unfortunately it is not as crippled as you thought. It immediately retaliates.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Plague ship
      • oTxtDeathCultCrippled_finishOff3

        As you near the vessel, you are met with an intense wave of psychic energy. "Great Endfather, I beseech you! Grant me the strength to enact vengeance upon my foe!"

        1. Continue
          oTxtDeathCultCrippled_finishOff3_1

          The enemy commander materializes on your deck, reinvigorated by dark power. The starship likewise appears to have experienced a miraculous renewal, with all traces of battle damage effaced by some supernatural power. You prepare to fight.

          • 1 Greater Necromancer boarder
          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Possessed ship
    3. (Death Cultist) Have your death cultist contact the ship
      oTxtDeathCultCrippled_deathCultist

      Your cultist speaks with the commander and is able to establish a rapport. The commander explains his falling out with his rival. After the battle, his rival left him here to die. "Let me join you. All I ask in return is the chance to hunt down my enemies."

      • Recruit enemy commander as crew
      1. Continue
    4. Ignore it and move on
      oTxtDeathCultCrippled_ignore

      You decide not to get involved.

      1. Continue

DeathCultCrippled_finishOff3

→ oTxtDeathCultCrippled_finishOff3_1

DeathCultEdifice

oTxtDeathCultEdifice_intro

You come across a large orbital structure. It is a massive floating edifice in the shape of a cross. Its surface is covered in human corpses.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/5 each):
    • oTxtDeathCultEdifice_investigateC

      The edifice suddenly releases a shockwave of infernal energy. You are under attack.

      • 1–3 Bloat Mite or Hungerer boarders
      1. Continue
      After the boarders are defeated:
      oTxtDeathCultEdifice_investigateC2

      You discover a collection of strange materials left behind by your attackers.

      • random Item (PLAGUECONSUMABLES) ×irandom_range(2, 4)
      1. Continue
    • oTxtDeathCultEdifice_investigateD

      You inspect the edifice. Despite its unusual appearance, it does not seem to carry any significance with regards to you and your journey. You move on.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtDeathCultEdifice_investigateB

      The edifice suddenly begins to emit clouds of sickly vapors. They soon envelop your ship, suffusing the corridors with noxious gas. An enemy starship materializes through the miasma. As you turn to engage the threat, you overhear a bellow of rage coming from somewhere on your ship. "At last, I have awakened."

      • 1 Death Knight boarder
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Possessed ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtDeathCultEdifice_investigateB2

      The sickly vapors subside. The edifice is inert.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtDeathCultEdifice_investigateE

      You approach the edifice. The central mass of corpses suddenly erupts with putrid energy. The energy twists through the void and tunnels into one of your crew. Their skin cracks and blisters, hurtling them through an accelerated process of decay. Your crew survives the ordeal, but it seems they are permanently transformed.

      • Greater Corpulent
      • Lose a random crew member
      1. Continue
    • oTxtDeathCultEdifice_investigateA

      You approach the edifice. It begins to glow. A deep roar reverberates across time and space. A single word repeats over and over in your mind: "Sacrifice."

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtDeathCultEdifice_investigateA_1

          Moments later, an unusual signature appears on your viewfeed. It moves into attack range.

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Possessed ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          Random outcome (1/3 each):
          • oTxtDeathCultEdifice_investigateA_1a

            You discover a strange relic on the deck beside you, alongside a clump of rotted bones.

            • Translocator
            1. Continue
          • oTxtDeathCultEdifice_investigateA_1b

            The stench of decay suddenly fills your nostrils. You look up to find a shambling figure, standing before you. It stares attentively.

            • Greater Corpulent
            1. Continue
          • oTxtDeathCultEdifice_investigateA_1c

            The edifice is no longer glowing. You move on.

            1. Continue
        • oTxtDeathCultEdifice_investigateA_2

          Suddenly, a sickly liquid spews out from the center of the cross. It coalesces into the shape of a starship.

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Possessed ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          Random outcome (1/3 each):
          • → oTxtDeathCultEdifice_investigateA_1a
          • → oTxtDeathCultEdifice_investigateA_1b
          • → oTxtDeathCultEdifice_investigateA_1c
      After the rewards from the fight:
      Random outcome (1/3 each):
      • → oTxtDeathCultEdifice_investigateA_1a
      • → oTxtDeathCultEdifice_investigateA_1b
      • → oTxtDeathCultEdifice_investigateA_1c
  2. Move on
    oTxtDeathCultEdifice_moveOn

    You decide to move on.

    1. Continue
  3. (Death Cultist) Send your cultist to investigate the structure
    oTxtDeathCultEdifice_deathCultist1

    Your cultist crewmember appears familiar with the structure and advises you to approach from a specific direction. You look on as your crewmember mutters an incomprehensible litany.

    1. Continue
      oTxtDeathCultEdifice_deathCultist2

      After some time, your sensors detect movement from the structure. A large mass of bodies begins accumulating along one of its surfaces. The mass detaches and drifts towards your vessel. Sensor analysis shows it to be composed of large quantities of useful material beneath the mummified corpses covering its exterior.

      • Large amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
        oTxtDeathCultEdifice_deathCultist3

        The structure is no longer responsive. You move on.

        1. Continue

DeathCultEdifice_investigateA

→ oTxtDeathCultEdifice_investigateA_1

DeathCultInvasionAftermath

oTxtDeathCultInvasionAftermath_intro

You arrive in the aftermath of a death cult invasion. Dilapidated space colonies fill your viewport, ravaged by the predation of the Gorgothian cults.

  1. Investigate the colonies
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtDeathCultInvasionAftermath_investigateC

      You barely cross the perimeter of the nearest colony when a starship reveals itself. Its weapons are armed.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Plague ship
    • oTxtDeathCultInvasionAftermath_investigateA

      You scan the nearest colony and pick up a few small signatures indicating the presence of storage vaults. You recover several of them. Few of the vaults contain anything of value.

      • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
    • oTxtDeathCultInvasionAftermath_investigateD

      Your investigation of the colonies has triggered an array of toxin mines. They must have been left behind by the death cult fleet.

      • 4–8 poison clouds spawned
      1. Continue
        oTxtDeathCultInvasionAftermath_investigateD_1

        To make matters worse, a hostile ship emerges from the debris.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Plague ship
    • oTxtDeathCultInvasionAftermath_investigateB

      While approaching the colonies, you notice a faint green light flickering between two large chunks of debris. You send an away team to investigate the light.

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtDeathCultInvasionAftermath_investigateB_1

          The light is revealed to be the power indicator of a half-functioning munitions locker. You retrieve the munitions inside.

          • Moderate amount of missile ammo (5–8)
          1. Continue
        • oTxtDeathCultInvasionAftermath_investigateB_2

          You discover the source of the light: miasmic energy released from a damaged storage vault. You recover a few intact materials from the vault.

          • Random Death Cult consumable
          1. Continue
        • oTxtDeathCultInvasionAftermath_investigateB_3

          By the time your away team has arrived, the light has completely vanished. They return to your ship with mysterious injuries covering their bodies. You feel a sharp pain on your chest and likewise discover similar injuries on yourself.

          • All crew damaged (50%)
          1. Continue
            oTxtDeathCultInvasionAftermath_investigateB_3a

            Suddenly, a starship emerges from the debris behind you. A leering visage appears on your viewscreen. "The pain-curse has taken hold. Now we strike."

            1. Continue
            Fight! vs Plague ship
  2. Move on
    oTxtDeathCultInvasionAftermath_moveOn

    You press onwards.

    1. Continue
  3. (Death Knight) Send your death knight crewmember to search for survivors
    oTxtDeathCultInvasionAftermath_plagueKnight1

    You send your death knight aboard one of the wrecked hab colonies still shrouded in toxic miasma. The altered physiology of your crew member is invigorated by the lingering toxic vapors. Empowered by unnatural vigor, they rapidly sift through the rubble.

    1. Continue
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtDeathCultInvasionAftermath_plagueKnight2b

        At length, your crewmember returns with a few weapons recovered from the slain.

        • Random Imperial weapon
        • Random Death Cult weapon
        1. Continue
      • oTxtDeathCultInvasionAftermath_plagueKnight2a

        At length, your crewmember returns cradling a lone survivor. The survivor had evaded capture by the cultists, but the mutagenic residues left in the wake of the invasion has changed them into something no longer quite human.

        • Greater Corpulent or Ghoul Magus
        1. Continue
  4. (Death Cultist) Search for survivors
    oTxtDeathCultInvasionAftermath_bt_plagueCultist

    Much of the equipment within the colonies have been rendered inoperative, corroded and twisted by mutagenic miasmas. Your death cult crewmember is able to quickly identify areas within the colonies affected the least by such hazards. A search of those areas turns up some still-functional equipment.

    • Random equipment item
    1. Continue

DeathCultInvasionAftermath_bt

→ oTxtDeathCultInvasionAftermath_bt_plagueCultist

DeathCultInvasionAftermath_investigateB

→ oTxtDeathCultInvasionAftermath_investigateB_1

DeathCultInvasionAftermath_investigateD

→ oTxtDeathCultInvasionAftermath_investigateD_1

DeathCultMoon

oTxtDeathCultMoon_intro

You detect a number of death cult vessels coming and going from a nearby moon.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtDeathCultMoon_investigateB

      There is an outpost on the far side of the moon. A cult vessel undocks from the outpost and approaches you. They wish to conduct business.

      • Proceeds to shop
      1. Continue
    • oTxtDeathCultMoon_investigateA

      The colony on the far side of the moon appears to be overrun by shambling horrors. The cultist vessels descend to the surface, not to rescue the few living survivors, but to collect the hordes of the walking dead.

      1. Attack the cultist vessels
        oTxtDeathCultMoon_attackCultist

        As you approach the nearest cult vessel you notice it radiating a vile energy. You realize the vessel has been possessed by a malicious presence with a mind of its own.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Possessed ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        oTxtDeathCultMoon_attackCultist2

        The colony on the moon below has been completely overrun. There is nothing more you can do.

        1. Continue
      2. Rescue the living
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtDeathCultMoon_rescue3

          Unfortunately by the time you arrive at the surface, the colony has been totally overrun by the hordes of shambling abominations.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtDeathCultMoon_rescue1

          You descend to the surface and try to pick up as many survivors as you can.

          1. Continue
            Random outcome (1/3 each):
            • oTxtDeathCultMoon_rescueOutcomeC

              The survivors are grateful to be rescued. You transfer them to a passing technocultist hauler, but one of them requests to join your crew.

              • Corsair or Scavenger or Imperial Recruit
              1. Continue
            • oTxtDeathCultMoon_rescueOutcomeA

              You transfer the survivors to a passing technocultist hauler, to be dropped off in a less chaotic sector. The survivors are grateful, and offer you all they can spare.

              • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
              1. Continue
            • oTxtDeathCultMoon_rescueOutcomeB

              The colonists you picked up were already tainted by the malefic energies. One by one, they transform into horrid mockeries of their former selves and begin rampaging through your ship.

              • 6–8 Greater Corpulent or Ghoul Magus boarders
              1. Continue
        • oTxtDeathCultMoon_rescue2

          You attempt to rescue as many of the living as you can. Unfortunately one of your crew is overwhelmed by the abominable horde in the process.

          • Lose a random crew member
          1. Continue
            Random outcome (1/3 each):
            • → oTxtDeathCultMoon_rescueOutcomeC
            • → oTxtDeathCultMoon_rescueOutcomeA
            • → oTxtDeathCultMoon_rescueOutcomeB
  2. Leave
    oTxtDeathCultMoon_leave

    You leave the scene and move on.

    1. Continue

DeathCultSlaveShip

oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_intro

You encounter a slave ship bearing the markings of the death cults.

  1. Hail them
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailB

      The ship responds to your hails with aggression. You have been boarded.

      • 1 Gorgothian Legionnaire boarder
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Plague ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailB_victory1

        You scan the aftermath and find no survivors.

        1. Continue
      • oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailB_victory2

        You search the area and manage to rescue a survivor.

        • Husk
        1. Continue
    • oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailA

      The commander of the slave ship responds. "These machine men are poorly suited for our rituals. We would be willing to sell them at a discount."

      1. Purchase one of the "machine men" for 15 scrap
        oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailA_purchase

        "Very well."

        • Technocult crew
        • −15 ⬗ Scrap
        1. Continue
      2. Decline
        oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailA_decline

        "How unfortunate."

        1. Continue
      3. Attack the slaver scum
        oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailA_attack

        You ready your weapons.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Plague ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailA_victory1

          You scan the aftermath and find no survivors.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailA_victory2

          You search the area and manage to rescue a survivor.

          • Technocult crew
          1. Continue
    • oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailC

      You hail the slave ship. They respond with a threat. "Hand over one of your men and we will let you live."

      1. Hand over a random crew member
        oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailC_handOver

        "You have made the right choice."

        • Lose a random crew member
        1. Continue
      2. Refuse
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailC_refuse1

          "So you have chosen death."

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Plague ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailC_victory1

            You scan the aftermath and find no survivors.

            1. Continue
          • oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailC_victory2

            You search the area and manage to rescue a survivor.

            • Husk
            1. Continue
        • oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailC_refuse2

          The slavers move in to attack. A blast echoes down the halls of your vessel. You have been boarded.

          • 1 Gorgothian Legionnaire boarder
          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Plague ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • → oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailC_victory1
          • → oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailC_victory2
  2. Ignore them
    oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_ignore

    You move on.

    1. Continue
  3. (Death Cultist) Solicit their assistance
    oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_bt_plagueCultist

    Using the secret signs and codes of the death cults, your cultist crewmember gains the trust of the slaver crew. They are unable to spare any crew to help, but offer you an artifact radiant with unholy energies instead.

    • Death Totem
    1. Continue

DeathCultSlaveShip_bt

→ oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_bt_plagueCultist

DeathCultSlaveShip_hailA

→ oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailA_attack

DeathCultSlaveShip_hailB

→ oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailB_victory1

→ oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailC_handOver
oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailC_victory3 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You scan the aftermath and find no survivors.

  1. Continue
oTxtDeathCultSlaveShip_hailC_victory4 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You search the area and manage to rescue a survivor.

  1. Continue

DeathFacility

oTxtDeathFacility_intro

You detect a small facility orbiting a nearby moon. The symbols on its exterior plating resemble those of the death cults.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/5 each):
    • oTxtDeathFacility_investigateC

      You dock with the facility and are suddenly ambushed by a horde of undead monstrosities.

      • 2–4 Greater Corpulent or Ghoul Magus boarders
      1. Continue
      After the boarders are defeated:
      oTxtDeathFacility_investigateC_1

      You notice a piece of equipment on one of the bodies. It could prove useful.

      • Random equipment item
      1. Continue
    • oTxtDeathFacility_investigateB

      The facility is in ruins, and most of the equipment is destroyed. However, you do spot a small, unopened storage container that appears to be undamaged.

      • Random cult equipment
      1. Continue
    • oTxtDeathFacility_investigateA

      You enter the facility and find shelves filled with vials of infectious substances and various plague-causing agents. Despite the danger, you manage to extract some potent reagents that could prove useful.

      • Random Death Cult consumable
      1. Continue
    • oTxtDeathFacility_investigateE

      A hostile warship suddenly undocks from the facility. The facility itself detonates, emitting massive clouds of toxic gas. The gas seems to be animated by a sorcerous vitality. You hear a voice over the comms. "Time to die."

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Plague ship
    • oTxtDeathFacility_investigateD

      As soon as you dock with the facility, a group of plague cultists emerge from the shadows, chanting strange verses of dark power. You can see that the laboratory has been turned into a massive breeding ground for infectious organisms.

      • 1–2 Death Cultist, Death Mage, or Necromancer or Greater Corpulent boarders
      • 1–2 Ghoul or Rotspawn boarders
      1. Continue
      After the boarders are defeated:
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtDeathFacility_investigateD_1

        The laboratory is too dangerous to explore. However you do manage to cover an unusual weapon from one of the bodies.

        • Plague Sword
        1. Continue
      • oTxtDeathFacility_investigateD_2

        The laboratory is too dangerous to explore. You move on.

        1. Continue
  2. Avoid it and move on
    oTxtDeathFacility_avoid

    You decide to avoid the facility and move on, not wanting to risk infection by potentially dangerous pathogens.

    1. Continue

DeathFacility_investigateC

→ oTxtDeathFacility_investigateC_1

DeathFacility_investigateD

→ oTxtDeathFacility_investigateD_1

DeathFleetBattle

oTxtDeathFleetBattle_intro

As you arrive at the system your multispex detects a small colony located on the third moon of a gas giant. As you approach, you are contacted by a partially-rotted Gorgothian necromancer. "There is death to be dealt here," he says, and gives you coordinates to a nearby system. "The Endfather's fleets will clash with the raider clans. Serve, and you will be rewarded. And you may take what you can from their corpses."

  1. Accept the offer
    oTxtDeathFleetBattle_accept

    You accept the task. A recently died ship-labourer shambles to the deck, coordinates carved onto his skin with Gorgothian sorcery.

    • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDeathFleetBattleQuest_intro
    1. Continue
  2. Decline and move on
    oTxtDeathFleetBattle_decline

    You decline and move on. A supernatural chill rushes through the ship, then abates.

    1. Continue

DeathFleetBattleQuest

oTxtDeathFleetBattleQuest_intro

As you arrive at the system you are immediately thrust into a raging maelstrom of explosions, ships, and the screams of the dead and the dying. Your crew takes battle stations and the pilot begins evasive maneuvers. You do not have the time to think or react as a raider ship charges towards you, sending boarders onto your ship.

  • 3–4 Corsair, Scavenger, Infiltrator, or Gene Soldier boarders
  1. Continue
Fight! vs Raider ship
After the fight:
oTxtDeathFleetBattleQuest_intro_win1

The first of the raider vessels has been silenced. You make preparations to face additional threats.

  • Crew recalled to ship
  1. Continue
    oTxtDeathFleetBattleQuest_intro_win2

    Another enemy ship charges through the debris to attack you.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Raider ship
    After the rewards from the fight:
    oTxtDeathFleetBattleQuest_intro_win3

    You disable the ship. It careens into a raider battleship, both vanishing in a cascade of explosions. Elsewhere the raider fleet is retreating in disarray. A corpulent Gorgothian ship covered in blisters approaches.

    1. Continue
      oTxtDeathFleetBattleQuest_intro_win4

      You are contacted. "Take what you desire, and may the Endfather bless your path, wanderer." An escape pod filled with corpses lands in your cargo bay. Within you find supplies and a ship weapon of Gorgothian make.

      • Large amount of ⬗ Scrap
      • Moderate amount of missile ammo (5–8)
      • Grave Beam
      1. Continue
        oTxtDeathFleetBattleQuest_intro_win5

        You scavenge some loot from the battlefield and head off.

        • Random equipment item
        • Breach Charge ×2
        1. Continue

DeathFleetBattleQuest_intro

→ oTxtDeathFleetBattleQuest_intro_win1

DeathNecropolis

oTxtDeathNecropolis_intro

[...]

  1. Explore the Necropolis
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtDeathNecropolis_investigateB

      As you prepare your boarding party for exploration, a hostile ship appears from the other side of the wreck. It trains its weapons on you.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Plague ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtDeathNecropolis_investigateB_win

      You find a small group of people hiding in the catacombs underneath the lunar surface. They bear markings of the Cult of Death. There is something suspicious about them: they do not seem to care about what is happening around them. However, without intervention they would die here, and soon.

      1. Rescue the cultists and bring them to your ship
        oTxtDeathNecropolis_investigateB_rescue

        You rescue the cultists. They explain that a nearby plague shrine was attacked by Imperials. The treasures within are yours to keep as long as the attackers are slain.

        • Mortician or Apostle of Decay
        • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDeathVaultQuest_intro
        1. Continue
      2. Kill the cultists and take their belongings
        oTxtDeathNecropolis_investigateB_kill

        You kill the cultists. The catacombs fall and you scavenge what you can.

        • 1 random equipment item
        1. Continue
    • oTxtDeathNecropolis_investigateA

      You find a small group of people hiding in the catacombs beneath the surface. They bear markings of the Cult of Death. There is something suspicious about them: they do not seem to care about what is happening around them at all. However, without intervention they would die here, and soon.

      1. Rescue the cultists
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtDeathNecropolis_investigateA_rescue2

          The cultists betray you. A death cult ship appears on your multispex as your vessel is boarded by hostiles.

          • 3–4 Gorgothian Legionnaire, Death Cultist, Death Mage, Necromancer, or Mortician boarders
          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Plague ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          oTxtDeathNecropolis_investigateA_rescue2_win

          The last cultist is dead. You take what you can and head for the next system.

          • Random Death Cult consumable
          1. Continue
        • oTxtDeathNecropolis_investigateA_rescue3

          The cultists betray you and their leader has sabotaged your systems. A death cult vessel attacks you and your ship is boarded by hostiles.

          • 3–4 Gorgothian Legionnaire, Death Cultist, Death Mage, Necromancer, or Mortician boarders
          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Plague ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          oTxtDeathNecropolis_investigateA_rescue3_win

          The cultists have been eradicated. You find some equipment.

          • 2 random equipment items
          1. Continue
        • oTxtDeathNecropolis_investigateA_rescue

          You rescue the cultists. They explain that a nearby plague shrine was attacked by Imperials. The treasures within are yours to keep as long as the attackers are slain.

          • Greater Necromancer or Apostle of Famine
          • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDeathVaultQuest_intro
          1. Continue
      2. Kill the cultists and take their belongings
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtDeathNecropolis_investigateA_kill

          You collapse the catacombs and take whatever is left.

          • 1 random equipment item
          1. Continue
        • oTxtDeathNecropolis_investigateA_kill2

          You bring down the catacombs and scavenge the dead. Suddenly, your ship's warning klaxons come to life! One of the cultists has survived and has boarded your ship.

          • 1 Strong Death Cult crew boarder
          1. Continue
          After the boarders are defeated:
          oTxtDeathNecropolis_investigateA_kill2_win

          The last cultist is dead. You find some equipment and leave.

          • Random Death Cult psychic item
          1. Continue
      3. Leave the cultists to their fate
        oTxtDeathNecropolis_investigateA_moveOn

        Something feels wrong. You depart and leave the cultists for dead.

        1. Continue
  2. Ignore the cultists and salvage whatever you can
    oTxtDeathNecropolis_salvage

    You ignore the cultists and take whatever you can with you. You find some scrap.

    • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
    1. Continue
  3. Move on
    oTxtDeathNecropolis_moveOn

    You move on.

    1. Continue

DeathRiftExperiment

oTxtDeathRiftExperiment_intro

You detect anomalous subspace fluctuations. They are emanating from a death cult vessel.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/5 each):
    • oTxtDeathRiftExperiment_investigateE

      The cultist vessel appears intact but abandoned. You recover an occult weapon before departing the area.

      • Random cult weapon
      1. Continue
    • oTxtDeathRiftExperiment_investigateC

      You see the cultist vessel visibly distort as it is overtaken by an infernal intelligence. It turns to attack.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Possessed ship
    • oTxtDeathRiftExperiment_investigateB

      You see the cultist vessel visibly distort as it is overtaken by an infernal intelligence. It jumps away, but you discover the presence of putrescent creatures aboard your vessel.

      • 3–4 Bloat Mite or Hungerer boarders
      1. Continue
    • oTxtDeathRiftExperiment_investigateD

      As you approach the cultist vessel disintegrates in a series of putrescent explosions. A subspace shockwave passes through your vessel, and you discover your crew is afflicted with an inability to recover from wounds.

      • −1 hull HP
      • Debuff applied to all crew
      1. Continue
    • oTxtDeathRiftExperiment_investigateA

      The cultists have stabilized a subspace rift aboard their vessel. They intend to conduct experiments upon it, and your arrival is seen as a convenient opportunity to obtain test subjects. "Endfather be praised, would you make an offering?" "The Endfather blesses those who give."

      1. Provide a test subject (sacrifice random crew)
        oTxtDeathRiftExperiment_sacrificeA

        The cultists select one of your crew. Later, the cultists announce the successful completion of their experiments. They present you the result, a shambling horror.

        • Greater Corpulent or Ghoul Magus
        • Lose a random non-demonic crew member
        1. Continue
      2. Refuse
        Random outcome (1/4 each):
        • oTxtDeathRiftExperiment_refuseB

          The cultists are disappointed and cease further communications. You move on.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtDeathRiftExperiment_refuseD

          The cultists are incensed and turn to attack. Your ship is suddenly awash with foul energies, as horrid creatures appear across your decks.

          • 2–3 Bloat Mite or Hungerer boarders
          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Plague ship
        • oTxtDeathRiftExperiment_refuseA

          The cultists are disappointed, but continue on with their work. Not long after leaving, your vessel suddenly fills up with a choking, corrosive miasma.

          • 6–8 poison clouds spawned
          1. Continue
        • oTxtDeathRiftExperiment_refuseC

          The cultists accept your refusal and proceed with their experiments. Unfortunately, things go awry. The rift destabilizes and tears their vessel apart.

          1. Continue
            Random outcome (1/3 each):
            • oTxtDeathRiftExperiment_refuseC1

              There is very little remaining of the destroyed vessel, but you manage to salvage an occult weapon.

              • Random Death Cult weapon
              1. Continue
            • oTxtDeathRiftExperiment_refuseC2

              There is very little remaining of the destroyed vessel, but you manage to salvage some occult equipment.

              • Random cult armor
              1. Continue
            • oTxtDeathRiftExperiment_refuseC3

              The remains of the cultist vessel are nearly unrecognizable. All that has survived are some strange reagents.

              • Random Death Cult consumable
              1. Continue
  2. Attack
    oTxtDeathRiftExperiment_attack

    The subspace distortions subside as you move in to attack the death cult vessel.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Plague ship
  3. Leave
    oTxtDeathRiftExperiment_leave

    You leave the cultists to their devices and move on.

    1. Continue
  4. (Death Cult Necromancer) Allow your crew to convene with the cultists
    oTxtDeathRiftExperiment_investigateA_necromancer1

    You send a necromancer to visit the death cult vessel. Together, the cultists perform arcane trials upon the subspace rift aboard their vessel. Your crewman returns with some knowledge gleaned from the experiments.

    • Random Death Cult summon
    1. Continue

DeathRiftExperiment_investigateA

→ oTxtDeathRiftExperiment_investigateA_necromancer1

DeathSlavePlanetRiot

oTxtDeathSlavePlanetRiot_intro

Engulfed in a sprawling venomstorm, you come across a small colony under Gorgothian occupation. In typical fashion, the death cultists have rendered the planet nigh uninhabitable. From its vast oceans to the air itself, the planet seems poisoned -- sickly.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeathSlavePlanetRiot_intro2

    A small fleet of Gorgothian ships linger in low orbit, one of which peels off to approach yours. You detect no signs of weapons charging, but do receive a comms request.

    1. Open comms
      oTxtDeathSlavePlanetRiot_comms

      A group of pallid, cloaked figures fill your comm screen. Interspersed with praise for their fell god Gorgoth, they recount the conquest of the planet below, and their efforts to enslave its denizens. A recent spate of riots have inhibited their slaving efforts, and they now request aid in pacifying the planet's populace.

      1. Assist the cultists
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtDeathSlavePlanetRiot_assistB

          By the time you reach the colony, the riots have begun to taper off. With the help of your crew the death cultists are able to largely quell the resistance. With their attention now turned to enslaving the populace, you pilfer supplies from the colony.

          • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
          • Random support consumable
          1. Continue
        • oTxtDeathSlavePlanetRiot_assistA

          Assisting planetside, your crew works with local Gorgothian forces in fighting the rioters. The rioters prove more tenacious than initially thought however, and your crew is quickly overwhelmed. Displeased with this outcome, the death cultists ignore further communications. You opt to leave.

          • All crew damaged (20%)
          1. Continue
      2. (Death Cultist) Concoct a solution
        oTxtDeathSlavePlanetRiot_concoct1

        You consult with your own death cultist on the best method to suppress the riots. Rather than pursue direct action against the rioters, they suggest an obscure enervation ritual to demoralize them. With assistance from their Gorgothian cohorts, a nauseating miasma soon envelops the colony, leaving the rioters sapped of their will to fight.

        1. Continue
          oTxtDeathSlavePlanetRiot_concoct2

          Many of the colonists are left hideously mutated or irreversibly changed by this miasma, a matter immaterial to their new masters. The death cultists thank you for ending the riots, and provide one of their freshly taken slaves as thanks.

          • Aberrant
          1. Continue
      3. (Imperial Enforcer) Enact riot control measures
        oTxtDeathSlavePlanetRiot_riot1

        You offer your Imperial enforcer and his relevant expertise to the death cultists. Before long, the Gorgothians are trained in all manner of crowd control, riot suppression, and demoralization techniques. The riots are quickly stamped out, and are now very unlikely to return. The cultists set about totally enslaving their newly pacified populace.

        1. Continue
          oTxtDeathSlavePlanetRiot_riot2

          Impressed, a ranking member of the Gorgothian fleet requests to join your ship, and brings with him a modicum of supplies. "Together, we shall bring yet more into Gorgoth's caustic embrace."

          • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
          • Noxious Blast ×2
          • Gain crew: Hek'Gurox
          1. Continue
      4. Fight
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtDeathSlavePlanetRiot_fightA

          The approaching ship is either unusually trusting or totally oblivious. By the time it's in firing range your weapons are fully charged.

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Plague ship
        • oTxtDeathSlavePlanetRiot_fightB

          Shortly after activating your weapons, the Gorgothian ship accelerates towards you. The gap between you rapidly closes, and you detect weapon signatures. You prepare to fight.

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Plague ship
      5. Leave
        oTxtDeathSlavePlanetRiot_leave2

        Neither the fate of this colony nor that of the Gorgothians is for you to decide. You close comms and move on.

        1. Continue
    2. Leave
      oTxtDeathSlavePlanetRiot_leave1

      You distance yourself from the dying planet, and the approaching ship reverses course. You move on.

      1. Continue

DeathSurpriseAttack

oTxtDeathSurpriseAttack_intro

You briefly detect a Gorgothian vessel on scan without it detecting you in return. Ionic interference from the nearby neutron star has masked your presence. This may be a good opportunity to gain the upper hand.

  1. Pursue
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtDeathSurpriseAttack_AttackA

      You close in on the death cult vessel. Your weapons are already charged by the time you arrive.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Plague ship
    • oTxtDeathSurpriseAttack_AttackB

      You attempt pursuit. Unfortunately, you fail to regain sensor contact with the cultist vessel, and they manage to escape.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtDeathSurpriseAttack_AttackC

      You close in on the death cult vessel. Unfortunately, your approach is detected, and the cultists quickly turn to engage.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Plague ship
  2. Ignore
    oTxtDeathSurpriseAttack_leave

    You decide not to risk it and slip away.

    1. Continue
  3. (Improved Sensors) Manually tune your sensors to track the enemy
    oTxtDeathSurpriseAttack_AttackBlue

    You are able to make full use of your improved sensor suite to locate the death cultist vessel undetected. The cultists are caught by complete surprise.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Plague ship

DeathTentacularMass

oTxtDeathTentacularMass_intro

You encounter a derelict ship of death cult parentage. It does not respond to hails and life signs are nil. You initiate a salvage operation when your sensors suddenly spike. Colossal tentacles, grey and mottled, erupt from the ship and wrap themselves around it. Efforts to approach are now met with impossibly fast swipes from what creature lays within.

  1. Bombard the tentacles
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtDeathTentacularMass_bombardA

      You attempt to subdue, if not dislodge this creature from its home. Each shot from your cannons is swatted away however, and the tentacular mass covering the ship is unshaken. With no other option, you move on.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtDeathTentacularMass_bombardB

      You train your cannons on the creature. Salvo after salvo reduces its great tentacles to putrescent gore. Collateral damage was unavoidable however; the ship is now little more than molten debris. You salvage what you can.

      • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
  2. (Terror Amplifier) Pacify the creature
    oTxtDeathTentacularMass_pacify

    Initial volleys from your Terror Amplifier simply enrage the beast. An errant shot at the heart of the ship however leaves its great tentacles limp. The beast is stunned, though your sensors indicate it's far from dead. You quickly loot the ship.

    • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
    • Random Death Cult weapon
    1. Continue
  3. (Death Cult Psyker) Soothe the beast
    oTxtDeathTentacularMass_soothe

    A death cultist aboard your ship recognizes the beast as his kin's foul work. He communes with the creature, and shortly after it retracts its tentacles. Assured by your cultist the creature is indeed calmed, you mount a full salvage of the ship. Its stores were replete with material and reagents to manifest even more disgusting entities.

    • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
    • Random Death Cult summon
    • Hungering Reagents ×2
    1. Continue
  4. Leave
    oTxtDeathTentacularMass_leave

    This creature does not wish to be disturbed. You leave it be.

    1. Continue

DeathTransportPair

oTxtDeathTransportPair_intro

You detect a large fleet of death cult vessels preparing to engage their subspace drives. In the midst of this activity, you notice several stragglers still aligning their jump trajectories. One is transporting tomes of occult knowledge, while another is loaded with a supply cache of infernal reagents. You consider launching an attack on one of the vessels.

  1. Attack the one transporting occult knowledge
    oTxtDeathTransportPair_secureOccult1

    You ready your weapons systems.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Plague ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtDeathTransportPair_secureOccult2

    You recover an intact psychogram from the remains.

    • Random cult psychic item
    1. Continue
  2. Attack the one transporting reagents
    oTxtDeathTransportPair_secureReagents1

    You maneuver into attack position..

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Plague ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtDeathTransportPair_secureReagents2

    You recover the reagents.

    • Random cult consumable
    1. Continue
  3. Avoid them
    oTxtDeathTransportPair_ignore

    You avoid the convoy.

    1. Continue

DeathTransportPairCrew

oTxtDeathTransportPairCrew_intro

You arrive near a large convoy and detect several transports lagging behind. They bear the heraldry of the death cults. One is carrying a prisoner in a reinforced stasis vault, while another is transporting starship ordnance data.

  1. Attack the vessel carrying ordnance data
    oTxtDeathTransportPairCrew_secureSupplies1

    You move into attack.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Plague ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtDeathTransportPairCrew_secureSupplies2

    You recover the ordnance blueprint.

    • Random missile blueprint
    1. Continue
  2. Attack the vessel carrying the prisoner
    oTxtDeathTransportPairCrew_securePrisoner1

    You move into attack.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Plague ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtDeathTransportPairCrew_securePrisoner2

    You rescue the prisoner and recruit them to your crew.

    • Random crew member
    1. Continue
  3. Ignore them
    oTxtDeathTransportPairCrew_ignore

    You ignore them.

    1. Continue

DeathVaultQuest

oTxtDeathVaultQuest_intro

You arrive at the location given to you by the cultists you rescued from the Necropolis. Hidden within a thick miasma of noxious gases you see a large temple built from bleached bone, somehow unaffected by the corrosive environment. It appears badly damaged, riddled with laser burns and impact craters. Two great doors lay at the end of a staircase, held shut by gargantuan skeletal claws.

  1. Enter the temple
    oTxtDeathVaultQuest_vaultA

    The lid of the sarcophagus yields and an alarm is tripped. Two ships appear on your sensors, but you manage to retrieve some valuables before the attack arrives.

    • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
    • Grave Beam or Putrefactor I or Putrefactor II
    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Empire ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtDeathVaultQuest_vaultA_victory

    The first Imperial vessel has been destroyed, but there is no time to salvage the wreckage. The second enemy ship is bearing down upon you.

    • Crew recalled to ship
    1. Continue
      oTxtDeathVaultQuest_vaultA_victory1

      "Halt! By the throne's sacred charge, none shall loose the evil contained herein." The second ship begins its attack.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the fight:
      oTxtDeathVaultQuest_vaultA_victory2

      Both Imperial vessels have been destroyed.

      1. Continue
        oTxtDeathVaultQuest_vaultA_victory3

        You return to the temple and salvage what remains. Suddenly, your ship's intruder alarms begin wailing.

        • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
        1. Continue
          oTxtDeathVaultQuest_vaultA_victory4

          Your internal sensors have detected movement amongst the cargo recovered from the Gorgothian temple. A great corpulent creature, pleased at its emancipation, now haunts your vessel.

          • Necrolurch
          1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtDeathVaultQuest_vaultC

    A barrage from your ship's weapons demolishes the profane structure. Your crew manage to scavenge some scrap. As you destroy the base, a Gorgothian vessel emerges from the void and attacks.

    • Random moderate reward
    • Random Death Cult equipment
    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Plague ship
oTxtDeathVaultQuest_investigate Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Your scouts blast through the entrance and investigate the temple thoroughly. Scores of slain Gorgothan cultists litter the rooms of this cathedral of bone.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeathVaultQuest_investigate1

    In the largest room there is a large sarcophagus of some kind. It appears to be rigged, either technologically or with psychic powers. You know that opening it will alert the Imperials who attacked this place earlier. You sense hidden danger but scans show powerful energy readings within the sarcophagus.

    1. Open the sarcophagus
      → oTxtDeathVaultQuest_vaultA
    2. Destroy the shrine and scavenge the remains
      → oTxtDeathVaultQuest_vaultC
oTxtDeathVaultQuest_leave Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You leave the system.

  1. Continue

DeathVaultQuest_vaultA

→ oTxtDeathVaultQuest_vaultA_victory

DeliverBloodRelic

oTxtDeliverBloodRelic_intro

As you enter the system, you come across a flotilla of ships docked to a lavish palace station. Approaching, you notice how the architecture resembles Imperial construction, but realise that all the symbols associated with the Radiant Dogma and the God-King Hyperion have been crudely removed. On closer inspection you notice a net of tiny crimson veins covering the station and extending to each ship of the flotilla, like umbilical cords.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeliverBloodRelic_intro2

    You are contacted by a thin, frail dignitary, half of their face completely flayed. "In the name of the Blood God. Ariok demands service. A neverborn embryo, a living relic imbued with Ariok's power, has been stolen by pirates. Find the Ariokine Heartseed, and plant it on a world not far from here. You will be rewarded."

    1. Accept the mission
      oTxtDeliverBloodRelic_accept

      You accept the mission. The flayed man sends coordinates to a nearby system.

      • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDeliverBloodRelicQuest_intro
      1. Continue
    2. Decline
      oTxtDeliverBloodRelic_decline

      You decline and move on. Surprisingly, the cultists do not pursue you.

      1. Continue

DeliverBloodRelicQuest

oTxtDeliverBloodRelicQuest_intro

Entering the coordinates given by the flayed man, you immediately spot the raider ships you are after. Something is very wrong with them: they are covered in a similar netting of pulsating, crimson veins. Both ships still seem operable. Wary, you move closer.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeliverBloodRelicQuest_battle

    The ships notice you and attack.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Raider Small ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtDeliverBloodRelicQuest_battle_win

    The first corrupted ship falls.

    • Crew recalled to ship
    1. Continue
      oTxtDeliverBloodRelicQuest_battle_win2

      Your vessel quakes as the other ship unleashes a barrage of weapon-fire against you. You quickly turn to face the remaining ship and attack.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider Large ship
      After the fight:
