      oTxtDeliverBloodRelicQuest_battle_win3

      The second ship falls silent. It is obvious the blood god's Heartseed holds a great corruptive power. You consider your options: you can recover and deliver the relic as instructed, or leave it to the void and scavenge the ships before you.

      1. Take the Ariokine Heartseed with you
        oTxtDeliverBloodRelicQuest_continue

        You trawl the ruined ships searching for the relic, and come across a small leaden box. Within you find it: a small tear-drop gem of deep crimson hues. You see movement, like that of the sea within. The longer you look, the clearer the eddies and waves in this infinite sea of blood become. Your head hurts. You realise that each wave consists of screaming, weeping souls, forever imprisoned, in ecstatic agony, incapable of dying, forever. Forever...

        1. Continue
          oTxtDeliverBloodRelicQuest_continue2

          The lead box snaps shut around the gem. The floor beneath you is stained with blood. Your blood. You suddenly feel so very hungry. You enter the coordinates given to you by the flayed man and move on.

          • Commander damaged (50%)
          • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDeliverBloodRelicQuestFinale_intro
          1. Continue
      2. Abandon the quest
        oTxtDeliverBloodRelicQuest_abandon

        The fate of the raider ships you fought against clear in your mind, you decide to abandon this quest. Instead, you simply scavenge the two ships and depart.

        • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
        • Moderate amount of missile ammo (5–8)
        • Kinetic Lance
        1. Continue

DeliverBloodRelicQuestFinale

oTxtDeliverBloodRelicQuestFinale_intro

In the system you enter, three planets orbit a blue sun. You notice that on the most distant planet, there appears to be an abandoned colony. You are certain that this is the target planet for the Heartseed. As you approach, your ship's alarms come to life. Your ship has been boarded, but there are no enemy ships. The gem has come alive, and the creatures it has summoned must be culled.

  • 2–3 Thrall boarders
  • 2 Fleshling boarders
  • 1 Stygian Worm boarder
  • 1–2 Tendril boarders
  1. Continue
After the boarders are defeated:
oTxtDeliverBloodRelicQuestFinale_deliver

The eldritch gem pulsates with crimson power, its corruption momentarily culled. This relic must be delivered to the planet as soon as possible. A spare shuttle will do, targeted to land in the middle of the abandoned colony. You gaze as the shuttle leaves, and wait in orbit.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeliverBloodRelicQuestFinale_deliver2

    It takes hours until you are alerted by your crew. You see a needle-thin tower of sanguineous metal rising from the surface. Like a serpent it coils around itself in a corkscrew, like the bloodstained horn of the mythical unicorn. It grows taller and taller, soon piercing the atmosphere.

    1. Continue
      oTxtDeliverBloodRelicQuestFinale_deliver3

      Thin vessels of what you now realise contain blood form a vast bulb at the top, and in a few moments it blooms. A great flesh-flower grown from tormented souls. You recognise this structure. A Haematite pleasure spire.

      1. Continue
        oTxtDeliverBloodRelicQuestFinale_deliver4

        A small thorn-shaped shuttle shoots out from the vast blooming wretched thing. It approaches your ship, but it is clear that it is not attacking you. Instead, it stops near the ship, and your crew recover its contents. Ariok has indeed blessed you, rewarding you for spreading his influence.

        • Summon Stygian Worm
        • Transfusion
        • Thirsting Glaive
        • Chaos Armor
        1. Continue

DeliverBloodRelicQuest_battle

→ oTxtDeliverBloodRelicQuest_battle_win

DeliverDeathRelic

oTxtDeliverDeathRelic_intro

You enter a system and find the remains of a very recent battle. The destroyed ships and their fragments bear Imperial signals, and you recognise that other wrecks bear the symbols of Gorgoth, the Endfather. You explore the battlefield and find a Gorgothian ship that seems to be mostly intact.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeliverDeathRelic_intro2

    Within, there is a mortally wounded apostle. Noticing you, she smiles just as she slips into the fetid embrace of her foul god. There is a letter and a satchel -- this cultist had an important mission. Others sharing her faith wanted her to retrieve and deliver a powerful relic-flora known as the "Corpseflower".

    1. Continue
      oTxtDeliverDeathRelic_intro3

      The letter contains the apostle's orders, the satchel contains a book detailing a location where Corpseflowers can be harvested. The coordinates lead to a nearby abandoned tomb-complex.

      1. Continue
        oTxtDeliverDeathRelic_intro4

        The letter also instructs the bearer of the Corpseflowers to deliver them to a hidden Gorgothian enclave, where the plants will be used for an important ritual. Surely there are rewards at the end of that road.

        1. Accept the mission
          oTxtDeliverDeathRelic_accept

          You take up the mantle of this dead cultist. The book contains coordinates to a nearby system.

          • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDeliverDeathRelicQuest_intro
          1. Continue
        2. Ignore
          oTxtDeliverDeathRelic_decline

          This apostle's mission died with them. You move on.

          1. Continue

DeliverDeathRelicQuest

oTxtDeliverDeathRelicQuest_intro

The system you enter is quiet. A dim star orbited by a handful of rocky planetoids. On the surface of the very first planetoid you find the remains of buildings -- likely the tomb-complex. Perhaps this was once an Imperial colony, but it has lain abandoned and battered by venomstorms for decades, if not longer.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeliverDeathRelicQuest_intro2

    You explore the tomb-complex with the dead Gorgothian book at hand, and in the depths of a communal gravesite you find what you seek: Bone-white flowers growing from and rooted within skeletal, mostly decomposed frames. You have an inkling on the source of the Corpseflower's name.

    1. Gather Corpseflowers
      oTxtDeliverDeathRelicQuest_gather

      As you grab a handful of flowers you suddenly feel faint, and waves of fatigue radiate across your body. Veins turn black on your right hand, snaking up your arm. Quickly, you stash the flowers into the container you found near the dead Gorgothian apostle. The pain fades, but you feel much weaker. You find the coordinates to the final system from the later pages of the tome.

      • Commander damaged (50%)
      • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDeliverDeathRelicQuestFinale_intro
      1. Continue
    2. (Death Cultist) Ward yourself against the Corpseflowers' corruption
      oTxtDeliverDeathRelicQuest_bt_gather

      The death cult crewmember stops you for a moment, and performs an elaborate ritual involving the pile of corpses and the Corpseflowers. After the ritual finishes, you gather a handful of these relic-plants with no adverse effects. You stash them into the small container you found earlier. The tome and supernatural intuition guides you to the next location

      • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDeliverDeathRelicQuestFinale_intro
      1. Continue
    3. (Imperial Psyker) Incinerate the Corpseflowers
      oTxtDeliverDeathRelicQuest_incinerate

      Your Imperial psyker protests the harvesting of these Corpseflowers. They view them as nothing more than malodorous abominations, and implores you to incinerate rather than gather them. Their description of this strange flora is certainly apt, and you otherwise have a sense of foreboding about them. You permit your psyker to burn the flowers.

      1. Continue
        oTxtDeliverDeathRelicQuest_incinerate2

        The Corpseflowers prove most flammable, and the tomb-complex soon becomes an uncontrollable conflagration. Before long the complex is reduced to ash, much to your Imperial psyker's satisfaction. Having witnessed such a momentous inferno, your Imperial psyker is left inspired. They enscribe visions of flame and burning ritual upon psychomantic wafers.

        • Fireball
        • Fireball
        1. Continue
    4. Avoid the Corpseflowers
      oTxtDeliverDeathRelicQuest_avoid

      These Corpseflowers carry an appalling odor, and their very name carries a sense of foreboding. Though this apostle may have been equipped to handle them, you certainly are not. You abandon your quest.

      1. Continue

DeliverDeathRelicQuestFinale

oTxtDeliverDeathRelicQuestFinale_intro

You enter the system and are immediately thrust into the midst of a hectic battle. A group of raiders are attacking the Gorgothian outpost.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeliverDeathRelicQuestFinale_attack

    Wasting no time, you charge the nearest raider vessel.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Raider Small ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtDeliverDeathRelicQuestFinale_attack_win

    You annihilate the first raider vessel.

    • Crew recalled to ship
    1. Continue
      oTxtDeliverDeathRelicQuestFinale_attack_win2

      There is a brief pause, after which you are attacked by another ship. Alarms on your ship go off, your ship has been boarded.

      • 1–2 Gene Soldier boarders
      • 1 Aberrant boarder
      • 1–2 Corsair boarders
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider Large ship
      After the fight:
      oTxtDeliverDeathRelicQuestFinale_victory

      The last enemy ship falls, and all is quiet again. Damaged Gorgothian warships slink back to a nearby station, and you follow. You are contacted by a high-ranking Apostle of Gorgoth, and you explain your task, showing them the tome and the satchel.

      • Crew recalled to ship
      1. Continue
        oTxtDeliverDeathRelicQuestFinale_victory2

        "I see you found Tachonis. May she dream and slumber in our Father's cold embrace forever. You have delivered what she could not. Most auspicious. These flowers will raise countless armies in the name of the Endfather. Rewards and gifts for your deeds are already on their way. You may go."

        1. Continue
          oTxtDeliverDeathRelicQuestFinale_victory3

          A shuttle filled with rewards arrives on your ship. You find a cache of supplies buried under a mound of decaying corpses. One of the corpses proves less dead than initially thought.

          • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
          • Bubonic Scythe
          • Putrescent Armor
          • Grave Beam
          • Greater Corpulent
          1. Continue

DeliverDeathRelicQuestFinale_attack

→ oTxtDeliverDeathRelicQuestFinale_attack_win

DeliverDeathRelicQuest_bt

→ oTxtDeliverDeathRelicQuest_bt_gather

DeliverImperialRelic

oTxtDeliverImperialRelic_intro

As you enter the system, you detect a small habitat on the surface of the second planet from the system's star. You approach, and are contacted by a minister of the Radiant Dogma. He introduces himself as Deacon Jarris.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeliverImperialRelic_intro2

    The habitat belongs to a subsect of the Dogma, the Mendicant Order of the Smouldering Ember. The order focuses on archeological work -- uncovering ancient relics from the Imperium's past.

    1. Continue
      oTxtDeliverImperialRelic_intro3

      Deacon Jarris explains they recently unearthed a sacred relic hailing from the first centuries of Hyperion's rule. Soon after, the relic was stolen by raider pirates prowling in a nearby system. He wants to hire you to retrieve the relic and transport it to a nearby Imperial outpost for cataloguing and internment into an appropriate reliquary.

      1. Continue
        oTxtDeliverImperialRelic_intro4

        Excitedly, Jarris mentions that while the artifact is just a mundane-looking needle, it is a historical relic and an incredibly potent talisman. You will be well rewarded at the outpost upon delivering the relic.

        1. Accept the mission
          oTxtDeliverImperialRelic_accept

          You accept the mission, and Deacon Jarris provides coordinates to a nearby system. "May His radiant light guide you."

          • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDeliverImperialRelicQuest_intro
          1. Continue
        2. Decline
          oTxtDeliverImperialRelic_accept2

          You decline Deacon Jarris' request. Though disappointed, he recites a ward bidding you good passage before closing comms. You move on.

          1. Continue

DeliverImperialRelicQuest

oTxtDeliverImperialRelicQuest_intro

You enter the system with screaming klaxons and panicking crew. Your ship has been attacked! Your ship manages to evade a barrage of enemy projectiles. A raider ship is pursuing you, no doubt the same pirates who stole the relic from the Imperial archeologists. The enemy sends boarders to your ship.

  • 1–2 Corsair boarders
  • 1 Scavenger boarder
  • 1 Infiltrator boarder
  1. Continue
Fight! vs Raider Large ship
After the fight:
oTxtDeliverImperialRelicQuest_intro2

The enemy ship lies broken and shattered, and you begin sifting through its remains for the relic. Among material and weaponry once belonging to the raiders, you locate a blast-proof safe. With the help of your crew its lock submits, and within you find the relic: a small needle constructed from intricate brass rings. How can such a small thing hold so much value? The Imperial outpost awaits.

  • Random small reward
  • Machine Rifle
  • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDeliverImperialRelicQuestFinale_intro
  1. Continue

DeliverImperialRelicQuestFinale

oTxtDeliverImperialRelicQuestFinale_intro

You enter the final system of your mission, and discover a large solar monastery orbiting the system's sun. You inform the local guard of your task and precious cargo, and dock at the station. A small group of imperial priests soon meet with you, and you present to them the needle. Terribly curious, a priest nearly snatches it from you and begins appraising it. You follow them as they take it to a room filled with altars and glass-walled containers.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeliverImperialRelicQuestFinale_intro2

    You notice that the needle is placed on a small altar, completing a set of other items: a vial of incandescent-looking ink, as well as a small hammer with a bone handle. You return to your ship. Imperial serfs load your cargo bay with rewards. "You have done our order an uncommon service. We thank you."

    • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
    • Purify
    • Exalted Reagents ×2
    1. Continue

DeliverRaiderRelic

oTxtDeliverRaiderRelic_intro

As you enter the system, you are immediately contacted by a badly damaged raider clan vessel. The scarred captain of the ship explains that they are trying to retrieve an individual kidnapped by Kromic slavers. The captain tried to rescue them, but was overpowered by the war cultists and barely survived.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeliverRaiderRelic_intro2

    The captain reveals that the individual in question is related to one of the Four Great Clans. He wants you to track the Kromic slave-ship, retrieve the target, and deliver them to a nearby raider enclave. "Return the girl, and my people will reward you well."

    1. Accept the mission
      oTxtDeliverRaiderRelic_accept

      You accept the mission. The captain sends coordinates to a nearby system. "Get her back, and give 'em hell."

      • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDeliverRaiderRelicQuest_intro
      1. Continue
    2. Decline
      oTxtDeliverRaiderRelic_decline

      This rescue operation will not be lead by you. You close comms as the captain curses you out.

      1. Continue

DeliverRaiderRelicQuest

oTxtDeliverRaiderRelicQuest_intro

Your ship exits subspace into the midst of a raging ionic storm. Despite the interference this storm causes for your ship's systems, you spot a damaged Kromic warship. Accompanying it is a slave hauler, undoubtedly the prison of your target.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeliverRaiderRelicQuest_attack

    You set a course towards the enemy, and charge.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs War ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtDeliverRaiderRelicQuest_success

    With their escort dealt with you move towards the slave hauler. The war cult's characteristic cruelty towards their captives is on full display here: everyone is malnourished, branded, and stripped naked. Navigating the tragically cramped hauler, you at last find your target. You notice ritualistic scarification across her right arms, hands, and fingers. She is changed, but alive. All that remains is returning the girl to a nearby raider enclave.

    1. Continue
      oTxtDeliverRaiderRelicQuest_success2

      With your task at hand you are unable to escort or rescue the many former slaves aboard the hauler. You do however provide them basic supplies, along with the location of a nearby hab-colony. Either out of thanks to you or to avoid the potentially risky journey, a former slaves asks to serve aboard your ship. The rest quickly work to leave the carnage and bad memories behind them.

      • Random crew member
      • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDeliverRaiderRelicQuestFinale_intro
      1. Continue
oTxtDeliverRaiderRelicQuest_failure Unreachable — not referenced by any event

The enemy ship explodes and takes the prisoner with it. Your task has failed, and there is no reason to continue to the nearby raider enclave. You trawl the ship's remains before departing.

  • Very small amount of ⬗ Scrap
  1. Continue

DeliverRaiderRelicQuestFinale

oTxtDeliverRaiderRelicQuestFinale_intro

You enter the system and find a raider enclave nestled in the shadow of a vast gas giant. You contact them about your task and the prisoner you have successfully freed. The enclave quickly transmits docking codes and you begin approach.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeliverRaiderRelicQuestFinale_intro2

    Upon entering the facility, you are met by a small gathering of raiders. Each is dressed in intricate brocades, and fine rings adorn their fingers. "Oh uncle," your freed prisoner cries out as she rushes towards the group. She is quickly taken in and embraced by them all, and the room is soon filled with exuberance and relief.

    1. Continue
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtDeliverRaiderRelicQuestFinale_intro3A

        As this family reunion continues on, you are met with a representative of the raider clans. He gives a nod, and subordinates of his begin hauling crates of materiel on your ship. "May this be appropriate compensation, and recognition of your work."

        • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
        • Moderate amount of missile ammo (5–8)
        • Flayer Cannon
        1. Continue
      • oTxtDeliverRaiderRelicQuestFinale_intro3B

        As this family reunion continues on, you are met with a representative of the raider clans. He gives a nod, and subordinates of his begin hauling crates of materiel on your ship. "May this be appropriate compensation, and recognition of your work."

        • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
        • Moderate amount of missile ammo (5–8)
        • Fusillade I
        1. Continue

DeliverSubspaceRelic

oTxtDeliverSubspaceRelic_intro

The system you arrive at is barren: a dim white dwarf is surrounded by an asteroid belt. There is a small planet resembling a dark blue-gray marble behind the asteroids. Is it curiosity that guides you to the planet? As you approach, you begin to feel a mounting pressure against your body. Your ears pop, and you can't help but stare at the dark planet you are approaching.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeliverSubspaceRelic_intro2

    Then, visions come. An archeological site, Imperial banners. A four-sided obelisk that reflects no light emanating shadow and dark. Another system, with a dead sun. Dead? Extinguished? A small asteroid behind a gas giant. A wound in reality, seeping ink on the frigid surface of the rock. Tendrils and insectile limbs emerging, tasting reality, only to return whence they came from. A whisper. "Return... what was stolen..."

    1. Accept your mission
      oTxtDeliverSubspaceRelic_accept

      You accept the task. The visions give clarity. You know where the archeological site is. You feel the cold embrace of a primordial shadow-sea.

      • Debuff applied to commander
      • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDeliverSubspaceRelicQuest_intro
      1. Continue
    2. Ignore it
      oTxtDeliverSubspaceRelic_ignore

      You ignore the whispers. A hallucination. Nothing more. Half shaken, you move on.

      1. Continue

DeliverSubspaceRelicQuest

oTxtDeliverSubspaceRelicQuest_intro

A wave of pressure hits you: it is the system from your vision. Even with closed eyes you could point out the planet where the Imperial dig site lays. A small, insignificant ocean world orbited by a habitat of Imperial design. A single warship guards this habitat, this distant expedition. The pressure you feel sharpens into a needle. You know what must be done.

  1. Attack the ship
    oTxtDeliverSubspaceRelicQuest_attack

    You rush in to attack and catch the ship by surprise.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs specific room ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtDeliverSubspaceRelicQuest_attack2

    The enemy ship is destroyed. You task your crew to salvage the enemy ship as you personally descend on the planet. The dig site is quickly located and is seemingly abandoned. At its center is the stygian thing you seek.

    • Random small reward
    • Blessed Hammer
    1. Continue
      oTxtDeliverSubspaceRelicQuest_attack3

      A small obelisk, four sides, reflecting no light whatsoever. The contrast is so sharp it looks like a small hole in reality itself. It reminds you of the vision. The place where this relic of some ancient power belongs to.

      • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDeliverSubspaceRelicQuestFinale_intro
      1. Continue

DeliverSubspaceRelicQuestFinale

oTxtDeliverSubspaceRelicQuestFinale_intro

The moment you enter the system you are attacked by two Imperial warships. The archeological site you plundered must have called for aid.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Empire Spell ship
After the fight:
oTxtDeliverSubspaceRelicQuestFinale_attack

You manage to deal with the first Imperial ship.

  • Crew recalled to ship
  1. Continue
    oTxtDeliverSubspaceRelicQuestFinale_attack2

    As you turn to attack the second vessel, your ship's boarding alarms begin to scream. You have been boarded.

    • 1 Centurion boarder
    • 1 Imperial Enforcer boarder
    • 1–2 Imperial Recruit boarders
    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Empire Large ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtDeliverSubspaceRelicQuestFinale_attack_win

    The second ship is destroyed. The chaotic battle ends in silence, and you have a chance to explore the system. The system's sun is as dead as you remember, and behind a monochrome gas giant lies that asteroid from your visions. The wound in reality which eats light.

    1. Continue
      oTxtDeliverSubspaceRelicQuestFinale_attack_win2

      The obelisk in your possession thrums with eldritch power. You feed it to the wound, the primordial dark, the echo of the depths of the cosmic ur-sea. As the relic is pushed into the portal, it closes like a rapidly healing wound.

      1. Continue
        oTxtDeliverSubspaceRelicQuestFinale_attack_win3

        You breathe, as does reality. The gas giant is no longer monochrome but a beautiful auburn swirl, and its sun reignites in a resplendant gold. Upon the asteroid, now freed from its wound and well illuminated, you find a large metallic container. Within this chest, arcane treasures.

        • Athame
        • Aetheric Gate
        • Summon Stygian Worm
        1. Continue

DeliverSubspaceRelicQuestFinale_attack

→ oTxtDeliverSubspaceRelicQuestFinale_attack_win

DeliverTechnocultRelic

oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelic_intro

You enter a restless-looking binary system consisting of a single, enormous planet the colour of soured mustard. Therein you spot a rarity: a planetary technocult research complex.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelic_intro2

    As you approach you are contacted by the station's administrators. They want to hire you to deliver a relic to a research nexus, but there is a catch. A nearby system is exhibiting unusual subspatial energy readings, and the administrators want you to expose the relic to a subspatial rift. They warn against tampering with the relic, but tersely offer a reward.

    1. Accept
      oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelic_accept

      You accept the quest, and before long receive a small transport shuttle from the administrators. Aboard you find a single husk holding the relic in question, an outwardly mundane metal container. Your new machine slave transmits a pre-recorded message from its former masters: you recieve coordinates along with a firm reminder to not open the box.

      • Husk
      • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelicQuest_intro
      1. Continue
    2. Decline
      oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelic_decline

      This research project will be left to the Technocult.

      1. Continue

DeliverTechnocultRelicQuest

oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelicQuest_intro

Arriving into the system, your eyes are immediately drawn to a geometrical group of tiny, scarlike fractures in the fabric of reality. You examine the container and notice that small, pale blue circuit-like grooves begin to glow. As you approach the subspatial nexus, the glow intensifies. You look ahead, and sense a dizzying pulse, an unnaturally fast wave of migraine.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelicQuest_intro2

    You maneuver the ship to the centerpoint of the geometric dimensional scar-pattern. The metal box begins to whir and emit quick bursts of steam from its sides. A part of you wants to know what is happening, yet another senses something unnatural.

    1. Indulge your curiosity and open the box
      oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelicQuest_indulge

      You take a small knife and carefully wedge the box open. The insides reveal a small fabrication device, and at the centre is a flower wrought from liquid metals. Repeatedly, the flower withers, regrows, and blooms.

      1. Continue
        oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelicQuest_indulge2

        Suddenly, you see a shape crawl out of one of the subspatial scars. A small, but dangerous looking ship appears, immediately targeting you. The tampered relic responds to the energy fluctuations, releasing a pulse of ionic energy.

        • Ionize random system
        1. Continue
        2. Continue
        Fight! vs Possessed ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelicQuest_indulge2_win

        The strange vessel begins to crystallize and fragment under your ship's weapons. The fragments soon pulverize into glinting cosmic dust. The relic you carry quiets down. You glance at it, and notice a small screen displaying the following text: "Cultivated synthetic flora sample 11-F-S914. Grown from a constructed seed, nourished with sacred code using a fabricator coffin. Proof of divinity, mechanical life. The sacred Vita Synthetica initiative may continue undisturbed."

        1. Continue
          oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelicQuest_indulge2_win2

          Your work too continues. You enter the coordinates to a nearby technocult research station.

          • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelicQuestFinaleTampered_intro
          1. Continue
    2. Keep the box shut
      oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelicQuest_boxShut

      You know well the dangers of unrestrained knowledge, and keep the relic box closed.

      1. Continue
        oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelicQuest_boxShut2

        The ship's proximity alarms suddenly scream. A dangerous-looking ship appears from empty space, a living shadow, dimming surrounding stars. The ship begins its attack.

        1. Continue
        2. Continue
        Fight! vs Possessed ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelicQuest_boxShut3

        The enemy vessel unravels with a bright flash of profane unlight. Your optical nerves jitter in your skull. You glance towards the relic and see that it is inert once again. You punch in the coordinates for delivery of the relic and exit the system.

        • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelicQuestFinaleUntouched_intro
        1. Continue

DeliverTechnocultRelicQuestFinaleTampered

oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelicQuestFinaleTampered_intro

Your ship emerges into reality near a pair of dwarf stars. Nearby you spot a large, well-defended technocult outpost. You approach and contact the authorities about the delivery mission.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelicQuestFinaleTampered_tampered

    You return the relic, and are soon contacted by a machine priest covered in cybernetics. It speaks in a heavily modulated voice: "Fleshbeing. Integrity of 11-F-S914 compromised. Interference detected. Docking pay."

    • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
    • Disruptor
    1. Continue

DeliverTechnocultRelicQuestFinaleUntouched

oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelicQuestFinaleUntouched_intro

Your ship emerges into reality near a pair of dwarf stars. Nearby you spot a large, well-defended technocult outpost. You approach and contact the authorities about the delivery mission.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelicQuestFinaleUntouched_untouched

    The relic is returned. A few moments pass, and there is no follow-up. Your ship's proximity alarm goes off, and a small shuttle containing your payment lands in the cargo bay.

    • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
    • Displacement Field Emitter
    • Heat Shielding
    1. Continue

DeliverTechnocultRelicQuest_indulge2

→ oTxtDeliverTechnocultRelicQuest_indulge2_win

DeliverWarRelic

oTxtDeliverWarRelic_intro

You enter the system and scans reveal a small, decrepit station orbiting a small moon. As you approach, you notice that the station is actually a warship bearing the mark of Krom, the Lord of Wrath. The ship appears to be powered, but it remains still despite your presence.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeliverWarRelic_intro2

    You dock at the ship and begin exploring. You discover a large room and realize that it is a repurposed cargo bay. It is covered with manufacturing equipment of all sorts and boasts a surprisingly large amount of books.

    1. Continue
      oTxtDeliverWarRelic_intro3

      You are drawn to a ringing round within the room. At its center you find a muscular old man working a traditional forge. The old man is shirtless, his hair and beard long, white, and strong like barbed wire. His skin is covered in ritual markings, scars, and tattoos. He looks at you and nods. "For the glory of Krom."

      1. Continue
        oTxtDeliverWarRelic_intro4

        The man explains that he is a rarity -- a Kromic weaponsmith-scholar. He creates, seeks, and delivers items of importance for the Cult of Krom. One such item, a Nemean Mantle, was recently discovered in a system ruled by worshippers of Gorgoth. The man requests that you find the item, and deliver it in the hands of a local Kromic warlord. He promises great rewards and the blessings of Krom to you and your ship.

        1. Accept the mission
          oTxtDeliverWarRelic_accept

          You accept the mission. The weaponsmith sends coordinates to a nearby system. Before you depart, he provides you a weapon of his own design. "This one will not fail you."

          • Cult Blade
          • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDeliverWarRelicQuest_intro
          1. Continue
        2. Decline
          oTxtDeliverWarRelic_decline

          You decline and move on. The weaponsmith allows you to leave in peace.

          1. Continue

DeliverWarRelicQuest

oTxtDeliverWarRelicQuest_intro

Fetid, corrosive clouds permeate the system you enter. You notice a small satellite engulfed by the clouds and flanked by two Gorgothian warships. You surmise the object of your search lies within. The corrosive miasma seems to roil around the station.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeliverWarRelicQuest_battle

    Suddenly, the two ships detach from the station and charge your way. You have been spotted.

    • 2–3 poison clouds spawned
    1. Continue
    Fight! vs specific room ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtDeliverWarRelicQuest_battle_win

    The enemy ship falls silent, leaking atmosphere and the remains of its crew.

    • Crew recalled to ship
    1. Continue
      oTxtDeliverWarRelicQuest_battle_win1

      Without wasting any time, the second ship attacks you. Your ship quakes under a barrage of projectiles, and alarms ring out: you have been boarded.

      • 1–2 Gorgothian Legionnaire boarders
      • 1 Death Mage boarder
      • 1 Apostle of Decay boarder
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs specific room ship
      After the fight:
      oTxtDeliverWarRelicQuest_battle_win2

      With its guardian ships defeated, disabled, and otherwise destroyed, you and your crew journey into the station hoping to uncover the artifact. Your search is most fruitful, you recover usable supplies and material once belonging to the Gorgothians, along with the Nemean Mantle.

      • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
      • Bloated Reagents ×3
      1. Continue
        oTxtDeliverWarRelicQuest_battle_win3

        The mantle itself appears to be a massive animal hide. One side is covered in coarse fur, the other, intricate Kromic patterns: symbols, phrases, images. Slowly, a cold wrath begins to blossom behind your temples. To die in glorious battle atop a pile of corpses you slew yourself... You store the object in your cargo bay, and move on.

        • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDeliverWarRelicQuestFinale_intro
        1. Continue

DeliverWarRelicQuestFinale

oTxtDeliverWarRelicQuestFinale_intro

You enter the system and come face to face with a small group of Kromic warships. The largest ship approaches with weapons ready to attack, but ceases their hostile approach after you contact them about the Kromic relic. The warlord demands you to deliver the relic personally, and in the face of vastly superior firepower, you agree.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDeliverWarRelicQuestFinale_intro_2

    The relic is heavy in your hands as you enter the Kromic ship. Dozens of onlookers chant and beat drums as the warlord takes the mantle and throws it around his shoulders. There is a sharp pain in your mind, as you see the mantle grow needle-like appendages which burrow themselves into the hide of the warlord. His body mutates, growing taller, larger, stronger.

    1. Continue
      oTxtDeliverWarRelicQuestFinale_finale

      The chanting stops. The warlord growls with a booming voice: "Mighty lord Krom. Witness me." and exits the room. A group of cultists haul a chest filled with rewards for your service.

      • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
      • Goreblaster
      • Ruinous Reagents ×2
      1. Continue

DeliverWarRelicQuestFinale_intro

→ oTxtDeliverWarRelicQuestFinale_intro_2

DeliverWarRelicQuest_battle

→ oTxtDeliverWarRelicQuest_battle_win

DemNothing

oTxtDemNothing_intro

[...]

  1. Continue

DemonAdriftBlobs

oTxtDemonAdriftBlobs_intro

You encounter a starship silently adrift within a rift storm.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/5 each):
    • oTxtDemonAdriftBlobs_investigateD

      The ship seems to have been deserted for some time. You are able to retrieve a few items from the engineering sections.

      • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
    • oTxtDemonAdriftBlobs_investigateE

      You board the drifting starship and find it deserted. A review of the ship logs reveal the crew to have been abducted by raiders shortly before it was engulfed by a rift storm. You salvage what you can and then move on.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtDemonAdriftBlobs_investigateB

      You detect no life signs aboard the drifting starship, but intermittent trails of slime suggest the activity of some strange creature. You strip the vessel of usable items.

      • Random moderate reward
      1. Continue
        oTxtDemonAdriftBlobs_investigateB2

        Back aboard your vessel, you find yourself confronted by a number of slime-covered abominations.

        • 3 Necrolurch boarders
        1. Continue
    • oTxtDemonAdriftBlobs_investigateC

      The surrounding rift storm suddenly dissipates as an Imperial patrol ship tears out of subspace. It fires a lance shot at the drifting ship, in accordance with the usual Imperial method of scuttling vessels.

      1. Continue
        oTxtDemonAdriftBlobs_investigateC2

        For an unknown reason, you suddenly find your ship in the line of fire. The deck under you bucks momentarily as the lance shot impacts your hull . "Foul heretic, you dare obstruct the will of the Immortal King?"

        • 2–3 breaches spawned
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Empire ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        oTxtDemonAdriftBlobs_investigateC3

        You board the drifting ship. The decks are littered with dead crewmen. One particularly bloated corpse suddenly bursts with a wet pop. A corpulent abomination arises out of the flaccid skin of the crewman, and seems to look at you expectantly.

        • Necrolurch
        1. Continue
    • oTxtDemonAdriftBlobs_investigateA

      You approach the starship. Your hails generate no response, and a cursory scan of the ship produces no life signs. A sickly wave of energy pulses outwards from the drifting vessel as it suddenly lurches and orients its cannons at you under some unseen command.

      1. Continue
        oTxtDemonAdriftBlobs_investigateA2

        At the last moment you notice on your sensor feed a number of large, globular creatures flying out from the enemy vessel towards yours. They impact your hull, their corpulent bodies smearing across the surface in a mass of splintered bones and quivering flesh. You ready your weapons as the broken carcasses begin crawling towards you with malicious intent.

        • 3 Carcass boarders
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Possessed ship
  2. Ignore
    oTxtDemonAdriftBlobs_ignore

    You avoid the starship and move on.

    1. Continue

DemonRaidMaze

oTxtDemonRaidMaze Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You have come across a distress signal emanating from a ruined Imperial colony. Further scans have located intact sections of underground techno-crypts, along with promising entry-points along the failing structure.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Demon Maze ship
oTxtDemonRaidMaze_weather Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You have come across a distress signal emanating from a ruined Imperial colony. Further scans have located intact sections of underground techno-crypts, along with promising entry-points along the failing structure. However, anomalous flux readings signal a significant presence of subspace entities infesting the interior.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Demon Maze ship
oTxtDemonRaidMaze_weatherTier2 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You have come across a distress signal emanating from a ruined Imperial colony. Further scans have located intact sections of underground techno-crypts, along with promising entry-points along the failing structure. However, anomalous flux readings signal a significant presence of subspace entities infesting the interior.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Demon Maze ship

DemonRaidRuin

oTxtDemonRaidRuin Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You triangulate the source of a distress signal to an installation on the surface of a deserted asteroid. With its defensive batteries out of order, what remains of the supplies inside are free for the taking.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Demon Ruin ship
oTxtDemonRaidRuin_weather Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You triangulate the source of a distress signal to an installation on the surface of a deserted asteroid. Its defensive batteries have long been silenced, though some auxiliary infrastructure remain online in serviceable, if not working, order. However, the nearby riftstorm is intensifying, and further scans indicate a significant quantity of subspace entities have taken up residence inside of the ruins.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Demon Ruin ship
oTxtDemonRaidRuin_weatherTier2 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You triangulate the source of a distress signal to an installation on the surface of a deserted asteroid. Its defensive batteries have long been silenced, though some auxiliary infrastructure remain online in serviceable, if not working, order. However, the nearby riftstorm is intensifying, and further scans indicate a significant quantity of subspace entities have taken up residence inside of the ruins.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Demon Ruin ship

DemonRaidRuins

oTxtDemonRaidRuins_vaultWin Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Scans indicate all storage vaults have been destroyed or secured.

  1. Continue
    oTxtEmpireRaidFort_leaveOrStay

    There is nothing left to be done. You may depart now, or remain on-site and leave at your own discretion.

    1. Depart now
      oTxtEmpireRaidFort_moveOn

      You prepare to break orbit and move on.

      • Crew recalled to ship
      1. Continue
    2. Remain on-site
      oTxtEmpireRaidFort_stay

      You remain on-site.

      1. Continue

Derelict

oTxtDerelict Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You approach the derelict craft.

DerelictDemonShip

oTxtDerelictDemonShip_intro

You detect a derelict ship of unknown origin drifting through a field of debris. Parts of the ship seem damaged and strange tendrils cover its hull.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDerelictDemonShip_intro1

    As you approach, you realize that the tendrils are flesh. Some of the larger breaches in the ship hull have been covered with an organic, cartilage-like material. Your sensors return multiple subspace anomalies, confirming that this is a supernaturally-corrupted starship. However, it is strangely inert.

    1. Investigate the demon ship
      oTxtDerelictDemonShip_investigateA

      You investigate the ship. The insides are a grotesque mesh of typical starship construction and the inner workings of some gargantuan beast. You see organs, limbs, maws, eyes and ears. Chorded, pulsating blood vessels lead towards the center of the ship.

      1. Approach the center
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtDerelictDemonShip_investigateA_heart2

          The ship trembles and a psychic shriek comes from its depths. You rush back to your ship and see that the demon ship is turning its weapons on you.

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Possessed ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          oTxtDerelictDemonShip_investigateA_rescue2_win

          The ship is destroyed. You scavenge whatever you can and leave. You discover an artifact containing a psychogram.

          • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
          • Random cult psychic item
          1. Continue
        • oTxtDerelictDemonShip_investigateA_rescue3

          The ship trembles violently, and you sense a malevolent gaze upon your ship. You rush back to the bridge and see the demon ship beginning its attack.

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Possessed ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          oTxtDerelictDemonShip_investigateA_rescue3_win

          The demon ship is annihilated. You scavenge the remains and recover a psychogram.

          • Random moderate reward
          • Random cult psychic item
          1. Continue
        • oTxtDerelictDemonShip_investigateA_heart

          The central room contains a large, writhing organic mass that pulses slowly. The walls of the room are covered with melted and integrated human crew. They lurch towards you, open their eyes, and scream.

          1. Continue
            oTxtDerelictDemonShip_investigateA_heart1

            Reality trembles, the heart pulses faster. Suddenly, you gain a profane understanding. It wants an offering, and in return it offers power.

            1. Sacrifice a random crew member
              oTxtDerelictDemonShip_sacrifice_crew

              A crew member is forced against a wall, where a conglomeration of gaping maws rips it apart. What remains of them does not die. Instead, their disfigured head and torso are assimilated to the flesh-wall.

              • Lose a random non-demonic crew member
              1. Continue
                oTxtDerelictDemonShip_sacrifice_crew1

                The heart keeps its side of the bargain. It transmits an unfamiliar location into your mind and fills you with unholy power.

                • Hungerer
                • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_intro
                1. Continue
            2. Sacrifice vitality
              oTxtDerelictDemonShip_sacrifice_vitality

              You place your hand on the surface of the beating heart, and are hit with an unimaginable pulse of agony. Minutes later you awaken, forever changed.

              • Commander damaged (50%)
              1. Continue
                oTxtDerelictDemonShip_sacrifice_vitality1

                The heart keeps its side of the bargain and you are filled with eldritch power. It also transmits the location of a system. It does not communicate what you will find there.

                • Drain Strength
                • Quest marker added to map: oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_intro
                1. Continue
            3. Escape
              oTxtDerelictDemonShip_sacrifice_escape

              You rush back to your ship. The demon ship does not attack you. Instead, a subspace rift opens and the vessel vanishes.

              1. Continue
    2. Attack the demon ship
      oTxtDerelictDemonShip_attack

      You launch a surprise attack against the demon ship.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Possessed ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtDerelictDemonShip_attack_win

      In the aftermath you recover a cache of unusual supplies.

      • Random small reward
      • Random cult consumable
      1. Continue
    3. Salvage the debris field
      oTxtDerelictDemonShip_salvage_moveon

      You salvage whatever you can from the debris field surrounding the ship. The corrupted ship remains motionless.

      • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue

DerelictDemonShipQuest

oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_intro

You arrive at the coordinates you received from the cursed ship, its psychic imprint still lingering in your mind. This system is rife with subspace anomalies: you see rifts, scars in reality itself, blinking in and out of existence.

  1. Continue
    oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_intro1

    You spot a single planet, partially devoured by a large dimensional tear. There is an orbital shrine to the God-King, and you feel an exceptionally powerful force emanating from it, suppressing the nearby dimensional tear. Suddenly, you realize what the cursed heart wants from you. It wants you to destroy the Imperial shrine keeping the dimensional tear at bay.

    1. Destroy the shrine
      oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_destroy

      You move to destroy the shrine and it responds to your attack. Two Imperial warships emerge from the structure and attack.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the fight:
      oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_destroy_victory

      The first Imperial vessel is destroyed, but there is no time to lose. You prepare for the next engagement.

      • Crew recalled to ship
      1. Continue
        oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_destroy_victory1

        A second Imperial ship moves in to attack.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Empire ship
        After the fight:
        oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_destroy_victory2

        The enemy vessel falls silent. The Imperial defenders have been defeated.

        1. Continue
          oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_destroy_victory3

          You salvage what you can before turning your attention to the shrine.

          • Random large reward
          1. Continue
            oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_heart_finale

            The shrine is defenseless. You unleash your weapons at the shrine. It fractures and explodes in a burst of pure light. Without the psychic force of the shrine stabilizing the region, the dimensional tear expands massively, with horrifying speed. Within moments, the entire planet is swallowed by the rift.

            1. Continue
              oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_heart_finale2

              Reality shifts once again, and you see a strange, flesh-crafted weapon growing and meshing itself with your ship's systems.

              • Warp Lance
              1. Continue
    2. Help the Imperials push back the demonic forces
      oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_help

      You approach the shrine and are contacted by an Imperial priest. She tells you there is a ritual taking place to push back the subspace incursion. It is almost finished and they are under constant attack. They need just a little more time.

      1. Continue
        oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_help1

        You fly in, ready to do whatever you can. Dozens of living ships appear from a subspace rift. The present Imperial ships charge in with you leading the way.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Possessed ship
        After the fight:
        oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_help_victory1

        Your first enemy has been destroyed. Subspace energy crackles across your hull. Interdimensional breaches appear on your ship as you plunge into the next battle.

        • Crew recalled to ship
        1. Continue
          oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_help_victory1_1

          The Imperial forces have lost a few ships, but they fight on bravely. Another horror emerges from subspace and attacks you.

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Possessed ship
          After the fight:
          oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_help_victory2

          The second ship is annihilated. Half of the Imperial ships have been destroyed, but the battle is not yet over. You prepare for the next engagement.

          • Crew recalled to ship
          1. Continue
            oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_help_victory2_1

            The ritual is almost finished. At this moment, the subspace monstrosities launch a final attack.

            1. Continue
            Fight! vs Possessed ship
            After the fight:
            oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_help_victory3

            As the eldritch incursion finally breaks through the defensive line, the shrine lights up like the sun. The subspace rift devouring the planet is at first pushed back, then incinerated in the holy light of the God-King. Only a few enemies remain and they are swiftly destroyed by the Imperials.

            1. Continue
              oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_help_victory4

              You are contacted by the shrine. The Imperials show their gratitude by permitting you to salvage a portion of battlefield.

              • Random large reward
              • Retributor III
              1. Continue

DerelictDemonShipQuest_destroy

→ oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_destroy_victory

DerelictDemonShipQuest_heart

→ oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_heart_finale

DerelictDemonShipQuest_help

→ oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_help_victory1

DerelictDemonShipQuest_help_victory1

→ oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_help_victory1_1

DerelictDemonShipQuest_help_victory2

→ oTxtDerelictDemonShipQuest_help_victory2_1

DerelictDemonShip_attack

→ oTxtDerelictDemonShip_attack_win

DerelictDemonShip_investigateA

→ oTxtDerelictDemonShip_investigateA_heart

DerelictDemonShip_investigateA_rescue2

→ oTxtDerelictDemonShip_investigateA_rescue2_win

DerelictDemonShip_investigateA_rescue3

→ oTxtDerelictDemonShip_investigateA_rescue3_win

DerelictDemonShip_sacrifice

→ oTxtDerelictDemonShip_sacrifice_crew

DerelictDemonShip_salvage

→ oTxtDerelictDemonShip_salvage_moveon

EZAbandonedColony

oTxtEZAbandonedColony_intro

A large nearby rift storm dissipates to reveal a derelict orbital colony floating through space. Somehow it has been transported here from its original orbit.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtEZAbandonedColony_investigateB

      The skeletal remnants of the colony yield nothing of value.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZAbandonedColony_investigateC

      As you approach, a corsair vessel leaves the derelict colony on an intercept course towards you. Its weapons are primed.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      Random outcome (1/4 each):
      • oTxtEZAbandonedColony_investigateC1

        It seems the colony has become a hive bastion for corsairs in the area. You make your escape as an entire swarm of ships undock and converge on your position.

        • Crew recalled to ship
        1. Continue
      • oTxtEZAbandonedColony_investigateC2

        You explore the orbital colony, but it appears that the corsairs had already stripped the ruins clean.

        • Crew recalled to ship
      • oTxtEZAbandonedColony_investigateC3

        You dock your ship to the abandoned colony and explore its interior. Suddenly the colony begins to disintegrate. You retreat back to your ship, but several large chunks of the colony have already impacted your vessel.

        • Crew recalled to ship
        • −1 hull HP
      • oTxtEZAbandonedColony_investigateC4

        You proceed to investigate the colony and discover a corsair crewman wandering aimlessly.

        • Crew recalled to ship
        1. Continue
          oTxtEZAbandonedColony_investigateC4_2

          "They marooned me... Do not leave me here!"

          1. Rescue him
            Random outcome (1/3 each):
            • oTxtEZAbandonedColony_rescue1

              You allow the marooned raider to join your crew

              • Recruit enemy commander as crew
              1. Continue
            • oTxtEZAbandonedColony_rescue2

              You bring the raider back with you. Soon he begins to show signs of agitation. Suddenly he begins attacking, all the while laughing maniacally.

              • 1 boarder (commander's crew type)
              1. Continue
            • oTxtEZAbandonedColony_rescue3

              You allow the raider aboard. Suddenly, an otherworldly monstrosity appears before him. "You begged for salvation. Your salvation has arrived. Now, honor your bargain with the Endfather..."

              1. Continue
                oTxtEZAbandonedColony_rescue3b

                It engulfs the raider before dissolving into a puddle of sludge. A rotted form emerges from the pool and seems to look at you questioningly.

                • Gain crew: Corsair
                1. Continue
          2. Leave him behind
            Random outcome (1/2 each):
            • oTxtEZAbandonedColony_leaveHimBehind2

              "Have you no heart?!" You press on, leaving the raider to his fate.

              1. Continue
            • oTxtEZAbandonedColony_leaveHimBehind1

              You refuse the corsair and return to your ship. Just as the hatch is closing the desperate raider jumps through and begins to attack.

              • 1 boarder (commander's crew type)
              1. Continue
              After the boarders are defeated:
              oTxtEZAbandonedColony_leaveHimBehind1b

              You recover a weapon from the raider's dead clutches.

              • 60%: Random Raider weapon / 40%: Random weapon item
              1. Continue
    • oTxtEZAbandonedColony_investigateA

      You board the colony and discover a gruesome story of war and famine. You manage to collect a few items of value that survived destruction.

      • Random moderate reward
      1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtEZAbandonedColony_leave

    You avoid the colony and move on.

    1. Continue

EZAbandonedColony_investigateC4

→ oTxtEZAbandonedColony_investigateC4_2

EZAutoShipyard

oTxtEZAutoShipyard_intro

You are passing through a vast asteroid field spanning the region when you happen upon a derelict shipyard. Preliminary scans indicate this facility possesses an unusually high level of automation.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/5 each):
    • oTxtEZAutoShipyard_investigateE

      You recover some salvage from the facility.

      • Random moderate reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZAutoShipyard_investigateA

      Innumerable asteroid impacts have rendered the entire facility beyond salvage. You move on.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZAutoShipyard_investigateC

      As you approach, the shipyard erupts into a frenzied display of activity. Automated machinery operates at an astonishing pace, rapidly assembling a nightmarish vessel of deformed metal. Adorned with malevolent appendages and shrouded in profane energies, it effortlessly glides off the slipway and attacks.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Possessed ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtEZAutoShipyard_investigateC2

      The automated shipyard has disintegrated into a cloud of fine dust, as if completely spent by the construction of the possessed starship. You move on.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZAutoShipyard_investigateB

      You discover the shipyard to be deserted, but strangely well-maintained and operational. Perhaps this is a chance to restore your ship to fighting trim.

      1. Attempt to repair your ship
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtEZAutoShipyard_attemptRepair2

          The shipyard automation restores a few armor plates before seizing up.

          • +2 hull HP
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEZAutoShipyard_attemptRepair1

          The shipyard automation expends all the fresh armor plating in its stock. Your ship is greatly restored.

          • +5 hull HP
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEZAutoShipyard_attemptRepair3

          Something went wrong. Rather than restoring your armor plating, the shipyard automation dismantles a number of structural members before you manage to break your ship away.

          • −1 hull HP
          1. Continue
      2. Raid the material stocks
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtEZAutoShipyard_raidMaterial1

          Though the shipyard is operational, there does not seem to be a large stock of parts for salvaging. You take what you can find before leaving.

          • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEZAutoShipyard_raidMaterial2

          You retrieve a quantity of armor plating. Not long after bringing them aboard, you discover that you were deceived: the plates morph into a great number of small, gibbering horrors that begin attacking everything in sight.

          • 5–6 Fleshling boarders
          1. Continue
    • oTxtEZAutoShipyard_investigateD

      A small technocultist vessel detaches itself from the shipyard and leaves.

      1. Pursue the technocultist vessel
        oTxtEZAutoShipyard_pursueVessel

        You manage to catch up to the fleeing technocult vessel. Intruder alarms sound as servo-husks teleport in from the enemy ship.

        • 1–3 Husk, Servo-husk, or Combat Husk boarders
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Techno Small ship
      2. Search the shipyard
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtEZAutoShipyard_searchShipyard2

          You search the shipyard. Whatever the technocultist vessel was doing, it seems it had left most of the shipyard untouched.

          • Random large reward
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEZAutoShipyard_searchShipyard1

          You turn your attention to the shipyard. It is likely the technocultist ship had already ransacked the facility, as you recover only a modest quantity of salvage.

          • Random small reward
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEZAutoShipyard_searchShipyard3

          Just as you begin to search the shipyard, another technocultist vessel arrives.

          1. Continue
            oTxtEZAutoShipyard_searchShipyard3_2

            "Warning. Unregistered vessel in contact with Class-7 Anomaly: Your hazardous activity will be terminated."

            1. Continue
            Fight! vs Techno ship
            After the rewards from the fight:
            oTxtEZAutoShipyard_searchShipyard3_3

            You are unable to find anything of value aboard the shipyard and move on.

            1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtEZAutoShipyard_leave

    You avoid the facility and move on.

    1. Continue
oTxtEZAutoShipyard_raidMaterial3 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You retrieve a quantity of armor plating. Surprisingly, there was little else of value at the shipyard. You move on.

  • Hull Integrity Patch
  • Hull Integrity Patch
  1. Continue

EZAutoShipyard_searchShipyard3

→ oTxtEZAutoShipyard_searchShipyard3_2

EZBloodHuffer

oTxtEZBloodHuffer_AttackA

You attack the Ariokine vessel.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Blood ship
After the rewards from the fight:
Random outcome (1/2 each):
  • oTxtEZBloodHuffer_AttackA_winA

    You note that the precursor compounds are too unrefined to be of use to you.

    1. Continue
  • oTxtEZBloodHuffer_AttackA_winB

    Amid the wreckage you discover several intact flasks used to store the psyk-chem precursors.

    1. Attempt to recover them
      oTxtEZBloodHuffer_DropFlask

      You attempt to bring a flask aboard. It fractures and erupts into a panoply of horrid creatures.

      • 2–3 Demonic Crew boarders
      1. Continue
      After the boarders are defeated:
      oTxtEZBloodHuffer_DropFlask_win

      You discover a weapon embedded within one of the creatures.

      • 60%: Random Blood Cult weapon / 40%: Random weapon item
      1. Continue
    2. Destroy them
      oTxtEZBloodHuffer_AttackScuttle

      You rig scuttling charges to the flasks and destroy them.

      1. Continue
oTxtEZBloodHuffer_AttackB

You catch the Ariokine vessel by surprise.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Blood ship
oTxtEZBloodHuffer_AttackC

A rift storm masks your approach. Your opening salvo triggers a massive secondary explosion aboard the Ariokine vessel.

  • 2–4 Fleshling, Blood Golem, or Tendril boarders
  1. Continue
Fight! vs Blood ship
oTxtEZBloodHuffer_EvadeA

You dive into another storm cluster. Just inside you come upon a raider vessel. "We were waiting on the damn bleeders, but you will have to do!"

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Raider ship
oTxtEZBloodHuffer_EvadeB

You dive into another storm cluster. After some time it dissipates, and you find yourself alone.

  1. Continue
oTxtEZBloodHuffer_Intro Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Rift storms periodically disrupt your sensor scans. You discover an Ariokine vessel in the process of gathering subspace psyk-chem precursor compounds.

  1. Attack
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • → oTxtEZBloodHuffer_AttackA
    • → oTxtEZBloodHuffer_AttackB
    • → oTxtEZBloodHuffer_AttackC
  2. Evade
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • → oTxtEZBloodHuffer_EvadeB
    • → oTxtEZBloodHuffer_EvadeA
  3. (Thrall Pit) Contact the Ariokine vessel
    oTxtEZBloodHuffer_bt_thrallPit1

    You make contact with the Ariokine vessel. They are able to detect the subspace emissions of your thrall pit and recognize you as an ally.

    1. Continue
      oTxtEZBloodHuffer_bt_thrallPit2

      "Our scryers see you on the path of a sacred journey. Allow us to bless your hallowed purpose." The cultists aboard the vessel initiate a series of dark rituals, marked by copious use of powerful psyk-chems.

      1. Continue
        oTxtEZBloodHuffer_bt_thrallPit3

        The unholy energies radiating from the rituals have a resonant effect on your thrall pit. Energized by eldritch powers, it births not the usual thrall, but a glistening blood golem.

        • Blood Golem
        1. Continue
          oTxtEZBloodHuffer_bt_thrallPit4

          "Ygnl geth bhilarb vraat..." The Ariokine commander, transformed by the rituals in turn, murmurs his approval. The Ariokine vessel then returns to harvesting the gaseous clouds. You move on.

          1. Continue

EZBloodHuffer_AttackA

→ oTxtEZBloodHuffer_AttackA_winA

EZBloodHuffer_DropFlask

→ oTxtEZBloodHuffer_DropFlask_win

EZBloodHuffer_bt

→ oTxtEZBloodHuffer_bt_thrallPit1

EZDarkTechnoSurrender

oTxtEZDarkTechnoSurrender_intro

A technocult ship emerges from a nearby riftstorm and begins shooting.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Techno ship
When enemy hull drops below 60%:
Random outcome (1/2 each):
  • oTxtEZDarkTechnoSurrender_surrenderBeg

    The machine priests surrender and beg for their lives. But something is wrong. New figures emerge from the shadows and begin chanting in some foul tongue. Dark energy whips across the deck, depositing throngs of nightmare creatures.

    • 2–4 Demonic Crew boarders
    • 1–3 Weak cultist crew boarders
    1. Continue
  • oTxtEZDarkTechnoSurrender_surrenderApology

    "Stop! The riftstorms here have scrambled our sensors and caused us to misidentify your ship. You have our deepest apologies."

    1. Accept their apology
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtEZDarkTechnoSurrender_dropGuard

        Suddenly, the machine priests fire all their weapons and score a few hits on your hull. "We never misidentified your ship. We simply made an error in our threat calculations. We will not make the same mistake next time." The technocult vessel jumps out.

        • Crew recalled to ship
        • 2–3 breaches spawned
        • Enemy ship warps away
        1. Continue
      • oTxtEZDarkTechnoSurrender_renegade

        The commander explains they were in pursuit of a vessel commandeered by renegade machine priests. "The unnatural energies in this region have corrupted their databanks, and they have gone mad."

        • Crew recalled to ship
        1. Continue
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • oTxtEZDarkTechnoSurrender_renegade2A

            Weapon fire suddenly sprays out from a nearby riftstorm, punching massive holes into the technocult ship. It explodes.

            • Enemy ship destroyed
            1. Continue
          • oTxtEZDarkTechnoSurrender_renegade2B

            Suddenly a second technocult ship appears. "No! They are the ones who have gone mad, not us!"

            1. Continue
              oTxtEZDarkTechnoSurrender_renegade2B_1

              The technocult ships start firing at each other. In moments, both ships are reduced to flaming debris. You retrieve what you can from the wreckage.

              • Random small reward
              • Enemy ship destroyed
              1. Continue
    2. Kill them

EZDarkTechnoSurrender_renegade2A

oTxtEZDarkTechnoSurrender_renegade2A_1 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Another technocult vessel bursts out from the riftstorm and begins firing on you.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Techno ship

EZDarkTechnoSurrender_renegade2B

→ oTxtEZDarkTechnoSurrender_renegade2B_1

EZDemonBattles

oTxtEZDemonBattles_intro

You arrive in-system to discover a horde of infernal creatures laying waste to an Imperial starship. Several creatures are visible on the ship's outer hull, tearing off huge sections of armor plating with their clawed hands.

  1. Assist the starship
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtEZDemonBattles_assistA

      You unleash your ship's weapons at the creatures. Each shot that connects pulverizes its target into red mist. After several minutes of shooting, all of the creatures are dead. The survivors aboard the Imperial ship thank you for your help and offer a small reward.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZDemonBattles_assistC

      You fire a few salvos, atomizing the putrid creatures infesting the hull. However, your action appears to catalyze a sinister reaction. A malevolent force seizes the Imperial vessel, convulsing it into an ungodly amalgamation of nightmarish growths. In this dire moment, a sinister, telepathic voice resonates in your mind. "At last, a worthy offering. Far superior to those foul brutes of Gorgoth."

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Possessed ship
    • oTxtEZDemonBattles_assistD

      You maneuver into attack range. Suddenly, an explosion of putrid energy engulfs the Imperial vessel. Abhorrent growths erupt along its hull, its form twisting under the corrupt influence. A sinister, guttural gurgling resonates from the now-possessed ship. A haunting voice echoes,"Even Krom's progeny cannot elude the grasp of the Lord of Death. Send them back to their frenzied master and expel this intruder."

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Possessed ship
    • oTxtEZDemonBattles_assistB

      Just as you maneuver to aid the Imperial vessel, you witness enormous spines erupt through its outer hull, shredding the infesting creatures into tattered remnants of iridescent gore. The vessel rapidly mutates, its hull twisting and bulging beyond recognition in a matter of moments. You sense an unholy presence addressing you. "Eradicating this filth was becoming tiresome. You promise a much more entertaining challenge."

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Possessed ship
  2. Remain hidden
    oTxtEZDemonBattles_remainHidden

    You decide to remain hidden. Eventually the Imperial starship crumbles and the infernal creatures depart, presumably after having devoured anyone aboard.

    1. Continue
  3. (Blood Psyker) Attempt to enslave a demon
    oTxtEZDemonBattles_bt_psykerBlood

    One of your crew steeped in the rites of the blood cults offers to capture one of the abominations. Over the course of hours, your crewmember manages to ensnare and trap one of the greater demons in a psychically-reinforced ampoule.

    • Fiendish Reagents
    1. Continue
  4. (Death Psyker) Attempt to enslave a demon
    oTxtEZDemonBattles_bt_psykerDeath

    One of your crew steeped in the rites of the death cults offers to capture one of the abominations. Over the course of hours, your crewmember manages to ensnare and trap one of the greater demons in a psychically-reinforced ampoule.

    • Necrotizing Reagents
    1. Continue
  5. (War Psyker) Attempt to enslave a demon
    oTxtEZDemonBattles_bt_psykerWar

    One of your crew steeped in the rites of the war cults offers to capture one of the abominations. Over the course of hours, your crewmember manages to ensnare and trap one of the greater demons in an psychically-reinforced ampoule.

    • Ruinous Reagents
    1. Continue

EZDemonBattles_bt

→ oTxtEZDemonBattles_bt_psykerBlood

EZDemonFestival

oTxtEZDemonFestival_intro

Sensors detect an unidentified starship within one of the riftstorms. As you approach it, the storm seems to thin out, and soon you see an old, derelict starship. Then you notice the large congregation of otherworldly monstrosities upon the hull. They seem to be engaged in an obscene ritual.

  1. Attack
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtEZDemonFestival_attackA

      Whatever they are doing, it cannot be good. You charge your weapons. The riftstorm surrounding you suddenly increases in intensity, as if reacting to your intent.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
    • oTxtEZDemonFestival_attackB

      You fire at the odious congregation. Your weaponry sears through the starship beneath them, but the monstrosities are totally unfazed. Instead, they point and laugh, first at the smoldering hole, and then at you.

      1. Attempt a psychic attack
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtEZDemonFestival_attackPsyC

          The psychic energies have only attracted the monstrosities. You ready yourself for combat as they rematerialize aboard your ship.

          • 2–5 Demonic Crew boarders
          1. Continue
          After the boarders are defeated:
          oTxtEZDemonFestival_attackPsyC_win

          The odious congregation has dissipated, and along with it the nearby riftstorms, and you salvage what you can from the derelict starship

          • Random ship weapon
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEZDemonFestival_attackPsyB

          You direct the focusing of psychic energies at the odious congregation. The monstrosities writhe in agony before disintegrating, some violently exploding into an almost festive spray of multicolored gore. The riftstorms subside, and you salvage what you can from the derelict starship.

          • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEZDemonFestival_attackPsyA

          You direct the focusing of psychic energies at the odious congregation. The monstrosities gather around in a more organized fashion, and begin gesturing grotesquely in unison. Their movements reach a crescendo and you hear a roaring in the back of your head as they all point at your ship. Suddenly your entire crew is agonized by psychic feedback.

          • All crew damaged (50%)
          1. Continue
            oTxtEZDemonFestival_attackPsyA_b

            The congregation of monstrosities disappear, and the riftstorms dissipate in turn. You manage to salvage some valuables from the derelict starship.

            • Random small reward
            1. Continue
      2. Leave
        oTxtEZDemonFestival_attackB_leave

        With no other option in mind, you decide to vacate the area.

        1. Continue
  2. Observe the creatures
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtEZDemonFestival_observeA

      You quietly continue to observe the gathering. Suddenly one of the monstrous creatures gasps and points out your presence, and countless otherworldly beings boil out of the derelict starship. They hurtle into the void towards your ship, and you have no choice but to flee from their immense numbers.

      1. Continue
        oTxtEZDemonFestival_observeA_b

        You command flank speed and race out of the riftstorm. The creatures are no longer in pursuit, but your overloaded drives ruptured a flux conduit, causing some injuries.

        • Random crew damaged (50%)
        1. Continue
    • oTxtEZDemonFestival_observeB

      You quietly continue to observe the gathering. Soon, more of the otherworldly beings materialize upon your ship. One of them dances grotesquely before gesturing at you with a flaming sword.

      1. Follow it
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtEZDemonFestival_follow2

          You attempt to match the creature's movements. It seems nonplussed, and bites you. Then the riftstorm along with the otherworldly congregation vanish, as if they were never there to begin with.

          • Commander damaged (50%)
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEZDemonFestival_follow

          You follow it as it moves hypnotically through your entire ship, finally arriving at your engine room. The tempo of the movements increase until the creature seems to glow with an inner light. Suddenly it explodes, showering you in wet, iridescent flaps of skin and entrails. A strangely colored tendril of energy rises up from the carcass towards a bank of flux coils before disappearing. You later discover your ship's thruster output has increased.

          • +1 to Engines
          1. Continue
      2. Duel it
        oTxtEZDemonFestival_duel

        You accept the challenge and engage the creature in a duel.

        1. Continue
        When the target crew member dies:
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtEZDemonFestival_duelWinA

          You best the otherworldly creature, and pick up its flaming sword. The other monstrosities around you hoot and clap. You blink. Then you awaken with a start. An empty and tranquil area of space is visible through the viewport before you. You are still gripping the flaming sword.

          • Hellfire Blade
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEZDemonFestival_duelWinB

          You best the creature, and looking around, realize that your monstrous guests are nowhere to be found. The derelict ship is gone, and what remains of the riftstorm around you is dissipating into a few wisps of dust and gas.

          1. Continue
      3. (Door 2) Lock down your vessel and capture it
        oTxtEZDemonFestival_bt_door

        You command the complete lockdown of your vessel. The demonic being cackles and attempts to pass through the nearest door, only to be repelled by the additional wardings reinforcing your interior defenses. You force the desperate creature to yield to your summons, and record the contract on a psychomantic wafer.

        • Summon Fiend
        1. Continue
          oTxtEZDemonFestival_bt_door2

          The riftstorm has dissipated, and the demonic congregation along with it.

          1. Continue
  3. (Blood Knight) Attempt to communicate
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtEZDemonFestival_talk

      The gathering freezes. The otherworldly beings all stop and turn to look at your ship in unison. The congregation then disperses, individuals scurrying away in every direction out of sight. The derelict starship disintegrates into dust, whipped by the increasingly agitated currents of the surrounding riftstorm.

      1. Continue
        oTxtEZDemonFestival_talk_reward

        You recover an interesting item from the aftermath.

        • random Item (BLOODWEAPONS) or random Item (BLOODCONSUMABLES) or random Item (BLOODARMORS) or random Item (BLOODSHIELDS) or random Item (BLOODSPELLS)
        1. Continue
    • oTxtEZDemonFestival_talk2

      The demons reply with a multitude of giggles, hoots, cackles, and raucous laughter.

      1. Continue
        oTxtEZDemonFestival_returnToIntro

        You consider your options.

        1. Attack
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • → oTxtEZDemonFestival_attackA
          • → oTxtEZDemonFestival_attackB
        2. Observe the creatures
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • → oTxtEZDemonFestival_observeA
          • → oTxtEZDemonFestival_observeB
        3. (Blood Knight) Attempt to communicate
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • → oTxtEZDemonFestival_talk
          • → oTxtEZDemonFestival_talk2
        4. Leave
          oTxtEZDemonFestival_introLeave

          You leave the otherworldly congregation to its festivities and depart.

          1. Continue
  4. Leave
    → oTxtEZDemonFestival_introLeave

EZDemonFestival_attackB

→ oTxtEZDemonFestival_attackB_leave

EZDemonFestival_attackPsyA

→ oTxtEZDemonFestival_attackPsyA_b

EZDemonFestival_attackPsyC

→ oTxtEZDemonFestival_attackPsyC_win

EZDemonFestival_bt

→ oTxtEZDemonFestival_bt_door

EZDemonFestival_observeA

→ oTxtEZDemonFestival_observeA_b

EZDemonFestival_talk

→ oTxtEZDemonFestival_talk_reward

EZDemonMass

oTxtEZDemonMass_intro

Your ship emerges inside one of the more turbulent regions of the exclusion zone. Waves of toxic miasma penetrate your shielded hull, ignoring the laws of physics. The miasma seems to be emanating from a strange mass in the distance.

  1. Investigate the mass
    oTxtEZDemonMass_investigate1

    A rotted, fish-like monstrosity, roughly the size of a small spacecraft, appears to be collecting nearby corpses and attaching them to the mass.

    1. Continue
      Random outcome (1/4 each):
      • oTxtEZDemonMass_investigate2c

        Your warning lights begin to flicker as a subspace rift yawns open on your flank. You pick up a voice on the open channel. "Ymg'rlax? My lovely pet, is the necro-engine complete? Oh? What do we have here? More meat for the engine?"

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Plague ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        oTxtEZDemonMass_investigate2c_2

        Angered by the loss of its keeper, the corpse creature lets out an ear-piercing shriek. It charges your ship, speeding head first into your line of fire. Starship-grade munitions rip through its soft dermis like tissue paper and in seconds the creature is reduced to nothing more than a cloud of mangled flesh.

        1. Continue
      • oTxtEZDemonMass_investigate2d
