        The rotted creature notices your approach and immediately begins devouring the corpse mass. You barely have a chance to react when a hostile vessel jumps in and target locks your ship. The vessel's commander proclaims, "The unclean shall be purged."

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Empire ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        oTxtEZDemonMass_investigate2d_2

        The rotted creature is nowhere to be found. It must have fled during the battle.

        1. Continue
      • oTxtEZDemonMass_investigate2a

        The corpse creature suddenly notices you. It sprays gouts of pestilent energy at your ship, coating your hull in a sorcerous slime. You catch a brief glimpse of the fish beast vanishing into the murk, just as the intruder alarms begin blaring in response to the foul entities crawling out of the slime.

        • 2–4 Demonic Crew boarders
        1. Continue
      • oTxtEZDemonMass_investigate2b

        The corpse creature suddenly turns around and swims off into the murk. The corpses begin to fall away from the mass, revealing a malformed starship. You scan the starship, detecting significant levels of movement, but not a single life signature. Possessed by an abominable intelligence, the starship begins powering its weapons. Then it locks on to your ship.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Possessed ship
  2. Avoid it
    oTxtEZDemonMass_avoid

    You decide to keep your distance.

    1. Continue
  3. (Cult Psyker) Psychically probe the mass
    oTxtEZDemonMass_bt_psykerCult1

    Your cultist crewmember discovers the presence of a massive and decaying creature. This fish-like corpse creature has collected the remnants of innumerable doomed starships to decorate its foul nest.

    1. Continue
      oTxtEZDemonMass_bt_psykerCult2

      The corpse creature is greatly distressed by your presence. Communicating through your cultist, it offers you a choice trinket from its nest on the condition you depart promptly afterwards.

      1. Request ship armament
        oTxtEZDemonMass_bt_psykerCultArmament

        The creature roots through the great mass of its nest before spitting a starship grade weapon towards you. You retrieve the offering and depart.

        • Random ship weapon
        1. Continue
      2. Request crew equipment
        oTxtEZDemonMass_bt_psykerCultEquipment

        The creature roots through the mass forming its nest before depositing a number of crew equipment near your vessel. You retrieve the items and move on.

        • Random tool item
        • Random consumable
        1. Continue
      3. Request munitions
        oTxtEZDemonMass_bt_psykerCultMunitions

        The creature dives into its hoard and returns with a stock of munitions. You collect the materiel and move on.

        • Large amount of missile ammo (8–12) and 1/2 chance for 1 missile blueprint
        • Random missile blueprint
        1. Continue
      4. Request psychomancy
        oTxtEZDemonMass_bt_psykerCultSpell

        The corpse creature nips at the nest mass, ingesting a number of heads from the innumerable frozen corpses that adorn its surface. At length, it regurgitates a smaller mass near your vessel. You retrieve the mass and the psychomancies contained within before moving on.

        • Random psychic item
        1. Continue

EZDemonMass_bt

→ oTxtEZDemonMass_bt_psykerCult1

EZDemonMass_investigate2c

→ oTxtEZDemonMass_investigate2c_2

EZDemonMass_investigate2d

→ oTxtEZDemonMass_investigate2d_2

EZDriftingDeathShip

oTxtEZDriftingDeathShip_intro

You emerge from subspace inside the murky depths of a riftstorm. An old starship floats into view. It looks like it has been drifting for centuries.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtEZDriftingDeathShip_investigateB

      You board the ship and search its depths for useful material. Not much can be recovered, but you do manage to secure an intact supply container.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZDriftingDeathShip_investigateD

      You search the ship and come across a deranged death cultist. Despite suffering from clear signs of intense paranoia, delusional thinking, and general insanity, he seems to take orders well.

      • Gorgothian Legionnaire, Death Cultist, Death Mage, Necromancer, or Mortician
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZDriftingDeathShip_investigateC

      You deploy a boarding team to check out the old starship. Unfortunately the riftstorms worsen and you are forced to call off the boarding action. Alarms begin sounding on your console indicating the presence of multiple subspace breaches.

      • 3–4 warp breaches spawned
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZDriftingDeathShip_investigateA

      You decide to send a boarding party to investigate the ship. Minutes into your excursion, a death cult vessel tears into realspace on your flank. Its commander cries, "What do you think you are doing? Breaking into my property without permission?"

      1. Continue
        oTxtEZDriftingDeathShip_investigateA1

        The death cultist conjures a storm of dark energy and in moments, the dead begin rising off the ground. You rush back to battle stations.

        • 1–3 poison clouds spawned
        • 2–4 Greater Corpulent or Ghoul Magus boarders
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Plague ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        oTxtEZDriftingDeathShip_investigateA2

        Without the power of its master to sustain it, the old starship soon crumbles into dust.

        1. Continue
  2. Move on
    oTxtEZDriftingDeathShip_moveOn

    You move on.

    1. Continue

EZEmpireCivilianPursuit

oTxtEZEmpireCivilianPursuit_intro

Your sensors detect a civilian vessel being pursued by an Imperial crusade ship. The crusade ship is closing fast.

  1. Assist the civilian vessel
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtEZEmpireCivilianPursuit_assist

      You target the crusade ship and prepare to fight.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtEZEmpireCivilianPursuit_assistA

        The civilian vessel is nowhere to be found.

        1. Continue
      • oTxtEZEmpireCivilianPursuit_assistB

        The civilian vessel contacts you. "We got lost in a riftstorm and stumbled into those Imperial crusaders. They did not even give us a chance to explain before they started shooting. Here, take these supplies. For saving us."

        • Random small reward
        1. Continue
    • oTxtEZEmpireCivilianPursuit_assistC

      You move in to intercept the crusade ship. They exclaim, "Get out of our way you fool, the witches aboard that ship are about to unleash an incomprehensible evil!" As if on cue, a sorcerous light explodes out of the civilian ship and obliterates the Imperial crusaders. Lightning envelops the region and the foul creatures of subspace begin materializing on your deck! The civilian ship turns around to attack.

      • 3–4 Demonic Crew boarders
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Cult ship
  2. Attempt to stay hidden
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtEZEmpireCivilianPursuit_hideA

      You cut power to your drives. The two ships fly off and disappear into a nearby riftstorm.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZEmpireCivilianPursuit_hideB

      You reduce thrust. The crusade ship launches a volley of cannon fire at the civilian vessel and blasts it to pieces. Then the ship turns around and broadcasts, "Surrender now. Though your lives are forfeit, your souls might yet be redeemed in the eyes of the Immortal King."

      1. Attack them
        oTxtEZEmpireCivilianPursuit_attack

        You dispense with the formalities and open fire.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Empire ship
      2. Ignore them
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtEZEmpireCivilianPursuit_dontRespond2

          The Imperial crusade ship moves in to attack.

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Empire ship
        • oTxtEZEmpireCivilianPursuit_dontRespond

          The Imperial crusade ship is unable to acquire a lock on your position and eventually leaves.

          1. Continue
oTxtEZEmpireCivilianPursuit_surrender Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You agree to surrender. Of course in truth you have hatched a plan to gain the upper hand. You wait until the crusaders dock with your ship, then detonate a cluster of jury-rigged flux capacitors. The docking clamps tear off the Imperial ship, causing severe damage to their hull.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Empire ship

EZEmpireToxin

oTxtEZEmpireToxin_intro

You come across an Imperial warship locked in combat with an unknown enemy. You consider getting involved, but a quick glance at your console shows high levels of toxic gasses flooding the area. Such subspace-infused toxins ignore the laws of reality and cannot be fully protected against by conventional means. Any sort of ship-to-ship action here would be exceedingly dangerous.

  1. Try to help the Imperial ship
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtEZEmpireToxin_tryHelpB

      You fire a few shots in the direction of the Imperial ship's mysterious assailant. Unfortunately visibility is poor and your long-range sensors have been going haywire with strange interference.

      1. Continue
        oTxtEZEmpireToxin_tryHelpB2

        The clouds of toxic gasses seem to grow thicker. A few minutes pass, and both the Imperial ship and its attacker disappear into the fog. You try searching for them but it is no use. You are alone.

        1. Continue
    • oTxtEZEmpireToxin_tryHelpA

      You make contact with the Imperial commander. "The enemy ship has been possessed by a demon! Do not underestimate its abilities!"

      1. Continue
        oTxtEZEmpireToxin_tryHelpA2

        Suddenly a volley of fire streaks out of the murk and rips into the Imperial ship. A possessed ship reveals itself, pausing in front of the exploding Imperial vessel as if gloating over its decisive victory. It then turns its weapons on you.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Possessed ship
  2. Attack the Imperial ship
    oTxtEZEmpireToxin_attack

    You open fire at the Imperial ship.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Empire ship
    After the rewards from the fight:
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtEZEmpireToxin_attack1

      The Imperial ship's mysterious assailant is nowhere to be found. The only trace of their presence is the residual energy of nearby subspace translation. They must have escaped while you were occupied.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZEmpireToxin_attack2

      The Imperial ship's mysterious assailant suddenly drifts into view. Cursed energy oozes off the vessel. Its command throne appears to be possessed by a malign subspace entity.

      • Crew recalled to ship
      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtEZEmpireToxin_attack3

          The possessed ship circles around the wreck of the Imperial ship. It then turns to attack you.

          • 2–4 Demonic Crew boarders
          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Possessed ship
        • oTxtEZEmpireToxin_attack4

          The possessed ship circles around the wreck of the Imperial ship. It ejects some material from its cargo bays before speeding away.

          • Very small amount of ⬗ Scrap
          1. Continue
  3. Stay hidden
    oTxtEZEmpireToxin_stayHidden

    You decide to play it safe. You remain hidden until the combating ships take their battle elsewhere.

    1. Continue

EZEvacOutpost

oTxtEZEvacOutpost_intro

You approach a small outpost. While the exclusion zones are ostensibly a deserted sector of space, numerous enterprising outcasts nevertheless manage to eke out a living from vessels wandering the region.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtEZEvacOutpost_investigateA

      The outpost invites you to dock and trade.

      • Proceeds to shop
      1. Trade
    • oTxtEZEvacOutpost_investigateB

      The metallic hull of the outpost suddenly seems to twist and bend. The outpost splits open, the outercasing peeling away like petals.

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtEZEvacOutpost_investigateB2

          Nothing else happens. Several hundred frozen corpses float out and away from the ruptured outpost. You move on.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtEZEvacOutpost_investigateB1

          A cultist vessel emerges from within the ruptured outpost. After a moment, it turns to attack you. "I hunger..."

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Blood ship
    • oTxtEZEvacOutpost_investigateC

      You notice that several corsair vessels are docked to the outpost. A few undock with haste and jump away.

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtEZEvacOutpost_investigateC3

          One of the corsair vessels pulls away from the outpost and approaches you, weapons primed. There is something strange about its crew.

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Raider ship
        • oTxtEZEvacOutpost_investigateC2

          "You should get out of here while you can."

          1. Continue
            Random outcome (1/3 each):
            • oTxtEZEvacOutpost_investigateC2_3

              The corsair vessels evacuate the area, leaving you with the now-deserted outpost. You inspect the interior and find it stripped bare, except for a small amount of raw materials.

              • Random moderate reward
              1. Continue
            • oTxtEZEvacOutpost_investigateC2_1

              Subspace breaches begin appearing across the outer hull of the outpost as the rest of the corsair vessels evacuate. You follow suit, but a fissure appears aboard your ship even as the outpost disappears behind you.

              • 2–3 warp breaches spawned
              • 1–3 Demonic Crew boarders
              1. Continue
              After the boarders are defeated:
              oTxtEZEvacOutpost_investigateC2_1a

              You recover a strange weapon from one of the monstrosities.

              • Random cult weapon
              1. Continue
            • oTxtEZEvacOutpost_investigateC2_2

              The remaining corsair vessels hurriedly jump out. Just as you are about to dock with the deserted outpost, a fleet of Imperial warships arrive and open fire, scoring a few hits. You have little choice but to escape before you are overwhelmed.

              • Crew recalled to ship
              • 2–3 breaches spawned
              1. Continue
    • oTxtEZEvacOutpost_investigateD

      The outpost sends you away. It seems their reactors suffered a containment failure, and they are having trouble sealing the resultant subspace breach. "We have some ...maintenance to do right now. Come back another time."

      1. Try to help them anyway
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtEZEvacOutpost_tryHelp1

          You go aboard the outpost to assist with their situation. The breach is rapidly sealed under your direction. The grateful denizens award you a prize in recognition of your help.

          • Random moderate reward
          1. Continue
            oTxtEZEvacOutpost_tryHelp1_a

            You return to your ship and discover a number of subspace breaches within.

            • 2–3 warp breaches spawned
            1. Continue
        • oTxtEZEvacOutpost_tryHelp2

          You go aboard the outpost to assist. You are met with the sight of horrific beings pouring out of the subspace breach in droves.

          1. Continue
            Random outcome (1/3 each):
            • oTxtEZEvacOutpost_tryHelp2_a

              After prolonged fighting you manage to beat back the invaders. The entire outpost is grateful for your assistance and offers you tribute.

              • Random small reward
              1. Continue
            • oTxtEZEvacOutpost_tryHelp2_c

              The outpost is unfortunately lost. You retreat to your ship, only to discover the subspace breach extending into the lower decks. You undock from the outpost and prepare to repel intruders.

              • 2–3 warp breaches spawned
              • 1–3 Monster crew boarders
              1. Continue
            • oTxtEZEvacOutpost_tryHelp2_b

              You are unable to stem the tidal wave of gurgling and howling creatures. You retreat to your ship.

              1. Continue
                oTxtEZEvacOutpost_tryHelp2_b1

                Suddenly, the outpost explodes. You find some worthwhile salvage in the debris and move on.

                • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
                1. Continue
      2. Leave
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtEZEvacOutpost_leave1

          As you leave, you pick up broadcasts of fearful screaming before the comms fall silent.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtEZEvacOutpost_leave2

          As you leave, the outpost explodes in a great conflagration. Countless pieces of debris are ejected at high velocity, and one of the fragments manages to penetrate your shielding, damaging your hull.

          • −1 hull HP
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEZEvacOutpost_leave3

          As you leave you see a couple of Imperial patrol ships speed past towards the outpost. One of them peels off to engage you.

          1. Continue
            oTxtEZEvacOutpost_leave3_2

            The Imperial commander's eyes glow with a strange malevolence. "Unidentified pirate vessel, your presence in the Exclusion Zone is in flagrant violation of Imperial navigation protocols. Your sentence is destruction."

            1. Continue
            Fight! vs Empire ship
  2. Ignore
    oTxtEZEvacOutpost_ignore

    You ignore the outpost and move on.

    1. Continue
oTxtEZEvacOutpost_investigateD2 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Leave

  1. Continue

EZEvacOutpost_investigateC2

→ oTxtEZEvacOutpost_investigateC2_1

EZEvacOutpost_leave3

→ oTxtEZEvacOutpost_leave3_2

EZEvacOutpost_tryHelp1

→ oTxtEZEvacOutpost_tryHelp1_a

EZEvacOutpost_tryHelp2

→ oTxtEZEvacOutpost_tryHelp2_a

EZEyeOfTheRiftstorm

oTxtEZEyeOfTheRiftstorm_intro

You come across a distress signal being broadcast from the center of a large riftstorm. Investigating it could be dangerous.

  1. Investigate the signal
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtEZEyeOfTheRiftstorm_investigateC

      You reach the origin of the signal and find a long-abandoned transport vessel. You salvage what you can from within.

      • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZEyeOfTheRiftstorm_investigateA

      You fly your ship directly into the riftstorm. Multiple subspace fissures streak across your deck and you find yourself under attack.

      • 2–4 Demonic Crew boarders
      1. Continue
      After the boarders are defeated:
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtEZEyeOfTheRiftstorm_investigateA1

        Unfortunately, the twisting energies of the riftstorm prove too great and you are forced to turn back. Your hull suffers some minor damage on your way out.

        • −1 hull HP
        1. Continue
      • oTxtEZEyeOfTheRiftstorm_investigateA2

        You recover some unusual objects from the remains.

        • Random cult consumable
        1. Continue
    • oTxtEZEyeOfTheRiftstorm_investigateB

      You maneuver through the raging maelstrom and make it to the distress signal. The ship is of Imperial origin, however something looks odd about its commander. "Laks roth makshub nikkk sath!"

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
    • oTxtEZEyeOfTheRiftstorm_investigateD

      You fly into the storm and arrive at the origin of the signal. It is a heavily damaged warship bearing the emblems of the Solar Hegemon. "Our crusade requires your assistance. We will- " You hear a gunshot as the officer's brains splatter out onto the screen, obscuring your view.

      1. Continue
        oTxtEZEyeOfTheRiftstorm_investigateD2

        Someone wipes the brains off the feed. "The crusade be damned, get us out of here!"

        1. Assist the Imperials
          oTxtEZEyeOfTheRiftstorm_assist

          You rescue the Imperials. They agree to get off your ship as soon as you get clear of the exclusion zone. One of them decides to join your crew.

          • Imperial Recruit or Imperial Scribe ×0
          1. Continue
        2. Destroy them
          oTxtEZEyeOfTheRiftstorm_attack

          You move in to attack the Imperial vessel.

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Empire ship
        3. Refuse to help
          oTxtEZEyeOfTheRiftstorm_refuse

          "No, you cannot leave us! Wait!" Suddenly a new ship bursts out from behind. It fires all its weapons and destroys the Imperial ship.

          1. Continue
            oTxtEZEyeOfTheRiftstorm_refuse2

            "Hmm? More weaklings? "

            1. Continue
            Fight! vs Blood ship
  2. Ignore it
    oTxtEZEyeOfTheRiftstorm_ignore

    You decide to play it safe.

    1. Continue

EZEyeOfTheRiftstorm_investigateA2

oTxtEZEyeOfTheRiftstorm_investigateA2_b Unreachable — not referenced by any event

The storm gets worse. Streaks of raw subspace energy tear great rents across your hull. You are forced to turn back.

  • −1 hull HP
  1. Continue

EZGhostShipyard

oTxtEZGhostShipyard_intro

You discover an abandoned shipyard. Just as you move closer to inspect it, a small pirate vessel shows up. "Hands off of that, I was here first."

  1. Attack
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtEZGhostShipyard_raiderB

      You charge your weapons and lock onto the pirate vessel.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider Small ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtEZGhostShipyard_raiderB_win

      You turn your attention back to the shipyard. It is in total disrepair and of no use to you.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZGhostShipyard_raiderA

      The pirate vessel flees before the target lock completes.

      1. Continue
        oTxtEZGhostShipyard_raiderA_win

        You turn your attention back to the shipyard. The facilities within are in a state of severe decay, but you manage to find some scrap.

        • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
        1. Continue
    • oTxtEZGhostShipyard_raiderC

      The pirate vessel backs off. "Woah alright, I was just kidding. Let's do this together, alright? Two guns are better than one."

      1. Work together
        oTxtEZGhostShipyard_alliance

        The pirate vessel follows you into the shipyard.

        1. Continue
          oTxtEZGhostShipyard_alliance_b

          Moored within is an ancient Imperial battlecruiser. The pirates trailing you mutter a few oaths. "That's the Edge of Eternity`. They say she's a cursed ship. A ghost. She should not be here."

          1. Set foot in it
            oTxtEZGhostShipyard_allianceEnter

            You have no time for spacefaring superstitions and board the ancient battlecruiser. The pirates are emboldened by your action, and run ahead deeper into the ship. Suddenly, you feel the deck move beneath your feet. You leap through one of the entry hatches just before it closes. The Edge of Eternity drifts off into the void, taking the unfortunate pirates with her.

            1. Continue
              oTxtEZGhostShipyard_allianceEnter_b

              You return back to your ship and loot the deserted pirate vessel.

              • Random large reward
              1. Continue
          2. Leave it alone
            Random outcome (1/2 each):
            • oTxtEZGhostShipyard_allianceLeaveB

              Superstition or not, you turn around and settle for the shipyard's facilities.

            • oTxtEZGhostShipyard_allianceLeaveA

              Space pirates are a superstitious lot, but generally for a good reason. You leave the battlecruiser alone, and search the shipyard instead.

              • Random moderate reward
              1. Continue
      2. Decline
        oTxtEZGhostShipyard_solo

        The pirate captain utters some profanities under his breath as he leaves.

        1. Continue
          oTxtEZGhostShipyard_solo_b

          Within the abandoned shipyard you find an Imperial battlecruiser. Upon her ancient flanks are countless victory marks. Her massive conning tower is graced by a row of 27 great enameled medallions, campaign commendations betokening a long and illustrious service.

          1. Search the ship for valuables
            Random outcome (1/4 each):
            • oTxtEZGhostShipyard_searchB

              You board the battlecruiser. You recover what you can from her armament halls.

              • Random ship weapon
              1. Continue
            • oTxtEZGhostShipyard_searchA

              You board the battlecruiser. You find a gleaming weapon resting upon the command throne.

              • Random ship weapon
              1. Continue
            • oTxtEZGhostShipyard_searchD

              Just as you board the battlecruiser its drives suddenly flare to life. You return to the boarding gantry and watch the ship disappear into the void.

              1. Continue
            • oTxtEZGhostShipyard_searchC

              You board the battlecruiser. As you search the ship it begins to disintegrate and collapse around you. You barely make it back to the nearest boarding gantry, but one unfortunate crewman was not so lucky.

              • Kill a random crew member
              1. Continue
          2. Leave it alone
            oTxtEZGhostShipyard_soloLeave

            You turn to leave. The ship seems to radiate a shimmering light. You see some gleaming in a distant pile of rubble, and discover some valuable salvage.

            • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
            1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtEZGhostShipyard_introLeave

    You leave the shipyard to the pirates.

    1. Continue

EZGhostShipyard_alliance

→ oTxtEZGhostShipyard_alliance_b

EZGhostShipyard_allianceEnter

→ oTxtEZGhostShipyard_allianceEnter_b

EZGhostShipyard_raiderA

→ oTxtEZGhostShipyard_raiderA_win

EZGhostShipyard_raiderB

→ oTxtEZGhostShipyard_raiderB_win

EZGhostShipyard_solo

→ oTxtEZGhostShipyard_solo_b

EZHugeDemonHulk

oTxtEZHugeDemonHulk_intro

You find yourself trapped within the currents of a large riftstorm. Violent waves of subspace energy batter your ship to and fro for what seems like an eternity. Eventually the storm subsides and your ship comes to a stop. In front of you is the dilapidated hull of a gigantic spacecraft. There is something deeply unsettling about the ship.

  1. Investigate the ship
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtEZHugeDemonHulk_investigateA

      You deploy a boarding party onto the ship. You discover a number of supplies strewn about its labyrinthine corridors.

      • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZHugeDemonHulk_investigateB

      You board the ship. After several hours of fruitless search, you decide to call it quits. You return to your vessel empty-handed.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZHugeDemonHulk_investigateD

      You board the ship and manage to retrieve an intact psychogram from the center of a large shrine.

      • 60%: Random cult psychic item / 40%: Random psychic item
      1. Continue
        oTxtEZHugeDemonHulk_investigateD2

        Unfortunately, removing the psychogram triggers some sort of infernal mechanism. Dark energy swirls out from the shrine. You retreat back to your ship as the hulk begins to collapse.

        1. Continue
          oTxtEZHugeDemonHulk_investigateD3

          The outer superstructure of the hulk has fallen away, revealing a smaller ship within. The new ship conjures a storm of infernal lightning which sears your hull and impregnates it with foul creatures. You manage to get your weapons online just as it opens fire.

          • 1–2 Monster crew boarders
          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Randomships ship
    • oTxtEZHugeDemonHulk_investigateC

      You board the ship and are able to secure some useful material.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
        oTxtEZHugeDemonHulk_investigateC2

        On your way back, one of your crewmen convulsing and laughing uncontrollably. They seem overtaken by a maniacal glee. You restrain them before they injure themselves.

        1. (Improved Auto-Surgeon) Treat the afflicted crew with the autosurgeon system
          oTxtEZHugeDemonHulk_investigateC_sysMed

          You treat the crewman with the autosurgeon. The advanced machinery is able to extract a writhing, wormlike creature from within the afflicted crewman's skull. Your crewman recovers from the ordeal and soon returns to duty.

          1. Continue
        2. (Imperial Psyker) Have your psyker treat the affliction
          oTxtEZHugeDemonHulk_investigateC_psykerImperial

          Your Imperial psyker determines the afflicted crewman to be under demonic possession. The psyker performs an exorcism upon the crewman over a period of hours and succeeds in banishing the malign influence. Your crewman returns to duty soon after.

          1. Continue
        3. Return to ship for medical treatment
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • oTxtEZHugeDemonHulk_investigateC4

            It turns out your crewman had acquired a neurological infection inside the hulk. Fortunately it is treatable. You administer the necessary medication to your crewman and move on.

            1. Continue
          • oTxtEZHugeDemonHulk_investigateC3

            Unfortunately you are too late. An infernal transformation consumes them, sharpening their teeth into fangs and swelling their muscles with diabolic strength. They break free from their restraints.

            • Lose a random crew member
            • 1 Demonic Crew boarder
            1. Continue
  2. Avoid it and move on
    oTxtEZHugeDemonHulk_avoid

    You decide to trust your instincts and avoid the ship.

    1. Continue

EZHugeDemonHulk_investigateC

→ oTxtEZHugeDemonHulk_investigateC_psykerImperial

EZHulkGlow

oTxtEZHulkGlow_intro

You discover the drifting hulk of a large starship. It looks as if it has been battered for decades by violent tides of subspace energy. A strange glow emanates from within the hulk.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtEZHulkGlow_investigateC

      You investigate the hulk but find nothing unusual. The strange glow seems to have mysteriously vanished.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZHulkGlow_investigateB

      You board the hulk and learn that the origin of the strange glow was simply the emergency lighting of a malfunctioning escape pod. A skeleton is visible inside the pod, its hands still clutching some valuables.

      • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZHulkGlow_investigateD

      As soon as you step foot onto the hulk, the sound of a great bell chimes. The air is suddenly filled with a hideous miasma and corpses begin to rise around you. A ghastly starship somehow grows out from one of the shattered engine pylons of the hulk. You retreat back to your ship and prepare to fight.

      • 3–4 Ghoul, Rotspawn, or Skeleton boarders
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Plague ship
    • oTxtEZHulkGlow_investigateA

      You search the hulk and determine the source of the glow: an ancient, ornate casket, likely of Machine Cult origin, hums with arcane power. You open the casket and discover an unusual relic.

      • Random cult equipment
      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtEZHulkGlow_investigateA2

          The casket suddenly starts to come alive with power. Suddenly, you find the vaulted interior of the hulk collapsing upon you. You retreat back to your ship, narrowly escape getting crushed.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtEZHulkGlow_investigateA1

          Bright webs of malefic lightning suddenly arc out from the casket, and within seconds you find yourself surrounded by a crowd of otherworldly creatures. You retreat back to your ship. Some of the creatures manage to clamber aboard before you get the hatches shut.

          • 1–2 warp breaches spawned
          • 2–3 Demonic Crew boarders
          1. Continue
  2. Ignore
    oTxtEZHulkGlow_ignore

    You ignore the hulk.

    1. Continue

EZIonOutpost

oTxtEZIonOutpost_intro

You arrive in the midst of a rift storm. An outpost looms out from the turbulent murk as ionic bursts crackle all around you, threatening to knock out key systems at an inopportune moment.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/5 each):
    • oTxtEZIonOutpost_investigateC

      You discover a small but thriving community within the battered walls of the outpost. "Living out here is rough, but the storms bring in enough to keep us going."

      • Proceeds to shop
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZIonOutpost_investigateB

      As you approach the outpost, its shape seems to change. Whether it was a trick of the light or a supernatural phenomenon, you soon find yourself under attack by a cultist starship.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Cult ship
    • oTxtEZIonOutpost_investigateD

      You hear the distinctive sound of an assault sled impacting your hull. The outpost has launched a boarding team to take over your ship. "Sorry to do this to you, but we have families to feed."

      • 2–3 Raider crew boarders
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZIonOutpost_investigateA

      The colony is of typical Imperial design. It is obvious from the battered cladding the outpost has endured the ravages of rift storms for many centuries, if not millennia. Though its occupants are long gone, you discover some signs of more recent occupancy. You help yourself to some supply caches left behind.

      • Random moderate reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZIonOutpost_investigateE

      Scans indicate the periodic ion bursts are somehow sustained by the outpost itself. Suddenly, you detect a rapidly approaching starship at extreme range. You have the opportunity to decide what to do about the outpost before the starship arrives.

      1. Destroy the outpost
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtEZIonOutpost_destroyOutpost1

          You scuttle the outpost. The ionic bursts rapidly dissipate along with the surrounding rift storm. Soon, the previously detected starship arrives. "Defiler! You have desecrated a sacred site."

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs War ship
        • oTxtEZIonOutpost_destroyOutpost2

          You scuttle the outpost. The ionic bursts dissipate along with the surrounding rift storm. The previously detected ship is nowhere to be found. It must have jumped out before arriving at the outpost.

          1. Continue
      2. Leave the outpost intact
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtEZIonOutpost_leaveOutpost1

          You engage the incoming ship from within the rift storm.

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs War ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • oTxtEZIonOutpost_leaveOutpost1A

            You explore the outpost. It is evidently long deserted, yet you find the internal systems still powered and functional. You search for the reactor but only discover a series of coils surrounding a pair of clawed gauntlets that are crackling with electrical energy. The outpost falls silent as you remove the claws and return with them to your ship.

            • Arc Talons
            1. Continue
          • oTxtEZIonOutpost_leaveOutpost1B

            The rift storm increases in intensity to the point of obscuring the outpost from all sensor contact. Suddenly, you find yourself in open space again, and the outpost is nowhere to be found.

            1. Continue
        • oTxtEZIonOutpost_leaveOutpost2

          The incoming ship is a Technocult artifact seeker.

          1. Continue
            Random outcome (1/2 each):
            • oTxtEZIonOutpost_leaveOutpost2B

              The Technocult vessel begins to attack you.

              1. Continue
              Fight! vs Techno ship
              After the rewards from the fight:
              oTxtEZIonOutpost_leaveOutpost2B_1

              The riftstorm suddenly increases in intensity. The outpost is torn apart. You move on.

              1. Continue
            • oTxtEZIonOutpost_leaveOutpost2A

              The machine priests are mounting an expedition to halt the local rift storm. The storm abates as they dismantle the complex machinery within the station, and they leave without acknowledging your presence.

              1. Continue
                oTxtEZIonOutpost_leaveOutpost2A_1

                You enter the outpost station and recover a sizable quantity of salvage.

                • Large amount of ⬗ Scrap
                1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtEZIonOutpost_leave

    You press on through the rift storm.

    1. Continue

EZIonOutpost_leaveOutpost2A

→ oTxtEZIonOutpost_leaveOutpost2A_1

EZIonOutpost_leaveOutpost2B

→ oTxtEZIonOutpost_leaveOutpost2B_1

EZLostMerchant

oTxtEZLostMerchant_intro

You arrive at a distress signal and find a drifting Imperial merchantman. "Oh thank the throne. We were on our way from Foucault Epsilon to Vandoris III when a subspace flare caught us. We have no idea where we are now... our stellar atlas has gone haywire."

  1. Transfer over your astrogation data
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtEZLostMerchant_helpA

      "...this places us in the Exclusion Zone. Well, we shall all be killed if the arbitrators ever caught wind of this. Let us do business while we still can. "

      • Proceeds to shop
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZLostMerchant_helpB

      The merchantman commander throws off his solicitude and reveals himself to be an Imperial arbitrator. "This will do. I know not whence you sourced this forbidden atlas, but we shall discover all once we place you upon the judgment spike." Naval batteries emerge from hidden compartments as the merchantman moves to engage you.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
    • oTxtEZLostMerchant_helpC

      The merchantman commander throws off his solicitude and reveals himself to be an Imperial arbitrator.

      1. Continue
        oTxtEZLostMerchant_helpC_a

        Suddenly, a band of corsair vessels dart out from a nearby rift storm. "You Imperials have no business here." The merchantman breaks off and speeds for the nearest riftstorm. The corsair band peels off in pursuit.

        1. Attempt to follow the corsairs
          Random outcome (1/3 each):
          • oTxtEZLostMerchant_helpC3

            You lose contact with the corsairs. Just as you are about to abandon the pursuit, you stumble across the wreckage of the Imperial merchantman. It is largely picked empty, but you still manage to secure some salvage.

            • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
            1. Continue
          • oTxtEZLostMerchant_helpC1

            Sensor contact with the corsairs is spotty within the riftstorm. You finally manage to catch up to find the corsair ships all arrayed in an odd circle. They melt into strange, puddle-like shapes, then disintegrate into dust. A maniacal laughter seems to fill your ears before you realize it to be the sound of the intruder alarm.

            • 2–3 Hound or Skeleton boarders
            1. Continue
          • oTxtEZLostMerchant_helpC2

            Sensor contact with the corsairs is spotty within the riftstorm, but you finally catch up to the corsairs. They are crowded around the tattered wreck of the Imperial merchantman. The corsair commodore hails you. "You again. This Imperial we captured insists he is on an important mission to stop a grave threat to the Empire, and I take it he is referring to you." He then unbuckles his sidearm from his belt. "I hope you will be able to put this to good use."

            • Goreblaster or Assault Pistol or Rotblaster or Machine Pistol
            1. Continue
        2. Leave
          oTxtEZLostMerchant_helpC4

          You decide to avoid further entanglements, but not before you scoop up some scrap left behind by the false merchantman.

          • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
          1. Continue
  2. Destroy them
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtEZLostMerchant_destroy2

      The merchantman unveils hidden armament ports. They are not as helpless as they appeared at first!

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
    • oTxtEZLostMerchant_destroy

      With nowhere to flee to, the merchantman makes for easy prey. You discover within the smoldering wreckage some valuable scrap.

      • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue

EZLostMerchant_helpC

→ oTxtEZLostMerchant_helpC_a

EZMutatedPatrol

oTxtEZMutatedPatrol_intro

Your sensors detect a ruined outpost deep within a rift storm. Surprisingly, it is home to a small but peaceful society. The outpost contacts you. "We have been recently attacked and need materials to repair our essential systems. Can you spare any?"

  1. Destroy the outpost
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtEZMutatedPatrol_attackWin

      You easily destroy the dilapidated outpost. You find some salvage in the wreckage.

      • Random large reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZMutatedPatrol_attackFail

      You obliterate the dilapidated outpost. The raging rift storm seems to have increased in intensity, and soon consumes the debris.

      1. Continue
  2. Give 10 scrap
    oTxtEZMutatedPatrol_donate

    The outpost leader is grateful for your assistance. "The Imperials attacked us, but thankfully they did not take everything we had." They send you some supplies in exchange.

    • Dark Matter Prism or Bloated Reagents or Necrotizing Reagents or Fiendish Reagents or Doom Reagents or Ruinous Reagents or Howling Reagents or Stygian Reagents or Hungering Reagents ×2
    • −10 ⬗ Scrap
    1. Continue
      oTxtEZMutatedPatrol_donate_b

      Suddenly your sensors alert you to an incoming contact. The outpost picks up the signal as well. "They have come back. You should get going while you still can."

      1. Stay
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtEZMutatedPatrol_fightIon

          The incoming ship broadcasts on widebeam: "More heretics? So be it. You will all burn together!" As the patrol ship initiates its attack, the rift storm surrounding you roils and develops into an ion storm.

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Empire ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          oTxtEZMutatedPatrol_fight_win

          Having observed the battle, the outpost hails you again. "Your valor is unmatched, but you really must leave. Go, before this accursed place changes you in the manner of those Imperials... and us."

          1. Continue
            oTxtEZMutatedPatrol_fight_win1

            The outpost ceases communications. You move on.

            1. Continue
        • oTxtEZMutatedPatrol_fightZombie

          The incoming ship broadcasts on widebeam: "Foulest of vipers, we shall not cease until your kind are cleansed from the realm. Immortal King, lend us your strength!" Your ship is suddenly surrounded by a putrid fog, and you find yourself beset upon by shambling horrors.

          • 3–6 Greater Corpulent or Ghoul Magus boarders
          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Empire ship
          After the rewards from the fight: → oTxtEZMutatedPatrol_fight_win
      2. Leave
        Random outcome (weighted):
        • 2/3
          oTxtEZMutatedPatrol_leaveA

          You act on the outpost leader's advice and slip away.

          1. Continue
        • 1/3
          oTxtEZMutatedPatrol_leaveB

          You attempt to escape. Somehow, the patrol ship is able to divine your exact location and intercepts you. "You cannot escape... the Immortal King sees all."

          • 4–5 Fleshling boarders
          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Empire ship
  3. (Machine Priest) Let your machine priest conduct the repairs.
    oTxtEZMutatedPatrol_repair

    Your machine priest deftly repairs the outpost using a minimum of the available materials. The outpost is grateful and offers you some of the bounty collected from the rift storms.

    • Random large reward
    1. Continue
  4. Leave

EZMutatedPatrol_donate

→ oTxtEZMutatedPatrol_donate_b

EZMutatedPatrol_fight

→ oTxtEZMutatedPatrol_fight_win

EZPlaguePursuit

oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_Evade

You seek to break contact using the rift storms. Unfortunately it seems the death cultists are channeling their Gorgothian rites through them, as you are suddenly beset upon by mortifying abominations.

  • 2–4 poison clouds spawned
  • 2–3 Bloat Mite or Hungerer boarders
  1. Continue
oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_Evade2

You attempt to break contact using the rift storms. You successfully escape the situation without incident.

  1. Continue
oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_FightCultistA

You attack the death cult vessel.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Plague ship
After the rewards from the fight:
Random outcome (1/2 each):
  • oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_FightCultistA2

    The raider vessel slows and hails you. "I thought nothing could be worse than them Imperial hunter-killers, but I was wrong. Thanks for saving us." Just as the raiders leave, an abnormally large subspace flare appears off their flank. In moments, it grows into a rift storm, and you see the raider ship shorn in half moments before being consumed by the rift altogether.

    1. Continue
  • oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_FightCultistA3

    The raider vessel hails you: "Aft fath get baph lebb." You are not sure what was said, but the raider vessel ejects some salvage out the airlock.

    • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
    1. Continue
oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_FightCultistB

The death cult vessel turns to meet your assault. Rift storms erupt around it, wreathing it in roiling tendrils. You hear a deep, guttural laughter echo throughout your ship.

  • 2–4 Greater Corpulent or Ghoul Magus boarders
  1. Continue
Fight! vs Plague ship
After the rewards from the fight:
Random outcome (1/2 each):
  • oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_FightCultistB2

    The raider vessel slows and hails you. "I thought nothing could be worse than them Imperial hunter-killers, but I was wrong. Thanks for saving us."

    1. Continue
  • oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_FightCultistB3

    The raider vessel hails you with a garbled message. "Viph dipph et onid laphleh..." Soon an escape pod from the raider ship is detected drifting towards you.

    1. Retrieve it
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_PodSave2

        The hatch opens and a highly necrotized crewman hobbles out and starts attacking.

        • 1 boarder (commander's crew type)
        1. Continue
      • oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_PodSave

        The hatch opens and a highly necrotized crewman hobbles out. Despite their appearance, they seem both intelligent and accepting of your command.

        • Recruit enemy commander as crew
        1. Continue
    2. Destroy it
      oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_PodKill

      The escape pod is atomized. You move on.

      1. Continue
oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_FightRaiderA

You intercept the raider ship. As you approach, the vessel is engulfed by a strange energy. You hear screams over the comms.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Raider ship
After the rewards from the fight:
oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_FightRaiderA2

The cultist vessel that was in pursuit passes into a rift storm and disappears.

  1. Continue
oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_FightRaiderB

Before you close to weapons range a rift storm erupts around the raider ship, engulfing it. The cultist vessel accelerates and disappears into the squall after it. The rift storm dissipates as quickly as it appeared, leaving you alone in space.

  1. Continue
oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_Intro Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Sensors detect a death cult vessel pursuing a raider ship. The raiders seem to be making a particular effort to avoid entering the nearby rift storms.

  1. Attack cultist vessel
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • → oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_FightCultistA
    • → oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_FightCultistB
  2. Attack raider ship
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • → oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_FightRaiderA
    • → oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_FightRaiderB
  3. Evade
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • → oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_Evade2
    • → oTxtEZPlaguePursuit_Evade

EZPossessedColony

oTxtEZPossessedColony_intro

You discover an ancient colony orbiting a barren planet.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/5 each):
    • oTxtEZPossessedColony_investigateB

      The colony likely predates the establishment of the Empire itself. You recover a weapon of unfamiliar styling from its armory hall.

      • Random cult weapon
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZPossessedColony_investigateE

      A cultist vessel wreathed in strangely colored energy emerges from subspace. It broadcasts an unintelligible screech before opening fire on you.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Possessed ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      Random outcome (1/3 each):
      • oTxtEZPossessedColony_investigateE_2

        You return to investigate the abandoned colony. The colonists apparently have degenerated to a hopelessly paleolithic existence. There is nothing of worth for you here.

        1. Continue
      • oTxtEZPossessedColony_investigateE_3

        The abandoned colony has vanished. A few scarlet traces of lighting lingering in space are all that remain.

        1. Continue
      • oTxtEZPossessedColony_investigateE_4

        Your ship is suddenly enveloped in a dense rift storm. Moments later, the storm dissipates. You find yourself in a different region of space.

        1. Continue
    • oTxtEZPossessedColony_investigateC

      You find evidence the colony is being used as a waystation by roaming corsairs. You take some of the cached supplies for yourself before leaving.

      • Random moderate reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZPossessedColony_investigateA

      The colony's orbit is in an advanced state of decay. There is little time for you to mount a salvage operation before it begins reentering the atmosphere. You retrieve a small amount of salvage and move on.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZPossessedColony_investigateD

      You discover the orbital colony is somehow still teeming with colonists. It is unclear how they survived this long, but the overpowering stench of rotting flesh accompanying them is not encouraging.

      1. Contact the colonists
        oTxtEZPossessedColony_investigateD2

        You make contact with the colonists. They usher you deeper into the colony before you are suddenly struck from behind. It appears you have been lured into a trap. You hear a great howling that seems to echo through the entire colony as its inhabitants surge towards you.

        1. Continue
          Random outcome (1/3 each):
          • oTxtEZPossessedColony_investigateD2_b

            You manage to retreat back to your ship, but not without sustaining injuries.

            • Commander damaged (50%)
            1. Continue
          • oTxtEZPossessedColony_investigateD2_a

            One of your crewmen is overwhelmed by assailants, but you manage to make it back to your ship.

            • Lose a random crew member
            1. Continue
          • oTxtEZPossessedColony_investigateD2_c

            You arrive back at your ship to the sight of more frenzied colonists waiting for you aboard your vessel.

            • 2–3 Corsair or Scavenger boarders
            1. Continue
      2. Leave
        oTxtEZPossessedColony_investigateD_leave

        You decide not to risk it and return to your vessel.

        1. Continue
  2. Move on
    oTxtEZPossessedColony_moveOn

    You move on.

    1. Continue

EZPossessedColony_investigateD

→ oTxtEZPossessedColony_investigateD_leave

EZPossessedColony_investigateD2

→ oTxtEZPossessedColony_investigateD2_a

EZPossessedColony_investigateE

oTxtEZPossessedColony_investigateE_1 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

In an apparent act of desperation, the crew destroys their oxygen system, decompressing their ship.

  • Enemy: destroy a system
  1. Continue

EZRaiderCargoFreighter

oTxtEZRaiderCargoFreighter_intro

A wrecked cargo freighter drifts across your viewport. Readings suggest that it must have been caught in a nearby riftstorm. You move in to look for salvage when another ship appears. "Back off, that cargo is mine."

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Raider ship
When enemy hull drops below 30%:
oTxtEZRaiderCargoFreighter_surrender

"Ok, ok, please don't kill me. Here, why don't we split the cargo? Fifty-fifty sound good?"

  1. Agree to fifty-fifty
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtEZRaiderCargoFreighter_fiftyB

      "Great. I knew you would be reasonable. I will be going now." The pirate takes his portion of the cargo and jumps out.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZRaiderCargoFreighter_fiftyC

      "Wonderful. Now... just give me a few minutes to set up my cargo scoop... " Suddenly another raider ship emerges and starts firing!

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtEZRaiderCargoFreighter_fiftyC2

      Unfortunately the other corsair made off with the cargo while you were occupied.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZRaiderCargoFreighter_fiftyA

      "Perfect. Now let's-" He screams as a hideous creature falls onto him and slashes his throat. The nearby riftstorm flares up and expands. Monsters begin materializing on your deck.

      • 2–4 Demonic Crew boarders
      • Enemy ship destroyed
      1. Continue
      After the boarders are defeated:
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtEZRaiderCargoFreighter_fiftyA2

        The cargo freighter tumbles into the riftstorm and is lost forever.

        1. Continue
      • oTxtEZRaiderCargoFreighter_fiftyA3

        The riftstorm subsides. You recover what you can from the aftermath.

        • Random small reward
        1. Continue
  2. Tell them you want eighty-twenty
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtEZRaiderCargoFreighter_eightyB

      "Not gonna work for me, nope. How about... " The corsair's response is cut off as a nearby riftstorm flares up, sending a gout of subspace energy across his ship and tearing it apart. He does not even have a chance to scream.

      • Enemy ship destroyed
      1. Continue
      After the rewards from the fight:
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtEZRaiderCargoFreighter_eightyB2

        You recover the cargo from the wrecked freighter

        • Random moderate reward
        1. Continue
      • oTxtEZRaiderCargoFreighter_eightyB3

        You investigate the cargo containers and find nothing inside them but human bones.

        1. Continue
    • oTxtEZRaiderCargoFreighter_eightyA

      "Ehhhhh... I don't know about that... "

      1. Fire a warning shot
        oTxtEZRaiderCargoFreighter_eightyA2

        "Ok, ok. Eighty percent for you. Good enough for me. Just let me live." You let the corsair go. Then you retrieve your portion of the cargo.

        • Random small reward
        1. Continue
  3. Tell them you want all of it
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtEZRaiderCargoFreighter_allA

      "No way, the boss will kill me if I come back empty-handed. If I don't... " The pirate's response gets cut off as a new vessel appears on your sensors and starts to open fire.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs random faction ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtEZRaiderCargoFreighter_allA1

        You return to the wrecked freighter only to find it stripped of all valuable resources. The pirate from earlier is nowhere to be found.

        1. Continue
      • oTxtEZRaiderCargoFreighter_allA2

        You move back to the cargo freighter and recover what you can.

        • Random moderate reward
        1. Continue
    • oTxtEZRaiderCargoFreighter_allB

      The corsair complains loudly and argues with you for what seems like forever. Unfortunately the nearby riftstorm expands during this time and swallows up both the corsair and the freighter.

      1. Continue
  4. Finish them off
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtEZRaiderCargoFreighter_finishA

      You recover the cargo from the freighter.

      • Random moderate reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZRaiderCargoFreighter_finishB

      Unfortunately the cargo freighter drifts into the nearby rift storm and is lost forever.

      1. Continue

EZTechnoFlux

oTxtEZTechnoFlux_intro

You are caught in a riftstorm, struggling to maintain course, when a technocult ship appears. "Our flux capacitors are damaged beyond repair. Without them, the relics aboard our ship will never make it back to the Enclaves."

  1. Offer them your spare flux capacitors
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtEZTechnoFlux_spareFluxC

      The technopriests accept your flux capacitors. They send you some metal in thanks.

      • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
      • Enemy becomes friendly
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZTechnoFlux_spareFluxA

      The machine priests accept your flux capacitors, and request more. You tell them that you gave them all the capacitors you can spare. They grow impatient and simply begin attacking your ship. "There is no time for negotiations. The relics must survive."

      1. Continue
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtEZTechnoFlux_spareFluxA2

      You recover some unusual equipment from the wreckage.

      • Random ship module
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEZTechnoFlux_spareFluxB

      The machine priests are unimpressed with your offering. "We require all of the flux capacitors aboard your ship. Any less and our ship will not be able to make the journey. Without flux capacitors, you will be stranded. But know this: your sacrifice will serve the divine God-Machine in ways that you cannot imagine. There is no death more worthy for a fleshbeing such as yourself."

      1. Give them all of your flux capacitors
        oTxtEZTechnoFlux_giveFlux

        You agree to sacrifice yourself and give them all of your flux capacitors. Before they leave, one of the machine priests gives you a small token. "This data-key was passed on to me by one of the original elders of the Archimite Tribunal. Perhaps it will help you somehow."

        1. Continue
          Random outcome (1/4 each):

          • Your ship drifts in the riftstorm for several weeks.

          • Your ship drifts in the riftstorm for several weeks, suffering minor damage.

            • 2–3 breaches spawned

          • Your ship drifts in the riftstorm for several weeks, suffering moderate damage.

            • −1 hull HP

          • Your ship drifts in the riftstorm for several weeks, suffering moderate damage.

            • −2 hull HP
          In all cases:
          oTxtEZTechnoFlux_consider

          You consider your options.

          1. Scan for signals
            Random outcome (1/2 each):
            • oTxtEZTechnoFlux_scan

              You pick up an encrypted signal. Using the data-key given to you by the machine priest, you decrypt the signal and find out that it contains coordinates to a nearby location. You arrive at the location and discover a trove of supplies, weapons, and equipment, including plenty of flux capacitors. Once you finish refitting your ship, you continue on with your journey.

              • Random large reward
              • Random ship weapon
              • +3–6 hull HP
              1. Continue
            • oTxtEZTechnoFlux_scan2

              You pick up a weak signal containing garbled messages. You follow the signal to its source and discover the remains of a long-dead Imperial battleship. Its distress signal must have been looping for decades. Inside the battleship you manage to locate some working flux capacitors. You haul them back to your ship and commence repairs. With the repairs complete, you continue on with your journey.

              1. Continue
          2. Drop a distress beacon
            oTxtEZTechnoFlux_beacon

            Your distress beacon attracts a hostile vessel. "Look, we have been stranded out here for weeks. Why do you not make this easy and power down your weapons?"

            1. Continue
            Fight! vs random faction ship
            After the rewards from the fight:
            oTxtEZTechnoFlux_beacon2

            You recover a few intact flux capacitors from the wreckage and manage to refit your ship. Your journey continues.

            1. Continue
          3. Investigate the area
            Random outcome (1/3 each):
            • oTxtEZTechnoFlux_investigateC

              You maneuver between a series of riftstorms and suddenly find yourself under attack!

              1. Continue
              Fight! vs random faction ship
              After the rewards from the fight:
              oTxtEZTechnoFlux_investigateC2

              You find an additional vault in the remains. It contains some metal and a few working flux capacitors. You use them to refit your ship and continue onward with your journey.

              • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
              1. Continue
            • oTxtEZTechnoFlux_investigateB

              Miraculously, you chance upon a supply container full of flux capacitors and are able to replace the ones you gave away. Despite the terrible condition of your hull, your journey is not over. You recover what you can and proceed onwards.

              • Random small reward
              1. Continue
            • oTxtEZTechnoFlux_investigateA

              You find a derelict shipyard. Most of the supplies have been stripped bare, however there is a single locked vault that appears fully intact. Unfortunately its armor plating makes it impervious to all weapon fire.

              1. Continue
                oTxtEZTechnoFlux_investigateA2

                You remember the data-key given to you by the machine priest and miraculously, it unlocks the vault. Inside you find plenty of supplies, weapons, and equipment, including a handful of working flux capacitors. You refit your ship and continue on with your journey.

                • Random moderate reward
                • Random equipment item
                1. Continue
      2. Refuse
        oTxtEZTechnoFlux_refuseFlux

        "We will have those flux capacitors whether you like it or not."

        1. Continue
  2. Attack them
    oTxtEZTechnoFlux_attack1

    You target lock their ship.

    1. Continue
    After the rewards from the fight:
    oTxtEZTechnoFlux_attack2

    You recover some unusual equipment from the wreckage.

    • Random cult consumable
    1. Continue
  3. Ignore them and leave
    oTxtEZTechnoFlux_refuseHelp

    You have no wish to help the machine cultists. Leaving their stranded ship to the mercy of the riftstorm, you move on.

    • Enemy becomes friendly
    1. Continue
  4. (Lose 1 Flux-Priest) Transfer a specialist to their command
    oTxtEZTechnoFlux_bt_fluxPriest1

    The technopriests quickly accept the transfer of your flux-priest. "This arrangement is optimal. We will be able to deliver our relics on schedule."

    • Lose crew: Flux-Priest
    • Combat ends
    1. Continue
      oTxtEZTechnoFlux_bt_fluxPriest2

      The machine priests estimate you to share common interests and issue you one of their excavators.

      • Servo-Excavator
      1. Continue
oTxtEZTechnoFlux_refuseHelp2 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You recover some unusual equipment from the wreckage.

  • 60%: Random cult equipment / 40%: Random equipment item
  1. Continue

EZTechnoFlux_bt

→ oTxtEZTechnoFlux_bt_fluxPriest1

EZWarDefector

oTxtEZWarDefector_intro

A damaged Kromic interceptor drifts out from one of the riftstorms scattered throughout the area. "Attack... Kill..." The voice coming through the comms is barely a croak. There is an ominous aura about it.

  1. Attack
    oTxtEZWarDefector_attack

    You move to engage the vessel.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs specific room ship
  2. Assist them
    oTxtEZWarDefector_assist

    Scans reveal a single occupant in poor health. You bring the wounded crewman aboard for treatment. Moments later, a Kromic warship appears, seemingly out of nowhere.

    1. Continue
      oTxtEZWarDefector_assist_b

      "The deserter cowers aboard your ship. Hand him over, for he does not deserve the honor of perishing in battle."

      1. Attack the warship
        oTxtEZWarDefector_fightA

        "You would fight in his stead? So much the better."

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs War ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtEZWarDefector_fightA2

          The wounded crewman has transformed into a shambling corpse. However, he seems to have retained his mental faculties, and offers to join your crew.

          • Greater Corpulent
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEZWarDefector_fightA3

          The wounded crewman makes a full recovery. Not having anywhere to go, he offers to join your crew.

          • Berserker
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEZWarDefector_fightA4

          The wounded crewman succumbed to his wounds during the engagement.

          1. Continue
      2. Offer up the deserter.
        oTxtEZWarDefector_fightB

        At this moment the wounded crewman convulses violently, then explodes in a shower of gore. A number of putrid monstrosities appear in the wake of the crewman's gruesome death.

        • 2–3 Bloat Mite or Hungerer boarders
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Plague ship
      3. (Death Psyker) Cure and defend the deserter
        oTxtEZWarDefector_bt_plaguePsyker1

        One of your crew performs healing rites upon the wounded defector. You inform the commander of the Kromic warship of your decision to shelter the deserter.

        1. Continue
          oTxtEZWarDefector_bt_plaguePsyker2

          The commander of the Kromic warship is incensed by your refusal to comply and attacks. "Wretch, if you seek to deny us justice, then I shall become the instrument of our lord's wrath."

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs War ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          oTxtEZWarDefector_bt_plaguePsyker3

          As you conclude your salvage operations, you witness the defector shuffling about the deck. Changed by their ordeal, they are nevertheless grateful for your efforts, and pledge allegiance to your command.

          • Random War Cult weapon
          • Greater Corpulent
          1. Continue
  3. Escape into a rift storm
    oTxtEZWarDefector_introLeave

    You dive into a rift storm. You nearly collide with a second Kromic warship. It promptly opens fire.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs War ship
oTxtEZWarDefector_introLeaveB Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You dive into a nearby rift storm. The damaged warship does not follow.

  1. Continue
oTxtEZWarDefector_turnIn Unreachable — not referenced by any event

The Kromic cultists proclaim that the wounded crewman will serve as a training target for eternity before disappearing into one of the rift storms. You salvage the damaged Kromic Interceptor before leaving.

  • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
  1. Continue

EZWarDefector_assist

→ oTxtEZWarDefector_assist_b

EZWarDefector_bt

→ oTxtEZWarDefector_bt_plaguePsyker1

EZWarPlanetoid

oTxtEZWarPlanetoid_intro

You arrive near a planetoid orbiting the outskirts of a large riftstorm.

  1. Investigate the planetoid
    oTxtEZWarPlanetoid_investigate

    You descend into the planetoid's atmosphere and glide over an endless rocky plain. Thousands of infernal creatures are visible below. They seem to be fighting each other, as if engaged in some sort of massive, planetary war. A voice sounds from your console.

    1. Continue
      oTxtEZWarPlanetoid_investigate2

      "I am the master of this world, appointed by the Lord of Hatred himself. Serve me by annihilating the devious servants of the Blood Prince, and I shall bestow upon you the gifts of wrath."

      1. Destroy the servants of the Blood Prince
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtEZWarPlanetoid_destroyA

          You bombard the planet from orbit, vaporizing thousands upon thousands of infernal creatures. After several days of non-stop siege, the blood creatures have all been pounded into dust. The demonic master of this world is pleased with your service and presents you with an infernal relic.

          • Random War Cult equipment
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEZWarPlanetoid_destroyB

          You unleash your ship's weapons at the creatures, disintegrating them from orbit. Within hours, the majority of the blood demons have been vaporized.

          1. Continue
            oTxtEZWarPlanetoid_destroyB1

            Suddenly your ship begins taking fire from an enemy craft.

            1. Continue
            Fight! vs Blood ship
            After the rewards from the fight:
            oTxtEZWarPlanetoid_destroyB2

            The forces of Krom have prevailed. Deprived of worthy opponents, the demons of the war god can no longer maintain their corporeal presence in realspace. One by one, they begin to vanish, and soon the entire planetoid falls silent. Later you discover a burnt relic on your deck, a gift left behind by the servants of Krom.

            • Fell Axe
            1. Continue
      2. Decline
        oTxtEZWarPlanetoid_decline

        You decline the demon's offer.

        1. Continue
      3. (Lose 1 War Cultist) Commit a cultist to the cause
        oTxtEZWarPlanetoid_bt_warCultist1

        You dispatch a crew member to marshal the demonic hordes of Krom against their hated enemies. Free to exult in worship of the Lord of War, your crew member personally massacres countless numbers of Ariokine demons. Invigorated by your crewmember's fierce fervor, the demons of Krom grow in strength, finally breaking the long stalemate that had befallen the conflict. Unfortunately, their luck would run out. Your crew member eventually perishes in the course of the intense and ceaseless campaign. The Kromic horde subsequently experiences a series of crushing defeats.

        1. Continue
          oTxtEZWarPlanetoid_bt_warCultist2

          The campaign against the forces of the blood prince unravels, until suddenly a mysterious figure emerges amongst the Kromic horde. The allied demons rally and put down the opposition once and for all.

          1. Continue
            oTxtEZWarPlanetoid_bt_warCultist3

            The mysterious figure reappears aboard your vessel. You sense a familiarity in the towering figure, and recognize your lost crewmember. Miraculously, they have returned from the dead, albeit changed. You hear a voice ring throughout your bridge. "The lord of war expects great things from you."

            • Random War Cult weapon
            • Chaos Knight
            1. Continue
  2. Move on
    oTxtEZWarPlanetoid_moveOn

    You move on.

    1. Continue
oTxtEZWarPlanetoid_destroyC Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You open fire at the blood creatures from orbit. Moments later, an Imperial crusade vessel drops in, its commander crying out over the comms, "Exterminate them!"

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Empire ship
After the fight:
oTxtEZWarPlanetoid_destroyC2

The Imperial vessel has been dispatched, but you detect an outgoing distress signal requesting fleet assistance sent shortly before its demise. You salvage what you can and move on.

  • Random small reward
  • Crew recalled to ship
  1. Continue
oTxtEZWarPlanetoid_investigate3 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Destroy the servants of the blood prince

  1. Continue
oTxtEZWarPlanetoid_investigate4 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Decline

  1. Continue

EZWarPlanetoid_bt

→ oTxtEZWarPlanetoid_bt_warCultist1

EZcivilianPossessed_bt

→ oTxtEZcivilianPossessed_bt_medBay1

EliteGeneric

oTxtEliteGeneric Unreachable — not referenced by any event

An elite enemy ship appears on your viewscreen. You ready your armaments.

EmpNothing

oTxtEmpNothing_intro

[...]

  1. Continue

EmpireAsteroidSupply

oTxtEmpireAsteroidSupply_intro

Streaks of energy fly past your ship. An Imperial vessel bears down on you, weapons locked. Sensors indicate the presence of a nearby asteroid field.

  1. Attempt to lose your attacker in the asteroid field
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtEmpireAsteroidSupply_evadeB

      You apply maximum thrust and speed into the asteroid field. The Imperial ship tracks you for almost two hours, but eventually gives up.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireAsteroidSupply_evadeC

      You set a course for the asteroid field but your attacker is undeterred. The enemy ship rapidly closes the distance and forces you to engage.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtEmpireAsteroidSupply_evadeC_2

      With the enemy neutralized, you spend a few moments surveying your surroundings. An hidden supply depot sits undefended on one of the larger asteroids. You approach the depot and help yourself to its contents.

      • Random moderate reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireAsteroidSupply_evadeA

      You maneuver your ship through the densest cluster of asteroids. Your pursuer does not follow. Impacts with the asteroids have caused some minor damage.

      • −1 hull HP
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireAsteroidSupply_evadeD

      You set thrusters to full burn and move into the asteroid field with the Imperial ship in hot pursuit. Despite colliding with a few sizable rocks, your attacker refuses to let go. Soon you are forced to engage.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
  2. Stand and fight
    oTxtEmpireAsteroidSupply_fightIntro

    You prepare to engage the incoming Imperial vessel.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Empire ship
    After the rewards from the fight:
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtEmpireAsteroidSupply_fightA

      After dealing with the ship, you take a moment to sweep your sensors across the nearby asteroid field. The readings suggest the presence of some sort of artificial structure.

      • Crew recalled to ship
      1. Investigate the structure
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtEmpireAsteroidSupply_investigateB

          As soon as you make visual contact, another Imperial vessel appears on your flank and opens fire.

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Empire ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          oTxtEmpireAsteroidSupply_investigateB_2

          No hostile signatures remain. The structure you detected turns out to be a covert Imperial shipyard, which is now undefended and under your control.

          1. Continue
            oTxtShipyard

            An abandoned shipyard floats into view. It appears to be a safe place to rest.

        • oTxtEmpireAsteroidSupply_investigateA

          You approach the structure and discover that it is a long-abandoned storage depot, perhaps several centuries old, likely built by early Imperial expeditions. Not much can be recovered, but some of its stores are still useful.

          • Random small reward
          1. Continue
      2. Ignore it and move on
        oTxtEmpireAsteroidSupply_moveOn

        You decide to move on.

        1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireAsteroidSupply_fightB

      Readings on your sensors show that the Imperial ship must have emerged from one of the larger rocks on the perimeter of the asteroid field. You take a closer look and discover an undefended supply cache.

      • Random moderate reward
      1. Continue

EmpireAsteroidSupply_evadeC

→ oTxtEmpireAsteroidSupply_evadeC_2

EmpireAsteroidSupply_investigateB

→ oTxtEmpireAsteroidSupply_investigateB_2

EmpireAttackBloodCult

oTxtEmpireAttackBloodCult_intro

You encounter a blood cult ship under attack by an Imperial patrol vessel.

  1. Attack the Imperial ship
    oTxtEmpireAttackBloodCult_attack

    You power your weapons and engage the Imperial ship in battle.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Empire ship
    After the rewards from the fight:
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtEmpireAttackBloodCult_winA

      The cultists express their gratitude and give you some of their supplies before moving on.

      • Random Blood Cult consumable
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireAttackBloodCult_winB

      The cultists curtly acknowledge your help before jumping out.

      1. Continue
  2. Leave the blood cultists to their fate
    oTxtEmpireAttackBloodCult_leave

    You decide to move on, leaving the blood cultists to fend for themselves against the Imperials.

    1. Continue
  3. (Hull Reaper) Attack the Imperial ship
    oTxtEmpireAttackBloodCult_bt_hullReaper1

    You prime your hull reaper system and engage the Imperial aggressor. Your vessel's hull is regenerated as the Imperial ship crumbles beneath the assault.

    • +5 hull HP
    1. Continue
      oTxtEmpireAttackBloodCult_bt_hullReaper2

      The blood cultists rejoice at your timely intervention. They utter prayers of gratitude to their deity before continuing on their way. You salvage the remnants of the destroyed Imperial ship and move on.

      • Random moderate reward
      1. Continue

EmpireAttackBloodCult_bt

→ oTxtEmpireAttackBloodCult_bt_hullReaper1

EmpireAttackDeathCult

oTxtEmpireAttackDeathCult_intro

You arrive at the jump node to see a death cult ship under attack by an Imperial patrol vessel.

  1. Attack the Imperial starship
    oTxtEmpireAttackDeathCult_attack

    You engage the Imperial starship. They turn from their quarry to meet your attack.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Empire ship
    After the rewards from the fight:
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtEmpireAttackDeathCult_winA

      The death cult starship expresses their gratitude and offers to share some of their forbidden knowledge with you before you move on.

      • Random Death Cult psychic item
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireAttackDeathCult_winB

      The death cultists laconically thank you for your aid before departing.

      1. Continue
  2. Leave the death cultists to their fate
    oTxtEmpireAttackDeathCult_leave

    You decide against intervening on behalf of the cultists and move on.

    1. Continue
  3. (Hull Reaper) Attack the Imperial ship
    oTxtEmpireAttackDeathCult_bt_hullReaper1

    You prime your hull reaper system and engage the Imperial aggressor. Your vessel's hull is regenerated as the Imperial ship crumbles beneath the assault.

    • +5 hull HP
    1. Continue
      oTxtEmpireAttackDeathCult_bt_hullReaper2

      The death cultists hurriedly move onwards. You are left alone to salvage the remnants of the destroyed Imperial ship.

      • Random moderate reward
      1. Continue

EmpireAttackDeathCult_bt

→ oTxtEmpireAttackDeathCult_bt_hullReaper1

EmpireAttackRaider

oTxtEmpireAttackRaider_intro

You encounter a raider vessel under attack by an Imperial patrol ship.

  1. Attack the Imperial ship
    oTxtEmpireAttackRaider_attack

    You power your weapons and move to engage the Imperial ship.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Empire ship
    After the rewards from the fight:
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtEmpireAttackRaider_winA

      The raiders the Imperials were after are nowhere to be found. They must have taken the opportunity to escape during your engagement.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireAttackRaider_winB

      You discover the raider vessel had been destroyed by a stray shot during your engagement with the Imperial ship. You salvage what you can from the wreckage before moving on.

      • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtEmpireAttackRaider_leave

    You see no reason to risk your safety for the sake of some corsairs. You move on.

    1. Continue
  3. (Hull Reaper) Attack the Imperial ship
