  • −1 hull HP
  1. Continue

EmpireCultSolarBattle

oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_intro

Fire engulfs your viewport. You seem to have stumbled into the midst of a major Imperial fleet action against the Infernal Cults. Worse still, proximity alerts indicate that you are dangerously close to the local star.

  1. Continue
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_intro1

      You barely have a chance to react before you are pulled into battle.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs random faction ship
      After the fight:
      oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFight

      You retrieve what you can and return to your ship.

      • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
      • Crew recalled to ship
      1. Continue
        oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFightB

        The battle continues.

        1. Stay
          Random outcome (1/4 each):
          • oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_stayC

            A damaged Imperial vessel speeds into your path.

            1. Continue
            Fight! vs Empire ship
            After the fight:
            Random outcome (1/3 each):
            • oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFight2_A

              The battle winds down. You hear an open broadcast: "To all faithful servants of the Throne: the enemy generals have been destroyed, their fleets scattered. Victory is at hand. Purge any who do not bear the Imperial insignia."

              • Crew recalled to ship
              1. Continue
                oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFight2_A1

                The situation becomes too dangerous. You are forced out of the fight.

                1. Continue
            • oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFight2_B

              The conflagration of battle begins to subside. Burning wrecks of both sides litter the void. You scavenge what you can and hastily make your escape.

              • Random small reward
              • Crew recalled to ship
              1. Continue
            • oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFight2_C

              More cult vessels drop in and soon the Imperial forces are overrun. You hear rasping over the comms: "Krom! Gorgoth! Ariok! We beseech you, grant us your favor!" Malevolent energies explode outward from the dead husks of starships. Thousands of sorcerous rifts tear open across the void, several of which appear inside your ship. You decide to retreat before things get out of hand.

              • Crew recalled to ship
              • 1–2 warp breaches spawned
              • 1–2 Demonic Crew boarders
              1. Continue
          • oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_stayD

            Several Imperial battle sorcerers unleash a barrage of fireballs at the cult starship on your flank. The cult ship turns toward you and opens fire, apparently believing you the attacker.

            1. Continue
            Fight! vs Cult ship
            After the fight: → oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFight2_C
          • oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_stayA

            You watch as an Imperial warship is torn apart by multiple aggressors. It unleashes a flurry of assault pods before it dies, some of which impact your ship. As you prepare to repel boarders, a cult vessel appears on your flank and begins firing at you.

            • 1–2 Imperial Recruit, Imperial Enforcer, Imperial Marshal, Imperial Scribe, or Imperial Acolyte boarders
            1. Continue
            Fight! vs Cult ship
            After the fight:
            Random outcome (1/3 each):
            • → oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFight2_A
            • → oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFight2_B
            • → oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFight2_C
          • oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_stayB

            A nearby Kromic warship presents itself as a target. Sensors show signs that they are dealing with Imperial boarders.

            1. Continue
              oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_stayB_1

              Several Imperial assault pods smash into the hull of the Kromic warship, depositing a group of boarders who join the fight.

              1. Continue
              After the fight:
              Random outcome (1/3 each):
              • → oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFight2_A
              • → oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFight2_B
              • → oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFight2_C
            After the fight:
            Random outcome (1/3 each):
            • → oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFight2_A
            • → oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFight2_B
            • → oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFight2_C
        2. Flee
          Random outcome (1/3 each):
          • oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_fleeA

            You perform a tactical retreat and leave the battle behind you.

            1. Continue
          • oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_fleeB

            You command full-burn to drives and get out of there. A few stray incendiary rounds impact your hull as you retreat.

            • −1 hull HP
            • 2 fires spawned
            1. Continue
          • oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_fleeC

            You prepare to retreat, but something is wrong with your drives. It is as if their mechanisms are being held in place by an invisible force. A cult warship floats into view and you suddenly hear a disturbingly gentle voice through your console. "Leaving so soon, little one?"

            1. Continue
            Fight! vs Blood ship
            After the fight:
            oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_fleeC_win

            You make another attempt to escape. This time you succeed and soon the battle is far behind you.

            • Crew recalled to ship
            1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_intro4

      A twisted cult starship rises out from the corpse of its last victim. It turns toward you and starts shooting.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Blood ship
      After the rewards from the fight: → oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFight
    • oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_intro2

      An Imperial ship is stricken by a blast of obscene magic. It attempts to retreat from the battle but instead plunges headlong into your path.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the fight: → oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFight
    • oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_intro3

      Suddenly a cloud of foul sorcery sweeps across your command deck. A twisted abomination of a spacecraft lumbers into view. "R'lhgah, srgothhh, fghath... NGL'YEH!"

      • 1–3 Bloat Mite or Hungerer boarders
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Plague ship
      After the rewards from the fight: → oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFight

EmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFight2

→ oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_afterFight2_A

EmpireCultSolarBattle_fleeC

→ oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_fleeC_win

EmpireCultSolarBattle_stayB

→ oTxtEmpireCultSolarBattle_stayB_1

EmpireForlornShrine

oTxtEmpireForlornShrine_intro

You come across a citadel shrine. These sanctuaries are integral parts of Imperial worship.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtEmpireForlornShrine_investigateD

      The sanctuary has been abandoned for some time. You search the shrine's library and recover an intact psychogram.

      • 60%: Random Imperial psychic item / 40%: Random psychic item
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireForlornShrine_investigateB

      An Imperial patrol ship closes in. "Halt, vandal. An unregistered vessel has no business lurking near our hallowed shrines." It begins firing at you.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
    • oTxtEmpireForlornShrine_investigateC

      The shrine has not seen maintenance in some time. You approach an elaborate reliquary displayed beneath a large, winged statue. Suddenly, the statue comes to life and swings its sword at you. You retreat to your ship, but not without having suffered some injuries.

      • Commander damaged (30%)
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireForlornShrine_investigateA

      The shrine seems to be abandoned, though the well-worn surfaces throughout the complex describe a more prosperous past. At length, you encounter an elderly curate. Upon learning of your travels, he presents you with some gifts. He prays for your safety before ritually blessing you in the name of the Immortal King.

      • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtEmpireForlornShrine_leave

    You decide to move on.

    1. Continue
  3. (High Magister) Task your crewmember to inspect the shrine
    oTxtEmpireForlornShrine_highMagister

    You dock with the shrine and watch as the high magister strides into the inner sanctum. It appears your crewmember remembers well the sacred rites of the Imperial faith. Having performed a long series of rituals, they return to your vessel bearing gifts of the holy order.

    • Random Imperial anti-demon weapon
    • Random Imperial anti-demon weapon
    1. Continue

EmpireInfightAftermath

oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_intro

You come across the wrecks of several Imperial ships at the edge of an asteroid belt.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_investigateD

      Another Imperial warship emerges from behind an asteroid. "If you were looking for these traitors, I will gladly send you to meet them." They promptly fire upon you.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_investigateD2

        An Imperial reinforcement fleet arrives nearby. The warship must have called in reinforcements before its destruction. You escape into the asteroid field before you are surrounded.

        • Crew recalled to ship
        1. Continue
      • oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_investigateD3

        An Imperial reinforcement fleet arrives nearby. The warship must have called in reinforcements before its destruction. You utilize the asteroid field to break out of the encirclement, but unfortunately sustain some hull damage in the process.

        • Crew recalled to ship
        • −1–2 hull HP
        1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_investigateC

      You discover these Imperial ships were destroyed while trying to track down an extremist faction within the Empire.

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/7 each):
        • oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice2

          You salvage the wreck and move on.

          • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice3

          You discover a weapon near the body of a dead crewman.

          • 60%: Random Imperial weapon / 40%: Random weapon item
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice6

          There is nothing else of value in the wreckage. You move on.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice1

          You salvage some supplies left among the wreckage and move on.

          • Random small reward
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice7

          You find a number of intact ordnance warheads and bring them back to your ship.

          • choose(4, 7, 10) missiles
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice4

          You are able to recover an intact psychogram wafer from one of the dead crewmen.

          • 60%: Random Imperial psychic item / 40%: Random psychic item
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice5

          The wrecks have been largely stripped clean, but you manage to find a few scraps.

          • Random small reward
          1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_investigateB

      A cursory review through the databanks aboard the wrecked ships reveals them to have belonged to a renegade faction within the Empire.

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/7 each):
        • → oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice2
        • → oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice3
        • → oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice6
        • → oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice1
        • → oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice7
        • → oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice4
        • → oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice5
    • oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_investigateA

      A cursory review through the databanks aboard the wrecked ships reveals them to have belonged to a renegade faction within the Empire. They were censured for attempting to produce an heir to the Auric Throne.

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/7 each):
        • → oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice2
        • → oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice3
        • → oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice6
        • → oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice1
        • → oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice7
        • → oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice4
        • → oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_afterChoice5
  2. Leave
    oTxtEmpireInfightAftermath_leave

    You pass through the asteroid field and press on.

    1. Continue

EmpireInsurrection

oTxtEmpireInsurrection_intro

Arriving at the system you receive an emergency message running on all frequencies. The head-priest of an Imperial shrine outpost is hiring mercenaries to cauterize a cult insurrection at a nearby colony habitat. The dark cults and their corruption are insidious, so crushing them must be done with extreme prejudice.

  1. Accept the task
    oTxtEmpireInsurrection_accept

    The heretics must die.

    • Quest marker added to map: oTxtEmpireInsurrectionQuest_intro
    1. Continue
  2. Decline
    oTxtEmpireInsurrection_decline

    You decline the offer and move on.

    1. Continue

EmpireInsurrectionQuest

oTxtEmpireInsurrectionQuest_intro

As you enter the system you immediately notice a battle raging around a large, segmented space station. Gleaming, pulsating flesh covers some of its compartments, and red tendrils constantly grow and slither across the habitat's hull. A blockade of Imperial ships surround the station: some are cauterizing and excising corrupted areas while others battle small Ariokine vessels.

  1. Continue
    oTxtEmpireInsurrectionQuest_approach

    An Imperial captain contacts you with orders. You are to stay with the blockade, attacking any ships that attempt to break free. As you take your place, you see an enemy ship navigating the battlefield, attempting to escape. You set your course to attack.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Blood ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtEmpireInsurrectionQuest_approach_win

    The enemy ship falls, but the battle rages on.

    • Crew recalled to ship
    1. Continue
      oTxtEmpireInsurrectionQuest_approach_win2

      The Imperial attack force has burnt a large amount of the corruption away with Hyperion's radiant wrath. Another ship breaks free from the massive space habitat. You could let the Imperials deal with it or attack the vessel yourself for loot and a potentially greater reward.

      1. Attack the second ship
        oTxtEmpireInsurrectionQuest_battle2

        You chase after the Ariokine ship and engage in battle.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Blood ship
        After the fight:
        oTxtEmpireInsurrectionQuest_battle2_win

        The ship breaks apart in the chaos of battle, and you scavenge some loot.

        • Random moderate reward
        • Fleshy Reagents ×2
        1. Continue
          oTxtEmpireInsurrectionQuest_battle2_win2

          The battle comes to a gradual end as the insurrectionist offense is broken. The Imperial habitat looks like a wounded beast: large cauterised wounds cover the hull as the cancerous Ariokine influence is burnt away. The captain thanks you for your assistance. Afterwards, a shuttle arrives from the Imperial fleet with a quantity of supplies.

          • Large amount of ⬗ Scrap
          • Moderate amount of missile ammo (5–8)
          • Retributor II
          1. Continue
      2. Leave this battle for the Imperials
        oTxtEmpireInsurrectionQuest_restraint

        You remain in the blockade and no other enemy ships come your way. The battle ends slowly. The enemies are beaten back but the damage sustained by the habitat is extreme: cauterized scars, burnt sections and compartments completely amputated from the station. Yet the insurrection was broken.

        1. Continue
          oTxtEmpireInsurrectionQuest_restraint2

          You move in after the departure of the Imperial fleet and recover a large quantity of salvage along with a survivor.

          • Large amount of ⬗ Scrap
          • Blood Priest or Elder Blood Mage or Mouth of Ariok or Bloodwitch
          1. Continue

EmpireInsurrectionQuest_approach

→ oTxtEmpireInsurrectionQuest_approach_win

EmpireInsurrectionQuest_battle2

→ oTxtEmpireInsurrectionQuest_battle2_win

EmpireInvasionBattle

oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_intro

You arrive to see an Imperial fleet engaging the local system militia.

  1. Approach the battle
    Random outcome (1/6 each):
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_approachA

      A militia vessel attacks you, igniting fires across the decks.

      • 1 fire spawned
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Sectorspecific ship
      After the fight:
      oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_approach_afterFight

      You retrieve what you can and return to your ship.

      • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_afterOutcome1

          More Imperial reinforcements arrive. Several ships begin firing at you, and you are forced to leave.

          • Crew recalled to ship
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_afterOutcome2

          The militia is routed. You pick up an Imperial broadcast. "Continue the purge. You are free to engage all who lack the proper fleet codes." You depart before you are crushed by the Imperial fleet.

          • Crew recalled to ship
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_afterOutcome3

          The militia has managed to repel the Imperial fleet. A militia broadcoast announces over the comms. "Beware of Imperial infiltrators. Treat any vessel in the area without our ident codes as suspect." You hear this, and make your escape.

          • Crew recalled to ship
          1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_approachB

      Assault sleds slam into your hull as one of the system militia vessels begins attacking you.

      • 1–2 Sector-appropriate crew boarders
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Sectorspecific ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • → oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_afterOutcome2
      • → oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_afterOutcome3
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_approachD

      You hear the thump of an Imperial assault pod slamming into your hull. One of the Imperial vessels is attacking you.

      • 1–2 Imperial Recruit, Imperial Enforcer, Imperial Marshal, Imperial Scribe, or Imperial Acolyte boarders
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the fight: → oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_approach_afterFight
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_approachF

      An Imperial assault pod slams into a nearby militia ship. The militia ship turns to engage you, believing you to be their attacker.

      • 1–2 Imperial Recruit, Imperial Enforcer, Imperial Marshal, Imperial Scribe, or Imperial Acolyte boarders
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider ship
      After the fight: → oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_approach_afterFight
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_approachC

      You are attacked by a damaged Imperial ship. You intercept a transmission from the Imperials: "They are bringing in reinforcements -- we are engaging!"

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the fight: → oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_approach_afterFight
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_approachE

      You watch as a militia vessel crashes into one of the Imperial warships. Explosions ripple through the internals of both ships, ejecting shattered armor plating at high velocity in all directions. Your ship is struck by one of the fast-moving shards.

      • −1 hull HP
      1. Continue
        → oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_afterOutcome1
  2. Leave
    oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_leave

    You avoid the battle and press on.

    1. Continue
  3. (Imperial Citizen) Ensure a militia victory
    oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_bt_empire

    A militia victory seems uncertain. A crewmember familiar with Imperial tactics offers vital data on Imperial doctrine that would greatly tilt the outcome in favor of the militia. You forward the information to the militia commander.

    1. Continue
      oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_bt_empire1

      Armed with insider knowledge of Imperial tactics, the system defense militia quickly gains superiority over the Imperial fleet. Grateful for your contribution, the militia assigns a portion of the battlezone salvage rights to you.

      • Random small reward
      • Random ship weapon
      1. Continue

EmpireInvasionBattle_approach

→ oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_approach_afterFight

EmpireInvasionBattle_bt

→ oTxtEmpireInvasionBattle_bt_empire

EmpireInvasionConvoy

oTxtEmpireInvasionConvoy_intro

Your ship tears into realspace in front of an Imperial supply convoy. They appear to be transporting munitions to a nearby invasion fleet.

  1. Raid the convoy
    Random outcome (1/6 each):
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionConvoy_raidE

      You perform a flawless raid on the convoy and manage to secure some extra supplies.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionConvoy_raidA

      You make an attack run on the convoy. Only a single Imperial ship shows up to defend them.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtEmpireInvasionConvoy_raidA1

      Most ships in the convoy had managed to escape while you were engaged, but one of them seemed to have been suffering from some sort of drive malfunction. You seize the opportunity to rob them of their cargo.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionConvoy_raidC

      You perform a flawless raid on the convoy and manage to come away with a few shipments of ordnance.

      • Moderate amount of missile ammo (5–8)
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionConvoy_raidD

      You perform a flawless raid on the convoy and manage to come away with a large shipment of ordnance.

      • Large amount of missile ammo (8–12) and 1/2 chance for 1 missile blueprint
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionConvoy_raidF

      You approach the convoy and are suddenly ambushed by an Imperial warship. It takes out your engines, buying the Imperials just enough time to engage their subspace drives and jump out.

      • −1 hull HP
      • Destroy Engines
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionConvoy_raidB

      A few Imperial ships move to intercept you. Suddenly a small corsair strike fleet drops in, evidently having the same idea as you.

      1. Continue
        oTxtEmpireInvasionConvoy_raidB2

        Both the Imperials and the corsairs launch a flurry of preliminary assault pods. A few of the Imperial pods impact your ship before disintegrating. Moments later, you hear warning sirens indicating that you have been target locked.

        • 1–2 Imperial Recruit, Imperial Enforcer, Imperial Marshal, Imperial Scribe, or Imperial Acolyte boarders
        1. Continue
          oTxtEmpireInvasionConvoy_raidB2_0

          As you close in to attack, several of the raider assault pods impact your target ship and dislodge friendly boarders.

          1. Continue
          After the rewards from the fight:
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • oTxtEmpireInvasionConvoy_raidB2_1

            A formidable squadron of Imperial ships arrive to defend the convoy. Outnumbered and outgunned, both you and the corsairs are forced to flee.

            • Crew recalled to ship
            1. Continue
          • oTxtEmpireInvasionConvoy_raidB2_2

            The Imperial forces have been neutralized. You claim a few cargo containers from the defenseless supply ships. The corsairs do not seem to notice you, or care.

            • Random small reward
            1. Continue
  2. Stay hidden
    oTxtEmpireInvasionConvoy_stayHidden

    You decide to lay low and wait for the convoy to pass.

    1. Continue
  3. (Imperial Citizen) Requisition the supplies
    oTxtEmpireInvasionConvoy_bt_empire

    An Imperial-trained crewmember forges a false requisition order to be issued to the convoy. The convoy overseer abides by the false request, and allots one of the supply vessels to replenish your stores.

    • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
    • Large amount of missile ammo (8–12) and 1/2 chance for 1 missile blueprint
    1. Continue
oTxtEmpireInvasionConvoy_raidA2 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Unfortunately, every single ship in the convoy had managed to escape while you were engaged.

  1. Continue

EmpireInvasionConvoy_bt

→ oTxtEmpireInvasionConvoy_bt_empire

EmpireInvasionConvoy_raidB2

→ oTxtEmpireInvasionConvoy_raidB2_0

EmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot

oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_intro

You come upon a militia depot. They are stockpiling munitions after learning of an imminent Imperial invasion.

  1. Raid the depot
    Random outcome (1/6 each):
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_raidE

      You are caught by the security patrol. They believe you to be Imperial saboteurs and promptly open fire upon your ship.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      Random outcome (1/3 each):
      • oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_afterOutcome1

        The depot suddenly vanishes in a series of violent explosions. Imperial saboteurs must have set off and destroyed the munitions stockpile.

        • Crew recalled to ship
        1. Continue
      • oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_afterOutcome2

        You receive a militia broadcast: "Infiltrators, we have infiltrators in our midst! Call in the fleet!" A large contingent of militia vessels soon drop out of subspace, and you are forced to flee.

        • Crew recalled to ship
        1. Continue
      • oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_afterOutcome3

        A large militia fleet suddenly arrives. They quickly notice you and open fire. You manage to escape after sustaining some minor damage.

        • Crew recalled to ship
        • 1–2 breaches spawned
        1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_raidF

      You avoid the militia patrol and make it inside the depot. Unfortunately you arrive just in time to see what appears to be Imperial saboteurs setting explosive charges onto the stockpiled munition. It is about to blow. You manage to escape moments before the explosion.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_raidB

      You are able to slip past the militia patrols. You secure a small number of warheads.

      • 3–6 missiles
      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • → oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_afterOutcome3
        • → oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_afterOutcome1
        • → oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_afterOutcome2
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_raidA

      Security is surprisingly light. You slip through and retrieve a large quantity of munitions.

      • 7–12 missiles
      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • → oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_afterOutcome3
        • → oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_afterOutcome1
        • → oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_afterOutcome2
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_raidC

      You are able to avoid the security detail. You retrieve a massively shielded canister containing a strange artifact.

      • Random cult equipment
      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • → oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_afterOutcome3
        • → oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_afterOutcome1
        • → oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_afterOutcome2
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_raidD

      You are able to avoid the security detail. You discover within the depot a massively shielded canister. As you bring it aboard your ship the contents destabilize and a bizarre creature bursts forth.

      • 1 Demonic Crew boarder
      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • → oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_afterOutcome3
        • → oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_afterOutcome1
        • → oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_afterOutcome2
  2. Leave
    oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_leave

    You leave the depot alone and move on.

    1. Continue
  3. (Launch Bay) Steal some supplies
    oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_bt_launchBay

    You launch an assault sled towards the depot. The defenders are too busy with the interloping raiders to notice. You come away with a number of munitions.

    • Moderate amount of missile ammo (5–8)
    1. Continue

EmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_bt

→ oTxtEmpireInvasionMilitiaDepot_bt_launchBay

EmpireInvasionSupplyLines

oTxtEmpireInvasionSupplyLines_intro

You arrive at the tail end of an Imperial invasion fleet. Several supply stations are visible nearby.

  1. Raid the supply stations
    Random outcome (1/5 each):
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionSupplyLines_raidC

      As you make your approach, an Imperial warship intercepts you.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtEmpireInvasionSupplyLines_raidC2

      You discover nothing of value remaining. You move on.

      • Crew recalled to ship
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionSupplyLines_raidA

      Fortunately the supply stations on the outer edge of the perimeter are only lightly defended. You smash through an automated weapon emplacement, then proceed to recover a few supply containers.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionSupplyLines_raidD

      You speed towards the supply stations. Suddenly, a massive squadron of capital-class ships drops in from subspace. There is no way you can get past them without them noticing. You call off the raid.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionSupplyLines_raidB

      You manage to break into one of the supply stations and steal a few untended shipments.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
        oTxtEmpireInvasionSupplyLines_raidB2

        Unfortunately one of the shipments had been containing some sort of unstable subspace device. The device explodes shortly after bringing it back to your ship, tearing a few subspace breaches into your hull.

        • 2–3 warp breaches spawned
        1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireInvasionSupplyLines_raidE

      You slip past the defenses and manage to secure a few untended cargo shipments.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
        oTxtEmpireInvasionSupplyLines_raidE2

        On your way out, you notice a pirate vessel docked at the same supply station as the one you just looted. You overhear comms chatter: "Blast! The Imperials have caught us! Assault pods, quickly!"

        1. Continue
          oTxtEmpireInvasionSupplyLines_raidE3

          Several assault pods slam into your hull, disgorging a few raiders before disintegrating. The pirate vessel activates its weapons.

          • 1–2 Raider crew boarders
          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Raider ship
  2. Avoid them
    oTxtEmpireInvasionSupplyLines_avoid

    You decide to avoid the Imperials.

    1. Continue
  3. (Imperial Citizen) Secure the supplies
    oTxtEmpireInvasionSupplyLines_bt_empire

    A crewmember knowledgeable about the layout of Imperial supply stations helps draw up plans to secure the supplies within. Masquerading as a passing merchant, you are able to approach closely for a boarding attempt. Thanks to good planning, you are able to retrieve a significant quantity of supplies. You make your escape before the Imperial fleet can respond.

    • Random moderate reward
    1. Continue

EmpireInvasionSupplyLines_bt

→ oTxtEmpireInvasionSupplyLines_bt_empire

EmpirePalace

oTxtEmpirePalace_intro

The system you arrive at contains a distant backwater Imperial colony. You receive an invitation from the planetary administration: the governor wishes to see you. The orbital palace is majestic in its bleakness and brutalist design. You note that the colony itself is small and impoverished and it lacks proper defenses. Whatever wealth this colony may have lies at the governor's palace.

  1. Visit the governor's palace
    oTxtEmpirePalace_investigateA

    The governor receives you, and after typical diplomatic pleasantries and gifts are traded, she makes a proposal. An Imperial pyre-temple has been defiled by Gorgothan cultists, and she wants them dead. In exchange she promises supplies and crew from her station and whatever treasure the temple may contain. Things can be replaced, she concludes, but Hyperion's flame lives eternal. You hesitate to take her words at face value, as something feels off.

    1. Accept the governor's offer
      Random outcome (1/3 each):
      • oTxtEmpirePalace_investigateA_accept2

        The governor betrays you! An enemy vessel appears on your multispex as your ship is boarded by hostiles.

        • 3–4 Imperial Recruit, Imperial Enforcer, or Imperial Marshal boarders
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Empire ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        oTxtEmpirePalace_investigateA_accept2_win

        The last Imperial ship lies in ashes. You scavenge the remains and leave.

        • 1 random equipment item
        1. Continue
      • oTxtEmpirePalace_investigateA_accept3

        The governor betrays you! Her engineers sabotage your ship's systems. An enemy vessel engages you as your ship is boarded by hostiles.

        • 3–4 Imperial Recruit, Imperial Enforcer, or Imperial Marshal boarders
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Empire ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        oTxtEmpirePalace_investigateA_accept3_win

        The last Imperial cur is dead. You loot their bodies and exit the system.

        • 1 random equipment item
        1. Continue
      • oTxtEmpirePalace_investigateA_accept

        You accept the governor's offer. She gives you coordinates to the Imperial pyre-temple desecrated by Gorgothan cultists. She sends you a crew member and offers some scrap.

        • Imperial Scribe or Imperial Enforcer
        • Quest marker added to map: oTxtEmpirePalaceQuest_intro
        1. Continue
    2. Raid the palace
      oTxtEmpirePalace_raidB

      The palace and its riches will be yours. You attack the orbital station. However, the Imperials were prepared for an attack, sending boarders onto your ship.

      • 3–4 Imperial Recruit, Imperial Enforcer, or Imperial Marshal boarders
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the fight:
      oTxtEmpirePalace_raidB_victory

      You have defeated the first Imperial attack, but there is no time to lose. Another Imperial defender moves to join the fight.

      • Crew recalled to ship
      1. Continue
        oTxtEmpirePalace_raidB_victory1

        The second Imperial ship begins its attack.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Empire ship
        After the fight:
        oTxtEmpirePalace_raidB_victory2

        The second ship is no more. You have broken the Imperial assault.

        1. Continue
          oTxtEmpirePalace_raid_victory3

          You salvage whatever you can from the palace. Many of the baubles and valuables are useless on your quest, but you find some useful equipment.

          • Random large reward
          1. Continue
  2. Move on
    oTxtEmpirePalace_moveOn

    You move on.

    1. Continue
oTxtEmpirePalace_raid Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You note the lack of defenses on the palace and choose to attack. The Imperials are caught off guard, and send their remaining ships to battle.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Empire ship
After the rewards from the fight:
oTxtEmpirePalace_raid_victory

A second ship joins the fray as the first breaks apart.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Empire ship
After the rewards from the fight: → oTxtEmpirePalace_raid_victory3

EmpirePalaceQuest

oTxtEmpirePalaceQuest_intro

You arrive at the location given to you by the governor of the colony you visited earlier. A blazing silhouette against the relentless fury of a red star, you see a ruined cathedral-esque Imperial temple station that has been badly damaged. You recall that these pyre-temples are rare, and are always built near stars to venerate the aspect of fire within the immortal God-King, Hyperion. The damage seems to be relatively recent. Whoever attacked the base might still be near.

  1. Investigate the temple station
    oTxtEmpirePalaceQuest_investigate

    Your scouts investigate the temple station thoroughly. The interior of the station radiates immense heat -- braziers, firepits and pyres still burn despite the death cult's attack. At first, your scouts note a curious lack of corpses only to realise that the majority of them have been fed to the temple's pyres.

    1. Continue
      oTxtEmpirePalaceQuest_investigate1

      The scouts have found a reinforced door -- perhaps this was the treasure the governor mentioned. The door is warded, and you are certain that breaking through the door will alert the death cultists. Whatever relics this place holds are surely contained within.

      1. Cut through the reinforced door
        oTxtEmpirePalaceQuest_vaultA

        The reinforced door yields and an alarm is tripped. You manage to retrieve the contents, but not before an enemy ship arrives, weapons primed.

        • Large amount of ⬗ Scrap
        • Retributor II or Retributor III or Flame Lance
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Cult ship
      2. Demolish the station and scavenge the remains
        oTxtEmpirePalaceQuest_vaultC

        A barrage from your weapons demolishes the entire station. Your crew manage to scavenge some scrap. As you destroy the temple, a death cult plague ship emerges from the void and attacks

        • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
        • Random Imperial equipment
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Plague ship
  2. Leave
    oTxtEmpirePalaceQuest_leave

    You leave the system.

    1. Continue

EmpirePalace_investigateA

→ oTxtEmpirePalace_investigateA_accept

EmpirePalace_investigateA_accept2

→ oTxtEmpirePalace_investigateA_accept2_win

EmpirePalace_investigateA_accept3

→ oTxtEmpirePalace_investigateA_accept3_win

EmpirePalace_raid

→ oTxtEmpirePalace_raid_victory
oTxtEmpirePalace_raid_victory2 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

The second ship falls. You are victorious.

  1. Continue

EmpirePalace_raidB

→ oTxtEmpirePalace_raidB_victory
oTxtEmpirePalace_raidB_victory3 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You raid the palace. Many of the baubles and valuables are useless on your quest, but you discover scrap, weapons, and equipment.

  • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
  • 2 random equipment items
  • Moderate amount of missile ammo (5–8)
  • Random Imperial psychic item
  1. Continue

EmpireRaidFort

oTxtEmpireRaidFort Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You arrive in orbit above an Imperial colony-world. Its planetary defense force issues a warning: "Unidentified vessel. Leave immediately or we will open fire." While planetary fortifications are indestructible for a lone starship, their role in collecting tithes from the surrounding colonies offers a lucrative opportunity for the opportunistic infiltrator. You detect a few dilapidated sections in one of the defensive bastions that may be used for covert insertion.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Empire Fort ship
oTxtEmpireRaidFort_crewKill Unreachable — not referenced by any event

The defending garrison has been eliminated. You ensure the retrieval of all storage vaults.

  1. Continue
    → oTxtEmpireRaidFort_leaveOrStay
oTxtEmpireRaidFort_vaultWin Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Scans indicate all storage vaults have been destroyed or secured.

  1. Continue
    → oTxtEmpireRaidFort_leaveOrStay

EmpireRitualBurning

oTxtEmpireRitualBurning_intro

You arrive to see several Imperial inquisition ships arrayed around a lone prison barge. They intend to incinerate the vessel, along with all the occupants within. You hear a confessor intone over the open channel: "You have profaned our faith..."

  1. Try to stop the inquisitors
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtEmpireRitualBurning_sacrificeB

      You make your presence known. One of the inquisition vessels moves to engage. "You dare interrupt our sacred ritual?"

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire Spell ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtEmpireRitualBurning_sacrificeB_win

      The remaining inquisition ships are nowhere to be seen. They must have fled during your battle. You are pleased to learn that all of the prisoners aboard the barge had survived.

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtEmpireRitualBurning_sacrificeB_win2

          One of the prisoners requests to join your crew.

          • Random prisoner crew
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEmpireRitualBurning_sacrificeB_win1

          Unfortunately a few of them are ungrateful, psychotic, or both. They manage to procure a functioning assault pod and use it to board your ship.

          • 2 Random prisoner crew boarders
          1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireRitualBurning_sacrificeA

      You try to stop the sacrifice but it is too late. The confessors unleash a tidal wave of psychic fire at the prison barge. One of them detects your vessel and redirects a portion of the inferno towards you. Divine fire spills out across your hull. "More blasphemers!"

      • 2–3 fires spawned
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire Spell ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtEmpireRitualBurning_sacrificeA_win

      You return to the prison barge and are disappointed to learn that your efforts were in vain. All prisoners aboard have been reduced to cinders. The other inquisition ships must have left soon after completing their ritual.

      1. Continue
  2. Wait and see what happens
    oTxtEmpireRitualBurning_wait

    The confessors aboard the inquisition ships summon a massive conflagration of psychic fire upon the prison barge. A few minutes later, the prison barge and all its occupants are reduced to ashes.

    1. Continue
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtEmpireRitualBurning_wait2

        You attempt to salvage the remains afterwards, but there is nothing left.

        1. Continue
      • oTxtEmpireRitualBurning_wait1

        You wait for the inquisition ships to leave, then collect what you can from the remains.

        • Random small reward
        1. Continue
  3. Avoid them and move on
    oTxtEmpireRitualBurning_leave

    You decide not to get involved.

    1. Continue

EmpireRitualBurning_sacrificeA

→ oTxtEmpireRitualBurning_sacrificeA_win

EmpireRitualBurning_sacrificeB

→ oTxtEmpireRitualBurning_sacrificeB_win

EmpireRogueCommander

oTxtEmpireRogueCommander_intro

You hear reports of a rogue Imperial commander in this system.

  1. Investigate the system
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtEmpireRogueCommander_investigateA

      You perform a sweep of the system but fail to find any trace of the rogue commander.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireRogueCommander_investigateB

      You spend some time investigating the various planets and moons within this system. There is no sign of the rogue commander, however you do discover a hidden cache of unmarked supply containers.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireRogueCommander_investigateD

      You maneuver towards a nearby protoplanet and discover a small hangar hidden on its surface. Upon approaching the hangar, several mines detonate and rip great chunks of plating from your hull.

      • −1 hull HP
      1. Continue
        oTxtEmpireRogueCommander_investigateD2

        Later you receive an anonymous transmission. "Stop following me. "

        1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireRogueCommander_investigateC

      You investigate a nearby asteroid field and see signs that an Imperial military ship had recently been docked here.

      1. Wait to see if anyone returns
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtEmpireRogueCommander_waitA

          You wait here for several days, but no one shows up.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtEmpireRogueCommander_waitB

          You decide to wait. A few hours later, an Imperial warship drops in from subspace and begins firing on you, believing you to be in league with the rogue commander.

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Empire ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          oTxtEmpireRogueCommander_waitB2

          Later you receive an anonymous message, along with a small shipment of ordnance. "Commander, you neutralized my pursuers, and for that, you have my thanks. I have left you some extra munitions for your troubles. Now I presume our business is concluded. Do not come looking for me again."

          • 6–14 missiles
          1. Continue
      2. Abandon your search
        oTxtEmpireRogueCommander_abandon

        You decide to abandon your search.

        1. Continue
  2. Ignore the reports and move on
    oTxtEmpireRogueCommander_ignore

    You move on.

    1. Continue
  3. Task your Imperial Marshal with tracking down the rogue commander
    oTxtEmpireRogueCommander_marshal1

    You consult your marshal regarding Imperial doctrine for escape and evasion. Aided by a thorough understanding of Imperial tactics, you are able to predict the rogue commander's location with accuracy.

    1. Continue
      oTxtEmpireRogueCommander_marshal2

      You locate the rogue commander. He appears to have settled in as the new leader of a hidden mining colony. Alarmed at being discovered, he leads a strike team aboard your vessel and attacks.

      • 1 Imperial Marshal boarder
      • 1–2 Corsair, Scavenger, Infiltrator, or Gene Soldier boarders
      1. Continue
      After the boarders are defeated:
      oTxtEmpireRogueCommander_marshal3

      The rogue commander is dead. You watch as shuttles depart from the mining colony one by one. You recover some equipment found on the dead before moving on.

      • Random Imperial equipment
      1. Continue

EmpireSupplyDepot

oTxtEmpireSupplyDepot_intro

Your ship arrives in front of a massive supply depot left behind by an Imperial fleet. The depot appears to have been abandoned for some time, but the sheer size of it suggests that there may still be supplies left to be scavenged.

  1. Search the depot
    Random outcome (1/7 each):
    • oTxtEmpireSupplyDepot_searchC

      You come across a storage room filled with munitions.

      • reward_missileAmmoMedium or reward_missileAmmoLow
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireSupplyDepot_searchG

      As you move in to search the depot, a raider ship appears on your sensors. It opens fire.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtEmpireSupplyDepot_searchG_1

      After dealing with the raiders, you return to the supply depot. Unfortunately it seems the raiders have already taken everything of value. You return to your ship empty-handed.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireSupplyDepot_searchE

      You search the depot. A group of raiders suddenly emerges from the shadows and confronts you. They are trying to steal your ship.

      • 2 Raider crew boarders
      1. Continue
      After the boarders are defeated:
      oTxtEmpireSupplyDepot_searchE_1

      You search the bodies and find a map that leads to a hidden cache of Imperial weapons.

      • Random weapon item
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireSupplyDepot_searchB

      You search the depot and discover a hidden armory. Among the various weapons, you find a rifle that is still in working condition.

      • Machine Rifle
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireSupplyDepot_searchD

      You stumble upon a well-preserved set of Imperial armor. It appears to be made of high-quality materials and is still fully functional.

      • Random armor item
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireSupplyDepot_searchA

      You explore the outer perimeter of the depot and find several abandoned cargo containers. Most of them are empty or filled with useless debris, but you do manage to find a few crates containing valuable scrap.

      • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireSupplyDepot_searchF

      Upon entering the depot, you approach the main storage room and hear the sound of voices inside. As you peek through the door, you see a group of raider merchants cataloging their inventory. They are surprised to see you, but welcome you as a potential customer. They offer to sell you some of their wares.

      1. Purchase random equipment for 15 scrap
        oTxtEmpireSupplyDepot_purchase

        The raiders are pleased with the transaction.

        • Random equipment item
        • −15 ⬗ Scrap
        1. Continue
      2. Decline
        oTxtEmpireSupplyDepot_decline

        You decline their offer.

        1. Continue
  2. Ignore the depot
    oTxtEmpireSupplyDepot_ignore

    You decide to leave the depot alone and move on, fearing that it may be booby-trapped or guarded by Imperial forces.

    1. Continue
  3. (Sensor 2) Conduct a detailed sensor sweep
    oTxtEmpireSupplyDepot_bt_sensor2

    A detailed sensor sweep of the depot reveals a number of caches that otherwise would have been difficult to uncover. You secure the bounty and move on.

    • Random large reward
    1. Continue

EmpireSupplyDepot_bt

→ oTxtEmpireSupplyDepot_bt_sensor2

EmpireSupplyDepot_searchE

→ oTxtEmpireSupplyDepot_searchE_1

EmpireSupplyDepot_searchG

→ oTxtEmpireSupplyDepot_searchG_1

EmpireSurpriseAttack

oTxtEmpireSurpriseAttack_intro

You briefly detect an Imperial vessel on scan without it detecting you in return. The nearby ion storm has masked your presence. This may be a good opportunity to gain the upper hand.

  1. Pursue
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtEmpireSurpriseAttack_AttackA

      You close in on the Imperial vessel. Your weapons are already charged by the time you arrive.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
    • oTxtEmpireSurpriseAttack_AttackC

      You close in on the Imperial vessel. Unfortunately, your approach is detected, and the Imperials quickly turn to engage.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
    • oTxtEmpireSurpriseAttack_AttackB

      You attempt pursuit. Unfortunately, you fail to regain sensor contact with the Imperial vessel, and they manage to escape.

      1. Continue
  2. Ignore
    oTxtEmpireSurpriseAttack_leave

    You decide not to risk it and slip away.

    1. Continue
  3. (Improved sensors) Manually tune your sensors to track the enemy
    oTxtEmpireSurpriseAttack_AttackBlue

    You are able to make full use of your improved sensor suite to locate the target ship undetected. The Imperial vessel is caught by complete surprise.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Empire ship

EmpireTechnoPursuit

oTxtEmpireTechnoPursuit_intro

Sensors pick up a technocult vessel. It is pursued by an Imperial starship.

  1. Intercept the Imperial ship
    oTxtEmpireTechnoPursuit_intercept

    You move to intercept the Imperial vessel.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Empire ship
    After the rewards from the fight:
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtEmpireTechnoPursuit_interceptA

      The technocult vessel manages to get away. There is nothing left to do here. You continue on with your journey.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireTechnoPursuit_interceptB

      The Technocult vessel hails you. "Acquisition of Class 9 artifact... successful. Ten of ten objectives... met. We will consider enlisting your aid in the future." The ship then jumps out.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtEmpireTechnoPursuit_interceptC

      The technocult ship suddenly erupts in a ball of strangely colored fire. Even as the explosion subsides, there still seems to be a strange haziness around the tumbling wreckage.

      1. Investigate
        oTxtEmpireTechnoPursuit_investigate1

        You feel an increasing sense of nausea as your instruments start showing abnormal outputs. Alarms blare as intruders are detected aboard.

        • 1–3 Demonic Crew boarders
        1. Continue
        After the boarders are defeated:
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtEmpireTechnoPursuit_investigate1A

          The anomaly subsides. In amongst the wreckage you find a strange weapon.

          • Random cult weapon
          1. Continue
        • oTxtEmpireTechnoPursuit_investigate1B

          Whatever energies causing the phenomenon have now dissipated. You scan the wreckage for salvage.

          • Random ship weapon
          1. Continue
      2. Leave it alone
        oTxtEmpireTechnoPursuit_leaveItAlone

        Whatever it is, it does not look like something you want to deal with.

        1. Continue
  2. Ignore the situation
    oTxtEmpireTechnoPursuit_ignore

    You decide not to intervene. The technocult vessel falls off the sensor scan as it passes behind a nearby planetoid, with the Imperial vessel still in pursuit.

    1. Continue
      Random outcome (weighted):
      • 1/3
        oTxtEmpireTechnoPursuit_imperialLoss

        Later, you encounter the still-burning remains of that same Imperial vessel. You salvage a quantity of useful material from the wreckage before moving on.

        • Random moderate reward
        1. Continue
      • 2/3
        oTxtEmpireTechnoPursuit_technoLoss

        Later, you discover the wreckage of that same technocult vessel. A portion of its cargo section has been obliterated. You recover the modest amount of useful material remaining in the wreckage before moving on.

        • Random small reward
        1. Continue
oTxtEmpireTechnoPursuit_destroy Unreachable — not referenced by any event

"You act to promote the uncontrolled possession of Class 9 technologies. You will not be allowed to succeed."

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Techno ship
After the rewards from the fight:
Random outcome (1/3 each):
  • oTxtEmpireTechnoPursuit_destroyA

    The pursuing Imperial ship arrives. "Pirate vessel, your actions today have helped ensure the stability of the Empire. We shall overlook your violations of Imperial codes, but do not test our leniency."

    1. Continue
  • oTxtEmpireTechnoPursuit_destroyB

    It appears the technocultists destroyed the artifact they were carrying. The Imperial captain is livid: "Pirate cur, your rash actions have resulted in a great loss for the Auric Throne today. You deserve death."

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Empire ship
  • oTxtEmpireTechnoPursuit_destroyC

    It seems whatever object of value the technocult vessel had been transporting was destroyed in combat.

    1. Continue
      oTxtEmpireTechnoPursuit_destroyC2

      The commander of the Imperial ship is incensed by this discovery. "If I cannot return to my lord with his artifact, I shall return with the head of the meddlesome pirate that thwarted me!"

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship

EnemyCrewKilled

oTxtEnemyCrewKilled Unreachable — not referenced by any event

The enemy crew has been killed. You search through the remains and find a few useful items.

  • Default post-fight reward (scrap, missiles, or choice reward)
  1. Continue
oTxtEnemyCrewKilled_merchant Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You have wiped out the merchant crew. Unfortunately they were prepared for this outcome. They had rigged their inventory to self destruct in the event they were attacked. You are only able to recover a small amount of scrap from the remains.

  • Very small amount of ⬗ Scrap
  1. Continue

EnemyDestroyed

oTxtEnemyDestroyed Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You search through the remains and find a few useful items.

  • Default post-fight reward (scrap, missiles, or choice reward)
  1. Continue
oTxtEnemyDestroyed_merchant Unreachable — not referenced by any event

The merchant ship tears apart. Several additional explosions go off as the munitions in thier cargo ignite. Nearly everything of value is incinerated. You are only able to recover a small amount of scrap from the remains.

  • Very small amount of ⬗ Scrap
  1. Continue
oTxtEnemyDestroyed_noReward Unreachable — not referenced by any event

The enemy vessel was destroyed.

EnemyLeft

The enemy ship has left the area.

EnforcerFight

oTxtEnforcerFight_intro

An Hegemonic Enforcer craft has found you! You turn to attack.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Empire ship

Error

oTxtError_emptyEncounterPool Unreachable — not referenced by any event

ERROR: Empty encounter pool. Check super function.

Exit

oTxtExit Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You approach the stargate.

  1. Continue

FactionShrine

oTxtFactionShrine Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You come across a small outpost. There is shrine within.

  1. Make a prayer
    oTxtFactionShrine_pray

    You make a prayer. You feel the stirring of unseen energies.

    1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtFactionShrine_leave

    You come across a small outpost. There is shrine within.

    1. Pray to the shrine
    2. Leave

Fortress

oTxtFortress Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You approach the enemy fortress.

FortuneTeller

oTxtFortuneTeller_intro

A dilapidated vessel hails you. "You... There's something different about you. Pardon me, I am Aamulrot, Oracle of Vistapur. Would you like me to read your fate?"

  • −5 ⬗ Scrap
  1. Scry your fate (5 scrap)
    oTxtFortuneTeller_agree

    "You tread a bloody path. I see you wrestling against malign spirits and great gods, and a hundred ships lie broken at your feet. Truly, you are destined for greatness. I have been amassing powerful relics worthy of such a great personage as you. Allow me to offer them - for a fee, of course."

    • Proceeds to shop
    1. Continue
    2. Continue
  2. Attack
    oTxtFortuneTeller_attack

    You lock on to the dilapidated vessel. Perhaps Aamulrot ought to have read their own fortune first.

    1. Continue
      oTxtFortuneTeller_attack2

      As you move in to attack, you receive another hail from the dilapidated vessel. This time, it is not Aamulrot. They seem confused. "Hey, what are you doing?"

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider Small ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtFortuneTeller_attack3

      Checking the ship logs you note there was no evidence of an Aamulrot ever being on the ship.

      1. Continue
  3. Decline
    oTxtFortuneTeller_decline

    You are too well-acquainted with the sham to waste time on it.

    1. Continue
  4. (Imperial Psyker) Psychically interrogate the oracle
    oTxtFortuneTeller_bt_psykerEmpire

    The oracle gives you a wry smile. "Child, you cannot hope to interrogate me. Your power is feeble. But you are amusing, so have a trinket for your efforts." The feed suddenly cuts out, and there is no further communication from the vessel. You hear a light clattering behind you, and turn to discover a psychomantic wafer lying upon the deck.

    • Random cult psychic item
    1. Continue
  5. (Cult Psyker) Psychically probe the oracle
    oTxtFortuneTeller_bt_psykerCult

    Your crew member attempts to probe the mind of the oracle. Your crew member is momentarily petrified, transfixed by some celestial vision. They then turn back to you and kneel before your feet with fearful deference. The oracle chortles cryptically at you. "Such a fate is too great a burden for one to bear alone, I think." The oracle gives you a protective ward.

    • Prismatic Veil or Chronofield or Unholy Talisman
    1. Continue
  6. (Psyker) Peer into the mind of the oracle
    oTxtFortuneTeller_bt_psykerPlayer

    You focus and enter a trance to reach into the oracle's mind. You hear the oracle's voice. "Such a prodigy, are we? Your curiosity deserves a reward..."

    1. Continue
      oTxtFortuneTeller_bt_psykerPlayer2

      You snap out of your trance to a splitting headache. You sense that you had witnessed something profound, but you hardly have time to remember what it was before your attention is drawn back to the feed by the oracle's sonorous voice. "The path you walk is treacherous. You will need powerful relics. I can help you with that - for a fee, of course."

      • Proceeds to shop
      1. Continue
oTxtFortuneTeller_agree2 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

"I see, you are indeed destined for great things. I have been amassing powerful relics worthy of such a great personage as you. Allow me to offer them - for a fee, of course."

  • Proceeds to shop
  1. Continue
oTxtFortuneTeller_decline1 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

So be it.

  1. Continue
oTxtFortuneTeller_decline2 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

"Wait. Allow me to read your fate regardless."

  1. Continue
oTxtFortuneTeller_decline2b Unreachable — not referenced by any event

"I behold you victorious, above a rancid world, as broken hulks rain down into the skies. I have been amassing powerful relics worthy of such a great personage as you. Allow me to offer them - for a fee, of course."

  • Proceeds to shop
  1. Continue

FortuneTeller_bt

→ oTxtFortuneTeller_bt_psykerCult

GameStart

oTxtGameStart Unreachable — not referenced by any event

The God-King has fallen. The Empire is in despair. Brother fights against brother as the Ruinous Cults sweep through the stars. The very fabric of reality trembles before the onslaught of demonic swarms. Deep within the Imperial Coreworlds one final hope exists: to rekindle the flames within the Vault of Souls. To the Empire you are a blasphemer. To the Cults you are a heretic. Trust no one. Let none stop you from bringing the renegades of this forsaken realm to their destined annihilation.

  1. Continue
    oTxtGameStart2
    1. Continue

GameStart2

→ oTxtGameStart2

GameStart3

oTxtGameStart3 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Ahead lies nothing but terror and madness, and yet, there are those who still cling to hope...

  1. Continue

GenericNothing

oTxtGenericNothing_intro

[...]

  1. Continue

HegemonColonyThreat

oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_contactA

You hail the Hegemon ship. They respond with contempt. "Who are you to challenge the authority of the Solar Hegemon?."

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Empire ship
After the rewards from the fight:
oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_contactA_2

The colonists thank you for your assistance. They agree to send over one of their men to join your crew.

  • Imperial Recruit or Imperial Scribe ×0
  1. Continue
oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_contactB

"To challenge the Hegemon is to challenge the will of the Immortal King himself. The only outcome is death."

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Empire ship
After the rewards from the fight:
oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_contactB_2

Suddenly several ships of the Hegemonic fleet jump in and begin firing. The colony takes the brunt of the attack, its thin hull collapsing like tissue paper. It explodes with violent force. As you prepare to retreat you notice a few lifeboats which had miraculously survived the initial blast.

  • Crew recalled to ship
  1. Attempt to save the lifeboats
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_lifeboatsA

      You manage to rescue one of the lifeboats before making your escape. Its lone survivor bursts into tears after safely making their way aboard your ship. They decide to join your crew.

      • Imperial Recruit or Imperial Scribe ×0
      1. Continue
    • oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_lifeboatsB

      You intercept several lifeboats only to find their occupants severely irradiated due to their close proximity to the initial plasma explosion. They succumb to their injuries over the next few days.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_lifeboatsC

      You make a brave attempt to save the lifeboats but it is no use. The Hegemon ships fire precision strikes at each lifeboat, vaporizing them before they even come close to the safety of your ship. Positional advantage compromised, your vessel suffers moderate damage as you make your escape.

      • Crew recalled to ship
      • 2–3 breaches spawned
      1. Continue
  2. Get out of there
    oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_contactB_leave

    It is too great a risk to recover the lifeboats in the presence of the Imperial fleet. You slip away undetected.

    1. Continue
oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_defendA

You move to intercept the Hegemon vessel. It opens fire at you without warning.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Raider ship
After the rewards from the fight:
oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_defendA_2

The colony's citizens are grateful for your defense and offer what supplies they can.

  • Random small reward
  1. Continue
oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_defendB

You power up your weapons and make your presence known to the Hegemon ship. Its capabilities are quite obviously outmatched by your own and they have no choice but to back down.

  1. Continue
    oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_defendB_2

    The colony prefect thanks you. "The damned Hegemon bastards are getting worse every year. We owe you our lives."

    • Random small reward
    1. Continue
oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_defendC

You ready your weapons for combat when a second ship drops in, bearing the markings of the Imperial Tetrarchy. It unleashes a torrent of cannon fire at the Hegemon ship and neutralizes it. It then turns its weapons on you. "Leave now, or suffer the consequences."

  1. Explain that the colonists had requested your help
    oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_explainHelp

    "The Tetrarchy can take care of their own. You, on the other hand, are flying an unauthorized vessel in Imperial space. If you refuse to leave, we will be forced to open fire."

    1. Refuse
      oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_explainRefuse

      "So be it."

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_explainRefuse2

      You make contact with the colony prefect once more, who is by now terrified beyond reason. He quickly orders his men to deliver a generous shipment of supplies to your ship.

      • Random large reward
      1. Continue
    2. Leave
      oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_explainLeave

      You decide not to press the matter.

      1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_leave

    You decide to leave.

    1. Continue
oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_ignore

You decide not to get involved.

  1. Continue

HegemonColonyThreat_contactA

→ oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_contactA_2

HegemonColonyThreat_contactB

→ oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_contactB_2

HegemonColonyThreat_defendA

→ oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_defendA_2

HegemonColonyThreat_defendB

HotObject

oTxtHotObject_intro

You detect an unknown object in the distance. Its temperature readings are abnormally high.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtHotObject_investigateD

      The object begins to glow brightly. Suddenly, it erupts in a flash of blinding light. It grows larger and larger, threatening to pull your ship into its gravity well. You overhear chatter over your comms, and are soon greeted by a technocult science ship. "...fusion test 773.1 is a resounding success. However the results have been contaminated by a foreign entity. Commencing termination of the entity."

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Techno ship
    • oTxtHotObject_investigateB

      You approach the object and discover it to be a starship of the blood cults. It is burning. There may be survivors aboard.

      1. Send one of your crew to rescue them
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtHotObject_investigateB_1

          Your crewman heroically sprints through the flames and successfully rescues several people aboard the ship. One of the survivors offers to join your crew.

          • Blood Priest, Elder Blood Mage, Ritualist, Mouth of Ariok, or Blood Golem
          1. Continue
        • oTxtHotObject_investigateB_2

          Your crewman dashes through the flames and attempts to rescue the survivors. They return with severe burns. Unfortunately they were only able to recover a single husk.

          • Husk
          • Random crew damaged (90%)
          1. Continue
      2. Attack them
        oTxtHotObject_attackBlood

        You engage the vessel.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Blood ship
      3. Send your [fire-immune crew member] to rescue them
        oTxtHotObject_investigateB_immune

        [fire-immune crew member] walks effortlessly through the flames and manages to rescue one of the crew.

        • Blood Priest, Elder Blood Mage, Ritualist, Mouth of Ariok, or Blood Golem
        1. Continue
      4. Rescue them yourself
        oTxtHotObject_investigateB_immunePlayer

        You walk effortlessly through the flames and manage to rescue one of the crew.

        • Blood Priest, Elder Blood Mage, Ritualist, Mouth of Ariok, or Blood Golem
        1. Continue
    • oTxtHotObject_investigateC

      You approach the object and determine it to be a cult warship. It is on fire. There may be survivors aboard.

      1. Send one of your crew to rescue them
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtHotObject_investigateC_1

          Your crewman heroically sprints through the flames and successfully rescues several people aboard the ship. One of the survivors offers to join your crew.

          • War Cult crew
          1. Continue
        • oTxtHotObject_investigateC_2

          Your crewman dashes through the flames and attempts to rescue the survivors. They return with severe burns. Unfortunately they were only able to recover a single husk.

          • Husk
          • Random crew damaged (75%)
          1. Continue
      2. Attack them
        oTxtHotObject_attackWar

        You engage the burning starship.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs War ship
      3. Send your [fire-immune crew member] to rescue them
        oTxtHotObject_investigateC_immune

        [fire-immune crew member] walks effortlessly through the flames and manages to rescue one of the crew.

        • War Cult crew
        1. Continue
      4. Rescue them yourself
        oTxtHotObject_investigateC_immunePlayer

        You walk effortlessly through the flames and manage to rescue one of the crew.

        • War Cult crew
        1. Continue
    • oTxtHotObject_investigateA

      You approach the object and determine it to be a starship of the death cults. It is on fire. There may be survivors aboard.

      1. Send one of your crew to rescue them
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtHotObject_investigateA_1

          Your crewman heroically sprints through the flames and successfully rescues several people aboard the ship. One of the survivors offers to join your crew.

          • Gorgothian Legionnaire, Death Cultist, Death Mage, Necromancer, or Mortician
          1. Continue
        • oTxtHotObject_investigateA_2

          Your crewman dashes through the flames and attempts to rescue the survivors. They return with severe burns. Unfortunately they were only able to recover a single husk.

          • Husk
          • Random crew damaged (75%)
          1. Continue
      2. Attack them
        oTxtHotObject_attackDeath

        You power up your weapons.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Plague ship
      3. Send your [fire-immune crew member] to rescue them
        oTxtHotObject_investigateA_immune

        [fire-immune crew member] walks effortlessly through the flames and manages to rescue one of the crew.

        • Gorgothian Legionnaire, Death Cultist, Death Mage, Necromancer, or Mortician
        1. Continue
      4. Rescue them yourself
        oTxtHotObject_investigateA_immunePlayer

        You walk effortlessly through the flames and manage to rescue one of the crew.

        • Gorgothian Legionnaire, Death Cultist, Death Mage, Necromancer, or Mortician
        1. Continue
  2. Ignore it
    oTxtHotObject_ignore

    You ignore it.

    1. Continue
