Hulk

oTxtHulk Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You encounter a massive, derelict hulk. There appears to be an intact vault aboard.

ImperialScholar

oTxtImperialScholar_intro

You approach an abandoned research installation and encounter a solitary Imperial scholar. "I require access to a crucial psychomancy held within, but the auto-defenses are too strong." It is rare for the Imperials to request help from outside their own ranks, but the scholar's desperation is evident.

  1. Assist
    Random outcome (1/5 each):
    • oTxtImperialScholar_assistC

      Tragically, the scholar falls to a stray shot from a turret. You proceed and discover a strange psychomantic wafer from within the archive.

      • Random cult psychic item
      1. Continue
    • oTxtImperialScholar_assistB

      You assist the scholar in disabling the defenses and accessing the archives, but the psychomancy he finds is not the one he sought. He gifts it to you in lieu of payment.

      • Random Imperial psychic item
      1. Continue
    • oTxtImperialScholar_assistA

      You methodically dismantle the station's auto-defenses, allowing the scholar to obtain a psychomantic wafer from the archives. In gratitude, he compensates you for your assistance.

      • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
    • oTxtImperialScholar_assistE

      You are in the midst of disarming the auto-defenses when an Imperial warship arrives. "Halt! None shall unseal the heresy contained within!" You quickly return to your vessel and engage the attacker.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtImperialScholar_assistE_1

        Unfortunately the station was destroyed in the engagement. You discover the Imperial scholar had followed you aboard. He pledges to serve under your command.

        • Recruit enemy commander as crew
        1. Continue
      • oTxtImperialScholar_assistE_2

        Unfortunately the station was obliterated in the engagement, and the scholar along with it. You move on.

        1. Continue
    • oTxtImperialScholar_assistD

      You methodically dismantle the station's auto-defenses. After obtaining a mysterious psychomancy from the archives, the scholar undergoes a hideous transformation and emerges as an otherworldly horror.

      1. Continue
        oTxtImperialScholar_assistD_1

        You retreat to your vessel, but discover the horrid creature has followed you aboard as well.

        • 1 Strong Demonic Crew boarder
        1. Continue
        After the boarders are defeated:
        oTxtImperialScholar_assistD_2

        The creature is defeated. You recover a strange-looking weapon from its rapidly dissolving corpse.

        • Random cult weapon
        1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtImperialScholar_leave

    You cut communications and move on.

    1. Continue
  3. (Empire Psyker) Send an Imperial crewmember to work with the scholar
    oTxtImperialScholar_empirePsyker1

    You dispatch your Imperial psyker to help the scholar with his request. Their presence seems to agitate the scholar. Your crewman quickly realizes something is amiss and dispatches the scholar without ceremony.

    1. Continue
      oTxtImperialScholar_empirePsyker2

      You learn the facility was constructed for the containment of dangerous psychomancies. Your crewmember has recovered the forbidden knowledge held within the vaults, suggesting it would be safer to secure such dangerous artifacts aboard your vessel.

      • Summon Fiend or Summon Necrolurch or Doom Ritual
      1. Continue
  4. (Cult Psyker) Send a cultist psyker to aid the scholar
    oTxtImperialScholar_cultPsyker1

    You dispatch a cultist crewmember to help the scholar. Together, they perform unholy rituals to disarm the automatic defenses. In time they gain access to the vault containing the psychomancy sought by the scholar.

    1. Continue
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtImperialScholar_cultPsyker2b

        Deeply impressed by the abilities of your crewman, the Imperial scholar begs to join your crew. They bring with them equipment unexpected of an Imperial adherent.

        • Random cult armor
        • Imperial Acolyte
        1. Continue
      • oTxtImperialScholar_cultPsyker2a

        Deeply impressed by the abilities of your crewman, the Imperial scholar begs to join your crew. They bring with them some equipment unexpected of an Imperial adherent.

        • Random cult consumable
        • Imperial Scribe
        1. Continue

ImperialScholar_assistD

→ oTxtImperialScholar_assistD_1

ImperialScholar_assistE

→ oTxtImperialScholar_assistE_1

ImperialTransportPair

oTxtImperialTransportPair_intro

You re-enter realspace near an Imperial expedition fleet. A couple of Imperial courier ships have broken away from the main fleet. One is carrying supplies, and the other appears to be carrying a reinforced stasis chamber containing a prisoner. You will be able to attack one of the vessels before reinforcements arrive.

  1. Attack the one transporting supplies
    oTxtImperialTransportPair_secureSupplies1

    You prepare to engage.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Empire ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtImperialTransportPair_secureSupplies2

    You recover the supplies from the remains.

    • Random equipment item
    1. Continue
  2. Attack the one transporting a prisoner
    oTxtImperialTransportPair_securePrisoner1

    You prime your weapons.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Empire ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtImperialTransportPair_securePrisoner2

    You rescue the prisoner and recruit them to your crew.

    • Any Blood Cult crew or Any War Cult crew or Any Technocult crew or Any Death Cult crew or Any Imperial crew or Any Raider crew
    1. Continue
  3. Let them pass
    oTxtImperialTransportPair_letPass

    You decide to let them pass, not wanting to risk a confrontation with Imperial forces.

    1. Continue

InfestCambion

oTxtInfestCambion_intro

You identify a distress signal originating from a nearby vessel.

  1. Respond
    Random outcome (1/5 each):
    • oTxtInfestCambion_respondB

      After hailing the distressed vessel several times with no response, you decide to deploy a boarding party. They search the ship and return with an unusual relic. Unfortunately something malevolent has come back with them.

      • Random cult equipment
      • 1–2 Fiend boarders
      1. Continue
    • oTxtInfestCambion_respondE

      You learn the crew of the distressed vessel is having some trouble with their engines. You transfer over some of your spare flux capacitors and manage to help them repair their ship. They offer you a small reward for your help.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtInfestCambion_respondA

      As you approach the distressed vessel, a blast of psychic energy explodes outwards from its hull, damaging one of your systems. You hear a psychic screech in the back of your mind.

      1. Continue
        oTxtInfestCambion_respondA2

        To make matters worse, your intruder alarms go off. Infernal entities have been detected in the lower decks.

        • 1 Fiend boarder
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Possessed ship
    • oTxtInfestCambion_respondC

      You board the vessel and discover a ragged officer hunched over the bloody corpses of several Imperial dignitaries. She appears to be eating them. Suddenly, the officer lunges at you. You open fire at her, shredding her emaciated body before she is able to close the distance.

      1. Continue
        oTxtInfestCambion_respondC2

        Unfortunately the officer is not yet dead. Dark energy inflates her limbs like balloons. She crawls up on all fours while gibbering goblin-like creatures leap off her back. They charge forward, emitting guttural cries.

        1. Continue
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • oTxtInfestCambion_respondC2_3

            You retreat back to your ship but are unable to prevent the monster from climbing aboard.

            • 1 Fiend boarder
            1. Continue
            After the boarders are defeated:
            oTxtInfestCambion_respondC2_3B

            You manage to secure some material from the now-abandoned vessel.

            • Random small reward
            1. Continue
          • oTxtInfestCambion_respondC2_2

            You make a hasty retreat back to your ship. Several of the smaller creatures manage to scuttle aboard before you seal the hatch.

            • 3–6 Fleshling boarders
            1. Continue
            After the boarders are defeated:
            oTxtInfestCambion_respondC2_2B

            You manage to recover a supply container from the distressed vessel.

            • Random small reward
            1. Continue
    • oTxtInfestCambion_respondD

      As you approach the distressed starship, an Imperial patrol craft drops in from subspace and stops you. "Unknown vessel, cease your approach. This ship has been tainted by infernal energies."

      1. Continue
        oTxtInfestCambion_respondD2

        Before you can respond, a blast of infernal lightning tears out from the distressed vessel and strikes the patrol craft. You watch with horror as the officer on your feed undergoes a grotesque transformation, his flesh melting together like wax. His last words are, "Buuuurn... the unclean... "

        1. Continue
          oTxtInfestCambion_respondD3

          The distressed vessel disintegrates. The corrupted Imperial ship turns its guns on you, its command throne now possessed by a demonic spirit. To make matters worse, your console indicates that an intruder has been detected in the lower decks.

          • 3–4 Fleshling boarders
          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Possessed ship
  2. Ignore
    oTxtInfestCambion_ignore

    You ignore the signal.

    1. Continue

InfestCambion_respondC2

→ oTxtInfestCambion_respondC2_2

InfestEidolon

oTxtInfestEidolon_intro

You arrive in-system near a small orbital colony. Preliminary scans show that the colony is currently performing an emergency lockdown. Their communications array appears to be damaged and none of the docked ships are responding to hails.

  1. Investigate the colony
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtInfestEidolon_investigateC

      You approach the colony and identify a lone starship in the process of undocking. You manage to establish a tightbeam connection with the ship. The person on the other end is shaking with fear. "We are the last survivors. Oh Throne, they have taken our ship! They..."

      1. Continue
        oTxtInfestEidolon_investigateC2

        Suddenly you hear maniacal cackling over the comms.You see a glimpse of the crew scrambling to defend themselves against skeletal apparitions. Then the feed cuts out. An explosion of dark energy rocks the ship. Warning lights flash and you realize you are being targeted.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Empire ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        oTxtInfestEidolon_investigateC3

        You salvage some material from the now-deserted colony.

        • Random small reward
        1. Continue
    • oTxtInfestEidolon_investigateA

      You board the colony and find it completely deserted. No bodies, no blood, no signs of struggle. It is as if the people had simply vanished.

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtInfestEidolon_investigateA_2

          You return to your ship empty-handed. Halfway through your ship's initialization routines, you feel a sudden jolt of fear -- you are being watched. Someone or something has followed you back to your ship. You sound the alarms and prepare for a fight.

          • 1 Doom Effigy boarder
          • 3–5 Skeleton boarders
          1. Continue
        • oTxtInfestEidolon_investigateA_1

          You return to your ship with a few old supply containers scavenged from one of the loading docks.

          • Random moderate reward
          1. Continue
            oTxtInfestEidolon_investigateA_1b

            After securing the containers, you feel a strange sense of foreboding. You suddenly realize that something sinister has followed you back to your ship.

            • 3 Doom Effigy boarders
            1. Continue
    • oTxtInfestEidolon_investigateB

      You investigate the colony and learn that it has been overrun by cultists. The colony's prefect must have locked down the colony in accordance with standard emergency protocols, not realizing that doing so would create the perfect killing grounds for the bloodthirsty worshippers of Krom.

      1. Continue
        oTxtInfestEidolon_investigateB2

        With the colonists boxed in, the cultists quickly slaughtered any who sought to contain the violence. They began performing savage rituals in reverence of their god, instilling hatred and fear while systematically orchestrating the colony's descent into madness. You consider your options.

        1. Destroy the colony and exterminate the cultists
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • oTxtInfestEidolon_investigateB2_a

            You open fire with all weapons at the colony's fusion pylons. It ignites a chain reaction which cascades through the colony's superstructure, disintegrating everything in its path.

            1. Continue
              Random outcome (1/2 each):
              • oTxtInfestEidolon_investigateB2_a2

                Nothing of value can be recovered from the wreckage.

                1. Continue
              • oTxtInfestEidolon_investigateB2_a1

                You recover a small amount of material from the wreckage.

                • Random small reward
                1. Continue
          • oTxtInfestEidolon_investigateB2_b

            You unleash your ship's full complement of weapons at the colony. Unfortunately none of your shots connect. Each one is repelled at the last moment by a mysterious force.

            1. Continue
              oTxtInfestEidolon_investigateB2_b1

              A possessed starship suddenly emerges from hiding, weapons armed. You receive a transmission. "I cannot allow you to ruin our work here."

              1. Continue
              Fight! vs Possessed ship
              After the rewards from the fight:
              oTxtInfestEidolon_investigateB2_b2

              The red fog surrounding the colony grows in size until it swallows up the entire structure. The fog then dissipates, and you realize that the colony is gone, whisked away by the dark machinations of the war cults.

              1. Continue
        2. Leave them be
          oTxtInfestEidolon_leaveThem

          You decide to leave them alone. Whatever it is they are doing, it is none of your business.

          1. Continue
  2. Ignore it and move on
    oTxtInfestEidolon_ignore

    You move on.

    1. Continue

InfestEidolon_investigateA

→ oTxtInfestEidolon_investigateA_1

InfestEidolon_investigateB2

→ oTxtInfestEidolon_investigateB2_a

InfestGolem

oTxtInfestGolem_intro

You encounter a starship maneuvering erratically, though it does not appear to be engaged in combat.

  1. Hail the ship
    Random outcome (1/6 each):
    • oTxtInfestGolem_hailF

      You hail the starship several times but receive no response. You decide to scuttle the ship for raw material.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtInfestGolem_hailD

      You attempt to make contact with the ship. Unfortunately its erratic trajectory brings it straight into the path of a large asteroid. It explodes in a ball of fire. Nothing can be recovered from the remains.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtInfestGolem_hailB

      You hail the ship. The only response you receive is a minutes-long audio capture of shrill, inhuman screeching. You do not know what to make of this, but it does not matter as you soon realize the ship has begun leveling its weapons at you.

      1. Continue
        oTxtInfestGolem_hailB2

        As you rush to battle stations, you notice strange, red figures standing ominously in the shadows. They do not look friendly.

        • 1–2 Blood Golem boarders
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Empire Raider ship
    • oTxtInfestGolem_hailE

      You hail the ship multiple times. There is no response. You decide to dock with the ship. You engage the clamps and release the locks, preparing for whatever might lie inside.

      1. Continue
        oTxtInfestGolem_hailE2

        A bloody figure stands in the open hatch. On the floor behind it are the mangled corpses of several Imperial biomancers. The figure stares at you silently, as if awaiting your command.

        • Blood Golem
        1. Continue
    • oTxtInfestGolem_hailC

      You establish a tightbeam link and hear only the sound of screaming and gunfire. Minutes pass, and both the noise and erratic maneuvering subside. Your viewscreen flickers on, revealing a crimson figure. It does not speak, but somehow you are certain it is hostile.

      1. Continue
        oTxtInfestGolem_hailC2

        As you prepare for battle, you notice strange red figures standing motionlessly in the shadows. They definitely were not there before. And they do not look friendly.

        • 1–2 Blood Golem boarders
        1. Continue
    • oTxtInfestGolem_hailA

      After several attempts, you are able to make contact with someone aboard the ship. It seems to have been commandeered by a deranged cultist. "This ship and its crew are now under my control. The time of ascension has arrived! Ngh'lth, rgh'lweh'kz yrg'thc!"

      1. Continue
        oTxtInfestGolem_hailA2

        The cultist invokes a foul incantation, bathing their crew in sorcerous energy. To make matter worse, your intruders alarms suddenly go off. The devil-worshippers must have somehow already breached your hull.

        • 1–2 Imperial Recruit, Imperial Enforcer, Imperial Marshal, Imperial Scribe, or Imperial Acolyte or Raider crew boarders
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Blood ship
  2. Avoid the ship
    oTxtInfestGolem_avoid

    You maneuver your ship away.

    1. Continue

InfestHound

oTxtInfestHound_intro

A raider transport vessel broadcasts a distress signal.

  1. Investigate the signal
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtInfestHound_investigateB

      As you close the distance to the raider ship, its reactor suddenly flares up with a blinding red light. Malevolent energy pours out, bathing your vessel in cursed lightning. Infernal beasts begin crawling out from the various nooks and crannies inside your ship. Warning lights blare, indicating that the raider command throne has been reactivated by what can only be supernatural means.

      • 1–3 Hound boarders
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider ship
    • oTxtInfestHound_investigateC

      You make contact with the raiders. They are panicking. "I knew we should not have opened those damned kennels! These blasted hounds, they are unnatural!"

      1. Continue
        oTxtInfestHound_investigateC2

        The raiders launch a variety of assault pods and lifeboats at your ship, desperate to use whatever means they can to escape their attackers. Unfortunately they end up bringing several of their attackers with them.

        • 1–3 Hound boarders
        1. Continue
    • oTxtInfestHound_investigateA

      You approach the transport and immediately hear shouts and gunfire over the comms. The commotion suddenly stops. You hear a gravelly voice in the background. "Your souls now belong to Krom. Now prove your worth in battle, or die in ignominy."

      1. Continue
        oTxtInfestHound_investigateA2

        You can only assume that whoever has gained control of the ship means to attack you. Before you can react, several assault pods slam into your hull. Dark energy streams off the pod's occupants, suggesting that something unusually sinister is afoot.

        • 1–3 Possessed Corsair boarders
        Fight! vs Raider ship
    • oTxtInfestHound_investigateD

      You board the raider transport to find the entire crew dead. You learn that they were transporting an assortment of beasts to the fighting pits on the far side of the local asteroid belt. But something went wrong. One of the beasts must have escaped its cage and slaughtered its captors, along with every other creature aboard the ship.

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtInfestHound_investigateE

          You return to your vessel to realize that whatever killed those raiders is now running amok on your ship.

          • 2–4 Hound boarders
          1. Continue
        • oTxtInfestHound_investigateD2

          When you return to your vessel, you discover a strange canine-like creature waiting there for you. It seems intimidated by your strength.

          • Hound
          1. Continue
  2. Ignore it
    oTxtInfestHound_ignore

    You do not want to have anything to do with these raiders.

    1. Continue

InfestHound_investigateC2

oTxtInfestHound_investigateC2_a Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Most of the raiders were mauled to death by the demon hounds during their escape. The few that survived sustained vicious injuries and died shortly afterward.

  1. Continue
oTxtInfestHound_investigateC2_b Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Only a single raider survived the demonic attack. With nowhere else to go, they decide to join your crew.

  • Raider crew
  1. Continue

InfestHuntsman

oTxtInfestHuntsman_intro

A starship bursts out from a turbulent rift storm. The exterior running lights flicker for a moment before going dark.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtInfestHuntsman_investigateA

      "Immortal King save us--" The brief transmission is cut off by a gurgle and the crunching of bones. The starship is overrun by massive, hound-like beasts.

      1. Continue
        oTxtInfestHuntsman_investigateA2

        You detect fighting aboard the ship.

        1. Continue
    • oTxtInfestHuntsman_investigateB

      The vessel attempts to ram you. You maneuver away, but the enemy nevertheless manages to scrape your hull, ripping away plating and exposing the underlying decks.

      • −0–1 hull HP
      • 1 breach spawned
      1. Continue
        oTxtInfestHuntsman_investigateB2

        A few massive, hound-like horrors that were clinging to the exterior of the enemy starship leap through the gash in your hull. They begin rampaging through your ship, hunting for your crew.

        • 1–3 Hound boarders
        1. Continue
    • oTxtInfestHuntsman_investigateC

      You receive a hail from the blacked-out starship. Through your viewscreen you see the visage of a Kromic warrior. "At last... I have been hunting you for weeks."

      1. Defend yourself
        oTxtInfestHuntsman_defend

        You go to battle stations and prepare to engage the enemy.

        1. Continue
          oTxtInfestHuntsman_defend2

          The Kromic warrior makes a series of strange gestures. Dark energy begins pooling around her. The energy then streams out from her ship and impacts your hull, before materializing into horrific canine beasts.

          • 1–3 Hound boarders
          1. Continue
      2. Attempt to flee
        oTxtInfestHuntsman_flee

        You look for options to deter pursuit.

        1. Head for the asteroid belt
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • oTxtInfestHuntsman_headAsteroid2

            You are unable to shake off the enemy vessel, and have little choice but to turn and engage.

            1. Continue
          • oTxtInfestHuntsman_headAsteroid1

            You command flank speed into the asteroid field and use every trick you know to mask your wake. You camouflage your ship upon an asteroid. The enemy ship fails to notice you and speeds into the distance.

            1. Continue
        2. Head for a blighted nebula
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • oTxtInfestHuntsman_headBlight1

            You dive into a blight nebula. Clouds of toxic, corrosive gas emerge from subspace within your hull. The Kromic warrior seems loath to follow.

            1. Continue
          • oTxtInfestHuntsman_headBlight2

            You dive into a blight nebula. Your pursuer follows you in, without hesitation. They fire a volley of shots and manage to land a glancing blow on your engines. You turn your vessel to defend yourself.

            • 1 system damage to Engines
            1. Continue
            After the rewards from the fight:
            oTxtInfestHuntsman_headBlight2A

            Just as you are concluding salvage operations you notice the flesh of the fallen Kromic warrior dissolving into a mass of viscous sludge. The corpse reaches into its decaying torso and draws forth a corroded sword. An otherworldly voice issues forth from the corpse: "You have done well to put these mad dogs to rest, child."

            • Plague Sword
            1. Continue
        3. Head for the corona of the nearest star
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • oTxtInfestHuntsman_headStar2

            The intense heat of the star does not deter the Kromic warrior. The enemy vessel gains on you, and you are forced to turn and engage.

            1. Continue
          • oTxtInfestHuntsman_headStar1

            You direct your ship into the intense heat of a solar corona. Your pursuer seems determined to follow, but soon you see his ship lashed by superheated plasma. The enemy vessel is set alight, and pulls off from its pursuit.

            1. Continue
        4. Head for a rift storm
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • oTxtInfestHuntsman_headRift2

            You dive into a roiling rift storm. A subspace breach yawns ahead of you, and in an instant, you are transported away from your pursuer.

            1. Continue
          • oTxtInfestHuntsman_headRift1

            You dive into a roiling rift storm. Subspace lightning crackles around your ship. Your sensors can hardly detect anything through the interference, but the Kromic warrior somehow finds you in short order. "I can sense your fear."

            1. Continue
  2. Avoid the starship
oTxtInfestHuntsman_avoid Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You give the drifting starship a wide berth and press onwards.

  1. Continue

InfestHydra

oTxtInfestHydra_intro

You pick up a distress signal coming from a nearby outpost.

  1. Respond
    Random outcome (1/5 each):
    • oTxtInfestHydra_respond1

      You arrive at the outpost and deploy a boarding team. The team returns with nothing but a few lumigraphs showing evidence that a grisly massacre must have taken place here.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtInfestHydra_respond3

      You board the outpost and are instantly beset by masses of writhing tentacles. A survivor from the outpost manages to scramble onto your ship, but not without bringing a few tentacles with them.

      • Random prisoner crew
      • 2–4 Stygian Worm boarders
      1. Continue
    • oTxtInfestHydra_respond4

      You arrive at the outpost to discover that its inhabitants have been massacred. There is little to recover, though you do manage to secure an intact supply container from one of the storage warehouses.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtInfestHydra_respond2

      You hail the outpost. A terrified worker responds, "Throne save us, those serpent beasts... they're everywhere!"

      1. Continue
        oTxtInfestHydra_respond2B

        The feed cuts out. A small explosion rocks a nearby ship docked to the outpost, causing it to slip its moorings. It drifts slowly toward you.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Empire ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        oTxtInfestHydra_respond2B_afterFight

        You return to the outpost and learn that everyone is dead. There is nothing of use for you to recover.

        1. Continue
        Trigger (CurrentPlayerBoardsEnemy):
        oTxtInfestHydra_respond2B_board

        The enemy ship's weapons suddenly come online, as if under the command of some invisible, malign presence.

        1. Continue
    • oTxtInfestHydra_respond5

      As you approach the outpost, a moored Imperial starship disengages its docking clamps and begins turning toward you. Its commander does not appear to be of Imperial origin. "I see you have brought more life essence with which to feed the children of Ariok. Very well. I will be happy to oblige."

      1. Continue
        oTxtInfestHydra_respond5B

        The sorcerer speaks a flurry of impossible words causing crimson energy to pool around the deck of your ship. Strange snake-like creatures rise out from the pools.

        • 2–4 Stygian Worm boarders
        1. Continue
        After the rewards from the fight:
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtInfestHydra_respond5B_after1

          It seems the outpost has collapsed after having sustained major structural damage. There is nothing of use for you to recover.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtInfestHydra_respond5B_after2

          The outpost collapses after suffering significant structural damage. You recover some material from the wreckage.

          • Random small reward
          1. Continue
  2. Ignore it
    oTxtInfestHydra_ignore

    You decide to ignore the signal.

    1. Continue
    2. Ignore it

InfestHydra_respond2B

→ oTxtInfestHydra_respond2B_afterFight

InfestHydra_respond5B

→ oTxtInfestHydra_respond5B_after1

InfestImp

oTxtInfestImp_intro

A dilapidated vessel appears in the distance. Its engines appear to be idling and it does not seem to have the usual ident codes marking its origin.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/5 each):
    • oTxtInfestImp_investigateC

      You board the vessel and find it infested with a legion of chittering flesh horrors. Some of them follow you back to your ship.

      • 4–6 Fleshling boarders
      1. Continue
      After the boarders are defeated:
      oTxtInfestImp_investigateC2

      With the horrors dealt with, you proceed to scrap the ship for useful material.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtInfestImp_investigateE

      You maneuver in for a closer look. As you approach, the ship begins to disintegrate, apparently undergoing some sort of reactor meltdown. By the time you arrive, it is no more than a heap of useless slag.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtInfestImp_investigateA

      Upon closer examination, you discern that the vessel is heavily infested with unnatural growths riddling its structure. Nevertheless, the ship appears inert, and a more thorough scan uncovers the existence of an intact vault aboard the ship.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Possessed ship
      After the fight:
      oTxtInfestImp_investigateA_win

      The infested derelict has been obliterated, reduced to a scattered field of shredded metal and grotesque flesh. Nothing of value remains. You move on.

      1. Continue
      Trigger (CurrentPlayerBoardsEnemy):
      oTxtInfestImp_investigateA2

      As soon as you step foot on the ship, a hideous giggling emanates from the walls. The ship's weapons come online and small clumps of flesh begin raining down from the ceiling. You are under attack.

      • 3–6 Fleshling boarders
      1. Continue
    • oTxtInfestImp_investigateD

      You perform a more detailed scan of the ship and learn that its crew was attacked by vicious packs of chittering subspace entities. The ship's command throne has been possessed by some infernal presence, however there may still be surviving members of the original crew aboard. To save them, you would first have to deal with the possessed starship.

      1. Attack the starship
        oTxtInfestImp_attack

        You arm your weapons. As you move into attack range, the possessed starship summons a cloud of profane energy unto your ship. Gibbering creatures spill out from the miasma.

        • 5–6 Fleshling boarders
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Possessed ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtInfestImp_attackA

          You manage to save a member of the ship's crew.

          • Imperial Recruit, Imperial Enforcer, or Imperial Marshal or Corsair or Scavenger
          1. Continue
        • oTxtInfestImp_attackB

          You manage to save a member of the ship's crew.

          • Imperial Recruit, Imperial Enforcer, or Imperial Marshal or Corsair or Scavenger
          1. Continue
      2. Get out of there
        oTxtInfestImp_getOut

        You decide the crew is not worth saving.

        1. Continue
    • oTxtInfestImp_investigateB

      The ship's commander appears on your feed. "Oh throne, the damn witch, she's cursed us somehow. Those... things....they are inside us!"

      1. Continue
        oTxtInfestImp_investigateB2

        You try to calm him to no avail. He continues babbling, becoming more and more incoherent, until eventually you notice his eyes twinkling with an air of supernatural malice. The commander target locks your ship. "Our souls... they... belong to the Blood Prince now."

        1. Continue
          oTxtInfestImp_investigateB3

          Enemy boarders have been detected on the lower decks. They seem to be drenched in infernal energies.

          • 1–2 Imperial Recruit, Imperial Enforcer, Imperial Marshal, Imperial Scribe, or Imperial Acolyte boarders
          1. Continue
  2. Avoid it
    oTxtInfestImp_avoid

    You decide to stay clear of the vessel.

    1. Continue

InfestImp_investigateA

→ oTxtInfestImp_investigateA_win

ItemSlotToUnequip

oTxtItemSlotToUnequip Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Your crew must unequip an item before equipping a new one. Which item do you wish to unequip?

JewelCivilianFleeRaider

oTxtJewelCivilianFleeRaider_intro

An Imperial civilian vessel is fleeing from a corsair warship. The corsairs contact you. "If the Empire learns of these worlds, then all will be lost. The seas will be drained, the forests stripped bare. Do not let them escape."

  1. Target the civilians
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtJewelCivilianFleeRaider_civilians

      You target and destroy the civilian vessel. The corsair commander thanks you for your help and allows you to claim the wreckage of the civilian ship.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelCivilianFleeRaider_civilians3

      You blast the civilian ship to pieces. Without warning, a subspace fissure rips open and disgorges a Kromic warship. It lets out a vicious salvo of energy and obliterates the corsairs. "I smell battle."

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs War ship
    • oTxtJewelCivilianFleeRaider_civilians2

      You target and destroy the civilian vessel. Suddenly cannon fire streaks out from behind a nearby dust cloud and destroys the corsair ship. The newcomer bears the markings of the Imperial Solar Hegemon. "Those who attack the servants of the Immortal King will be met with a swift end."

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
    • oTxtJewelCivilianFleeRaider_civilians4

      You start firing at the civilians. They contact you. "Stop! We stumbled into this system by accident and are only trying to get back to our homes. We do not care about any of this. We will not say a thing to the Arbitrators."

      1. Let them go
        oTxtJewelCivilianFleeRaider_letGo

        The civilian ship powers up its subspace drive and jumps out. The corsairs are furious. "You do not realize the damage you have done by letting them escape. These worlds are no longer safe."

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Raider ship
      2. Destroy them
        oTxtJewelCivilianFleeRaider_destroy

        You release the firing locks and annihilate the civilian ship. The corsairs congratulate you on a clean kill and allow you to keep the salvage.

        • Random small reward
        1. Continue
  2. Target the corsairs
    oTxtJewelCivilianFleeRaider_corsairs

    "No... you do not realize what you are doing."

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Raider ship
  3. Ignore them

JewelNothing

oTxtJewelNothing_intro

[...]

  1. Continue

JewelRaiderFight

oTxtJewelRaiderFight_intro

"You do not belong here. The Great Clans have claimed these worlds as their own."

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Raider ship

JewelRaiderQuestion

oTxtJewelRaiderQuestion_intro

You are approaching the orbit of a small, ocean planet when a corsair vessel drops in on your location, weapons armed. They demand answers. "Identify yourself."

  1. Tell them you are nobody
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtJewelRaiderQuestion_nobody2

      "I do not believe you."

      1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelRaiderQuestion_nobody

      "Do not ...lie... to me!"

      1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelRaiderQuestion_nobody3

      "Good. Let's keep it that way. Now get out of here."

      • Enemy becomes friendly
      1. Continue
  2. Tell them you represent the Empire
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtJewelRaiderQuestion_trader2

      "You realize we are pirates, right?"

      • 1–2 Corsair or Scavenger boarders
      1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelRaiderQuestion_empire

      "What? The Empire has discovered us? You will not leave this place alive..."

      1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelRaiderQuestion_trader

      "A trader? Here? When did traders start packing that much firepower?

      1. It is a dangerous galaxy out there
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtJewelRaiderQuestion_dangerous

          "I don't buy it."

          1. Continue
        • oTxtJewelRaiderQuestion_dangerous2

          "You have a point. I will let you go this time."

          • Enemy becomes friendly
          1. Continue
  3. Tell them you are just a trader passing by
  4. Tell them you are a servant of the dark gods
    oTxtJewelRaiderQuestion_darkGods

    "We do not need your superstitious nonsense here."

    1. Continue

JewelShangriLa

oTxtJewelShangriLa_intro

There is a raider starship in orbit around a lush planet. You overhear some panicked chatter from within. They seem to be dealing with some mysterious intruders.

  1. Aid the ship
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtJewelShangriLa_aidA

      You approach the raider starship. Suddenly you hear an explosion and realize you have been boarded. The distressed chatter was all a ruse.

      • 2–3 Corsair, Scavenger, Infiltrator, or Gene Soldier boarders
      1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelShangriLa_aidB

      You draw closer to the raider ship and find them attempting to repel supernatural entities. Unfortunately, your approach has attracted the entities to your own vessel.

      • 2–4 Demonic Crew boarders
      1. Continue
      After the boarders are defeated:
      oTxtJewelShangriLa_aidB_a

      The raiders are thankful for your intervention. "We were searching for the planet Kivira, where it is said that even the poorest denizen lives in eternal idyll, in crystalline palaces of diamond and gold. Why do we not look for it together? I am certain there would be more than enough riches for both of us."

      1. Agree
        oTxtJewelShangriLa_questAgree

        "Great. Here are the coordinates to another likely location. If you find it, relay us a fatbeam message."

        • Quest marker added to map: oTxtJewelShangriLaQuest_intro
        1. Continue
      2. Disagree
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtJewelShangriLa_questDeclineB

          You decline the offer and move on.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtJewelShangriLa_questDeclineA

          You decline the offer, and return to cleaning up after the earlier battle. You recover a weapon among the remains.

          • Random cult armor
          1. Continue
  2. Attack the ship
    oTxtJewelShangriLa_attack

    You move in to attack.

    • 2–3 Demonic Crew boarders
    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Raider ship
  3. Ignore
    oTxtJewelShangriLa_introLeaveA

    As you leave, the raider starship starts tumbling end over end, then lights its main thrusters. It violently deorbits, plunging into the atmosphere of the lush planet below.

    1. Continue
      oTxtJewelShangriLa_introLeaveB

      You shut off the comms and move on.

      1. Continue

JewelShangriLaQuest

oTxtJewelShangriLaQuest_intro

You arrive at the coordinates given by the treasure seeking raiders.

  1. Continue
    Random outcome (1/7 each):
    • oTxtJewelShangriLaQuest_intro8

      There is nothing here but a large riftstorm. A subspace flare erupts near your ship, igniting a few flux conduits.

      • 1–3 fires spawned
      1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelShangriLaQuest_intro7

      There is nothing here but a large riftstorm. A subspace flare suddenly ruptures one of your flux capacitors, injuring your crew.

      • Random crew damaged (50%)
      1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelShangriLaQuest_intro5

      An Imperial starship arrives soon after. Perhaps they are also looking for Kivira. You do not get a chance to ask before they open fire.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtJewelShangriLaQuest_intro5_a

      With the enemy neutralized, you proceed down to the surface of the nearby planet. Kivira's landscape is a wondrous thing, a symphony of verdant landscapes and shimmering seas. Though the civilization that once stood here has long vanished, they left behind a great quantity of riches. You collect as much as you can and return to your ship.

      • Very large amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelShangriLaQuest_intro3

      In front of you is the solid, diamond-like core of what was once a gaseous planet. Despite its wonder, there is nothing to be done here. You move on.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelShangriLaQuest_intro4

      Before you is a brilliant planet of oceans and forests. You discover magnificent cities full of vacant edifices, punctuated occasionally by technocult excavation equipment. This planet indeed seems to match descriptions of the fabled Kivira, but whatever riches the civilization once held has long been despoiled.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelShangriLaQuest_intro6

      Before you is a jewel world of surpassing beauty. Innumerable waterfalls descend from massive plateaus into fertile valleys verdant with vast tracts of wildflowers. Unfortunately, the planet bears no signs of the legendary civilization of Kivira.

      1. Continue
        oTxtJewelShangriLaQuest_intro6_a

        As you conclude your survey you discover a member of your crew has been driven mad by the splendor of the landscape. The crewman steals an escape pod to the surface below and is not heard from again.

        • Lose a random crew member
        1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelShangriLaQuest_intro2

      Below you is a majestic planet of vast mountains and lush valleys. You detect some intense subspace energies centered in one of the major river valleys and discover a flourishing civilization. While their architecture does not match the fabled descriptions of Kivira, the inhabitants live strikingly peaceful and idyllic existences.

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtJewelShangriLaQuest_intro2_b

          One of them is greatly taken by the sight of your starship and insists on joining your crew.

          • Gene Soldier
          1. Continue
        • oTxtJewelShangriLaQuest_intro2_c

          They invite you to participate in one of their sacred ceremonies. The strange ritual conjures forth a grotesque creature, which they bestow upon you as a boon from their guardian deity.

          • Stygian Worm
          1. Continue
        • oTxtJewelShangriLaQuest_intro2_a

          They inform you that they owe their prosperity to the wonderful crystals mined deep within the mountains, which they regard as material blessings from their deity. They present you a generous quantity of the crystals before your departure.

          • Dark Matter Prism ×4
          1. Continue

JewelShangriLaQuest_intro2

→ oTxtJewelShangriLaQuest_intro2_a

JewelShangriLaQuest_intro5

→ oTxtJewelShangriLaQuest_intro5_a

JewelShangriLaQuest_intro6

→ oTxtJewelShangriLaQuest_intro6_a

JewelShangriLa_aidA

oTxtJewelShangriLa_aidA_a Unreachable — not referenced by any event

The intruders have been neutralized. You strip their vacated starship of serviceable ordnance.

  • Moderate amount of missile ammo (5–8)
  1. Continue
oTxtJewelShangriLa_aidA_b Unreachable — not referenced by any event

As the final raider dies, he pulls out a small remote and activates it. It lights the raider starship's thrusters, sending it hurtling towards your ship.

  1. Continue
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtJewelShangriLa_aidA_b1

      The unmanned starship misses and deorbits into the lush planet below.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelShangriLa_aidA_b2

      The uncrewed starship manages to strike a glancing blow against your hull before rebounding off. At length you disable the gyrating hulk and recover some valuables from it.

      • Random moderate reward
      • −1 hull HP
      1. Continue

JewelShangriLa_aidB

→ oTxtJewelShangriLa_aidB_a

JewelSirenWorldV2

oTxtJewelSirenWorldV2_intro

As you pass through another star system your sensors lock onto a beautiful planet and refuse further commands. You attempt to change the sensor target, to no avail. Something seems to be drawing your ship to this planet. Furthermore, several members of your crew are becoming strangely agitated.

  1. Visit the planet
    Random outcome (1/7 each):
    • oTxtJewelSirenWorldV2_visitF

      You descend into the planet's atmosphere and identify a network of ancient ruins on one of the larger landmasses. You send an away team to recover what they can.

      • Random cult equipment
      1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelSirenWorldV2_visitA

      You decide to approach the planet. Beyond its prepossessing appearance it lacks anything of interest to you. Your ship sensors resume normal operations soon after.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelSirenWorldV2_visitB

      You decide to approach the planet. Your crew is soothed by the majestic appearance of the planet. Your ship sensors resume normal operations soon after, and you leave feeling reinvigorated.

      • Heal all crew by 100%
      1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelSirenWorldV2_visitG

      You descend into the planet's atmosphere and identify a network of ancient ruins on one of the larger landmasses. Your away team fails to find anything of value, and sustains some injuries in the process.

      • Random crew damaged (20%)
      • Commander damaged (20%)
      1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelSirenWorldV2_visitE

      You send an away team down to the surface. Besides the vaguely ominous forms of a dozen or so crashed starships, the atmosphere is serene. You collect what you can from the remains. Then you make ready to depart.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelSirenWorldV2_visitD

      As you approach the planet you notice it is wreathed by a graveyard of derelict ships. You salvage what you can.

      • Random large reward
      1. Continue
        oTxtJewelSirenWorldV2_visitD_1

        Upon completing salvage operations, you notice your navigation system operating correctly once again. You continue onwards.

        1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelSirenWorldV2_visitC

      Your crew becomes increasingly agitated by the proximity to the planet. One of them finally steals an escape pod and leaves for the surface.

      • Lose a random crew member
      1. Continue
        oTxtJewelSirenWorldV2_visitC_1

        Though you are unable to recover your wayward crewman, your sensors are operating correctly once again. You move on.

        1. Continue
  2. Ignore the planet
    oTxtJewelSirenWorldV2_ignoreA

    You momentarily shut off your sensors, assume manual control over the helm, and continue on course.

    1. Continue

JewelSirenWorldV2_visitC

→ oTxtJewelSirenWorldV2_visitC_1

JewelSirenWorldV2_visitD

→ oTxtJewelSirenWorldV2_visitD_1

JewelTechnoFight

oTxtJewelTechnoFight_intro

You exit the orbit of large jungle planet when you are confronted by a technocult surveyor. "Fleshbeing. Your careless maneuvers have contaminated the bioecology of this world. This behavior cannot stand."

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Techno ship

JewelWonder

oTxtJewelWonder_intro

You are orbiting a jungle world when an Imperial starship suddenly tumbles into realspace in front of you. Its crew gazes in wonder at the lush forests and blue seas of the planet below. "This place... it is beautiful. Like a dream."

  1. Tell them such a place can only last if the Empire never learns of its location
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtJewelWonder_neverLearn

      "No! You are wrong. The Empire must know of this. The people must know of this. The people... they deserve better. Do not stand in our way."

      1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelWonder_neverLearn2

      "Yes. You are right. On behalf of all the crew aboard this ship, I hereby renounce my oaths to the Tetrarchy, the Empire, and the Immortal King. We have been lied to for far too long. Thank you for showing us the error of our ways. Please, take this."

      • Random small reward
      • Enemy becomes friendly
      1. Continue
  2. Tell them this place is not what it seems
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtJewelWonder_notWhatItSeemsB

      "Yes. Those trees have been extinct for centuries. This place cannot possibly be real. Either we are dreaming or this is... sorcery! Die, witch!" The Imperial ship turns its guns on you.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelWonder_notWhatItSeemsC

      "There is only one way to find out. Send a team down to the surface at once." They assign half their crew to a landing team and send them down to the planet via shuttle craft. Thirty minutes later, the landing team confirms that the planet is indeed as wondrous as it looked from orbit. The Imperials ignore any further attempts at communication.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtJewelWonder_notWhatItSeemsA

      "How can this be? Look at those trees, those species have been extinct for millennia. What splendor..." The Imperials break contact and prepare a shuttle for landing. The entire crew descends into the atmosphere of the planet below.

      1. Continue
        oTxtJewelWonder_notWhatItSeemsA2

        Hours later you hear screaming and gunfire over the comms. Something terrible must have happened. The only surviving crewmember was the comms technician who remained in orbit on the ship. He decides to join your crew. Together you strip his former ship of useful material.

        • Imperial Recruit or Imperial Scribe ×0
        1. Continue
  3. Attack them
    oTxtJewelWonder_attack

    The Imperials are so distracted by the planet's majesty that they are unprepared for your attack.

    1. Continue
  4. Ignore them and move on
    oTxtJewelWonder_ignore

    You ignore them. The Imperial ship makes no attempt to contact you.

    1. Continue

LastSubsystemSlot

oTxtLastSubsystemSlot Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You have reached the maximum number of SUBSYSTEMS (max 4). If you install this subsystem, you will not be able to install any additional subsystems. Do you wish to proceed?

  1. Proceed to select install location
  2. Cancel

LastSystemSlot

oTxtLastSystemSlot Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You have reached the maximum number of SYSTEMS. If you install this system, you will not be able to install any additional systems. Do you wish to proceed?

  1. Proceed to select install location
  2. Cancel

LeviathanSurvey1

oTxtLeviathanSurvey1_intro

You intercept a signal from a hidden raider satellite and you catch a fragment of the transmission. A raider fleet is conducting a special operation in a nearby system. Though the quality is low and the transmission unstable, you hear the phrases "great beast" and "treasure." The transmission ends.

  1. Continue
    oTxtLeviathanSurvey1_intro2

    An Imperial survey station contacts your ship and requests aid. The station's administrator explains that recently a number of ships travelling through the region have reported seeing some kind of a gargantuan vessel of unknown make. Some describe it as a stellar apparition, a glitch in the sensors. Others tell a darker tale, describing it as a horrific star-spawned monstrosity larger than an Imperial stellar habitat. The administrator requests you to find and identify the source of these rumours. He adds that while he cannot outright guarantee a reward, he suggests there may be valuables found at the source of these rumours.

    1. Accept the task
      oTxtLeviathanSurvey1_accept

      You accept the task. You receive coordinates to the target system.

      • Quest marker added to map: oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_intro
      1. Continue
    2. Decline the offer
      oTxtLeviathanSurvey1_decline

      You decline and move on.

      1. Continue

LeviathanSurvey1Quest

oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_intro

You enter the system, and immediately notice three fleets of different origins surrounding a vast, dark shape. As you approach, the thing begins to come into view. At first it appears to be a relic starship of advanced technology, but then you notice the eyes and the gargantuan maw filled with baleen plates common with some oceanic mammalian species.

  1. Continue
    oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_intro2

    The fleets surrounding this beast belong to the Imperials, raider clans and Gorgothian cultists. The fleets are equal in strength and number, locked in a tense stalemate. You realise that any application of force from you would tip the scales to favor one of the factions.

    1. Continue
      oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_intro3

      Soon, captains of the fleets begin contacting you. They all promise great rewards in return for your aid. Your decision will change the fate of this strange beast, this Leviathan. Each faction is sure to have their own uses for the creature.

      1. Assist the Imperial fleet
        oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_imperial

        You affirm your support to the captain of the Imperial fleet and attack. You catch the enemy fleets by surprise and tip the precarious balance of power. Conflict erupts, every fleet fights for itself now. You charge into the fray and attack the closest enemy

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Raider ship
        After the fight:
        oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_imperial_win

        The first ship has been defeated.

        • Crew recalled to ship
        1. Continue
          oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_imperial_win1

          Suddenly, a death cult warship appears and catches you off-guard.

          • 3–4 poison clouds spawned
          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Plague ship
          After the fight:
          oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_imperial_win2

          The battle ends, you and your Imperial allies victorious. Broken enemy ships surround you, and you are surprised to notice that the great Leviathan has neither attacked nor fled -- instead, it elegantly swims by.

          1. Continue
            oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_imperial_epilogue

            You are contacted by the captain of the Imperial fleet. He thanks you for the assistance. While he cannot offer much in terms of direct payment, he grants you salvage rights to the destroyed fleet. He also informs you of holy reagents available from one of the nearby Hyperionate temples. Lastly, he mentions that a ritual of purification is beginning soon, as the Leviathan needs to be dealt with.

            • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
            • Shattered Reagents ×3
            • Retributor II
            1. Continue
              oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_imperial_epilogue2

              You decide to stay for a little while, and you observe the Imperial fleet's attack on the great beast. Sacred retributors crater the hide of the creature; lines of divine laserfire etch smoldering canyons in its flesh. The remains are pushed to collide with the system's sun. In the name of the God-King, his fire purifies all.

              1. Continue
      2. Join forces with the raider fleet
        oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_raiders

        You join forces with the raiders, their captain promising you loot and plunder. The fleet's attack catches the other two enemy forces by surprise. You join the battle and attack a death cult vessel. The enemy has little time to react.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Plague ship
        After the fight:
        oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_raider_win

        The enemy ship has been defeated.

        • Crew recalled to ship
        1. Continue
          oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_raider_win1

          There is no time to rest: an Imperial warship trains its weapons on you, launching a barrage of incendiary projectiles.

          • 3 fires spawned
          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Empire Large ship
          After the fight:
          oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_raider_win2

          The battle ends and you are surrounded by the remains of your enemies. The great beast has not fled nor attacked. Instead, it slowly swims by as the raider captain contacts you. He thanks you for the assistance, and offers you a portion of the ship salvage.

          • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
          • Moderate amount of missile ammo (5–8)
          • Fusion Charge ×4
          1. Continue
            oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_raider_epilogue

            You begin making your way out of the system. As you exit, you see that the raiders have already slain the Leviathan. The raiders descend upon the lifeless body of the creature like a swarm of hungry insects. The butchering is fast and brutal. Its meat is harvested for food, the rest for whatever their fleets will need the most.

            1. Continue
      3. Join forces with the death cult
        oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_deathcult

        You accept the necromancer-captain's request for aid and join their fleet. Your enemies are already as good as dead. The Gorgothians launch a full-scale attack, and the chaos of conflict catches the other fleets by surprise. You rush in and target an Imperial warship.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Empire Large ship
        After the fight:
        oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_deathcult_win

        The enemy ship has been defeated.

        • Crew recalled to ship
        1. Continue
          oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_deathcult_win1

          As you track your next target, a raider ship catches you off-guard. Alarms begin screaming. Your ship has been boarded.

          • 2–3 breaches spawned
          • 1 Corsair boarder
          • 1–2 Infiltrator boarders
          • 1–2 Commando boarders
          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Raider Small ship
          After the fight:
          oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_deathcult_win2

          With the last Imperial ship defeated the battle dies down. Enemy ships lay battered and broken. The Leviathan, strangely, has not fled nor attacked. It swims by gracefully. Soon it is surrounded by Gorgothian ships. You are contacted by the necromancer-captain. "Come and see the Endfather's design..."

          1. Continue
            oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_deathcult_epilogue

            A wave of sickly green energy coalesces around the surrounded space-beast. A vile ritual takes place, and you see the creature beginning to convulse and mutate violently. You see life leave the being's eyes. A moment passes, and the Leviathan twitches and opens its clouded eyes. Mostly harmless in life, it has become an unliving nightmare, a growbed for plagues, parasites and unnamable rot-beasts conceived in the festering caverns of its putrid flesh.

            1. Continue
              oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_deathcult_epilogue2

              A shuttle carrying rewards lands in your cargo bay. Filled to the brim with corpses, you find a cache of rewards buried underneath.

              • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
              • Putrefactor I
              • Necrotizing Reagents ×3
              1. Continue

LeviathanSurvey1Quest_deathcult

→ oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_deathcult_epilogue

LeviathanSurvey1Quest_imperial

→ oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_imperial_epilogue

LeviathanSurvey1Quest_raider

→ oTxtLeviathanSurvey1Quest_raider_epilogue

LeviathanSurvey2

oTxtLeviathanSurvey2_intro

As you enter the system, you are contacted by the administrator of a small technocult data repository outpost: "Fleshbeing. Biological anomaly detected by multiple surveyor drones. Latest reported location scouted by seeker fleet. Subspatial anomalies detected in vicinity. Kromic incursion detected in vicinity. Assistance required. Data: estimated valuable."

  1. Assist the technocult
    oTxtLeviathanSurvey2_accept

    You accept the task. The administrator sends you coordinates to a nearby system

    • Quest marker added to map: oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_intro
    1. Continue
  2. Decline the task
    oTxtLeviathanSurvey2_decline

    You close comms with the technocult outpost.

    1. Continue

LeviathanSurvey2Quest

oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_intro

You enter the system and are immediately flung into the midst of a battle. Evading projectiles and explosions, you notice that the fight is between the worshippers of Krom, the Machine Cult, and strange, glowing translucent insectoid ships emerging and vanishing into subspace rifts. In the distance you spot a gargantuan shape; a great beast slowly gliding through the void, seemingly undisturbed by the battle. Your focus snaps back to the chaos of battle, and you realise that aiding any of the three sides would turn the tide of this conflict.

  1. Assist the technocult
    oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_technocult

    You side with the technocult as instructed by the data-repository administrator. In mere seconds, you contact the fleet captain, draw up assault plans, reorient your ship and rush into the fray of combat. Through explosion and debris you spot one of the many subspatial entities. You rush onwards, pressing the attack.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Possessed ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_technocult_win

    The strange vessel shatters into glasslike shards.

    • Crew recalled to ship
    1. Continue
      oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_technocult_win1

      Before you manage to reorient your ship, you are attacked by a Kromic warship. Alarms begin screeching. There is a tremor: boarding pods wrack your ship.

      • −1 hull HP
      • 2 breaches spawned
      • 1–2 Conqueror boarders
      • 2 Berserker boarders
      • 1–2 Warseer boarders
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs War ship
      After the fight:
      oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_technocult_win2

      The battle is over, and the field is littered with broken ships. The Leviathan remains still, seemingly unbothered by the sudden and brutal violence.

      1. Continue
        oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_technocult_epilogue

        You are contacted by the captain of the technocult fleet. "Perfect research specimen contained. Contributing fleshbeing, compensation has been transmitted. Beginning experiment Delta 1173-M."

        • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
        • Heavy Ion Lance
        1. Continue
          oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_technocult_epilogue2

          As you begin to exit the system, you notice that the technocult fleet has surrounded the Leviathan. Hundreds of small shuttles and drones spread from the fleet like a cloud of spores, landing and attaching onto the beast's hide. They begin vivisecting the creature and replacing the organic with cybernetics. The creature thrashes, blood spills from its wounds and immediately freezes over.

          1. Continue
            oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_technocult_epilogue3

            Slowly, its movements quieten. After some time, most of the biomass of this fleshbeing has been completely replaced with cybernetics. You see that the machine priests leave mid-operation, apparently satisfied with whatever knowledge they have gained. A biological creature no longer, a strange machine intellect now controls the mechanized Leviathan.

            1. Continue
  2. Side with the worshippers of Krom
    oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_warcult

    You choose to ally yourself with the war cult. Instead of being contacted by whoever leads the assault, you join a fleet-wide comms channel filled with chaotic battlecries and boasts. This manic energy spreads, and you find yourself smiling. This battle will be glorious. Charging forth, your first target is a technocult warship. The ferocity of your attack catches it by surprise.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Techno ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_warcult_win

    The technocult ship falls silent.

    • Crew recalled to ship
    1. Continue
      oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_warcult_win1

      The thrill of battle has become a symphony and you its leading instrument. One of the strange, insect-like ships you saw earlier attacks, and you respond in kind. A subspatial resonance rings through your ship.

      1. Continue
      2. Continue
      Fight! vs Possessed ship
      After the fight:
      oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_warcult_win2

      The enemy ship crumbles away. You notice the great beast slowly coasting by, seemingly ignoring the chaotic battle.

      1. Continue
        oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_warcult_epilogue

        Slowly, the battle comes to an end, with the followers of Krom the undisputed victors. Fleet-wide comms are filled with post-battle euphoria, along with praise for your bloody contribution. You are contacted by one of the Kromic fleetmasters. "You fought with uncommon distinction. Take this, that you may sow further destruction." You soon recieve a cache of wargear.

        • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
        • Havoc Maul
        • Siege Armor
        1. Continue
          oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_warcult_epilogue2

          Keeping an ear to fleet comms, you gather that half of the Kromic cultists want to fight and slay the beast while the other wishes to tame it. A small but brutal internecine conflict breaks out.

          1. Continue
            oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_warcult_epilogue3

            Those who remain begin taming the large beast. Through a process of violence and Kromic rituals, the beast is collared to the will of their foul god. An unholy, savage rage fills the creature's many eyes.

            1. Continue
  3. Assist the inscrutable subspatial creatures
    oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_subspace

    You align yourself with the strange ships emerging from subspatial rifts. You feel an unnerving, ephemeral cloud of psychic energy envelop your mind. Images come into being, snapshots from the surrounding battle. You know what to do next. A Kromic warship blocks your way and you attack. In response, the enemy sends boarders towards your ship.

    • 1–2 breaches spawned
    • 1 Conqueror boarder
    • 1–2 Berserker boarders
    • 1 Warseer boarder
    1. Continue
    Fight! vs War ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_subspace_win

    The Kromic ship has been defeated

    • Crew recalled to ship
    1. Continue
      oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_subspace_win1

      A technocult ship is being attacked nearby. You press the advantage and catch it off-guard.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Techno ship
      After the fight:
      oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_subspace_win2

      With the technocult ship defeated the battle is finally over. Broken ships litter the battlefield and nearby, the great Leviathan glides ever onward, seemingly unbothered by the battle. The glowing subspatial insect-ships converge on the great beast. They open a large subspatial rift and pass through, one by one, and the Leviathan follows. It passes through and inside the portal, time suddenly begins to accelerate.

      1. Continue
        oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_subspace_epilogue

        A vast cocoon of darkened chitin forms around the Leviathan. Metastable threads of exotic matter wrap around the shell, spun into being by ethereal light-beings.

        1. Continue
          oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_subspace_epilogue2

          Moments pass. Cracks begin to appear around the cocoon. A few seconds later, an entity of twisted light emerges. Far larger than its infantile form, it resembles a great celestial bird and possesses four large wings. You notice eyes opening on every inch of its glowing skin... Human eyes. You avert your gaze; the sight is maddening. Your nose begins to bleed.

          • Commander damaged (50%)
          1. Continue
            oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_subspace_epilogue3

            Abruptly, the portal closes, and all is left in silence. Recovering from this strange vision, you begin to scavenge the battlefield and find some supplies.

            • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
            • Fiendish Reagents ×2
            • Rift Bomb
            1. Continue

LeviathanSurvey2Quest_subspace

→ oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_subspace_epilogue

LeviathanSurvey2Quest_technocult

→ oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_technocult_epilogue

LeviathanSurvey2Quest_warcult

→ oTxtLeviathanSurvey2Quest_warcult_epilogue

MechaEmpirePursuit

oTxtMechaEmpirePursuit_intro

You encounter an unusual bio-mechanical vessel attacking what appears to be an Imperial supply barge. It is unclear what an undefended transport ship would be doing in an exclusion zone. The Imperial are broadcasting an open distress signal. "We are under attack by an unknown vessel. We have never seen anything like it. Requesting aid."

  1. Defend the Imperial ship
    oTxtMechaEmpirePursuit_defend1

    You move into attack range of the enemy vessel. Your comms array intercepts one of their transmissions. "...hostile posture confirmed. EM scatter indicates weapons charging. Forward batteries are active. Recommend immediate combat posture..."

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Mecha ship
    After the rewards from the fight:
    oTxtMechaEmpirePursuit_defend2

    You make contact with the supply barge. You learn they were en route to an ore processing facility before being ambushed by a squadron of strange starships. Their entire escort complement was destroyed. The Imperials are grateful for your assistance and offer you a spare supply container.

    • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
    1. Continue
  2. Ignore them
    oTxtMechaEmpirePursuit_ignore

    You ignore them and continue on with your journey.

    1. Ignore them

MechaSignature

oTxtMechaSignature_intro

A sleek, contoured vessel emerges from the riftstorm. It has no identifiable drive signature, no insignia save for a line of archaic, barely-legible lettering that reads: "Space Defense Force". It opens fire.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Mecha ship
After the rewards from the fight:
oTxtMechaSignature_afterFight

Your sensors detect an unusual signature left behind by the enemy ship.

  1. Approach the signature
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtMechaSignature_approachC

      You trace the signal to a sealed storage vault housing a starship weapon unlike any you've seen: compact, refined, and clearly built with advanced engineering.

      • random Armament (SDF)
      1. Continue
    • oTxtMechaSignature_approachA

      You approach the signature and soon realize it is emanating from a volatile subspace containment unit. You try to maneuver away but it is too late. It explodes, tearing multiple subspace rifts into your hull.

      • 2–3 warp breaches spawned
      1. Continue
    • oTxtMechaSignature_approachB

      You approach the signal and retrieve what appears to be a compact reactor unit, miniaturized and far beyond Imperial design. It is incompatible with your systems. You manage to successfully dismantle it for usable scrap.

      • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
  2. Ignore the signature
    oTxtMechaSignature_ignore

    You ignore the signature and continue with your journey.

    1. Continue

MechaSurvey

oTxtMechaSurvey_intro

Your sensors pick up what appears to be a survey vessel of unknown origin. Its communication arrays are clearly non-standard.

  1. Hail the vessel
    oTxtMechaSurvey_hail

    You hail the vessel and receive a response within moments. "Transmission acknowledged. This is a Class-IV survey operation under Directive […]. Your vector intersects a restricted zone. Adjust course by 0.[…] radians to avoid contamination."

    1. Adjust course
      oTxtMechaSurvey_adjust

      As you adjust course, a low-band transmission follows. "Deviation corrected. Harmonic integrity preserved."

      • Enemy becomes friendly
      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtMechaSurvey_adjustA

          The vessel emits a deep subharmonic pulse as it vanishes into the rift. Your hull picks up trace particulate. It is a small sample of high-grade composite material.

          • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
          1. Continue
        • oTxtMechaSurvey_adjustB

          Satisfied with your compliance, the unknown vessel powers up its drives and departs. In its wake your sensors detect an unusual harmonic signature. Decoding the signature reveals a set of stellar coordinates.

          1. Plot the coordinates
            oTxtMechaSurvey_adjustPlot

            You enter the coordinates to your navigation console.

            • Quest marker added to map: oTxtMechaSurveyQuest_intro
            1. Continue
          2. Ignore the coordinates
            oTxtMechaSurvey_adjustIgnore

            You decide to ignore the coordinates.

            1. Continue
    2. Maintain present course
      oTxtMechaSurvey_maintain

      You ignore the directive and maintain course. The survey vessel interprets this as a hostile act and immediately opens fire.

      1. Continue
    3. Request more information
      oTxtMechaSurvey_requestInfo

      They respond. "Inquiry rejected. Persistence will result in vector suppression."

      1. Adjust course
        → oTxtMechaSurvey_adjust
      2. Maintain present course
        → oTxtMechaSurvey_maintain
  2. Move on
    oTxtMechaSurvey_moveOn

    You move on.

    1. Continue

MechaSurveyQuest

oTxtMechaSurveyQuest_intro

You arrive at the stellar coordinates decoded from the harmonic signature of the unknown survey vessel you hailed earlier. You encounter the wreckage of several similar survey vessels. Among the wrecks you discover a few containers marked "Space Defense Force Classified Asset - Division: Instrumentality."

  • random Armament (SDF)
  1. Continue

oTxtMemTest_chainedFight

MetaNothing
