  1. Continue

MinersCometDistress

oTxtMinersCometDistress_intro

A cometary ice-mining operation is emitting a distress signal.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/6 each):
    • oTxtMinersCometDistress_investigateA

      The miners are nowhere to be found. You turn off the beacon before salvaging their equipment.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtMinersCometDistress_investigateC

      An Imperial patrol ship arrives. They accuse you of sabotaging the mining operations and open fire.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtMinersCometDistress_investigateC_a

        You return to the distress signal and discover the miners were abducted by a passing technocult harvester ship.

        1. Continue
      • oTxtMinersCometDistress_investigateC_b

        An examination of the mining camp's databanks reveals that the miners were abducted by raiders.

        1. Continue
    • oTxtMinersCometDistress_investigateD

      A review of the mining camp's databanks reveals that one of the miners suffered a mental breakdown and slaughtered the rest of the camp. You return to your ship to find him waiting for you.

      • 1 Conqueror boarder
      1. Continue
    • oTxtMinersCometDistress_investigateB

      The miners are nowhere to be found, but you encounter trace quantities of frozen blood. A strangely colored jet suddenly shoots out from one of the mining pits before impacting your ship. You quickly return aboard to find bizarre creatures rampaging through the decks.

      • 1–2 warp breaches spawned
      • 2–3 Demonic Crew boarders
      1. Continue
    • oTxtMinersCometDistress_investigateE

      You find all the miners dead from various causes, ranging from asphyxiation to starvation. The camp's databanks reveal that their last scheduled resupply never arrived.

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtMinersCometDistress_investigateE1

          You recover a weapon off the corpse of the overseer.

          • 60%: Random Raider weapon / 40%: Random weapon item
          1. Continue
        • oTxtMinersCometDistress_investigateE2

          You salvage some of the protective gear before leaving.

          • 60%: Random Raider defensive item / 40%: baseReward
          1. Continue
        • oTxtMinersCometDistress_investigateE3

          None of the heavy equipment seems to be of immediate use. You salvage what you can and move on.

          • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
          1. Continue
    • oTxtMinersCometDistress_investigateF

      The miners ask you to transport them off the comet. Apparently they had stopped receiving pay since the last resupply.

      1. Accept
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtMinersCometDistress_accept3

          Once aboard, the miners decide to hijack your vessel.

          • 2–3 Imperial Recruit boarders
          1. Continue
        • oTxtMinersCometDistress_accept2

          You drop off the miners at the nearest outpost. One of them begs to join you in your journey.

          • Imperial Recruit
          1. Continue
        • oTxtMinersCometDistress_accept1

          You drop off the miners at the nearest outpost. They are grateful for your help, and offer a small reward.

          • Random small reward
          1. Continue
      2. Decline
  2. Ignore
    oTxtMinersCometDistress_ignore

    You press on.

    1. Continue
oTxtMinersCometDistress_decline1 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

The miners swear at you bitterly. One of them fires an excavation laser at your ship as you leave, causing some damage.

  • 3 breaches spawned
  1. Continue
oTxtMinersCometDistress_decline2 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

The miners are incensed at your decision and a few manage to force their way aboard your ship.

  • 2 Imperial Recruit boarders
  1. Continue
After the boarders are defeated:
oTxtMinersCometDistress_decline2B

You salvage some materials from the site.

  • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
  1. Continue

MinersCometDistress_investigateC

→ oTxtMinersCometDistress_investigateC_a

MinersPirateTrouble

oTxtMinersPirateTrouble_intro

A group of miners has been having trouble with pirates raiding their storage facilities near the asteroid belt. They want to know if you would be willing to help them deal with the pirates.

  1. Agree to help
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtMinersPirateTrouble_agreeA

      You spend a few days patrolling the asteroid belt. Sure enough, a pirate vessel turns up, clearly looking for trouble.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtMinersPirateTrouble_agreeA1

        The raider ship has been dispatched, but logs recovered from the wreckage indicate additional raider crews operating nearby. You prepare to resume your patrol.

        • Crew recalled to ship
        1. Keep patrolling the area
          oTxtMinersPirateTrouble_agreeA1a

          You continue patrolling the asteroid belt.

          1. Continue
            oTxtMinersPirateTrouble_agreeA1b

            Another pirate vessel shows up.

            1. Continue
            Fight! vs Raider ship
            After the rewards from the fight:
            oTxtMinersPirateTrouble_agreeA1_2

            The miners report that pirate activity has dropped to zero. They thank you for your help and send you a small reward.

            • Random small reward
            1. Continue
        2. Leave
          oTxtMinersPirateTrouble_agreeA1_leave

          After reviewing the latest status reports, you decide to terminate your contract with the miners. The miners are unhappy, but you must move on.

          1. Continue
      • oTxtMinersPirateTrouble_agreeA2

        No other pirate vessels appear. It seems that you have scared them off. The miners present you a quantity of ore as a token of appreciation.

        • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
        1. Continue
    • oTxtMinersPirateTrouble_agreeB

      You patrol the asteroid belt for several days. Finally, you detect a small fleet of raider vessels. You move to intercept them before they can attack the mining colony.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider ship
      After the fight:
      oTxtMinersPirateTrouble_agreeB_1

      The raiders are undeterred. Another vessel moves in to attack.

      • Crew recalled to ship
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtMinersPirateTrouble_agreeB_win

      The miners are grateful for your assistance. They offer some supplies as payment. You move on.

      • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
      • Breach Charge or Combat Mesh or Energy Beam I ×2
    • oTxtMinersPirateTrouble_agreeC

      You patrol the area around the storage facilities. Soon a pirate vessel speeds into attack range. They launch a few preliminary salvos which slam into your hull, knocking out your life support systems. You arm your weapons and prepare to fight.

      • Destroy Oxygen
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtMinersPirateTrouble_agreeC2

      You attempt to make contact with the miners but soon learn that all of them were killed in a separate raider attack while you were occupied. You decide to get out of there.

      • Crew recalled to ship
      1. Continue
  2. Decline
    oTxtMinersPirateTrouble_decline

    You decide you are not interested.

    1. Continue
  3. (Centurion) Dispatch your centurion to guard the mining storage facilities
    oTxtMinersPirateTrouble_centurion1

    You drop off your crewmember at the storage facilities before concealing your vessel within a nearby asteroid to await the arrival of the pirates.

    1. Continue
      oTxtMinersPirateTrouble_centurion2

      Within days, the pirate vessel makes an appearance. Without any outward indication to arouse their suspicion, the raiders debark at the storage facilities with a relaxed routineness. They are soon thrown into disarray as your lone centurion bursts from the shadows, tearing into the group of surprised pirates. The pirates are thoroughly decimated, and the skeleton crew remaining aboard the pirate vessel soon surrender to the miners.

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtMinersPirateTrouble_centurion3b

          The miners express their gratitude towards your role in dispelling the pirate threat. They offer you a weapon stripped from the pirate vessel in addition to their payment.

          • Large amount of ⬗ Scrap
          • Random ship weapon
          1. Continue
        • oTxtMinersPirateTrouble_centurion3a

          The miners express their gratitude towards your role in dispelling the pirate threat. With the capture of the pirate vessel, the miners have gained a significant windfall and share a portion of the profits with you.

          • Very large amount of ⬗ Scrap
          1. Continue

MinersPirateTrouble_agreeA1

→ oTxtMinersPirateTrouble_agreeA1_2

MinersPirateTrouble_agreeB

→ oTxtMinersPirateTrouble_agreeB_1

Miniboss

oTxtMiniboss_demon01 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Your path crosses something ancient and pulsing. Not dead, not quite alive. Your instruments twitch. The void has grown a tongue.

  1. Continue
    oTxtMiniboss_demon01b

    A lifeless meat puppet flashes erratically on your viewscreen, body fused with what used to be a command throne. It does not speak.

    1. Continue
      oTxtMiniboss_demon01c

      You power your weapons and prepare to engage the monstrosity.

      1. Continue
      After the fight:
      oTxtMiniboss_demon01_win

      A series of violent convulsions rip through the creature's spine.

      • Large ⬗ Scrap reward (miniboss)
      1. Continue
        oTxtMiniboss_demon01_win2

        A gush of entrails and valuables pour from the creature's sundered maw. You snatch what you can before the rest liquefy in a cloud of bubbling acid.

        • Fixed-pool choice reward (2–4 options drawn from preset pools)
      Trigger (CurrentCrewKilled):
      oTxtMinibossEventSpawnDemonHeart_demon01_start

      You have exterminated the enemy crew. The abomination falls silent...

      1. Continue
        oTxtMinibossEventSpawnDemonHeart_demon01

        Suddenly, the enemy begins maneuvering again. Its command throne glows with infernal light - a malefic, hungering force has assumed control of the ship.

        • Crew recalled to ship
        • Enemy rooms deoxygenated
        After the fight: → oTxtMiniboss_demon01_win
oTxtMiniboss_empire01 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You arrive near a large subspace rift. An Imperial crusade vessel rises from the breach, engines searing with holy purpose.

oTxtMiniboss_empire01b

It descends upon you, immaculate, merciless. The God-King's will, forged into a celestial weapon. And it sees you.

After the fight:
oTxtMiniboss_empire01_win

The Imperial crusade ship ruptures, sending fire and debris spinning in all directions.

  • Large ⬗ Scrap reward (miniboss)
  1. Continue
    oTxtMiniboss_empire01_win2

    You recover what you can before the rest burns to ashes.

    • Fixed-pool choice reward (2–4 options drawn from preset pools)
oTxtMiniboss_raider01 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You catch movement in the asteroid field. Slow, deliberate. Not drifting. Not dead. An old miner, armored and armed, hull bristling with stolen weapons and bad intentions.

oTxtMiniboss_raider01b

It charges from cover, engines howling like a wounded thing.

After the fight:
oTxtMiniboss_raider01_win

The hulking mining vessel goes silent. You are able to recover some useful material from the remains.

  • Large ⬗ Scrap reward (miniboss)
  1. Continue
    oTxtMiniboss_raider01_win2

    You search the wreckage and are able to locate some additional material.

    • Fixed-pool choice reward (2–4 options drawn from preset pools)
oTxtMiniboss_techno01 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You detect an unknown pattern of Technocult vessel. Sensors indicate unusual energy fluctuations emanating from its reactor.

oTxtMiniboss_techno01b

Before you can react, it locks on to your ship and arms its weapons. A masked visage appears on your viewscreen. "Flesh-signal stable. Ion resonance stabilized. Experimental process has been initiated. Commencing sequence."

After the fight:
oTxtMiniboss_techno01_win

Arcs of ion energy spiral through the enemy vessel as it crumples.

  • Large ⬗ Scrap reward (miniboss)
  1. Continue
    oTxtMiniboss_techno01_win2

    You manage to recover something functional before the rest disintegrates.

    • Fixed-pool choice reward (2–4 options drawn from preset pools)

MinibossEventPhase2

oTxtMinibossEventPhase2_demon01

A droning noise surrounds you. Infernal energies sweep across the hull of the monstrosity, newly awakened by a malefic intelligence.

  • Enemy systems repaired
After the fight: → oTxtMiniboss_demon01_win

MinibossEventPhase2_demon01

oTxtMinibossEventPhase2_demon01_start Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You feel the stirring of unseen energies...

  1. Continue
    → oTxtMinibossEventPhase2_demon01

MinibossEventSpawnDemonHeart

→ oTxtMinibossEventSpawnDemonHeart_demon01

MinibossEventSpawnDemonHeart_demon01

→ oTxtMinibossEventSpawnDemonHeart_demon01_start

→ oTxtMiniboss_demon01_win

→ oTxtMiniboss_empire01_win
oTxtMiniboss_empire01_win3 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You discover a nearby repair facility. You collect what you can from its dilapidated hulk before moving on.

  • +10 hull HP

→ oTxtMiniboss_raider01_win

Miniboss_techno01

→ oTxtMiniboss_techno01_win

MunitionsProduction

oTxtMunitionsProduction_intro

There is an abandoned munitions production facility here. It can be reactivated, but the machinery requires chemical fuels. DEPRECATED (consolidating artillery charges and missiles)

  1. Reactivate artillery charge production (Spend 5 missile parts)
    oTxtMunitionsProduction_activate

    (DEPRECATED) You restart the machinery using missile propellants. The production line rumbles and fabricates a batch of artillery charges before malfunctioning.

    1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtMunitionsProduction_leave

    The facility is of no use to you. You move on.

    1. Continue

Nothing

oTxtNothing Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You arrive at your destination and find nothing but the cold void of space.

ObsidianPillarTransport

oTxtObsidianPillarTransport_intro

Orbiting the only planet in this system, you discover a half-destroyed technocult hab-station. Great streaks of lightning crackle from the facility's wounds down to the planet below, and your sensors detect zero signs of life. You log the location and prepare to jump, when you detect a garbled distress signal originating from the station.

  1. Investigate the distress signal
    oTxtObsidianPillarTransport_investigate1

    As you approach the station's mangled bridge the distress signal becomes clear; the static once coating your comms screen gives way to a lone flux-priest of the technocult. "Raids from Virelux pirates have crippled Station Lem-161. Analysis of recovered artifacts rendered impossible. Requesting transport of Class-6 artifact to Cyberine Conclave Facility."

    1. Continue
      oTxtObsidianPillarTransport_investigate2

      The screen moves to the artifact in question. A towering pillar, jet black, and spewing sparks without end. You feel an unshakable sense of foreboding about this unknown device.

      1. Agree to transport the artifact
        oTxtObsidianPillarTransport_accept

        After providing coordinates and ident codes for your destination, the flux-priest works to contain the artifact's random arcs of lightning, protecting your crew as they carry it onboard. He then shambles back to the station bridge, seemingly malfunctioning from the sheer quantity of electricity absorbed. The deteriorating state of the station aside, he is unlikely to survive this ordeal.

        1. Attempt to treat
          Random outcome (1/4 each):
          • oTxtObsidianPillarTransport_treatD

            The flux-priest's malfunctioning state was short lived, and he quickly regains consciousness. Understanding the danger of remaining aboard this station, he offers to serve aboard your ship.

            • Gain crew: Ophiuchus-33
            • Quest marker added to map: oTxtObsidianPillarTransportQuest_intro1
            1. Continue
          • oTxtObsidianPillarTransport_treatA

            The flux-priest is a lost cause. Offers of a place on your ship are met with incoherent data-strings spoken aloud, and his erratic flailing of limbs renders medical treatment impossible. You are left with no choice but to abandon him.

            • Quest marker added to map: oTxtObsidianPillarTransportQuest_intro1
            1. Continue
          • oTxtObsidianPillarTransport_treatB

            The flux-priest is a lost cause. Offers of a place on your ship are met with incoherent data-strings spoken aloud, and his erratic flailing of limbs renders medical treatment impossible. You are left with no choice but to abandon him.

            • Quest marker added to map: oTxtObsidianPillarTransportQuest_intro1
            1. Continue
          • oTxtObsidianPillarTransport_treatC

            The flux-priest is a lost cause. Offers of a place on your ship are met with incoherent data-strings spoken aloud, and his erratic flailing of limbs renders medical treatment impossible. You are left with no choice but to abandon him.

            • Quest marker added to map: oTxtObsidianPillarTransportQuest_intro1
            1. Continue
        2. Attempt to kidnap
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • oTxtObsidianPillarTransport_kidnapB

            The flux-priest puts up minimal resistance to his capture, a product of involuntary spasming more than conscious protest. Once awake and stable, he serves aboard your ship as thanks for "saving" him.

            • Gain crew: Ophiuchus-33
            • Quest marker added to map: oTxtObsidianPillarTransportQuest_intro1
            1. Continue
          • oTxtObsidianPillarTransport_kidnapA

            You set your crew upon the flux-priest hoping to press him into service. Once on board he grows ever more unstable and uncontrollable, eventually succumbing to his condition. The moment of his passing, your ship is engulfed in terrible lightning, disrupting vital systems and injuring crew.

            • −1–2 hull HP
            • 2–3 breaches spawned
            • 2–3 fires spawned
            • All crew damaged (30%)
            • Quest marker added to map: oTxtObsidianPillarTransportQuest_intro1
            1. Continue
        3. (Machine Priest) Have your machine priest render aid
          oTxtObsidianPillarTransport_aid

          Your machine priest attends to his cohort's condition with speed and precision. An electrolytic serum flush, paired with firmware reboots returns the flux-priest to normalcy. In thanks to his brother's service, and with nowhere else to go, he swears loyalty to him. By extension, he swears loyalty to you.

          • Gain crew: Ophiuchus-33
          • Quest marker added to map: oTxtObsidianPillarTransportQuest_intro1
          1. Continue
        4. (Ritualist) Seduce the vulnerable flux-priest
          oTxtObsidianPillarTransport_seduce

          While typically resistant to psychic influences, the malfunctioning flux-priest presents a unique opportunity for your ritualist. After a series of hypnotic trances and inscrutable rituals, the flux-priest is left in a stable, pliable state. Seizing the opportunity, your ritualist puppeteers his new servant to your ship's reactor, and slowly releases his excess energy into it. Your ship is left energized, stronger. He soon makes you a pledge of undying loyalty as well, believing you to be an emissary of the Machine-God.

          • Gain crew: Ophiuchus-33
          • +8–10 hull HP
          • Quest marker added to map: oTxtObsidianPillarTransportQuest_intro1
          1. Continue
        5. Leave
          oTxtObsidianPillarTransport_leave2

          The flux-priest is hopelessly unstable. He poses a risk not just to the station he's on, but to your ship should you take him aboard. You install the artifact and quickly depart.

          • Quest marker added to map: oTxtObsidianPillarTransportQuest_intro1
          1. Continue
      2. Inquire into the artifact
        oTxtObsidianPillarTransport_inquire

        The technocult are seldom forthcoming with the nature of their research and discoveries. The desperate circumstances at hand however have encouraged greater transparency. "Class-6 artifact damaged in pirate raid. Data-savant analyses claim artifact contains tech-prints originating from hitherto unknown civilization. Unstable -- presence poses critical risk to station."

        1. Agree to transport the artifact
          → oTxtObsidianPillarTransport_accept
        2. Decline
          oTxtObsidianPillarTransport_decline

          This "artifact" is at best a risk to your own ship, or at worst an elaborate trap. You depart post haste.

          1. Continue
      3. Decline
        → oTxtObsidianPillarTransport_decline
  2. Leave
    oTxtObsidianPillarTransport_leave1

    Likely a fluke or an automated signal; the station is surely dead and otherwise dangerous. You move on.

    1. Continue

ObsidianPillarTransportQuest

oTxtObsidianPillarTransportQuest_intro1

You at last reach your destination, a grand orbital facility of the Cyberine Conclave. Myriad armaments quickly train on your ship. You are ordered to state your purpose.

  1. Continue
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtObsidianPillarTransportQuest_intro2

      You inform the machine priests of your given task, and present to them the artifact in question. Their interest is immediate, and they soon send a hauler to obtain their latest fascination. A group of machine priests retrieve the artifact, entering your ship and promptly leaving with barely a word. One of their own remains however, and in possession of a wealth of equipment. "Your service to the Machine-God is not without note, nor compensation."

      • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
      • Repair Drone ×2
      • Gain crew: Carina II
      1. Continue
    • oTxtObsidianPillarTransportQuest_intro2b

      You inform the machine priests of your given task, and present to them the artifact in question. Their interest is immediate, and they soon send a hauler to obtain their latest fascination. A group of machine priests retrieve the artifact, entering your ship and promptly leaving with barely a word. One of their own remains however, and in possession of a wealth of equipment. "Your service to the Machine-God is not without note, nor compensation."

      • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
      • Microshield ×2
      • Gain crew: Bootes 65
      1. Continue

OrbitalCivilWar

oTxtOrbitalCivilWar_intro

You receive a message from an orbital station. Its commandant informs you that rebels on the planet below are staging a surface-to-orbit attack. The commandant is determined to avert disaster, and wishes to execute a preemptive strike before they are attacked.

  1. Agree to help
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtOrbitalCivilWar_attackA

      You launch an orbital bombardment against the targets supplied by the outpost. The targets turn out to be antimatter powerplants, which soon erupt in cataclysmic explosions.

      1. Continue
        oTxtOrbitalCivilWar_attackA_1

        The outpost commandant thanks you for your help in exterminating the surface colonies and presents you with a lavish gift.

        • Random large reward
        1. Continue
    • oTxtOrbitalCivilWar_attackB

      You launch an orbital bombardment against the targets supplied by the outpost and successfully destroy the rebel surface-to-orbit batteries. Unfortunately, one of them manages to fire a shot before it is destroyed.

      1. Block the artillery shot with your ship
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtOrbitalCivilWar_blockA

          Your ship takes some damage from the rebel artillery. The outpost commander is moved by your heroism and offers one of his officers to act as a liaison.

          • Infiltrator or Greater Warseer
          • −1 hull HP
          1. Continue
        • oTxtOrbitalCivilWar_blockB

          You move to interpose yourself between the station and the incoming artillery shot. Fortunately, the shot misses both your ship and the orbital station. The outpost commander thanks you for your assistance.

          • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
          1. Continue
      2. Ignore it
        oTxtOrbitalCivilWar_ignore

        The artillery strikes the orbital outpost. You salvage what you can from the disintegrated outpost.

        • Large amount of ⬗ Scrap
        1. Continue
    • oTxtOrbitalCivilWar_attackC

      The commandant transfers you the coordinates of the rebel artillery batteries. You commence your bombardment.

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtOrbitalCivilWar_attackC_2

          Unfortunately, the hardened shelters housing the batteries prove resistant to your weapon fire, and you are unable to prevent the rebels from firing and destroying the orbital station. The artillery batteries then target lock your vessel, forcing you to retreat.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtOrbitalCivilWar_attackC_1

          Unfortunately, the hardened shelters housing the batteries prove resistant to your weapon fire, and you are unable to prevent the rebel artillery from firing. The barrage of shells obliterates the orbital station. Several more impact your hull as you make your escape.

          • 2–3 breaches spawned
          1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtOrbitalCivilWar_leave

    You decide not to get involved.

    1. Continue

OrbitalCivilWar_attackA

→ oTxtOrbitalCivilWar_attackA_1

OrbitalCivilWar_attackC

→ oTxtOrbitalCivilWar_attackC_1

The cultists seize one of your crewmen and amputate all their limbs before hauling them off. Your devotion to Ariok is strong, but it could yet be stronger. A bondkin shall join you to further instruct you in the ways of the blood god."

  • Random boarders teleport in

The cultists seize one of your crewmen and amputate all their limbs before hauling them off. Your devotion to Ariok is strong, but it could yet be stronger. A bondkin shall join you to further instruct you in the ways of the blood god."

  • Thrall, Blood Priest, or Ritualist
  • Random crew damaged (30%)

oTxtPauseMenu_resetToShipSelectConfirm Unreachable — not referenced by any event

  1. Exit to hangar

Placeholder

oTxtPlaceholder Unreachable — not referenced by any event

THIS IS A PLACEHOLDER ENCOUNTER.

PlagueColonyHealers

oTxtPlagueColonyHealers_intro

You detect an engagement between local defense militia ships and cultist vessels near an orbital outpost.

  1. Attack the cultists
    oTxtPlagueColonyHealers_cultist

    You move in to attack a cultist vessel.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Plague ship
    After the rewards from the fight:
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtPlagueColonyHealers_cultist_b

      Your victory has allowed the militia to annihilate the rest of the cultist vessels. The outpost commandant hails you with a scowl. "You meddlesome swine. Those acolytes were the only ones delivering medicine to this throneforsaken place."

      1. Offer your medical surplus
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtPlagueColonyHealers_medicineB

          The militia intercepts your transfer. "We shall be taking that. You may have helped us, but we do not know you. Take this compensation and leave."

          • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
          1. Continue
        • oTxtPlagueColonyHealers_medicineA

          "Are you joking? We have 75 million souls with chronic conditions. This amount of medicine would not last an hour. Begone, before you cause us any more trouble."

          1. Continue
      2. Leave
        oTxtPlagueColonyHealers_cultistLeaveB

        You leave the area, but not before having picked through a few more of the destroyed cultist vessels.

        • Random small reward
        1. Continue
    • oTxtPlagueColonyHealers_cultist_c

      Your victory has allowed the militia to drive off the rest of the cultist vessels. The fleet of militia ships encircle the outpost and bombard its habitation blocks. The commander explains: "This colony has been overrun by cultists. Such malignancy must be purged." By the time you leave the outpost is little more than a smoldering ruin.

      1. Continue
  2. Attack the militia
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtPlagueColonyHealers_militiaA

      You move in to attack a militia ship.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire Raider ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtPlagueColonyHealers_militiaKillA

        Your victory has allowed the cultists to rout the local defense militia. The outpost commandant hails you. "A thousand blessings upon you. If those thugs had won they would surely persecute all our unfortunate ill."

        1. Continue
          oTxtPlagueColonyHealers_militiaKillA_1

          The commandant presents you with a psychomantic wafer containing an engram of one of their celebratory rituals.

          • Random Death Cult psychic item
          1. Continue
      • oTxtPlagueColonyHealers_militiaKillB

        Your victory has allowed the cultists to rout the local defense militia. The outpost commandant hails you. "What have you done? Those death cultists have been working their nefarious sorcery for years, bringing sickness to our home."

        1. Continue
          oTxtPlagueColonyHealers_militiaKillB_1

          The outpost refuses any further communications, and you move on.

          1. Continue
    • oTxtPlagueColonyHealers_militiaB

      You engage a militia vessel. "So, you too are an enemy!"

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire Raider ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • → oTxtPlagueColonyHealers_militiaKillA
      • → oTxtPlagueColonyHealers_militiaKillB
  3. Leave
    oTxtPlagueColonyHealers_introRecover

    You avoid involvement and continue on.

    1. Continue
oTxtPlagueColonyHealers_cultistLeaveA Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You bring your ship about and leave the scene.

  1. Continue

PlagueColonyHealers_cultist

→ oTxtPlagueColonyHealers_cultist_b

PlagueColonyHealers_militiaKillA

→ oTxtPlagueColonyHealers_militiaKillA_1

PlagueColonyHealers_militiaKillB

→ oTxtPlagueColonyHealers_militiaKillB_1

PlagueDelivery

oTxtPlagueDelivery_intro

You are hailed by a decrepit vessel. They offer you payment to help them deliver a shipment of life-saving medicine to a distant hab-colony.

  1. Accept
    oTxtPlagueDelivery_accept

    You are transferred a great quantity of vials containing a sickly colored fluid and given the coordinates for the delivery. "Here is half of the payment now... The rest of the payment ...after safe delivery."

    • Random small reward
    • Quest marker added to map: oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_intro
    1. Continue
  2. Decline
    oTxtPlagueDelivery_decline

    You shut off the comms and move on.

    1. Continue
  3. (Death Cultist) Administer the medicine to your own crew
    oTxtPlagueDelivery_bt_plagueCultist

    You accept the commission with no intent of delivering it to its intended recipient. A cultist crewmember analyzes the concoctions and informs you the medicine would not be beneficial to any but those prepared by the teachings of the death cults. You relegate its disposal to your cultist, who promptly imbibes the entire stock before falling insensible.

    1. Continue
      oTxtPlagueDelivery_bt_plagueCultist1

      Your cultist crewmember awakens from their stupor. They are euphoric, and speak of revelatory visions. At length, they transcribe their experience as a psychomantic ritual.

      • Random Death Cult psychic item
      1. Continue

PlagueDeliveryQuest

oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_intro

You arrive at the delivery destination, an Imperial orbital hab-colony. Suddenly, you are intercepted by a local system defense patrol vessel. They ask if you have any cargo to declare before they inspect your ship.

  1. Declare the medication in your cargo
    oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_declare

    "We have become aware of cases where death cultists have been approaching passing starships to spread their blight under the guise of delivering medication. Submit your cargo for inspection."

    1. Submit
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_submitB

        The contents of the vials indeed turn out to be death cult mutagenic agents. "You have done right to yield us this deadly cargo. Its destruction represents the saving of nearly 800 million souls. Nevertheless, we cannot allow you to dock at the colony as we cannot trust you any further."

        1. Continue
      • oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_submitA

        The contents of the vials indeed turn out to be death cult mutagenic agents. Suddenly, you witness through the viewscreen the crew of the patrol ship writhing in agony. The patrol ship changes course and collides into the hab-colony. As you scoop up some of the scrap ejected from the collision, your comms pick up various choking, garbled screams of terror and anguish.

        • Large amount of ⬗ Scrap
        1. Continue
    2. Refuse
      oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_refuseDeclare

      "Then we shall take it by force."

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_refuseDeclare_win

      You offload your cargo at the hab-colony, whereupon the lord governor issues you the remainder of your payment. "You have saved me a lot of work by getting rid of that pesky militia ship."

      • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
  2. Proceed to the inspection
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_proceedB

      The inspection team notices the vials of sickly colored fluid. "These are death cult class B mutagenics. Did you really think you could smuggle these in?"

      • 2–3 Imperial Recruit or Imperial Scribe boarders
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_proceedB_win

      You hear a loud detonation. The inspection team had placed demolition charges on your toxic cargo. While the majority of the toxins vented out through a breach in the hull, the little remaining residue has a malign effect on your crew.

      • −1 hull HP
      • 2 breaches spawned
      • Debuff applied to all crew
      1. Continue
    • oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_proceedA

      The inspection team reviews your cargo and notices nothing unusual. You are able to offload your cargo at the hab-colony. The lord governor seems relieved. "This new medication has arrived just in time. The diseases have been becoming more resistant for some time now."

      • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
  3. Refuse the inspection
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_refuseInspectionB

      "Very well. By the grace of the God-King, I hereby charge you with defiance of Imperial authority. The punishment is death." The patrol vessel powers up its weapons to attack.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_refuseInspectionB_win

      You offload your cargo at the hab-colony, whereupon the lord governor issues you the remainder of your payment. "You have saved me a lot of work by getting rid of that pesky militia ship."

      • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
    • oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_refuseInspectionA

      "You are obviously up to no good." The patrol vessel powers up its weapons to attack.

      1. Continue
        oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_refuseInspectionA_1

        Suddenly, subspace rifts erupt all around you, and a small fleet of death cult bloatskiffs emerge. They smother the patrol vessel with corrosive cannon fire. It melts away in seconds, leaving only molten slag.

        1. Continue
          oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_refuseInspectionA_2

          You offload your cargo at the hab-colony, and the lord governor issues you the remainder of your payment. "This medication will be distributed promptly." He invites you to witness the results.

          1. Accept
            oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_acceptResults

            You watch as the colonists are rapidly transfigured into waddling, pustulent creatures. Inhuman sounds of terror fill the air as cultists puncture the mutated colonists with various tubes, and begin siphoning the colonists' liquified innards into themselves.

            1. Continue
              Random outcome (1/2 each):
              • oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_acceptResultsA

                The lord governor leans over. "This medicine is the only salve for these Endwenders." You cough and stagger back to your ship before you can be overwhelmed by the toxin consuming the hab-colony.

                • Commander damaged (20%)
                1. Continue
              • oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_acceptResultsB

                One of the transformed colonists is being dissected by a group of cultists. At length, a cultist scurries over from the group and presents a psychomantic wafer to you, still glistening with blood. The lord governor whispers in your ear. "For you, a fruit of our labors."

                • Random Death Cult psychic item
                1. Continue
          2. Decline
            oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_declineResults

            As you depart, you overhear the gleeful hoots of the cultists over the anguished wailing of the colonists.

            1. Continue
  4. Leave
    → oTxtEZIonOutpost_leave
oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_leave Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You decide to avoid the situation. Later, you dump your cargo into the void.

  1. Continue

PlagueDeliveryQuest_proceedB

→ oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_proceedB_win

PlagueDeliveryQuest_refuseDeclare

→ oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_refuseDeclare_win

PlagueDeliveryQuest_refuseInspectionA

→ oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_refuseInspectionA_1
oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_refuseInspectionA_1a Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You complete your delivery to the hab-colony. You receive payment and move on.

  • Random moderate reward
  1. Continue

PlagueDeliveryQuest_refuseInspectionB

→ oTxtPlagueDeliveryQuest_refuseInspectionB_win

PlagueDelivery_bt

→ oTxtPlagueDelivery_bt_plagueCultist

PlagueRaidFort

oTxtPlagueRaidFort_generic Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You arrive near an imperial colony recently overtaken by the death cult. Imperial dorm blocks and temples now sit in ruins beneath the toxic clouds above. Your scanners detect a Gorgothian defensive fortress; given its recent construction it is undoubtedly vulnerable to infiltration. The colony's riches taken by the Gorgothians may soon be taken by you.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Plague Fort ship

PlagueRaidFort_boarding

oTxtPlagueRaidFort_boarding_demonic Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You arrive near a planet under the death cult's total control. Poisoned serfs toil under the acid rain, all in service of their decaying deity. You scan the surface for orbital defences, quickly uncover a neglected planetside fortress, and advance on the facility. Your intrusion has not gone unnoticed by the Gorgothians however. "Repellant interloper, you shall face our god's noxious wrath." A bolt of emerald lightning suddenly rises from the fortress and strikes your ship. Sirens blare -- you have been boarded.

  • 3–4 poison clouds spawned
  • 5–6 Ghoul or Rotspawn boarders
  1. Continue
Fight! vs Plague Fort ship

PrisonerFreed

oTxtPrisonerFreed Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Your crew breaks open the container. The prisoner inside escapes back to your ship.

  1. Continue

PrisonerFreedPlayer

oTxtPrisonerFreedPlayer Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You break open the container. A teleport beacon is offered to the slave inside, who immediately accepts it and recalls back to your ship. Grateful, the freed slave enthusiastically joins your crew.

  1. Continue

PsyZombieInfest

oTxtPsyZombieInfest_intro

You exit subspace to a region smothered in toxic gas. You pick up a distress signal somewhere deep within the gaseous clouds.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtPsyZombieInfest_investigateB

      The distress signal disappears before you can triangulate its source. Soon after, the sleek hull of a technocultist vessel sails out of the gas clouds. The ship has been taken over by a death cultist.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Techno ship
    • oTxtPsyZombieInfest_investigateC

      The signal is being emitted by an outpost. A panicked civilian hails you. "By the Throne, save us!" You hear in the background the din of countless fists beating against a metal bulkhead.

      1. Continue
        oTxtPsyZombieInfest_investigateC2

        You begin to respond, but the sudden appearance of toxic gas chokes you back. You soon find yourself under attack by shambling abominations that seemingly precipitated from the toxic gas itself.

        • 1–3 poison clouds spawned
        • 6 Greater Corpulent or Ghoul Magus boarders
        1. Continue
        After the boarders are defeated:
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtPsyZombieInfest_investigateC2_1

          You turn your attention back to the colony. It seems to have suffered some catastrophic rupture while you were busy repelling boarders. You check the wreck for salvage, but discover that everything has been contaminated by foul energies.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtPsyZombieInfest_investigateC2_2

          Having repelled the invasion aboard your ship, you board the outpost to investigate. You carve your way through a seemingly interminable horde of shambling horrors before you discover a lone survivor. They request to join your crew, having nowhere else to go.

          1. Accept
            oTxtPsyZombieInfest_acceptCrew

            You accept them into your crew.

            • Corsair or Scavenger or Imperial Recruit or Imperial Scribe
            1. Continue
          2. Decline
            oTxtPsyZombieInfest_declineCrew

            You drop them off at the nearest adjacent outpost.

            1. Continue
    • oTxtPsyZombieInfest_investigateA

      You track the signal back to a disabled cult warship. You board it to find cultists lying dead all over the decks. They seem to have been overwhelmed by the corrosive gas suffusing the ship.

      1. Continue
        oTxtPsyZombieInfest_investigateA2

        Putrid energy assaults your senses as the metallic halls of the vessel begin to twist and distort. The vessel undergoes an eerie transformation. You retreat to your vessel, and as you pass, you witness the fallen cultists reanimating, their corpses rising from the ground.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Possessed ship
  2. Ignore
    oTxtPsyZombieInfest_ignore

    You decide to move on.

    1. Continue

PsyZombieInfest_investigateC2

→ oTxtPsyZombieInfest_investigateC2_1

QuestMarkerAdded

oTxtQuestMarkerAdded Unreachable — not referenced by any event

The coordinates have been added to your map.

RaiderAbandonedSmuggler

oTxtRaiderAbandonedSmuggler_intro

You discover an abandoned corsair hauler drifting in space. Scorch marks on the engines suggest this vessel was intercepted by an Imperial patrol ship.

  1. Investigate further
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtRaiderAbandonedSmuggler_investigateA

      You board the abandoned hauler and recover a few things of value left behind.

      • Random moderate reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtRaiderAbandonedSmuggler_investigateC

      Little of worth remains on the hauler. You uncover a hidden crawlspace used for smuggling contraband. Unfortunately, it is empty. You take what you can and return to your ship.

      • Very small amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
    • oTxtRaiderAbandonedSmuggler_investigateB

      You manage to discover a hidden crawlspace used for smuggling contraband. Inside you find the desiccated remains of an unfortunate corsair that must have hid there for too long, as well as a bizarre artifact.

      • Random cult equipment
      1. Continue
    • oTxtRaiderAbandonedSmuggler_investigateD

      Little of value remains on the ship, but you recover its data-logs. You learn that the hauler was not intercepted by an Imperial patrol, but rather by raiders from a rival clan.

      1. Continue
        oTxtRaiderAbandonedSmuggler_investigateD2

        Suddenly, you are alerted to the arrival of a new contact. You return aboard your ship to see a raider ship enter visual range. "Oh, it's my lucky day. Power down your weapons and prepare to surrender your vessel." You feel a solid thump as an assault pod slams into your hull.

        • 1–2 Raider crew boarders
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Raider ship
  2. Ignore
    oTxtRaiderAbandonedSmuggler_leave

    You ignore the wreck and move on.

    1. Continue
  3. (Commando) Search the vessel
    oTxtRaiderAbandonedSmuggler_bt_commando

    You take advantage of your crew member's knowledge of corsair smuggling operations to conduct a thorough search of the hauler. Large portions of the vessel had been ransacked and burned out in the typical manner of Imperial attacks, but a number of secret compartments managed to avoid discovery.

    1. Continue
      oTxtRaiderAbandonedSmuggler_bt_commando2

      Your raider crewmember is able to identify the hidden compartments. Some of the hidden compartments lie deep within airless sections of the vessel. Fortunately, your raider commando is protected from the vacuum by his exosuit and is able to slowly pick through the rubble to access the compartments beyond.

      1. Continue
        oTxtRaiderAbandonedSmuggler_bt_commando3

        Your crewmember discovers within the previously inaccessible space a cryopod. It has managed to remain intact, although the self-contained backup power source appears to be nearly expended. From the initial readouts you notice the occupant of the cryopod is not a raider. Perhaps this was the cause of the hauler's doom.

        1. Continue
          oTxtRaiderAbandonedSmuggler_bt_commando4

          You wake the individual within the cryopod. They emerge disoriented, and with nowhere else to go, join up as a member of your crew.

          • Random crew member
          1. Continue

RaiderAbandonedSmuggler_bt

→ oTxtRaiderAbandonedSmuggler_bt_commando

RaiderAmbushAsteroid

oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_intro

You emerge from subspace inside a large asteroid belt. Several wrecked starships are visible in the distance.

  1. Investigate the wrecks
    Random outcome (1/10 each):
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigateA

      You spend a few hours investigating the wrecks but turn up with nothing.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigateI

      You move closer to the wrecks and are suddenly ambushed by a pirate vessel.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider ship
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigateE

      You move in to inspect what looks like a long-abandoned cult starship. You retrieve some unusual items from the wreck.

      • 60%: Random cult psychic item / 40%: Random psychic item
      1. Continue
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigateD

      You investigate the wrecks and are rewarded for your efforts when you discover a fully intact weapons storage container.

      • Random ship weapon
      1. Continue
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigateC

      You move in for a closer look. A few intact supply containers are visible in what looks like an old, technocult survey vessel. You secure them and bring them back to your ship.

      • Random moderate reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigateB

      You approach one of the larger wrecks. It looks to have been some sort of Imperial capital ship. Not much is left to take, but you do manage to retrieve some valuables from the crew quarters.

      • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigateF

      You move in to investigate. One of the wrecks looks like it was destroyed fairly recently. You board the ship and discover a survivor who must have managed to lock themselves in a stasis pod before things went awry.

      • Low-tier mixed crew reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigateJ

      You maneuver your ship through the asteroid field for a closer look. Suddenly you hear an explosion from the lower decks. You have been boarded! To make matters worse, a hostile ship suddenly appears on your sensors.

      • 1–2 Corsair or Scavenger boarders
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider ship
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigateH

      You embark onto the boarding craft and prepare to launch, when you are interrupted by the sudden blaring of warning klaxons.

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigateH1

          You rush back to your ship and discover intruders aboard. They have already sabotaged your life support system and are clearly looking for blood.

          • 2–3 Corsair or Scavenger boarders
          1. Continue
        • oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigateH2

          You hastily retreat to your ship and find that intruders have sabotaged your systems. A pirate vessel suddenly emerges from hiding, weapons armed. "Looks like we caught another one, boys..."

          • Destroy random system
          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Raider ship
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigateG

      You maneuver your ship closer to the wrecks. You are able to locate some supplies within the first wreckage.

      • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
        oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigateG2

        You experience increased meteorite activity as you move towards the other wrecks. It would be hazardous to commit to a salvage operation under such conditions, but not impossible. Furthermore, you detect a few anomalous energy emissions that may indicate the presence of a marauding vessel.

        1. Continue
          Random outcome (1/3 each):
          • oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_proceedA

            Your salvage operation is successful, and you are able to recover a generous quantity of supplies. However, your crew suffers injuries from high-speed meteoroid impacts.

            • Random large reward
            • Random crew damaged (30%)
            1. Continue
          • → oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigateF
          • oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_proceedB

            You move on to salvage the remaining wreckage. During the attempt, a particularly large meteoroid impact causes hull plating to smash into one of your crew, pulverizing their innards and killing them instantly.

            • Kill a random crew member
            1. Continue
              oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_proceedB2

              To make matters worse, a pirate vessel suddenly emerges from hiding and begins firing at your ship. You hear maniacal laughter over the comms as they move in to attack.

              1. Continue
              Fight! vs Raider ship
        2. Leave
          oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigate_leave

          Whatever the remaining wreckage may contain is not worth the risk. You break off and leave the area.

          1. Continue
  2. Avoid the wrecks
    oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_avoid

    You decide it is not worth the trouble.

    1. Continue
  3. (Sensor 2) Scan for suspicious activity
    oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_bt_sensor

    You order an enhanced scan of the area. Your sensors reveal a previously undetected raider vessel lurking among the asteroids. With some clever maneuvering, you surprise the raiders. Caught unaware, they eject some supplies as they hastily make their escape.

    • Random moderate reward
    1. Continue
  4. (Raider Outlaw) Sweep for an ambush before investigating
    oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_bt_raider

    One of your crewmembers insists on conducting further sensor sweeps. They advise you of certain areas that in their experience were ideal locations for hiding starships.

    1. Continue
      oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_bt_raider1

      You conduct multiple sensor sweeps before finally picking up a lurking pirate vessel. The pirates are discouraged by your alertness and leave the area.

      1. Continue
        oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_bt_raider2

        You return to search the wreckage field you spotted previously.

        1. Continue
          Random outcome (1/6 each):
          • → oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigateA
          • → oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigateE
          • → oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigateD
          • → oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigateC
          • → oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigateB
          • → oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigateF

RaiderAmbushAsteroid_bt

→ oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_bt_raider

RaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigate

→ oTxtRaiderAmbushAsteroid_investigate_leave

RaiderAmbushMunition_bt

oTxtRaiderAmbushMunition_bt_sensor

A detailed scan uncovers the depot as part of an elaborate raider trap. Realizing the failure of their ruse, the raiders recklessly attack.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Raider ship
After the rewards from the fight:
oTxtRaiderAmbushMunition_bt_sensor2

With the raider threat dissipated, you search the munitions depot. You recover a functioning starship armament from within the depot.

  • Random ship weapon
  1. Continue

RaiderAmbushMunitions

oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_intro

You come across an abandoned munitions depot. It is in suspiciously good shape, but whatever supplies that lay within may very well be worth the risk.

  1. Check it out
    Random outcome (1/8 each):
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_checkItOutA

      You check out the abandoned depot. Unfortunately it appears everything of value has long been looted.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_checkItOutB

      You enter the munitions depot. The silence is eerie but nothing unusual happens. You return to your ship with a few crates of supplies.

      • Random moderate reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_checkItOutF

      You maneuver your ship in for a closer look. As soon as you drop your guard, a corsair starship bursts forth and looses a volley of explosives at your ship, taking out one of your systems. You scramble to battle stations and prepare to fight.

      • Destroy random system
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider ship
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_checkItOutG

      You prepare to enter the munitions depot. As you approach the entrance, the airlocks suddenly disengage and a gang of lowlifes bursts through the opening, pushing past you into your ship. This was a trap. You turn around and get ready to repel boarders.

      • 2–4 Raider crew boarders
      1. Continue
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_checkItOutH

      You enter the munitions depot and successfully retrieve a functioning starship armament.

      • Random ship weapon
      1. Continue
        oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_checkItOutH2

        As you return to your ship, you are ambushed by a gang of pirates. They have neutralized your security systems and wounded one of your crew. You ready your weapons.

        • Destroy choose(o Sys Door Control, o Sys Sensors)
        • Random crew damaged (50%)
        • 2–3 Corsair or Scavenger boarders
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Raider Small ship
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_checkItOutE

      You check out the munitions depot. You discover the structure was built without ever being utilized for its intended purpose. You retrieve a few useful objects of value and return to your vessel.

      • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
        oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_checkItOutE2

        On your return you are greeted by a couple of thugs, along with a heavily-armed corsair starship. Looks like this was a trap.

        • 1–3 Corsair or Scavenger boarders
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Raider ship
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_checkItOutD

      You move in to investigate the munitions depot. Inside, you manage to recover a large cache of unexploded ordnance.

      • Large amount of missile ammo (8–12) and 1/2 chance for 1 missile blueprint
      1. Continue
        oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_checkItOutD2

        Unfortunately this was a trap. Corsair pirates are waiting for you back at your ship. They must have destroyed your security systems while you were away. You get ready to fight.

        • Destroy Door Control
        • 1–2 Corsair or Scavenger boarders
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Raider ship
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_checkItOutC

      You explore the munitions depot. Your instincts prove correct when you discover a container holding a starship-grade armament in pristine condition.

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_checkItOutC1

          You secure the weapon and bring it back to your ship.

          • Random ship weapon
          1. Continue
        • oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_checkItOutC2

          Unfortunately, your suspicions also prove correct when a raider starship suddenly floats into view. You hastily bring the weapon back to your vessel.

          • Random ship weapon
          1. Continue
            oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_checkItOutC2_a

            The raider ship begins firing at you.

            1. Continue
            Fight! vs Raider ship
  2. Move on
    oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_moveOn

    You decide to move on.

    1. Continue
  3. (Sensor 2) Scan for suspicious activity
    → oTxtRaiderAmbushMunition_bt_sensor
  4. (Raider Outlaw) Search for an ambush
    oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_bt_raider1

    The raiders are unwilling to give up the munitions depot. However, you are well prepared for a fight, and engage with weapons already primed and ready.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Raider ship
    After the rewards from the fight:
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_bt_raider_winA

      You return to check out the munitions depot. You recover a large quantity of missile parts.

      • Large amount of missile ammo (8–12) and 1/2 chance for 1 missile blueprint
      1. Continue
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_bt_raider_winB

      You return to check out the munitions depot. You discover a starship weapon in serviceable condition.

      • Random ship weapon
      1. Continue

RaiderAmbushMunitions_bt

oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_bt_raider Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Feeling certain to be ambushed, you consult a raider clansman among your crew for the most likely locations of a hidden attacker. After a number of search attempts, you discover a lurking raider vessel.

  1. Continue

RaiderAmbushMunitions_bt_raider

→ oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_bt_raider_winA

RaiderAmbushMunitions_checkItOutC2

→ oTxtRaiderAmbushMunitions_checkItOutC2_a

RaiderAmbushSolar

oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_intro

You exit subspace and find yourself dangerously close to a white-hot dwarf star. An Imperial transport ship idles silently in the distance, its crew having succumbed to the intense heat of the stellar mass. The situation is risky but there may be material worth recovering aboard that ship.

  1. Board the ship
    Random outcome (1/6 each):
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_boardB

      You board the vessel and recover a turret armament.

      • Random ship weapon
      1. Continue
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_boardA

      You board the vessel and manage to recover a few supply containers.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_boardC

      You board the vessel and find several storage container near the crew quarters.

      • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_boardE

      You approach the transport vessel and are instantly attacked by corsair raiders. Some of them even managed to board your ship. Looks like it was a trap.

      • 2–4 Corsair or Scavenger boarders
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider ship
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_boardD

      You approach the transport. Suddenly, a raider ship emerges from behind the dwarf star and begins firing at you. "Another one falls into our trap. This is getting too easy."

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider ship
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_boardG

      You board the ship and secure a large storage container full of material. Unfortunately one of your crew members loses their footing and careens through a weakened section of the ship's hull. Floating through empty space and exposed to the intense heat of the star, their body is quickly incinerated.

      • Random large reward
      • Lose a random crew member
      1. Continue
  2. Avoid the ship
    oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_avoid

    You decide it is too risky and move on.

    1. Continue
  3. (Sensor 3) Scan for suspicious activity
    oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_bt_sensor

    Despite the intense plasma interference of the nearby star, your powerful sensor suite manages to detect a raider ship lying in wait.

    1. Continue
      oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_bt_sensor2

      Your advanced sensor suite masks your presence and you are able to retrieve a number of valuables from aboard the abandoned Imperial transport without alerting the raiders. You move on.

      • Random large reward
      1. Continue
  4. (Raider Outlaw) Search for an ambush before investigating
    oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_bt_raider

    One of your crew is well acquainted with the ambushing techniques employed by the raider clans. Under their direction you conduct fine sensor sweeps to search for signs of a lurking raider ship.

    1. Continue
      oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_bt_raider1

      On the sweep you detect a previously undetected raider vessel. Alarmed at their discovery, the raiders flee.

      1. Continue
        oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_bt_raider2

        With the threat dissipated, you proceed towards the adrift transport ship.

        1. Continue
          Random outcome (1/3 each):
          • → oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_boardB
          • → oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_boardA
          • → oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_boardC
oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_goInAfter Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You go in after them. The boarding team is surprised to see you but does not complain. Suddenly one of your fellow boarders receives a short tightbeam message. "Oh Throne, this was a trap! The pirates, they destroyed our ship!"

  1. Continue
    oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_goInAfter2

    You hastily return to your own ship after hearing this news. The surviving boarders agree to join your crew and help you fight off the pirate ship.

    • Imperial Recruit, Imperial Enforcer, or Imperial Marshal
    • Imperial Recruit or Imperial Scribe
    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Raider ship
oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_wait1 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You decide to wait and observe the other boarding party. A quick scan shows that they have left their main starship, a simple salvage ship, just outside the solar flare radius of the dwarf star. Their boarding craft reaches the idling transport vessel.

  1. Continue
    oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_wait2

    Moments later, a pirate ship emerges from a nearby debris field and opens fire at the transport vessel, obliterating it along with the unlucky boarding team. The pirates proceed to capture the now-undefended salvage ship, loot its contents, and retreat back to the safety of their own ship. Before you have a chance to react, the pirates are gone.

    1. Continue
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_wait2B

        You attempt to scavenge what remains inside the salvage ship but there is nothing left.

        1. Continue
      • oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_wait2A

        You survey the salvage ship and discover some left over supply containers that the pirates must have overlooked.

        • Random small reward
        1. Continue

RaiderAmbushSolar_bt

→ oTxtRaiderAmbushSolar_bt_raider

RaiderAmbushSpace

oTxtRaiderAmbushSpace_intro

Glittering stars dot your viewport. Despite the war-ravaged state of humanity's great projects, moments like these can almost seem peaceful.

  1. Continue
    Random outcome (1/4 each):
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushSpace_intro1

      Naturally, these moments do not last, and reality sets in once more as a pirate vessel crawls out from a nearby dust cloud.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider ship
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushSpace_intro3

      The calm is suddenly interrupted by the piercing shriek of combat sirens. Several breaches appear across your ship and you realize you have been ambushed by a corsair strike vessel.

      • 2–3 breaches spawned
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider ship
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushSpace_intro4

      Suddenly the moment is interrupted by streaks of cannon fire, though none of it seems to be directed at you. Ahead, you see an unlucky transport ship being ambushed by corsairs. A few minutes later, the transport ship is destroyed, leaving only a smoldering wreck. By the time you arrive at the scene, the wreckage has been stripped bare of anything useful.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushSpace_intro2

      Suddenly a weak signature appears on your data-feed. It is a faint distress signal, emanating from a barely-functioning stasis pod. You pick up the pod and resuscitate its occupant. The survivor's eyes snap open. "It is a trap! Full power to engines! Activate shields!"

      1. Continue
        oTxtRaiderAmbushSpace_intro2A

        You barely register the words when explosions begin to rock your hull. Your shields go down. A corsair starship reveals itself on your sensors. The survivor collects himself. "By Grace of the Immortal King, if we are to die, let us die in glory."

        • Destroy Shields
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Raider ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        oTxtRaiderAmbushSpace_intro2A_win

        The survivor is inspired by your victory and elects to join your crew.

        • Gain crew: Survivor
        1. Continue
oTxtRaiderAmbushSpace_intro5 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Unfortunately the quiet calm of your surroundings has caused you to drop your guard. Blasts sound from below decks and you realize you have been ambushed by corsair reavers.

  • 1–2 Raider crew boarders
  1. Continue
Fight! vs Raider ship

RaiderAmbushSpace_intro2A

→ oTxtRaiderAmbushSpace_intro2A_win

RaiderAmbushTechno

oTxtRaiderAmbushTechno_intro

A technocult vessel idles near the jump point. Its crew of machine priests makes contact with you and asks you to escort them to a nearby penal colony. Apparently they have business with one of the prisoners there.

  1. Agree to escort them
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushTechno_agreeB

      You agree to escort them.

      • Quest marker added to map: oTxtRaiderAmbushTechno_agreeB1
      1. Continue
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushTechno_agreeA

      The technopriests are pleased with your decision. You allow their ship to trail behind your own.

      1. Continue
        oTxtRaiderAmbushTechno_agreeA2

        Soon after embarking on your journey, you are ambushed by a corsair starship. "You cannot escape."

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Raider ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        oTxtRaiderAmbushTechno_agreeA3

        After surviving the ambush, the rest of your journey is relatively uneventful. You reach the penal colony and part ways with the technopriests. They thank you for your assistance and offer a small payment.

        • Random small reward
        1. Continue
  2. Refuse
    oTxtRaiderAmbushTechno_refuse

    You refuse.

    1. Continue
oTxtRaiderAmbushTechno_agreeB1

You arrive in-system a short distance away from the penal colony. The technopriests trailing behind you bristle with anticipation, having nearly reached their destination. "Take heed, commander. Our cognition engines have calculated a high probability of danger."

  1. Continue
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushTechno_agreeB2

      As you make your final approach, warning sirens suddenly begin sounding. Incendiary missiles collide with your hull, causing multiple fires across your ship. You target lock the aggressor.

      • 2–3 fires spawned
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtRaiderAmbushTechno_agreeB2_a

      You contact the technopriests to confirm their status. Their overseer reports that their ship has suffered moderate damage, but fortunately, nobody aboard was injured during the attack.

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtRaiderAmbushTechno_agreeB2_a2

          You arrive at the penal colony without further incident. The technopriests transfer over a small reward before parting ways.

          • Random moderate reward
          1. Continue
        • oTxtRaiderAmbushTechno_agreeB2_a1

          You arrive at the penal colony without further incident. The technopriests are grateful for your service and offer a set of servo-husks as payment.

          • Husk
          • Husk
          1. Continue
    • oTxtRaiderAmbushTechno_agreeB3

      The technopriests' warning proves correct as suddenly a pirate frigate drops into realspace and begins firing at you. You maneuver your ship between the technopriests and the pirate frigate.

      1. Continue
        oTxtRaiderAmbushTechno_agreeB3_a

        As you engage the pirate frigate, your technopriest allies contact you, offering assistance.

        1. Request assistance from the technopriests
          oTxtRaiderAmbushTechno_agreeB3_b

          The technopriests confirm your request. "Commander, we have deployed our combat husks to assist you."

          1. Continue
          After the rewards from the fight:
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • → oTxtRaiderAmbushTechno_agreeB2_a1
          • → oTxtRaiderAmbushTechno_agreeB2_a2

RaiderAmbushTechno_agreeB2

→ oTxtRaiderAmbushTechno_agreeB2_a

RaiderAmbushTechno_agreeB3

→ oTxtRaiderAmbushTechno_agreeB3_a

RaiderArmisticeZone

oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_intro

You have entered one of the fabled Armistice Zones designated by the raider clans.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_investigateB

      You move forward into the Armistice Zone. A corsair vessel emerges from behind the husk of a dead starship. "Alright, hand over the metal. You think I will not shoot? These artillery AI's have been dormant for thousands of years. There is no way they still work. Just a myth to scare the greenhorns."

      • −30 ⬗ Scrap
      1. Pay 30 scrap
        oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_pay

        "Yeah. That's what I thought." The raider collects your payment and promptly jumps out.

        1. Continue
      2. Refuse
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_refuse2

          The raider seems irritated, but soon withdraws. It turns out he was bluffing after all.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_refuse

          The raider ship opens fire, genuinely undeterred by the threat of the artillery AI.

          1. Continue
            Random outcome (1/2 each):
            • oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_refuseA

              You get ready to defend yourself. Fortunately for both of you, the raider pilot was correct in his assessment -- the AI does not activate.

              1. Continue
              Fight! vs Raider ship
            • oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_refuseB

              Suddenly you hear a thunderous tone signaling the activation of the artillery AI. An ion wave sweeps across both of your ships and you realize you are in trouble. You hear the raider pilot over the comms, "I'm not afraid of some AI ghost. You're dead meat."

              • Ionize up to 3 random systems
              1. Continue
              Fight! vs Raider ship
      3. (Raider Outlaw) Negotiate the situation
        oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_bt_raider

        You delegate the negotiations to a corsair crewmember. The crewmember is well versed in secret signs and signals of their culture, and quickly establishes a rapport with the other raiders. Rather than demanding a toll from you, they send you and your crew off with some supplies.

        • Random moderate reward
        1. Continue
    • oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_investigateA

      Several raider clan fleets are present. They appear to be having some sort of negotiation.

      1. Continue
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_investigateA3

          After a few minutes, the negotiations conclude. The raider fleets activate their drives and jump out. You move through the Armistice Zone and find nothing of interest.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_investigateA2

          Suddenly you see flashes of cannon fire. A battle breaks loose between the raider fleets, triggering the ancient, automated artillery system guarding this region. Huge waves of ordnance bombard the battling fleets and soon all raider ships have either fled or been destroyed.

          1. Continue
            Random outcome (1/3 each):
            • oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_investigateA2_2

              The wreck of one of the raider frigates drifts past you. You manage to secure a substantial number of intact storage vaults before making your escape.

              • Random large reward
              1. Continue
            • oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_investigateA2_1

              A loose supply container hurtles past your ship, along with collection of miscellaneous debris from the battle. You quickly snatch up the container and continue on with your journey.

              • Random small reward
              1. Continue
            • oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_investigateA2_3

              Several containers of unexploded ordnance drift past you. They must be remnants from one of the corsair warships destroyed by the artillery AI. You pick them up before continuing onwards with your journey.

              • Large amount of missile ammo (8–12) and 1/2 chance for 1 missile blueprint
              1. Continue
  2. Consult your databanks
    oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_consult

    Clan Armistice Zones are star systems designated as no-fire zones between the rival warlords of the Five Great Clans.

    1. Continue
      oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_consult2

      These zones are strictly enforced by ancient AI-driven artillery systems, thought to have been built in an age when mankind first reached the stars and was forced to defend itself against hostile invaders. The technology and armaments driving these systems is far beyond the reach of the present day Empire, making these sites the perfect neutral ground for the emissaries of the Five Clans to conduct their business.

      1. Investigate
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • → oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_investigateB
        • → oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_investigateA
      2. Move on
        oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_moveOn

        You move on.

        1. Continue
  3. Move on
    → oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_moveOn
  4. (Shield 6) Call the corsair's bluff
    oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_bt_shield

    You ensure your shield emitters are operating at peak power before calling the corsairs bluff. They manage to fire one ineffectual salvo off before a brilliant lance of energy from one of the AI artillery eviscerates their vessel. You collect some scraps from the wreckage before moving on.

    • Random moderate reward
    1. Continue

RaiderArmisticeZone_bt

→ oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_bt_raider

RaiderArmisticeZone_investigateA2

→ oTxtRaiderArmisticeZone_investigateA2_1

RaiderAttackColony

oTxtRaiderAttackColony_intro

You are transiting close to a backwater orbital colony when an artillery shot smashes into your hull.

  • −1–2 hull HP
  1. Continue
    oTxtRaiderAttackColony_intro_b

    The shot originated from the colony's defense grid.

    1. Contact the colony
      oTxtRaiderAttackColony_colony

      The colonial governor offers his apologies. According to him, raiders have been regularly attacking the colony and carrying off supplies. Your arrival unfortunately coincided with the next expected raider attack. The governor requests your aid in defending the colony.

      1. Defend the colony
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtRaiderAttackColony_raidA

          A band of raider ships emerges from subspace. You challenge the largest among them to a duel.

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Raider Large ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • oTxtRaiderAttackColony_raidA_winA

            The smaller raider ships scatter and jump away upon seeing their flagship reduced to a twisted heap. Your display of valor has inspired countless requests from colonists hoping to leave the colony as part of your crew. With permission from the colonial governor, you select the most promising candidate to serve under your command.

            • Imperial Recruit
            1. Continue
          • oTxtRaiderAttackColony_raidA_winB

            The destruction of their flagship has scattered the remnants of the raider band. Unfortunately, stray weapon fire from the engagement has wrought havoc upon the orbital hab-colony. There is little more you can do but move on.

            1. Continue
        • oTxtRaiderAttackColony_raidB

          A raider ship emerges from subspace. You interpose your vessel between it and the colony. The raider is surprised to encounter so much resistance and promptly flees.

          1. Continue
            Random outcome (1/2 each):
            • oTxtRaiderAttackColony_raidB_winB

              The colonial governor expresses his relief at the peaceful conclusion. You receive some scrap in thanks, and you move on.

              • Very small amount of ⬗ Scrap
              1. Continue
            • oTxtRaiderAttackColony_raidB_winA

              The colonial governor expresses his relief at the peaceful conclusion. Unfortunately, the colony has nothing to offer in way of recompense. You move on.

              1. Continue
      2. Attack the colony
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtRaiderAttackColony_attackA

          You strike and disable the long range artillery that formed the centerpiece of the colony's defenses. The governor quickly capitulates and offers you tribute from their treasury.

          • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
          1. Continue
            Random outcome (1/2 each):
            • oTxtRaiderAttackColony_attackA_winA

              Soon after leaving, you detect a raider vessel in pursuit. It closes in and attacks.

              1. Continue
              Fight! vs Raider ship
            • oTxtRaiderAttackColony_attackA_winB

              You proceed onwards. A small band of raider vessels arrive to attack the outpost just as it fades out of your sensor range.

              1. Continue
        • oTxtRaiderAttackColony_attackB
