        You send an anonymous tip to the authorities. Within hours, multiple detachments of heavily-armed Imperial arbitrators descend upon the conspirators. All of them are immediately captured and promptly scheduled for execution.

        1. Continue
          oTxtRebelConspiracy_alertA2

          The commander stationed here decides to ramp up security in response to these recent events. As you undock your ship from the outpost, you are intercepted by an Imperial patrol ship. Unable to produce the requisite ident codes, you are forced to fight.

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Empire ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          oTxtRebelConspiracy_alertA3

          You get out of there before anyone else bothers you.

          • Crew recalled to ship
          1. Continue
      • oTxtRebelConspiracy_alertB

        You send an anonymous tip to the authorities. They deploy a squad of arbitrators to come detain the man, but he has somehow caught wind of their movements and managed to escape.

        1. Continue
          oTxtRebelConspiracy_alertB1

          The Imperial forces stationed here ramp up security in response to these events. You decide to leave before anyone recognizes how many laws you have broken by possessing such a heavily-armed starship. Unfortunately, it is too late. An Imperial strike craft locks on to you just as you undock from the outpost. "Surrender now, traitor, and your death will be swift and painless."

          1. Continue
            oTxtRebelConspiracy_alertB2

            You barely have a chance to respond when a sudden volley of cannon fire slams into the ship from behind, blowing it to pieces. You hear shouting over the comms. "The rebellion has begun. The people of the colonies will fight!"

            1. Continue
              oTxtRebelConspiracy_alertB3

              A massive firefight breaks out as rebel starships emerge from hiding to engage the Imperial authorities. Another Imperial warship appears on your sensors, speeding toward you, weapons primed.

              1. Continue
              Fight! vs Empire ship
              After the rewards from the fight:
              Random outcome (1/2 each):
              • oTxtRebelConspiracy_alertB3_1

                The Imperial ships have all been destroyed. The battle dies down. A few of the rebels welcome you with open arms, thinking you an ally to their cause. They send you a few shipments to help resupply your vessel.

                • Random moderate reward
                1. Continue
              • oTxtRebelConspiracy_alertB3_2

                Your efforts mark a critical turning point in the battle. Soon afterwards, the last remaining Imperial ships are destroyed. You leave the rebels to their celebrations and move on.

                • Crew recalled to ship
                1. Continue
    2. Raid the station before the attack
      Random outcome (1/3 each):
      • oTxtRebelConspiracy_raidA

        You decide to raid the station before the conspirators execute their plan. The raid goes smoothly, and you come away with a decent haul.

        • Random small reward
        1. Continue
      • oTxtRebelConspiracy_raidC

        You decide to raid the station at the aforementioned time. Unfortunately it appears the man was wrong about the station being undefended.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Empire ship
        After the fight:
        oTxtRebelConspiracy_raidC2

        There is no time to mount a salvage operation, as the station is on full combat alert. You move on.

        1. Continue
      • oTxtRebelConspiracy_raidB

        You decide to raid the station. Unfortunately you miscalculate your timing, and the bombs go off before you are able to get clear of the station. You manage to retrieve some supplies, but not without taking significant hull damage.

        • Random small reward
        • −2–4 hull HP
        1. Continue
    3. Do nothing
      oTxtRebelConspiracy_doNothing

      You decide not to get involved

      1. Continue
    4. (Imperial Citizen) Consult your crew before raiding the station
      oTxtRebelConspiracy_bt_empireCrew1

      A member of your crew is familiar with the layouts of Imperial supply stations. Armed with this knowledge, you formulate a plan to raid the station.

      1. Continue
        oTxtRebelConspiracy_bt_empireCrew2

        The information provided by your crewman proves accurate. Your raid on the supply station goes smoothly. You are able to make away with a large amount of useful equipment.

        • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
        • Random ship weapon
        • Large amount of missile ammo (8–12) and 1/2 chance for 1 missile blueprint
        1. Continue

RebelConspiracy_alertB3

→ oTxtRebelConspiracy_alertB3_1

RebelConspiracy_bt

→ oTxtRebelConspiracy_bt_empireCrew1

RebelHaulerStrike

oTxtRebelHaulerStrike_intro

You pass by a large number of cargo haulers loitering aimlessly. One of the haulers broadcasts a haranguing speech and is met by the wild cheers of the other hauler captains.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/5 each):
    • oTxtRebelHaulerStrike_investigateC

      The haulers decide to go on strike and begin ejecting their cargo into space. You help yourself to the bounty before an Imperial fleet arrives and forces you away.

      • Random large reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtRebelHaulerStrike_investigateD

      A few corsairs arrive at the scene. Some of the haulers transfer their cargo to the raider ships. One of the corsairs becomes suspicious of your presence and attacks.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtRebelHaulerStrike_investigateD2

      The other haulers and raiders are nowhere to be found. They must have been alarmed by your engagement and escaped while you were occupied.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtRebelHaulerStrike_investigateA

      Suddenly a small Imperial fleet drops out of subspace. They begin attacking the haulers, which scatter and flee in all directions. One of the Imperial ships heads for you.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
    • oTxtRebelHaulerStrike_investigateE

      A few corsairs arrive at the scene. Just as they begin to receive cargo from the haulers, several Imperial ships drop out of subspace and ambush the corsairs. The corsairs are caught by surprise and annihilated. You leave before the Imperials can turn their attention on you.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtRebelHaulerStrike_investigateB

      An Imperial patrol ship arrives and begins harassing the haulers.

      1. Continue
        oTxtRebelHaulerStrike_investigateB_1

        One of the haulers splits off from the rest and approaches close to the patrol ship. It detonates in a violent explosion, disabling the Imperial vessel.

        1. Continue
          oTxtRebelHaulerStrike_investigateB_2

          The haulers occupy themselves subduing the crew of the patrol ship, leaving you to salvage the wreck.

          • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
          1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtRebelHaulerStrike_leave

    You ignore the scene and press on.

    1. Continue
  3. (Launch Bay) Steal from the haulers
    oTxtRebelHaulerStrike_bt_launchBay

    The haulers appear oblivious as you launch a series of assault sleds to raid their cargo holds. While most are empty, you end up recovering some useful supplies before moving on.

    • Random cult equipment
    1. Continue

RebelHaulerStrike_bt

→ oTxtRebelHaulerStrike_bt_launchBay

RebelHaulerStrike_investigateB

→ oTxtRebelHaulerStrike_investigateB_1

RewardTest

oTxtRewardTest Unreachable — not referenced by any event

testing rewards here...

  • Random ship module
  • Imperial Sorcerer
  1. Continue

RiftLightningRod

oTxtRiftLightningRod_intro

You re-enter realspace at the edge of a great rift storm. In spite of its size it is remarkably calm, save a single region deep within the storm. You detect constant, massive lightning strikes within, paired with anomalous signals.

  1. Investigate
    oTxtRiftLightningRod_investigate1

    You brace yourself upon entry to the anomalous region. Your shields prove no match against the furious lightning about you.

    • −1–2 hull HP
    1. Continue
      oTxtRiftLightningRod_investigate2

      You discover the source of this lightning, a one man courier ship of the raider clans. Constant lightning strikes have left the ship disabled and heavily damaged. Its pilot begs you to save him.

      1. Rescue
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtRiftLightningRod_rescueB1

          Utterly thankless, the raider attempts to seize your ship once aboard. "A full ship on top of my cargo oughta earn me a nice promotion."

          • 1 Commando boarder
          1. Continue
          After the boarders are defeated:
          oTxtRiftLightningRod_rescueB2

          With its pilot slain, you fire upon the courier ship, hoping to destroy the cause of this anomaly. With the ship now in pieces, the lightning abates. You exit the rift storm post haste.

          1. Continue
        • oTxtRiftLightningRod_rescueA1

          The raider pilot drops to his knees upon entering your ship, the relative safety of your vessel a luxury he had nearly forgotten. You obliterate the courier ship, whatever cargo it held was surely the cause of the lightning strikes.

          1. Continue
            oTxtRiftLightningRod_rescueA2

            With the courier ship gone, so too is the terrible lightning. Wasting no time, you escape the rift storm. The raider, with neither a ship nor place to go, decides to serve on your ship.

            • Gain crew: Shock
            1. Continue
      2. (Imperial Scribe) Consult with the pilot
        oTxtRiftLightningRod_consult1

        Your scribe, recognizing the anomaly at hand, requests to speak with the pilot. Inquiring into the courier's cargo, the raider details the main object of his trip: delivery of an alabaster brazier, decorated with sunstone. "A live Riftdrain," your scribe says, "Judging by its design, its age stretches back millennia." He guides the raider on deactivating this ancient device.

        1. Continue
          oTxtRiftLightningRod_consult2

          The lightning attacking the courier ship comes to an abrupt stop, yet the rift storm remains calm overall. You use this opportunity to tow the ship out of the storm. Once in empty space, the raider pilot requests to join your ship. "I'm much safer running with you guys." He transfers over the courier ship's cargo as well.

          • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
          • Random support consumable
          • Gain crew: Shock
          1. Continue
      3. Leave
        oTxtRiftLightningRod_leave2

        This raider's misfortune is not your own. You leave him to the storm.

        1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtRiftLightningRod_leave1

    This subspace anomaly poses too great a risk. You move on.

    1. Continue

SectorStart

oTxtSectorStart Unreachable — not referenced by any event

[...]

  1. Continue

SectorStart2

oTxtSectorStart2_finalBoss Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Understand that once you have entered the Vault of Souls, there is no turning back.

  1. Continue
oTxtSectorStart2_portent1 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Whispers echo through your mind.

  1. Continue
    oTxtSectorStart2_portent2

    Through me is the way to the city of woe. Through me is the way to sorrow eternal. Through me is the way to the lost below.

    1. Continue

Shipyard

→ oTxtShipyard
oTxtShipyard_artilleryConfirm Unreachable — not referenced by any event

DEPRECATED You search your surroundings and find enough parts to assemble a few makeshift rounds for your artillery systems.

  1. Continue
oTxtShipyard_gainHusk Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You scavenge the shipyard for corpses and manage to cobble together a functioning husk.

  • Husk
  1. Continue
oTxtShipyard_healCrewConfirm Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You conceal your vessel and power down your reactor, providing your crew with a brief respite from their duties.

  • Fully heal all crew
  1. Continue
oTxtShipyard_hullRepairConfirm Unreachable — not referenced by any event

While most of the shipyard's facilities had long been abandoned, a small handful are still functional. You use them to repair your hull.

  • +10 hull HP
  1. Continue
oTxtShipyard_missileAmmoConfirm Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You scavenge the shipyard and find some intact munitions.

  • 6–9 missiles
  1. Continue

ShrineNodeBlood

oTxtShrineNodeBlood_intro

You come across a small, deserted outpost. Nothing appears out of the ordinary, save for a pattern of complex sigils pointing toward the single flayed corpse nailed to the entrance. It is a shrine to an ancient god.

  1. Continue
    oTxtShrineNodeBlood_intro2

    A ship suddenly emerges from hiding. It bears the darkly regal heraldry of the Blood Cults. "You will be my sacrifice to Ariok."

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Blood ship
    After the rewards from the fight:
    oTxtShrineNodeBlood_afterFight

    After disposing of the enemy ship, you set your sights on the shrine ahead. It pulses with malevolent fire, fed from the sacrifice of the destroyed vessel.

    1. Pledge yourself to the blood god
      oTxtShrineNodeBlood_pledge

      You resolve to pledge yourself to the blood god. Further sacrifice must be made.

      1. Offer your blood
        oTxtShrineNodeBlood_offerBlood

        A wave of perverse energy floods your psyche. Blood trickles from your eyes. The pain is excruciating.

        1. Continue
          oTxtShrineNodeBlood_offerBlood2

          Delirious with agony, you begin to lose consciousness. You feel something akin to rapture.

          • Commander damaged (50%)
          1. Continue
            oTxtShrineNodeBlood_offerBlood3

            You wake up to discover a strange brand carved into your neck. It bleeds.

            • Debuff applied to commander
            1. Continue
      2. Cancel
        oTxtShrineNodeBlood_cancel

        You decide against it. You leave the shrine and move on with your journey.

        1. Continue
    2. Leave
      oTxtShrineNodeBlood_leave

      You continue onwards with your journey.

      1. Continue

ShrineNodeDeath

oTxtShrineNodeDeath_intro

A great mass of human bones is visible ahead. As you move closer, you realize it is an abandoned medical outpost, so covered with human remains as to be nearly unrecognizable. A death cult warship guards the entrance.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Plague ship
After the rewards from the fight:
oTxtShrineNodeDeath_afterFight

With the enemy ship neutralized, you approach the grisly outpost and discover a shrine within. Strangely, you hear the buzzing of flies, even though none are present.

  1. Pledge yourself to the god of death
    oTxtShrineNodeDeath_pledge

    You resolve to pledge yourself to the god of death. Further sacrifice must be made.

    1. Embrace mortality
      oTxtShrineNodeDeath_embrace

      The atmospheric temperature suddenly drops with a supernatural chill. The command deck goes dark. A sickening, crushing weight seizes you.

      • Destroy Sensors
      1. Continue
        oTxtShrineNodeDeath_embrace2

        Suddenly you hear the rasping of shredded throats. The dead are crawling out of the walls.

        • minCt–maxCt crew boarders
        • 2 Greater Corpulent or Ghoul Magus boarders
        1. Continue
        After the boarders are defeated:
        oTxtShrineNodeDeath_embrace3

        With the undead horde dealt with, you suddenly feel a wave of nausea. Your skin develops a sickly pallor and your eyes weep with blackened fluid. You cough up a handful of your own teeth.

        1. Continue
          oTxtShrineNodeDeath_embrace4

          You tear open your shirt to discover a hideous rune carved into your chest. Maggots squirm in the open wound.

          • Debuff applied to commander
          1. Continue
    2. Cancel
      oTxtShrineNodeDeath_cancel

      You reconsider your decision and continue onwards with your journey.

      1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtShrineNodeDeath_leave

    You move on.

    1. Continue

ShrineNodeEmpire

oTxtShrineNodeEmpire_intro

You arrive at an old Imperial Shrine. Its structure is worn, but its solemn presence commands respect.

  1. Pledge yourself to the Imperial Cult
    oTxtShrineNodeEmpire_pledge

    You swear fealty and devotion to the Imperial Cult. A radiant warmth floods your veins, like the embrace of the sun itself, igniting your faith with renewed strength.

    • Fully heal all crew
    • +10 hull HP
    1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtShrineNodeEmpire_leave

    You do not have time for such superstition. You recalibrate your astrogation instruments and prepare to jump.

    1. Continue

ShrineNodeRaider

oTxtShrineNodeRaider_intro

You come across a hidden outpost of the raider clans. Though it appears to have been deserted long ago, there is a still-functioning signal relay in one of the side antechambers.

  1. Continue
    oTxtShrineNodeRaider_intro2

    The relay utilizes an ancient and powerful technology capable of broadcasting messages at ranges far greater than conventional equipment. It is said that certain raider clans use this technology for managing their networks of operatives across the galaxy. You have the opportunity to broadcast a transmission.

    1. Pledge allegiance to a raider clan
      oTxtShrineNodeRaider_pledge

      You broadcast a transmission, announcing your allegiance to whichever clan this relay belongs to. You set the transmission to loop continuously and move on with your journey.

      1. Continue
        oTxtShrineNodeRaider_pledge2

        Several days pass, and an encrypted message arrives, along with a small package. "We hear you. We will put the word out. You better not mess this up. We will be watching."

        • Random small reward
        1. Continue
    2. Move on
      oTxtShrineNodeRaider_leave

      You move on.

      1. Continue

ShrineNodeTechno

oTxtShrineNodeTechno_intro

There is a monument to the Machine God here. It bristles with arcane circuitry and ghostly data streams. You make your way inside and locate an active terminal.

  1. Pledge yourself to the Machine God
    oTxtShrineNodeTechno_pledge

    You resolve to pledge yourself to the Machine God. You access the terminal.

    1. Continue
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtShrineNodeTechno_riddle1

        "The Divine Machine requires precision in thought. Which of these truths governs all systems?"

        1. Emotion is truth
          oTxtShrineNodeTechno_denied

          "Denied. Corrupt process threads detected. Initializing secure erasure protocol."

          1. Continue
            oTxtShrineNodeTechno_denied2

            The terminal goes dark. You are able to make it back to your ship moments before the atmospheric generators cut out. A technocult warship is waiting for you, weapons primed.

            1. Continue
            Fight! vs Techno ship
        2. Order sustains function
          oTxtShrineNodeTechno_confirmed1

          "Confirmed. Incrementing."

          1. Continue
            Random outcome (1/3 each):
            • oTxtShrineNodeTechno_riddle4

              "The system fails. You are the cause. Do you feel guilt?"

              1. Yes, I failed
                → oTxtShrineNodeTechno_denied
              2. No, failure is data
                oTxtShrineNodeTechno_confirmed2

                "Confirmed. Incrementing."

                1. Continue
                  Random outcome (1/2 each):
                  • oTxtShrineNodeTechno_riddle6

                    "A worker unit sacrifices itself to prevent a system collapse. Do you celebrate its devotion?"

                    1. Yes, it served with honor
                      → oTxtShrineNodeTechno_denied
                    2. No, it merely functioned as intended
                      oTxtShrineNodeTechno_pass

                      "All diagnostics concluded. Logical coherence maintained."

                      1. Continue
                        oTxtShrineNodeTechno_pass2

                        "Your devotion has been recorded. The Divine Machine accepts your service. New resource allocations have been sanctioned."

                        • Random small reward
                        1. Continue
                    3. I mourn its loss
                      → oTxtShrineNodeTechno_denied
                  • oTxtShrineNodeTechno_riddle7

                    "A human engineer pleads for mercy as the Machine god requires their dismantling. How do you react?"

                    1. Spare them, they can still serve
                      → oTxtShrineNodeTechno_denied
                    2. Grant them mercy, but carry out the command
                      → oTxtShrineNodeTechno_denied
                    3. Proceed without hesitation
                      → oTxtShrineNodeTechno_pass
              3. I will atone
                → oTxtShrineNodeTechno_denied
            • oTxtShrineNodeTechno_riddle5

              "Two units malfunction. One is repaired, the other is scrapped for parts. How do you justify this?"

              1. Resources must be optimized
                → oTxtShrineNodeTechno_confirmed2
              2. The strongest survive
                → oTxtShrineNodeTechno_denied
              3. It is unfair
                → oTxtShrineNodeTechno_denied
            • oTxtShrineNodeTechno_riddle3

              "A machine has served its purpose and is no longer efficient. It is decommissioned. What do you feel?"

              1. Relief
                → oTxtShrineNodeTechno_denied
              2. Regret
                → oTxtShrineNodeTechno_denied
              3. Nothing
                → oTxtShrineNodeTechno_confirmed2
        3. Chaos reigns supreme
          → oTxtShrineNodeTechno_denied
      • oTxtShrineNodeTechno_riddle2

        "The flow of data is constant, but there are those who disrupt it. How does the faithful servant act?"

        1. Correct the error
          → oTxtShrineNodeTechno_confirmed1
        2. Punish the disruptor
          → oTxtShrineNodeTechno_denied
        3. Leave it to fate
          → oTxtShrineNodeTechno_denied
  2. Leave
    oTxtShrineNodeTechno_leave

    You decide to press onwards.

    1. Continue
oTxtShrineNodeTechno_confirmed3 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

"Confirmed. Incrementing."

  1. Continue
    → oTxtShrineNodeTechno_pass

ShrineNodeWar

oTxtShrineNodeWar_intro

An ominous spiked structure drifts silently in your viewport. It is a shrine to an ancient war god.

  1. Continue
    oTxtShrineNodeWar_intro2

    As you approach, your sensors detect an incoming hostile signature.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs War ship
    After the rewards from the fight:
    oTxtShrineNodeWar_afterFight

    No new threats detected. You return to the shrine.

    • Crew recalled to ship
    1. Pledge yourself to the god of war
      oTxtShrineNodeWar_pledge

      You pledge yourself to the god of war. Moments later, you find yourself surrounded by dozens of cult warships. You begin spooling your engines, preparing to engage. But you soon realize they are attacking each other. Not you. You consider joining the battle.

      1. Join the fight
        oTxtShrineNodeWar_join

        A supernatural rage overwhelms your senses. You activate your combat systems and target the nearest ship.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs War ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        oTxtShrineNodeWar_join1

        The enemy has been defeated.

        • Crew recalled to ship
        1. Continue
          oTxtShrineNodeWar_join2

          With the enemy defeated, another ship confronts you. It is enormous. Its commander proclaims, "I am Krom's champion."

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs specific room ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          oTxtShrineNodeWar_win

          The Kromic champion has been defeated. There is a brief period of silence and stillness.

          1. Continue
            oTxtShrineNodeWar_win2

            A sudden flash of argent lightning pierces the stellar backdrop and sends a shockwave rippling across the battlezone. Every cultist vessel winks out of existence. Your ship is alone once more.

            1. Continue
              oTxtShrineNodeWar_win3

              Your hands tremble uncontrollably. A white hot sigil has been burned onto the inside of your left wrist. You feel an insatiable lust for battle.

              • Debuff applied to commander
              1. Continue
      2. Perform a tactical retreat
        oTxtShrineNodeWar_retreat

        You reconsider your pledge to the god of war and perform a tactical retreat.

        1. Continue
    2. Leave
      oTxtShrineNodeWar_leave

      You press on.

      1. Continue
oTxtShrineNodeWar_intro3 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You open a comms channel but it is no use. You receive nothing but incoherent screaming.

  1. Continue

SpellForget

oTxtSpellForget Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Forget this psychic power?

SpellLearn

oTxtSpellLearn Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Consume psychogram to teach your crew a new psychic power?

SpellSlotToUnequip

oTxtSpellSlotToUnequip Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Your crew must unequip an item before equipping a new one. Which item do you wish to unequip?

Squadron

oTxtSquadron Unreachable — not referenced by any event

A squadron of […] hostile ships appears on your sensors. You approach the first vessel.

Squadron2

oTxtSquadron2 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You scavenge what you can from the wreck as your augurs alert you to the approach of a second vessel.

  • Random small reward

SquadronEnd

oTxtSquadronEnd Unreachable — not referenced by any event

A shockwave rips through the void as the last ship is obliterated. You scavenge the remains of the enemy squadron and find a substantial amount of useful material.

  • Reward: "large"

SquadronLastShip

oTxtSquadronLastShip Unreachable — not referenced by any event

The enemy vessel is neutralized. You maneuver to engage the remaining ship.

StartBossFightDemon1

oTxtStartBossFightDemon1 Unreachable — not referenced by any event

The enemy vessel begins to come apart, but you notice a dark shape looming within the debris. An infernal force has taken over and transformed the crew of the ship into grotesque creatures. Operated by a malign intelligence, the vessel renews its attack.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs specific room ship
After the fight: → oTxtBossFight_win

StartBossFightTier0

oTxtStartBossFightTier0_blood Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You encounter an ominous-looking vessel. Adorned with the runes and sigils of the blood cults, this vessel could only be one of the great enslavement engines used by the fell nobles to subjugate entire worlds. "The Vault of Souls is sealed, but I shall be the one to deliver it to Ariok."

After the fight: → oTxtBossFight_win
Trigger (CurrentCrewKilled): → oTxtBossEventSpawnDemonHeart_blood_start
oTxtStartBossFightTier0_empire Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You find yourself before a great and forbidding Imperial vessel. "None shall break this seal, not least the likes of you."

  1. Continue
    → oTxtBossEventAttack
After the fight: → oTxtBossFight_win
Trigger (CurrentCrewKilled): → oTxtBossEventSpawnDemonHeart_empire_start
oTxtStartBossFightTier0_plague Unreachable — not referenced by any event

A ponderous vessel drifts into view. Once an instrument of a venerated champion who swore to uphold a sacred oath, its twisted hull has been ravaged by centuries of subspace corruption. "The Vault of Souls can only be reached through the halls of death."

  1. Continue
    → oTxtBossEventAttack
After the fight: → oTxtBossFight_win
Trigger (CurrentCrewKilled): → oTxtBossEventSpawnDemonHeart_plague_start
oTxtStartBossFightTier0_war Unreachable — not referenced by any event

A vast and terrible vessel appears before you. It is a doom engine, an infernal monument to interminable war. Its transmissions are incoherent, but the hostile intent is clear.

  1. Continue
    → oTxtBossEventAttack
After the fight: → oTxtBossFight_win
Trigger (CurrentCrewKilled): → oTxtBossEventSpawnDemonHeart_war_start

StartFightBlood

oTxtStartFightBlood Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Soon after arrival you discover multiple ships adrift, all missing their crews. Suddenly a vessel emerges from the shadow of a nearby planet, weapons firing.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Blood ship

StartFightBloodAsteroid

oTxtStartFightBloodAsteroid Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You find yourself taking fire from a demon-possessed starship.

  1. Continue
Fight! vs Possessed ship
oTxtStartFightBloodPossessed_boarders Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Moments after your arrival, a tidal wave of dark energy washes over your ship. Disturbing apparitions appear across your security feeds. In the distance, a decrepit warship lurches forward, weapons aimed directly at you.

  • 1–2 Fleshling, Tendril, Stygian Worm, Fiend, or Blood Golem boarders
oTxtStartFightBloodPossessed_weather Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Your ship tumbles back into realspace. An enemy vessel moves toward you, bringing along with it a maelstrom of dark energy.

After the rewards from the fight:
oTxtWarpStormSubsides

The subspace maelstrom subsides.

  1. Continue
oTxtStartFightBloodPossessed_weatherBoarders Unreachable — not referenced by any event

Your vessel emerges inside a large riftstorm and immediately begins taking fire. To make matters worse, a massive energy spike was detected in the lower decks. You have been boarded.

  • 1–2 Fleshling, Tendril, Stygian Worm, Fiend, or Blood Golem boarders
After the rewards from the fight: → oTxtWarpStormSubsides

StartHegemonColonyThreat

oTxtStartHegemonColonyThreat Unreachable — not referenced by any event

A heavily-armed starship of the Imperial Solar Hegemon is threatening to destroy a nearby civilian colony for refusing to recognize the authority of the Hegemon. The colony's prefect has contacted you requesting help.

  1. Defend the colony
    Random outcome (1/3 each):

    • → oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_defendB
    • → oTxtHegemonColonyThreat_defendC

StrangeWordsOnStone

oTxtStrangeWordsOnStone_intro

You come across the shattered remnants of what must have once been a gigantic, stone edifice, floating in space. At the top of the largest chunk of stone is a sequence of strange words.

  1. Read the words
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtStrangeWordsOnStone_readB

      "Ygh'nom gthr'kyk no-shab'lgnfk, ga, wgh'ultm!" Reality tears open and a fell starship emerges, all weapons firing.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs War ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtStrangeWordsOnStone_readB2

      A frozen corpse drifts past you. It suddenly becomes animated, eyes lit with unnatural life. It speaks. "What a strong warrior you are!"

      1. Demand to know what's going on
        oTxtStrangeWordsOnStone_demand

        "Just doing a little housekeeping. Lord Haima finds these brutes extremely distasteful, and he loves how enthusiastic you are about getting rid of them. Ah, here comes another one. Keep up the good work."

        1. Continue
      2. Shoot the corpse
        oTxtStrangeWordsOnStone_shoot

        You fire upon the corpse. It explodes into crimson shards of frozen blood. Another corpse twists its head around to look at you. "That was rude." The corpse opens its hand revealing a miniature death cult ship. The ship expands to full size and opens fire.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Plague ship
    • oTxtStrangeWordsOnStone_readA

      "Mgle'df fthagsh, ngah-cgf'ak rylag vut'lytk." You hear a faint noise, like the sound of someone screaming far off in the distance. The screaming gets louder and louder, until eventually it threatens to burst your eardrums. Sorcerous fire explodes onto your deck, and you realize you are under attack.

      • 3–6 Hound or Skeleton boarders
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs War ship
    • oTxtStrangeWordsOnStone_readC

      "Ur'dgh wdshk'lsh cf'tagna shk'lgh, shk'lgh." An explosion of light blinds you for a second, then you realize an Imperial warship is floating in front of you. You hear their confusion over the comms. "...what the hell just happened? Where are we? And who is... oh Throne, all power to weapons! Attack, attack, attack!"

      • 2–3 Demonic Crew boarders
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
  2. Ignore it and move on

StygianRaid

oTxtStygianRaid_intro

As you enter the system, both you and your crew are overwhelmed by a sensation of intense pressure. Blood drips from your nose and you struggle to draw breath. Through the view ports of the ship you see countless subspace rifts opening and unmaking themselves, like waves, liquid black drenching the star-spotted canvas of the void.

  1. Continue
    oTxtStygianRaid_intro2

    A distant voice speaks to you, straining and otherworldly. "The Drowned Lord requires your assistance, and offers its favour. Drown the servants of the Radiant God that defile a nearby place of power, and you will gain my master's blessing."

    1. Nod
      oTxtStygianRaid_accept

      The pressure abates, transforming into the soothing balm of cool water. You open your eyes; in your palm is a black pearl. In a rush, you see a planet - no, a planetary system. You recognise that it is near. You give the order to move on.

      • Quest marker added to map: oTxtStygianRaidQuest_intro
      1. Continue
    2. Shake your head
      oTxtStygianRaid_decline

      "A pity," the voice says. The sensation of pressure increases. You quickly prepare your vessel to jump away.

      1. Continue

StygianRaidQuest

oTxtStygianRaidQuest_intro

You enter the system and initiate a scan with your multispex. You discover an Imperial construction site for a new habitat on the orbit of a large oceanic world hidden behind a gas giant. Why the Empire desires to colonise this sunless realm eludes you, but the voice of the deep has guided you here.

  1. Extinguish the colony's light for good
    oTxtStygianRaidQuest_attack

    You approach the site, weapons ready. An Imperial warship moves to defend the station. You catch the enemy by surprise and start combat with an advantage.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Empire ship
    After the rewards from the fight:
    oTxtStygianRaidQuest_attack_victory

    You turn your vessel towards the partially constructed habitat and fire upon the defenseless station until it falls into a decaying orbit. The roiling atmosphere of the stygian planet soon swallows the remnants raining down upon it.

    1. Continue
      oTxtStygianRaidQuest_attack_victory2

      Suddenly, the pearl you received earlier vibrates and cracks open. Jet black tentacles unfurl from within, revealing strange abyssal artifacts.

      • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
      • Random cult consumable
      • Random cult consumable
      • Dark Matter Prism ×2
      1. Continue

StygianRaidQuest_attack

→ oTxtStygianRaidQuest_attack_victory

Surrender

oTxtSurrender_generic Unreachable — not referenced by any event

"Enough! You have proved your point. Take these supplies and let us go."

  • Combat ends
  • Default post-fight reward (scrap, missiles, or choice reward)
  1. Accept their surrender
  2. Refuse

TecNothing

oTxtTecNothing_intro

[...]

  1. Continue

TechRetrieval

oTxtTechRetrieval_intro

You arrive at the next system. Its sun burnt out millennia earlier, leaving a ghostly white remnant star and a few desolate planetoids. You receive a message from a technocult research outpost orbiting the star. A union of cybernetic voices scream in a strange language. Eventually, the cacophony resolves into the flat intonations of a machine priest. It tells you how the outpost was raided by pirates, and requests their elimination and retrieval of valuable technology. You are given coordinates to a nearby system and the machine priest expects your compliance.

  1. Accept the mission
    oTxtTechRetrieval_accept

    You accept the machine priest's offer. It lets out a sharp metallic gurgle and you can only assume that it is satisfied with your answer.

    • Quest marker added to map: oTxtTechRetrievalQuest_intro
    1. Continue
  2. Decline the offer
    oTxtTechRetrieval_decline

    You turn down the machine priest's offer. Displeased, the cyborg disconnects from the ship comms.

    1. Continue

TechRetrievalQuest

oTxtTechRetrievalQuest_intro

Arriving at the system, you quickly detect the raider ships which attacked the technocultist outpost. As the machine priest informed you earlier, the ships are refueling and making some repairs. They have not spotted your ship yet. You note that the ships are located in an open stretch of space, making a stealthy approach impossible.

  1. Continue
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtTechRetrievalQuest_attack2

      Your ship engines roar as you attack the enemies. They turn towards you and attack. An alarm rings: your ship has been boarded.

      • 2–3 Raider crew boarders
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider ship
      After the fight:
      oTxtTechRetrievalQuest_attack2_win

      The enemy ship has been rendered into burning scraps. You move to attack the remaining vessel.

      • Crew recalled to ship
      1. Continue
        oTxtTechRetrievalQuest_attack2_win1

        The second ship attempts to flee, and your scanners indicate that they are trying to initiate an emergency subspace jump.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Raider ship
        After the fight:
        oTxtTechRetrievalQuest_attack2_win2

        You manage to destroy the second raider ship before it flees. From its wreckage you salvage a strange device bearing the mark of the Machine Cult. You deliver it to a small satellite in the asteroid belt on the far edge of the system. The satellite's electronic intelligence dispenses a reward for a task well done.

        • Large amount of ⬗ Scrap
        • Moderate amount of missile ammo (5–8)
        • Repair Drone ×4
        1. Continue
    • oTxtTechRetrievalQuest_attack1

      You give the order for a direct attack and your crew takes battle stations. It does not take long for the enemy to notice your approach. The enemy ships charge toward you.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Raider ship
      After the fight:
      oTxtTechRetrievalQuest_attack1_win

      One of the enemy vessels breaks apart. You quickly prepare to deal with the remaining threats.

      • Crew recalled to ship
      1. Continue
        oTxtTechRetrievalQuest_attack1_win1

        The second ship turns towards you and attacks ferociously.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Raider ship
        After the rewards from the fight:
        oTxtTechRetrievalQuest_attack1_win2

        The raiders have been dealt with. Your scanners detect an energy signature in the wreckage: the stolen machine cult goods. It is a simple task to grab the storage container and drop it off at the small satellite orbiting the smallest planetoid in the system. An automatic reward is given to you.

        • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
        • Moderate amount of missile ammo (5–8)
        • Servo-Arm
        1. Continue

TechRetrievalQuest_attack1

→ oTxtTechRetrievalQuest_attack1_win

TechRetrievalQuest_attack2

→ oTxtTechRetrievalQuest_attack2_win

TechnoArmada

oTxtTechnoArmada_intro

You encounter a technocult armada at the jump node. These caravans are assembled by machine priests returning from their treasure hunting expeditions. It would be impossible to take on the entire fleet at once, but there may be a chance you remain undetected and pick off one of the prize vessels as the fleet attempts to jump out.

  1. Attack a vessel transporting armament
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtTechnoArmada_attackB

      The technocultists are vigilant and quickly jump out before you can close to engage.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtTechnoArmada_attackA

      You launch an attack on a straggling vessel and knock out its drive unit just as the rest of the fleet jumps out.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Techno ship
    • oTxtTechnoArmada_attackA4

      You launch an attack on a straggling vessel and knock out its drive unit just as the rest of the fleet jumps out.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Techno ship
  2. Attack a vessel transporting equipment
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • → oTxtTechnoArmada_attackB
    • oTxtTechnoArmada_attackA2

      You launch an attack on a straggling vessel and knock out its drive unit just as the rest of the fleet jumps out.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Techno ship
  3. Attack a vessel transporting captives
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • → oTxtTechnoArmada_attackB
    • oTxtTechnoArmada_attackA3

      You launch an attack on a straggling vessel and knock out its drive unit just as the rest of the fleet jumps out.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Techno ship
  4. Leave
    oTxtTechnoArmada_leave

    You move on.

    1. Continue
  5. (Machine Priest) Lure in a random vessel
    oTxtTechnoArmada_bt_technocult

    Among your crew is a technocult machine priest. They spoof an ident code of another vessel within the caravan, requesting assistance. One of the prize vessels approaches to dock with your ship in response.

    1. Continue
      oTxtTechnoArmada_bt_technocult1

      You quickly assault the prize vessel, overwhelming the surprised machine cultists within. The remaining vessels of the caravan flee, but you are left with a mostly intact haul.

      • Random large reward
      1. Continue
oTxtTechnoArmada_attackC Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You move to attack. Unfortunately, the technocultists are vigilant and well-prepared against your attack. You sustain a number of

  1. Continue

TechnoArmada_bt

→ oTxtTechnoArmada_bt_technocult

TechnoHiredGun

oTxtTechnoHiredGun_intro

You discover a large deep-space construction project. Several technocult vessels are arrayed around it, actively engaged in the ongoing work. Despite the machine priests' reputation for being reclusive, they occasionally require assistance.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtTechnoHiredGun_investigateB

      One of the vessels peels off to attack you. Evidently, the technocultists wish to keep whatever it is they are building away from prying eyes.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Techno ship
    • oTxtTechnoHiredGun_investigateA

      The technocultists report that their construction efforts have been delayed by repeated raider attacks. They wish to enlist your aid to ward off the next expected raid.

      1. Defend the technocultists
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtTechnoHiredGun_defendA

          A raider vessel arrives as forecasted. They seem surprised at your presence, but nevertheless initiate an attack. Intruder alarms blare as enemies teleport aboard your vessel.

          • 2–3 Raider crew boarders
          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Raider ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          oTxtTechnoHiredGun_defendA_1

          "All events have progressed in accord with the Machine God's calculations." The technocultists are pleased with your performance. "A servo-kin will be allocated permanently to your service as payment."

          • Combat Husk
          1. Continue
        • oTxtTechnoHiredGun_defendB

          A raider vessel arrives as forecasted. They are alarmed at your presence, and promptly flee.

          1. Continue
            oTxtTechnoHiredGun_defendB_1

            The machine priests are satisfied with the outcome and pay you a moderate sum.

            • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
            1. Continue
      2. Decline
        oTxtTechnoHiredGun_investigateLeave

        You close the comms channel and move on.

        1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtTechnoHiredGun_leave

    You leave the technocultists alone and proceed onwards.

    1. Continue
  3. (Servo-Excavator) Assist with the construction
    oTxtTechnoHiredGun_bt_excavator

    The machine priests welcome the additional heavy lift capabilities of your excavator unit. They had lost their own excavator to a raider attack not too long before. The technocultists are able to complete construction well ahead of their previous estimates. They hurriedly depart, leaving you to scavenge their leftover equipment.

    • Random moderate reward
    1. Continue

TechnoHiredGun_bt

→ oTxtTechnoHiredGun_bt_excavator

TechnoHiredGun_defendA

→ oTxtTechnoHiredGun_defendA_1

TechnoHiredGun_defendB

→ oTxtTechnoHiredGun_defendB_1

TechnoListeningPost

oTxtTechnoListeningPost_intro

You arrive at an uncharted system and discover a small listening post hidden within an asteroid field. You learn that it is broadcasting an emergency signal on all frequencies. Once you have a visual of the station, you see that it is badly damaged.

  1. Investigate the listening post
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtTechnoListeningPost_investigateB

      The moment your scouts step onto the station an alarm is tripped and a shrieking klaxon comes to life. A small Technocult vessel detaches from the structure and engages your ship.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Techno Small ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      oTxtTechnoListeningPost_investigateB_win

      The station belongs to the Technocult, that much is clear. Now that the station's defenses are dealt with, you investigate the listening station and see that its surface is covered with circuit-like glyphs. Your scouts once again explore the station proper and discover an encrypted signal that fills the ship's multispex with a shrill static.

      1. Decrypt the emergency signal
        oTxtTechnoListeningPost_investigateB_rescue

        Your crew successfully decrypts the signal. The digital shrieking is translated into coordinates to a hidden Technocult research station that was recently attacked by an unknown foe. An automated husk rack activates and gruesomely constructs a new crew member out of both organic and mechanical parts.

        • Servo-husk or Combat Husk
        • Quest marker added to map: oTxtTechnoListeningPostQuest_intro
        1. Continue
      2. Destroy the listening post and take what you can
        oTxtTechnoListeningPost_investigateB_kill

        The station's hulls can do nothing against the might of your weapon systems. The data-scream ceases and you manage to find some equipment.

        • Random moderate reward
        1. Continue
    • oTxtTechnoListeningPost_investigateA

      You investigate the listening post and see that its surface is covered with circuit-like glyphs used by the Technocult. As soon as your scouts take their first steps on this satellite, your ship's sensors are overwhelmed with piercing static.

      1. Decrypt the emergency signal
        Random outcome (1/3 each):
        • oTxtTechnoListeningPost_investigateA_rescue2

          Decrypting the signal activates the listening post's hidden defenses! A Technocult vessel appears on your multispex as a horde of hostile husks boards your ship.

          • 4–6 Husk boarders
          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Techno ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          oTxtTechnoListeningPost_investigateA_rescue2_win

          The biomechanical attackers are dead.

          • Random moderate reward
          1. Continue
        • oTxtTechnoListeningPost_investigateA_rescue3

          You attempt to decrypt the signal. Unfortunately it contains a profane code injection which sabotages your ship's systems and activates the remaining defenses of the station. A Technocult drone-ship attacks as your vessel is boarded by husks.

          • Ionize random system
          • 3–4 Husk, Servo-husk, or Combat Husk boarders
          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Techno ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          oTxtTechnoListeningPost_investigateA_rescue3_win

          The last cyborg lies dead at your feet. You take what you can and leave.

          • Random moderate reward
          1. Continue
        • oTxtTechnoListeningPost_investigateA_rescue

          You decrypt the signal successfully. The digital howling ceases and you receive coordinates for a secret Technocult fusion research site which was recently attacked. An automated husk rack activates, allowing you to construct a new crewmember before your very eyes.

          • Servo-husk or Combat Husk
          • Quest marker added to map: oTxtTechnoListeningPost_intro
          1. Continue
      2. Destroy the station and scavenge whatever remains
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtTechnoListeningPost_investigateA_kill

          Shaped charges quickly annihilate the listening post. The binaric screaming subsides, and you scavenge some equipment.

          • Random moderate reward
          1. Continue
        • oTxtTechnoListeningPost_investigateA_kill2

          Your ship's weapons make short work of the outpost but before its main generator detonates, a group of Technocult husks boards your ship.

          • 4–5 Husk, Servo-husk, or Combat Husk boarders
          1. Continue
          After the boarders are defeated:
          oTxtTechnoListeningPost_investigateA_kill2_win

          The husks have been decommissioned. You scavenge the remains and leave.

          • 2 random equipment items
          1. Continue
      3. Leave the station
        oTxtTechnoListeningPost_investigateA_moveOn

        There is something very wrong with the station and the strangely oscillating signal it is sending. You depart and leave the station to be explored by some other unfortunate souls.

        1. Continue
  2. Ignore the signal and scavenge the ship
    oTxtTechnoListeningPost_salvage

    Your crew quickly disassembles sections of the outpost and loads the scrap onto your ship.

    • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
    1. Continue
  3. Move on
    oTxtTechnoListeningPost_moveOn

    You move on.

    1. Continue

TechnoListeningPostQuest

oTxtTechnoListeningPostQuest_intro

You arrive at the coordinates you obtained from the listening post earlier, and you are met with an incredible sight: a gargantuan claw-like space station of immense size. In the space surrounded by the claw-structures is a small bead of condensed light. You quickly notice the station is badly damaged, yet miraculously it remains in operation. You have heard rumours of Technocultist fusion research stations, but no description does its true appearance any justice.

  1. Investigate the fusion research station
    oTxtTechnoListeningPostQuest_investigate

    Your scouts investigate the station thoroughly. The walls and doors are reinforced with lead and durasteel, yet even these materials show the signs of a past attack. Countless husks and machine cultist corpses litter the rooms and hallways that barely fit normal humans. Your scouts have found a large, sealed door and note that its highly advanced alarm systems are still powered. This is clearly a storage room and whatever they kept within must be valuable. This feels like a trap, but you are certain there is something valuable on the other side.

    1. Enter the room by force
      oTxtTechnoListeningPostQuest_enter

      The door to the storage room yields and the alarm is tripped. An enemy ship appears on your sensors. Your crew loots the storage room and rush back to your ship to prepare for combat.

      • Large amount of ⬗ Scrap
      • Auto-Disruptor or Heavy Ion Lance or Twin Disruptor
      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Techno ship
    2. Destroy the station
      oTxtTechnoListeningPostQuest_destroy

      A barrage from your weapons demolishes the entire station. The contained protostar collapses, bathing the remains in nuclear fire. Before it detonates, your crew manages to scavenge some scrap.

      • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
      • Random Technocult equipment
      1. Continue
        oTxtTechnoListeningPostQuest_destroy1

        As the station is destroyed, a raider assault ship emerges from the void and attacks.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Raider ship
  2. Leave
    oTxtTechnoListeningPostQuest_leave

    You leave the system.

    1. Continue

TechnoListeningPost_investigateA

→ oTxtTechnoListeningPost_investigateA_kill

TechnoListeningPost_investigateA_kill2

→ oTxtTechnoListeningPost_investigateA_kill2_win

TechnoListeningPost_investigateA_rescue2

→ oTxtTechnoListeningPost_investigateA_rescue2_win

TechnoListeningPost_investigateA_rescue3

→ oTxtTechnoListeningPost_investigateA_rescue3_win

TechnoListeningPost_investigateB

→ oTxtTechnoListeningPost_investigateB_kill

TechnoOrbitingMoon

oTxtTechnoOrbitingMoon_intro

You come across a Technocult vessel holding station over a large moon. It appears to be in the midst of conducting an excavation.

  1. Contact them
    oTxtTechnoOrbitingMoon_excavate

    "We detect a subterranean Class-4 artifact. We are currently re-calibrating excavators to break through final obstruction layers."

    1. Offer to assist
      Random outcome (1/3 each):
      • oTxtTechnoOrbitingMoon_assistB

        The machine priest's response is drowned out by an intense howling. Streams of demonic energy fly up from the surface of the moon and impact the Technocult vessel. Now possessed by an infernal intelligence, it turns to attack you.

        1. Continue
        Fight! vs Techno ship
      • oTxtTechnoOrbitingMoon_assistC

        The machine priest declines your assistance. It appears they have completed excavation.

        1. Continue
          oTxtTechnoOrbitingMoon_assistC_b

          The technopriests lift up an enormous black cube out from under the surface of the moon before hastily departing. You are able to collect some scrap left behind.

          • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
          1. Continue
      • oTxtTechnoOrbitingMoon_assistA

        "Assistance approved. Stand by to receive parameters." You adjust your armament and fire a salvo in accordance with the provided data. Your weapon fire craters through the surface, revealing a densely populated subterranean hab-arcology.

        1. Continue
          oTxtTechnoOrbitingMoon_assistA_b

          Thousands of smoking corpses are strewn about the debris. Bits of bloody meat and viscera stain the walls, ejected across the arcology by the destructive force of your weapons. The technopriests recover their artifact and congratulate you on a job well done.

          • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
          1. Continue
    2. Attempt to dissuade
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtTechnoOrbitingMoon_dissuadeB

        As you attempt your negotiation, the dig site is suddenly consumed in coruscating fire. The ground rips asunder to reveal a vast underground hab-arcology, its billions of denizens all contorting in strange and profane gestures skyward. A subspace shockwave slams into your vessel, while you pick up frantic comms bursts from the technocult ship.

        • 3–4 Monster crew boarders
        1. Continue
        After the boarders are defeated:
        oTxtTechnoOrbitingMoon_dissuadeB_win

        You have survived, but the machine priests were not so lucky. You strip their derelict vessel of intact parts and move on.

        • Random large reward
        1. Continue
      • oTxtTechnoOrbitingMoon_dissuadeA

        You request they search for artifacts elsewhere. "Unacceptable. Every artifact is a teaching of the Divine Machine. They exist to be reclaimed." The Technocult vessel fires its excavators and bores deeper into the moon.

        1. Continue
          oTxtTechnoOrbitingMoon_dissuadeA_b

          Suddenly the technocult ship breaks orbit and departs. It becomes quickly apparent that they miscalculated and accidentally destroyed their coveted artifact. You help yourself to the aftermath of their operation.

          • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
          1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtTechnoOrbitingMoon_leave

    You ignore the machine cult vessel and move on.

    1. Continue
  3. (Hex Priest) Terminate their excavation
    oTxtTechnoOrbitingMoon_bt_prosecutor

    A cascade of alarm transmissions goes out from the technocult vessel as their excavation equipment breaks down. Your machine priest prosecutor has mounted a systemic attack to confound the excavators efforts. Discovering themselves at an impasse, the technocult vessel sulks away. Perhaps they intend to return later, but they have been dissuaded for the moment.

    1. Continue
      oTxtTechnoOrbitingMoon_bt_prosecutor2

      Another starship appears nearby. It is marked with the insignia of the local system militia, and they are looking for the technocult vessel from before. You learn that the moon that had been the target of the excavations bears sacred meaning for the local colonies. Having been charged with defending the sacred moon, the captain of the militia starship thanks you for your part in protecting the moon from any further damage. They issue you a starship weapon of their local manufacture before sending you off.

      • Random ship weapon
      1. Continue

TechnoOrbitingMoon_assistA

→ oTxtTechnoOrbitingMoon_assistA_b

TechnoOrbitingMoon_assistC

→ oTxtTechnoOrbitingMoon_assistC_b

TechnoOrbitingMoon_bt

→ oTxtTechnoOrbitingMoon_bt_prosecutor

TechnoOrbitingMoon_dissuadeA

→ oTxtTechnoOrbitingMoon_dissuadeA_b

TechnoOrbitingMoon_dissuadeB

→ oTxtTechnoOrbitingMoon_dissuadeB_win

TechnoShrine

oTxtTechnoShrine_intro

Your ship drops out of subspace in front of a technocult shrine. The shrine seems to have been abandoned for some time, but there may still be valuable artifacts to be found within its walls.

  1. Explore the shrine
    Random outcome (1/7 each):
    • oTxtTechnoShrine_searchC

      You stumble upon a secret storage room filled with spare parts for advanced cybernetic implants.

      • Ocular-Laser Implants
      1. Continue
    • oTxtTechnoShrine_searchD

      You come across a well-preserved set of ancient armor. It appears to be made of a rare alloy and is still fully functional.

      • Chaos Armor
      1. Continue
    • oTxtTechnoShrine_searchG

      Your sensors pick up the presence of a hostile ship nearby. It belongs to a rival technocult sect that seeks to claim the shrine for themselves.

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Techno ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      Random outcome (1/3 each):
      • oTxtTechnoShrine_searchG_1

        You resume your search of the shrine, but find nothing of value.

        1. Continue
      • oTxtTechnoShrine_searchG_2

        You resume your search of the shrine and discover a few containers full of valuable scrap.

        • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
        1. Continue
      • oTxtTechnoShrine_searchG_3

        You resume your search of the shrine and discover a hidden vault within. Inside the vault, you find an unusual relic.

        • Random ship module
        1. Continue
    • oTxtTechnoShrine_searchB

      You discover a hidden armory within the shrine. Among the various weapons, you find a technologically advanced weapon that is still in good condition.

      • Ion Carbine
      1. Continue
    • oTxtTechnoShrine_searchE

      As you search the shrine, you hear a faint humming sound growing louder. Suddenly, a group of zealots emerge from the shadows and confronts you. They are determined to protect their shrine.

      • 2–3 Technocult crew boarders
      1. Continue
      After the boarders are defeated:
      oTxtTechnoShrine_searchE_1

      You search the bodies and find a holy relic of the Machine God.

      • Arc Rifle
      1. Continue
    • oTxtTechnoShrine_searchA

      As you explore the outer area of the shrine, you come across several hidden chambers filled with ancient machinery. Most of it appears to be too old to be of any use, but you do manage to salvage some valuable scrap.

      • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
      1. Continue
    • oTxtTechnoShrine_searchF

      You enter the main chamber of the shrine and encounter a group of raider merchants. They are looting the shrine and offer to sell you some of their finds.

      1. Purchase random equipment for 15 scrap
        oTxtTechnoShrine_purchase

        The raiders are satisfied with the transaction.

        • Random equipment item
        • −15 ⬗ Scrap
        1. Continue
      2. Decline
        oTxtTechnoShrine_decline

        You decline their offer.

        1. Continue
  2. Ignore the shrine
    oTxtTechnoShrine_ignore

    You ignore it and move on.

    1. Continue
  3. (Assault Cyborg) Secure the techno-artifacts
    oTxtTechnoShrine_bt_automaton

    You command your automaton to board the shrine and secure any techno-artifacts still remaining within. The shrine is in an advanced state of disrepair, and much of its automated defenses are operating in wildly unpredictable manners. Your combat automaton blasts its way through much of the berserk defensive network and locates a starship class weapon for retrieval.

    • Random ship weapon
    1. Continue

TechnoShrine_bt

→ oTxtTechnoShrine_bt_automaton

TechnoShrine_searchE

→ oTxtTechnoShrine_searchE_1

TechnoShrine_searchG

→ oTxtTechnoShrine_searchG_1

TechnoSurvivor

oTxtTechnoSurvivor_intro

You arrive to witness the last few moments of a fight between two technocult vessels. The victor quickly jumps away, leaving the crippled vessel to burn. Your sensors register increasing energy levels from the crippled ship's reactors. It may be moments away from a catastrophic explosion.

  1. Investigate
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtTechnoSurvivor_investigateA

      You close in further. Suddenly, the crippled technocult vessel explodes. A few chunks of hull plating slam into your hull, damaging it.

      • −1 hull HP
      1. Continue
    • oTxtTechnoSurvivor_investigateB

      The crippled vessel still contains valuable cargo aboard. Your sensors also register the faint life signs of a survivor on board. Unfortunately, the ship's reactor flux capacitors are destabilizing, and you will only have time to mount one recovery operation.

      1. Attempt to save the crewman
        Random outcome (1/2 each):
        • oTxtTechnoSurvivor_SaveA

          The rescued machine cultist recovers from his injuries, and elects to join your crew.

          • Technocult crew
          1. Continue
        • oTxtTechnoSurvivor_SaveB

          Unfortunately, the machine cultist did not survive. You dispose of the corpse and proceed onwards.

          1. Continue
      2. (Psyker) Psychically confine the flux capacitors
        oTxtTechnoSurvivor_ConfineBlue

        You successfully recover both the survivor and the equipment. The survivor makes a full recovery.

        • Random Technocult tool
        • Any Technocult crew
        1. Continue
      3. Salvage the equipment
        oTxtTechnoSurvivor_Salvage

        You salvage the equipment, then quickly pull away from the crippled vessel. The technocult ship disintegrates in a series of explosions as you speed away.

        • Random Technocult tool
        1. Continue
  2. Leave
    oTxtTechnoSurvivor_leave

    The danger is too great. You decide to leave.

    1. Continue
  3. (Machine Priest) Stabilize the reactors
    oTxtTechnoSurvivor_bt_technocult

    In spite of the risk of being caught in the impending explosion, you quickly maneuver your starship alongside the crippled technocult vessel. You send over an engineer priest of the machine cults. Working together with the surviving crew of the crippled vessel, they struggle to shunt excess flux out of the capacitors.

    1. Continue
      oTxtTechnoSurvivor_bt_technocult1

      Your machine priest crewmember manages to contain the subspace flux induction and stabilize the reactor at the last moment. The technocult vessel is saved from certain doom, but nevertheless needs to remain in place to conduct further repairs throughout the hull.

      1. Continue
        oTxtTechnoSurvivor_bt_technocult2

        The crew of the crippled vessel offers no gratitude. However, your machine priest manages to liberate an artifact from the crippled vessel's cargo vaults before returning to your vessel.

        • Random armor item
        1. Continue
oTxtTechnoSurvivor_Save Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You recover a battered machine cultist and evacuate the area. The crippled vessel explodes in a great conflagration.

  1. Continue

TechnoSurvivor_bt

→ oTxtTechnoSurvivor_bt_technocult

TechnoTransportPair

oTxtTechnoTransportPair_intro

A pair of technocult couriers have been separated from a larger fleet and are vulnerable to attack. One is carrying some combination of relics and advanced technology. The other is carrying starship armaments.

  1. Attack the vessel transporting the relics
    oTxtTechnoTransportPair_secureRelics1

    You maneuver to attack.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Techno ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtTechnoTransportPair_secureRelics2

    You recover the relics from the remains

    • Random ship module
    1. Continue
  2. Attack the vessel transporting the armaments
    oTxtTechnoTransportPair_secureWeapons1

    You advance on the target.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Techno ship
    After the fight:
    oTxtTechnoTransportPair_secureWeapons2

    You recover the weapons from the remains.

    • Random ship weapon
    1. Continue
  3. Let them pass
    oTxtTechnoTransportPair_letPass

    You decide to let them pass.

    1. Continue

TechnoVortexPrism

oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_intro

You arrive in orbit around a barren, desert moon. A preliminary scan of the moon's surface reveals a series of geometric tunnel structures clustered around the south pole, likely the remnants of some long lost civilization.

  1. Investigate the structures
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_investigateA

      You search the tunnel structures and find nothing but a few skeletons.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_investigateB

      You approach the tunnel structures but soon learn that every single entrance is sealed. You have no way in. You continue looking around and find a few abandoned supply containers half-buried in the sand. You decide to bring them back to your ship.

      • Random small reward
      1. Continue
    • oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_investigateC

      You search the tunnel structures and discover an unusual, black prism. It pulses with supernatural energy. You bring it back to your ship.

      1. Continue
        oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_investigateC2

        Back in orbit, a technocult vessel approaches. They explain that the prism is extremely dangerous and demand you hand it over.

        1. Hand over the prism
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_handOverA

            You transfer over the prism. The machine priests send you a small payment for your compliance.

            • Random small reward
            1. Continue
          • oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_handOverB

            You make preparations to hand over the prism when a second technocult ship drops in and blasts the the first ship apart. "The prism is too dangerous. It must be destroyed."

            1. Continue
            Fight! vs Techno ship
            After the rewards from the fight:
            oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_handOverB2

            Three more technocult ships tear out of subspace and speed toward you. Two of them seem to notice each other and begin firing at one another. The third one opens a comms channel. "Quickly, give me the prism before the others arrive. "

            1. Give them the prism
              Random outcome (1/2 each):
              • oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_givePrismA

                You comply with their wishes and hand over the prism. They leave you a small cargo container for your efforts, then activate their drives and jump out.

                • Random small reward
                1. Continue
                  oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_givePrismA2

                  The remaining two technocult ships end up destroying each other in battle. You salvage what you can from the remains.

                  • Random small reward
                  1. Continue
              • oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_givePrismB

                You hastily make the exchange with the machine priest. As soon as the prism is in their hands, they power up their subspace drives and jump out.

                1. Continue
                  oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_givePrismB2

                  The victor of the remaining two ships exclaims, "No! That prism contains the algorithms responsible for maintaining the structure of realspace in this region! In the wrong hands, reality could collapse, causing subspace to -- " The machine priest is interrupted as a sudden subspace rift opens up near his ship and swallows it whole.

                  1. Continue
                    Random outcome (1/2 each):
                    • oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_givePrismB2_1

                      More and more rifts appear, and suddenly the entire region is blanketed in subspace energy. You set drives to full burn and manage to escape before taking any serious damage.

                      1. Continue
                    • oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_givePrismB2_2

                      More rifts appear and soon the entire region is blanketed by them. You manage to escape the maelstrom, but not before a few arcing streaks of subspace energy impact your hull.

                      • 3–4 warp breaches spawned
                      1. Continue
            2. Refuse
              oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_refuseGive

              "Then you are a greater fool than I anticipated." They look as if they are about to attack when one of the earlier two ships, having emerged victorious from combat, opens fire at the third ship. You manage to slip away while they are occupied.

              1. Continue
        2. Refuse
          oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_refuse

          "You cannot comprehend the magnitude of what you are dealing with."

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Techno ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_refuseA

            Heeding the machine priest's last words, you keep the creature in an ultra high security storage vault until you can figure out what to do with it.

            1. Continue
              oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_refuseA2

              Several days later, you arrive at a civilian colony and manage to trade the prism to a local trader for some old equipment.

              • 2 random equipment items
              1. Continue
          • oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_refuseB

            You store the prism in a stasis pod while you figure out what to do with it.

            1. Continue
              oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_refuseB2

              Days pass. You have some disturbing dreams but otherwise nothing out of the ordinary has happened. Eventually you end up selling the prism to local merchant for a paltry sum.

              • Random small reward
              1. Continue
  2. Ignore it
  3. (Machine Priest) Explore the tunnels
    oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_bt_technocult

    The tunnels are labyrinthine. It is owing to the calculative capabilities of your machine priest crewmember that you are able to quickly navigate through the tunnel complexes. At the center of one of the structures is a cryopod.

    1. Continue
      oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_bt_technocult1

      You wake the cryopod's occupant. With nowhere else to go, they join your crew.

      • Cultist crew
      1. Continue
oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_refuseGive2A Unreachable — not referenced by any event

A few days later you end up pawning off the prism to a rare goods collector at a local civilian colony. Within hours, large riftstorms begin appearing around the colony, but by then you are long gone.

  • Random small reward
  1. Continue
oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_refuseGive2B Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You end up trading the prism away for some old equipment at a nearby raider outpost.

  • 1 random equipment item
  1. Continue
    oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_refuseGive2B_2

    As you pull away from the outpost, multiple riftstorms suddenly coalesce on its position. Your ship suffers some minor damage.

    • 2–3 breaches spawned
    1. Continue

TechnoVortexPrism_bt

→ oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_bt_technocult

TechnoVortexPrism_givePrismB2

→ oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_givePrismB2_1

TechnoVortexPrism_refuseGive2B

→ oTxtTechnoVortexPrism_refuseGive2B_2

TechnocultAttackEmpire

oTxtTechnocultAttackEmpire_intro

You detect an Imperial ship under attack by a technocult vessel.

  1. Attack the technocult ship
    oTxtTechnocultAttackEmpire_attack

    You move to engage the technocult vessel.

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs Techno ship
    After the rewards from the fight:
    Random outcome (1/3 each):
    • oTxtTechnocultAttackEmpire_winA

      The Imperial commander does not respond to you, although the vessel ejects a small supply crate before jumping out.

      • Random Imperial psychic item
      1. Continue
    • oTxtTechnocultAttackEmpire_winB

      With the enemy ship neutralized, you turn your attention back to the Imperial ship. Unfortunately it appears to have escaped during your combat.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtTechnocultAttackEmpire_winC

      An Imperial reinforcement fleet arrives and begins engaging you. You escape, but not without suffering minor damage.

      • Crew recalled to ship
      • 2–3 breaches spawned
      1. Continue
  2. Leave the Imperials to their fate
    oTxtTechnocultAttackEmpire_leave

    You watch as the technocult vessel overpowers the Imperial ship. You move in to recover what valuables are left after the technocult vessel jumps away.

    • Small amount of ⬗ Scrap
    1. Continue
  3. (Hull Reaper) Attack the Imperial ship
    oTxtTechnocultAttackEmpire_bt_hullReaper1

    You prime your hull reaper system and engage the beleaguered Imperial ship. Your vessel's hull is regenerated as the Imperial ship crumbles beneath the assault.

    • +3–5 hull HP
    1. Continue
      oTxtTechnocultAttackEmpire_bt_hullReaper2

      The technocult vessel promptly continues on its way, unconcerned by your appearance. You salvage the remnants of the destroyed Imperial ship and move on.

      • Random moderate reward
      1. Continue

TechnocultAttackEmpire_bt

→ oTxtTechnocultAttackEmpire_bt_hullReaper1

Template

oTxtTemplate Unreachable — not referenced by any event
  1. Continue
    Unknown node: -1

TetrarchyMagistrate

oTxtTetrarchyMagistrate_intro

You come across a small shuttle carrying a high-ranking magistrate of the Imperial Tetrarchy. The magistrate seems desperate. "Those blasted Hegemon fateseers accused me of treason, and now they are trying to have me executed. Get me out of here, and I promise I will make it worth your while."

  1. Help the magistrate
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtTetrarchyMagistrate_helpA

      You bring the magistrate aboard your ship and input coordinates for a nearby star. Moments later, an Imperial arbitrator vessel drops in from subspace. "Unknown vessel, you are guilty of piracy and consorting with traitors. The punishment is death."

      1. Continue
      Fight! vs Empire ship
      After the rewards from the fight:
      Random outcome (1/2 each):
      • oTxtTetrarchyMagistrate_helpA1

        After spending several days in transit, you finally make it to your destination and drop off the magistrate. He transfers you a substantial reward.

        • Random large reward
        1. Continue
      • oTxtTetrarchyMagistrate_helpA2

        Several days later, you are ambushed by a ship belonging to the Cult of War. As you report to battle stations, you hear an explosion coming from the lower decks and realize that the magistrate has sabotaged your shield generators and fled your ship in a lifeboat. The cult warship picks up the lifeboat. You receive one final transmission from the magistrate. "My people have come for me. Your part in this is over. "

        • choose(1, 2, 3) system damage to Shields
        1. Continue
        Fight! vs War ship
    • oTxtTetrarchyMagistrate_helpB

      You agree to help the magistrate. After getting him aboard your ship, you set course for a nearby system.

      1. Continue
        oTxtTetrarchyMagistrate_helpB2

        Two days into your journey, you are stopped by a group of naval officers at an Imperial checkpoint. "Hand over the magistrate, or prepare to face the consequences."

        1. Hand over the magistrate
          oTxtTetrarchyMagistrate_handOver1

          The magistrate looks at you, stunned. "You double-crossing snake..."

          1. Continue
            oTxtTetrarchyMagistrate_handOver2

            The Imperial officers apprehend the magistrate. "You have saved us a lot of trouble by bringing us this dangerous criminal. For that, we are willing to look the other way with regards to your illegal armaments. Take this bounty payment and be on your way."

            • Moderate amount of ⬗ Scrap
            1. Continue
        2. Refuse
          oTxtTetrarchyMagistrate_refuse1

          "Then you leave us no choice."

          1. Continue
          Fight! vs Empire ship
          After the rewards from the fight:
          Random outcome (1/2 each):
          • oTxtTetrarchyMagistrate_refuse1A

            You manage to arrive at your destination and drop off the magistrate. He thanks you for the help and transfers you a reward.

            • Random small reward
            1. Continue
          • oTxtTetrarchyMagistrate_refuse1B

            Unfortunately the magistrate must have panicked during the battle and accidentally got himself locked outside the airlock. His frozen corpse is seen drifting just a few meters beyond your viewport.

            1. Continue
  2. Refuse
    Random outcome (1/2 each):
    • oTxtTetrarchyMagistrate_refuseB

      You refuse to help.

      1. Continue
    • oTxtTetrarchyMagistrate_refuseA

      "You swine, you better hope they kill me. If I ever find you again, you are dead."

      1. Continue

TormentSkip

oTxtTormentSkip Unreachable — not referenced by any event

You come across a subspace rupture of unprecendented magnitude. It bleeds distortion across the fabric of space-time. Within the rupture lies the promise of unseen terrors and crueler fates.

  1. Approach the rupture
    oTxtTormentSkip_fight

    As you approach the rupture, your sensors begin issuing a warning alert. A hostile entity has been detected. Your viewscreen flickers to life. […]

    1. Continue
    Fight! vs specific room ship
  2. Ignore it
    oTxtTormentSkip_ignore

    You decide to avoid the rupture.

oTxtTormentSkip_fightWin Unreachable — not referenced by any event

The thing that stood watch dies without ceremony, as if this moment had already been decided. Ahead, the subspace rupture awaits.

  1. (Increase game difficulty) Pass through the rupture
    oTxtTormentSkip_fightWin_passThrough

    You pass through the rupture. The galaxy stirs. The stars recoil.

    • Skip four difficulty levels
  2. Leave
oTxtTormentSkip_skipTorment Unreachable — not referenced by any event
